title: Specialist, SAP Master Data
description: About The Company e.l.f. Beauty, Inc. stands with every eye, lip, face and paw. Our deep commitment to clean, cruelty free beauty at an incredible value has fueled the success of our flagship brand e.l.f. Cosmetics since 2004 and driven our portfolio expansion. Today, our multi-brand portfolio includes e.l.f. Cosmetics, e.l.f. SKIN, pioneering clean beauty brand Well People, Naturium, high-performance, biocompatible, clinically-effective and accessible skin care, and our newest brand, rhode, a line of curated skincare essentials, formulated for a variety of skin types and needs with high performance ingredients, it’s a daily routine that nourishes your skin barrier over time. In our Fiscal year 25, we had net sales of $1 Billion and our business performance has been nothing short of extraordinary with 29 consecutive quarters of net sales growth. We are the #2 mass cosmetics brand in the US and are the fastest growing mass cosmetics brand among the top 5. Our total compensation philosophy offers every full-time new hire competitive pay and benefits, bonus eligibility (200% of target over the last four fiscal years), equity, and a hybrid 3 day in office, 2 day at home work environment. We believe the combination of our unique culture, total compensation, workplace flexibility and care for the team is unmatched across not just beauty but any industry. Visit our Career Page to learn more about our team: https://www.elfbeauty.com/work-with-us Position Summary The SAP Master Data Steward is responsible for managing e.l.f.’s data assets and ensuring their quality and usefulness. In addition, the SAP Data Steward will partner with Operations, Supply Chain, and IT to ensure data accuracy in downstream systems and root cause data issues. They will be responsible for ensuring that the organization's data is accurate, current, usable, accessible, safe, and trusted throughout its lifecycle. The MD Steward will act as a data curator, focusing on data awareness, accessibility, releases, and management. Place of work: Ahmedabad, Gujarat/India-Remote Shift Timings: 4:30 PM to 1:30 AM IST Responsibilities Data Collection: Defining data collection, reporting, and quality assurance processes. Working closely with the PLM Master Data Steward on source data, while creating SAP-specific master data Data Issues: Proactively identify, root cause, and resolve data quality issues — and propose systemic fixes to prevent recurrence Data Advocacy: Championing data best practices and enabling data owners through training and communications compliance with governance policies and procedures Data Governance & Quality: Lead the development and continuous improvement of master data governance policies and scalable workflows Drive data requirements for system enhancements, new integrations, and data model changes — from scoping through UAT Identify opportunities to automate, streamline, or improve master data processes; propose and pilot solutions in partnership with IT and Operations Requirements 2-4 years of experience working with SAP Material Master Data, with demonstrated ability to identify process gaps and drive improvements. Experience with Centric and/or PLMs a plus Bachelor’s degree required Beauty industry, retail background and/or product life cycle management strongly preferred Excellent communication skills both verbal and written Excellent analytical, follow-up, and attention to detail skills Ability to work autonomously across functions to meet deadlines and drive tasks to completion System implementation experience a plus Proficient in Microsoft Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) This job description is intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed in this position. It also reflects the general details considered necessary to describe the principal functions of the job identified, and shall not be considered, as detailed description of all the work required inherent in the job. It is not an exhaustive list of responsibilities, and it is subject to changes and exceptions at the supervisors’ discretion. e.l.f. Beauty respects your privacy. Please see our Job Applicant Privacy Notice (www.elfbeauty.com/us-job-applicant-privacy-notice) for how your personal information is used and shared. We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. title: Capgemini Engineering_Data Engineer & Reporting_Stage_Aix-en-Provence
description: Capgemini Engineering, leader mondial des services d'ingénierie , rassemble des équipes d'ingénieurs , de scientifiques et d'architectes pour aider les entreprises les plus innovantes dans le monde à libérer leur potentiel . Des voitures autonomes aux robots qui sauvent des vies, nos experts en technologies digitales et logicielles sortent des sentiers battus en fournissant des services uniques de R&D et d'ingénierie dans tous les secteurs d'activité . Rejoignez nous pour une carrière pleine d'opportunités , où vous pouvez faire la différence et où aucun jour ne se ressemble Vos Missions Au sein du projet Ordering, tu joueras un rôle clé dans l'exploitation et la valorisation des données pour les équipes de Capgemini et un de nos client dans l'aéronautique. Dans Ce Rôle, Tu Seras Amené(e) à Assurer l'extraction hebdomadaire des données SAP de manière fiable et structurée, en garantissant la disponibilité des informations pour les différentes équipes Transformer les données brutes en rapports clairs et visuels, directement exploitables par les équipes métier, en utilisant Excel et les outils du Pack Office Développer et automatiser des scripts (Python, Google Apps Script / JavaScript) pour optimiser les processus de traitement de la donnée et gagner en efficacité Construire et suivre des KPIs pertinents afin d'orienter le travail des équipes et de faciliter la prise de décision Être l'interlocuteur(trice) de référence pour répondre aux questions des différentes parties prenantes ayant besoin des données et des rapports Votre Profil Nous recherchons un(e) étudiant(e) en 4ème ou 5ème année d'école d'ingénieur ou de master, avec les compétences et qualités suivantes : Maîtrise de Python et JavaScript (Google Apps Script) pour l'automatisation et le traitement des données Bonne maîtrise d'Excel et du Pack Office avec une capacité à produire des livrables clairs et exploitables Esprit analytique et de synthèse avec la capacité de transformer des données complexes en indicateurs clés de performance (KPIs) compréhensibles par tous Sens de l'organisation et rigueur, indispensables pour assurer des extractions hebdomadaires fiables et dans les délais Aisance relationnelle et communication claire, pour interagir efficacement avec des interlocuteurs variés aux besoins différents Compétences appréciées en plus : Connaissance de SAP, Looker Studio ou du domaine de la Supply Chain 3 Raisons De Nous Rejoindre Qualité de vie au travail : accord de télétravail en France et à l’international, accord sur l’égalité professionnelle, la parentalité, l’équilibre des temps et la mobilité durable. Apprentissage en continu : certifications et formations en libre accès, accompagnement sur mesure avec votre career manager, parcours d’intégration sur 9 mois. Avantages groupe & CSE : plan actionnariat, activités à tarifs préférentiels, remboursement partiel vacances, remboursement de votre abonnement sportif ou culturel. Nos Engagements Et Priorités Le groupe Capgemini encourage une culture inclusive dans un cadre multiculturel et handi-accueillant. En nous rejoignant, vous intégrez un collectif qui valorise la diversité, développe le potentiel de ses talents, s’engage dans des initiatives solidaires avec ses partenaires, et se mobilise pour réduire son impact environnemental sur tous ses sites et auprès de ses clients. Verbal Communication Leader mondial des services d’ingénierie et de R&D, Capgemini Engineering met en oeuvre une connaissance sectorielle approfondie avec la maîtrise des dernières technologies digitales et logicielles pour accompagner la convergence des mondes physique et numérique. Avec plus de 55 000 ingénieurs et scientifiques dans plus de 30 pays, nous aidons nos clients à accélérer leur transformation vers l'Intelligent Industry. Get The Future You Want* |www.capgemini.com/fr fr Capgemini, le futur que vous voulez Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Full-time Job function Information Technology Industries Engineering Services Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Capgemini Engineering by 2x See who you know

title: Administrativ@ de Compras
description: PORCELANOSA GRUPO O Grupo Porcelanosa, com mais de 50 anos de história, estamos presentes em mais de 140 países em todo o mundo e somos uma marca de referência a nível mundial em desenho e qualidade de produto. Durante toda a nossa história sempre estivemos na vanguarda do nosso setor, especializando-nos na fabricação e comercialização de pavimentos e revestimentos cerâmicos e produtos naturais. O nosso desafio é sempre melhorar, por isso queremos incorporar na nossa empresa um@ Administrativ@ de Compras paraa a nossa delegação em Lisboa com experiência para continuarmos a crescer e assegurar que a nossa marca continua a ser uma referência a nível mundial. Quando faças parte da nossa equipa, encontrarás uma ampla gama de produtos de máxima qualidade de um nível tecnológico inovador para ajudar-te a conseguir os teus objetivos. Vamos ajudar-te a materializar os teus projetos, sendo que a confiança que depositamos nas nossas equipas é um dos aspetos mais valorizados na nossa equipa. Oferecemos acima de tudo a tua estabilidade, formação e um plano profissional que te vai permitir desenvolver todo o teu potencial, mas acima de tudo, um compromisso mútuo para juntos consolidar o êxito ao longo da nossa história. Queres formar parte desta equipa? Residência na área de Cascais, Oeiras ou Sintra. Experiência prévia em funções similares. Conhecimento essencial de SAP. Gosto pelo trabalho em equipa. Bons conhecimentos de Excel. Bons conhecimentos de Inglês e Espanhol. Proatividade, dinamismo e grande capacidade de resistência ao stress e resolução de problemas. title: SAP Developer
description: Are you looking for a new challenge as a SAP Developer? Does working at the Head Office of one of the most progressive Medical Device Manufacturers sound exciting? Applied Medical gives you the opportunity to contribute to our global mission of high standards of integrity and social responsibility. The SAP Developer is responsible for designing, developing, testing, and supporting SAP solutions that meet business requirements and organizational objectives. Working within a collaborative team environment, this team member participates in technical project activities, requirements-gathering sessions, and solution design discussions. Leveraging a strong understanding of SAP development principles and best practices, the SAP Developer contributes to application design and technical architecture, evaluates solution alternatives, and develops, documents, and unit tests software solutions to ensure quality, reliability, and alignment with business and user needs. The SAP Developer collaborates with functional and technical stakeholders to deliver project objectives, provides technical expertise and guidance as needed, and supports system enhancements, troubleshooting, and ongoing maintenance to ensure the stability and performance of SAP applications. You will be part of the Application Development team at our European Head office in Amersfoort (NL) and report to the Manager Applications. Key Responsibilities Manage development activities for project implementations, enhancements, and ongoing application support Design, develop and maintain ABAP objects and configuration including reports, interfaces, user-exits, BAPIs, BADIs, SmartForms, LSMW, RESTful services using OData Service, and dialog programs Participate in requirements gatherings and investigating alternatives. Research and evaluate relevant technologies and present alternative solutions Develop technical specifications and participate in the development, testing, implementation, and documentation of solutions What You Bring Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Systems, Software Engineering, or equivalent technical experience Three or more years of ABAP programming experience, including user-exits, ALV reports, Web Dynpro, interfaces, workflows, BAPI, BAdIs, Smartforms, Adobe Forms, LSMW, dialog programs, OData, CDS, and HANA views Proven experience in modifying standard SAP Fiori Applications and developing custom Fiori Applications Strong knowledge of ABAP Objects and modern ABAP (version 7.40+) Recent experience with SAP S/4HANA and familiarity with multiple SAP modules, such as FI, CO, PP, PLM, SD, LE, WM, QM, PM Understanding relational databases and Structured Query Language (SQL) Strong analytical skills with the ability to quickly comprehend technical information Excellent verbal and written communication skills Ability to manage and prioritize multiple tasks in a team environment Familiarity with project management methodologies such as Waterfall, Agile, and Scrum Preferred Qualifications Knowledge of SAP technologies such as ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), Fiori Elements, and SAP BTP Understanding and experience in cGMP validated environment Understanding and experience with SAP SuccessFactors integrations What We Offer An international working environment, fueled by respect and a healthy work-life balance tailored to the business and team member’s needs 27 paid vacation days (based on fulltime contract) with option to purchase five additional days Commuting allowance of €0,23/km or full coverage of public transportation costs A wide variety of internal trainings and development possibilities and a tuition program for external trainings The opportunity to pro-actively work on your vitality and fitness State-of-the-art facilities, including green areas, in-house restaurant and gym Additional benefits, such as: Attractive pension scheme (your contribution at 4%, ours at 12%) €50 net monthly health insurance contribution Two (paid) days per year dedicated to volunteering activities Several fun company events and meaningful initiatives organized throughout the year At Applied Medical, we are committed to make a meaningful, positive difference, each and every day. Within our European headquarters in Amersfoort (NL), we develop, manufacture and support the implementation of our advanced medical devices across Europe and the MENA region. Applied’s team members enjoy working in an environmentally friendly and international environment, in which people of diverse backgrounds are valued, challenged, and acknowledged. We support our team members by giving them opportunities of ongoing learning and education, possibilities to take part in community volunteering activities and a variety of onsite exercise classes and health activities. If you are passionate about working in an organization that allows you to make decisions based upon what is right for the patient, customer, community and environment, Applied Medical is the place for you. Interested? Would you like to be part of our expanding team? If the answer is yes, we'd love to get to know you and look forward to hearing from you! For more information regarding our hiring process, click here. If you have any questions, feel free to contact us via TA-Europe@appliedmedical.com or +31 (0)33 7548713. title: Senior Consultant SAP Supply Chain Management
description: Would you like to play an important role within the evolution of supply chain management within one of the fastest growing SAP consultancy teams in the Netherlands and become part of our team? Then read on and apply for this vacancy! At KPMG, we are actively expanding our SAP consultancy department, with a focus on supply chain solutions. By joining this young team within a renowned company, you enjoy a lot of benefits. Working for KPMG means that we share our knowledge and expertise on an international level. Joining this team gives you the opportunity to contribute to the growth of our SAP department. We offer you the opportunity to develop your expertise in digital transformation while also supporting the growth of your team members. Our flexible approach welcomes new ideas. Join our team and help shape the future of SAP consulting at KPMG. Your challenges As Senior Consultant SAP Supply Chain Management, you’ll be responsible for working of S/4HANA transformations by translating business requirements into solutions designs and configuring/testing S/4HANA solutions in international business transformations for our clients. How do you deal with the interests of different stakeholders? How do you make the translation between business and IT? How do you ensure that SAP Supply Chain solutions meet the customer's business objectives? How do you integrate the latest digital trends within SAP? How do you contribute to large-scale transformation projects? How do you standardise complex business processes by using best practices? How do you ensure good cooperation within an international team? How do you share your knowledge and experience with starting SAP consultants to support their development? This is what you bring 2+ years of hands-on SAP Supply Chain experience (Planning, Production, Logistics, Operations, etc.). SAP implementation or consulting project experience. A proactive, self-motivated attitude and the ability to work as part of a team. Motivated to participate in both Dutch and international projects and willing to travel for work. Familiarity with current technology trends and a passion for digital innovation. Completed university Master's degree. Preferably in business, IT, or engineering. Willingness and drive to learn and grow, and to help make the team even more successful. Proficiency in Dutch and English. Not sure whether to apply because you don’t meet every requirement? If this role excites you and you see yourself in the profile, we’d love to hear from you. Because we believe talent grows and shows up in many different ways. What will you do? As a Senior Consultant Digital Transformation SAP Supply Chain, you will be involved in large national and international projects. Within these projects, you advise our clients on how they can carry out their business transformation using SAP, or how they can get more value out of their SAP system. This can take place before, during or after an implementation. As a senior consultant, your focus is not only on your personal goals, but you will also be given more responsibilities, ranging from determining the strategic direction within our projects and/or team, to coaching junior team members. Together with experienced colleagues and the development opportunities KPMG will offer you, our goal is to lead every project to a successful outcome. ‘As a member of our Platforms SAP department, you will be immersed in an environment of constant change. Learning quickly is key and you are immediately embraced as part of the team.’ Within our projects, you play an essential role by supporting and guiding the business through all stages of SAP implementation. Your expertise ranges from process and system design to change management, testing and training. Think of specific projects such as optimising processes within SAP, setting up shared service centres, or implementing, integrating and dismantling SAP environments during mergers and acquisitions. You are also involved in optimising master data, reporting or monitoring. These tasks form a challenging and varied portfolio in which your analytical skills and eye for detail are essential. Every day offers a new opportunity to take on challenges, find solutions and make a positive impact within our organization, together with our customers. This is what we offer Gross salary between € 4.000,- and € 6.700,- per month depending on your work experience; Annual performance bonus, mobility allowance, a fixed expense allowance, and a working from home allowance of €2.40 per working day. Pension accrual without a compulsory personal contribution. 30 vacation days (on a full-time basis) and the option to buy more days or sell your vacation days. At KPMG we work hybrid, so you can work from home or at the office. A completely furnished home office. A laptop and iPhone which also can be used privately. Choice to pick from different courses which contribute to your own personal and professional development. Diversity networks in the areas of pride, gender, ability, cultural diversity, and interests that regularly organize various activities to celebrate differences! Focus on well-being! There is a gym at the Amstelveen office or you can get a discount for a gym near your house and you get access to different health and/or vitality programs. ‘Together’ is one of our core values. So you can count on different social activities, like team events, drinks with colleagues and events with all your KPMG colleagues. Application procedure Step 1. Your application When you apply, we will see if we find a match with your resume and the job requirements. The next step is usually an online assessment. This assessment consists of an aptitude test and a personality questionnaire. The results provide insight into your preferred behavior and competencies. Step 2. The first interview After a positive completion of the assessment, we will invite you for a first interview with a recruiter and a colleague from the relevant department. Here, we mainly want to get to know you, and you also get the chance to get to know us. Step 3. Follow-up interview Everyone enthusiastic? In many cases, a second meeting follows. During the second interview, we will delve deeper into the role and the substantive themes you will be dealing with. A business case may be part of the follow-up interview. Sometimes there is a final interview. Step 4. Offer Do we have a match? Then you will receive an offer from us to start at KPMG. The offer will not only include your salary but also more about our complete package of employment conditions, such as your pension, vacation days, and more! Do you say “yes”? Then we congratulate you as our new colleague and take you to the very last step. Step 5. Screening We want to be an integral and reliable organization in all respects. Therefore, we screen everyone who comes to work with us. DISA conducts this screening and checks references, among other things. Is everything in order? Then the path to a great career at KPMG is open to you. You will then start with the onboarding program. Would you like to know more about our application procedure? Then contact HR Recruitment via recruitment@kpmg.nl. title: Customer Solutions Analyst - Supply Chain
description: Customer Solution Analyst – Supply Chain, dit is je uitdaging Als Customer Solution Analyst ben jij verantwoordelijk voor het leveren van uitstekende service aan onze klanten en het efficiënt beheren van de order-to-cash flow. Je werkt nauw samen met ons magazijn en logistieke dienstverleners om te zorgen dat onze dagelijkse leveringen op tijd en foutloos worden uitgevoerd. In deze rol werk je met een divers productportfolio, variërend van voedingssupplementen en medische voeding (Health & Nutrition) tot food, koffie en diervoeding. Met jouw inzet zorgen we ervoor dat onze producten elke dag weer beschikbaar zijn voor onze consumenten. Als Customer Solution Analyst heb je de volgende verantwoordelijkheden: Beantwoorden van logistieke klantvragen via telefoon en e-mail; Het beheren van het serviceniveau en de klanttevredenheid van jouw eigen klantportfolio; Het onderzoeken en oplossen van operationele uitdagingen in de order-to-cash flow; Fungeren als het centrale aanspreekpunt voor onze klanten, logistieke dienstverlener en Nestlé collega’s in o.a. Nederland, België en Portugal. Als Customer Solution Analyst rapporteer je aan de Customer Solutions Manager en ben je deel van de afdeling Operations binnen Nestlé Nederland. Dit breng jij voor de vacature Customer Solution Analyst mee: Je hebt passie voor het opbouwen van relaties met klanten en vindt het interessant om een proces te overzien; je zoekt continu naar verbeteringen voor onze klanten, maar vindt het ook niet erg om in de dagelijkse operatie te werken; je hebt affiniteit met tools zoals Excel, kennis van PowerBI en SAP is een pré; uitstekende beheersing van de Nederlandse en Engelse taal; Operationele werkervaring (bijvoorbeeld in supermarkt of distributiecentrum) is een pré; daarnaast ben je zelfstandig en stressbestendig, geen dag is hetzelfde bij Nestlé. De vacature biedt meer dan alleen een uitdaging: Een fulltime functie (39 uur per week); uitstekende arbeidsvoorwaarden, waaronder een 13e maand, 25 vakantiedagen en 6 ATV dagen; good food, good life activiteiten, zoals dagelijks gratis fruit en groente, een gezond en gevarieerd voedingsaanbod in ons restaurant, korting op een fitness abonnement & Onefit; goede mogelijkheden om jezelf te ontwikkelen middels trainingen en coaching; een veelheid aan professionele trainings- en ontwikkelingsprogramma’s; verschillende carrièremogelijkheden, zowel in Nederland als internationaal; professionele werkomgeving, teamgeest, bedrijfstrots en gedreven collega’s. je draagt bij aan de missie van Nestlé om de kwaliteit van leven te verbeteren en levert daarmee een bijdrage aan een betere toekomst. En uiteraard mag jij je hond meenemen naar kantoor! Respect staat centraal in de cultuur van Nestlé, een belangrijk onderdeel daarvan is ruimte voor verschillen. Verschillen in mensen die onze producten kopen én bij ons op de werkvloer. We vinden het belangrijk dat iedereen zich welkom en thuis voelt bij Nestlé, ongeacht je gender, geloof, culturele achtergrond, leeftijd, beperking of van wie je houdt. We zijn ervan overtuigd dat diversiteit leidt tot meer creativiteit en innovatie en onze organisatie vooruithelpt. Daarom werken we actief aan een cultuur en leiderschap dat gelijke kansen biedt voor iedereen! Solliciteren naar de vacature Customer Solution Analyst - Supply Chain? Onze recruiters staan voor je klaar om je vragen te beantwoorden. Stuur je vraag naar careers@nl.nestle.com. title: SAP S/4HANA Data Transformation Advisor (all levels)
description: The work Accenture is looking for a strategic SAP S/4HANA Data Transformation Advisor to help our clients navigate their S/4HANA transformation journeys. In this role, you will be helping to shape enterprise-wide data strategies, implementing robust governance frameworks, and guiding the seamless transformation of data across SAP landscapes. Working as part of our SAP Advisory Practice, you’ll collaborate with business and IT stakeholders across domains such as Finance, Supply Chain, and HR. You’ll help shape data architectures, guide technology adoption, and ensure that clients are ready to scale and innovate with clean, consistent, and connected data at the core of their operations. Our ideal candidate brings expertise in one or more of the following areas: Data Governance and Master Data Management Analytics and Reporting enablement on SAP platforms Data Migration and Transformation Responsibilities Data Strategy & Governance: Engage with diverse stakeholders from data owners to C-suite executives to align IT and data strategies with overall business goals, shaping roadmaps for S/4HANA transformations. Define and implement data governance frameworks, including ownership models, stewardship roles, and escalation paths. Act as a trusted advisor for SAP Master Data Governance (MDG), supporting clients in designing governance models and workflows for core data domains (e.g., customer, vendor, material, finance). Promote data as a strategic asset by aligning governance with broader business and compliance requirements. Stay informed on industry trends and regulatory changes that impact enterprise data governance. Contribute to business development by identifying opportunities to expand our advisory services across governance, migration, and analytics. Foster a learning culture by mentoring team members and sharing expertise across the SAP data transformation space Responsibilities Data Design & Blueprinting: Facilitate workshops to assess current-state data processes and define future-state designs across key functional areas. Lead the design of end-to-end data solutions for either SAP MDG, SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC), or SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). Support the development of enterprise data models and architecture that enable high-quality analytics and insights. Document and validate data requirements with business and IT stakeholders across Finance, Supply Chain, HR, and other domains. Translate business reporting needs into scalable analytics solutions using SAP HANA Cloud and SAC. Ensure that blueprinting activities are informed by both governance needs and migration feasibility. Responsibilities Data Integration & Enablement: Provide advisory guidance on data migration strategies early in the transformation lifecycle, including scope definition, tooling selection, and sequencing. Recommend and support tools such as SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit and SAP Data Services for structured data movement and harmonization. Advise clients on data profiling, quality assessment, and cleansing initiatives to improve readiness for transformation. Support the enablement of integrated analytics by aligning SAP Datasphere and BDC solutions with clean, governed source data. Ensure that foundational data structures support downstream enablement of analytics, AI/ML innovations, and real-time reporting. Who We Are – Accenture Technology Through unmatched industry experience, leading technologies from our ecosystem partners and startups, and the largest delivery network in the world, we provide a powerful range of capabilities that can be tailored to our client’s most complex business needs. With over 100 innovation hubs deployed around the world, we help clients continuously innovate at speed and at scale so they can outpace their peers. You will bring innovation, intelligence, and industry experience together with the newest technologies to help clients innovate at scale and transform their businesses. Technology is one of five services that make up one Accenture – the others are Strategy & Consulting, Operations, Song and Industry X. Here’s What You’ll Need Master's degree in Information Technology, Business Administration, or a related field or bachelor degree and strong relevant experience 2 - 10+ years of consulting experience in SAP data-related domains (Master Data Management/Governance, Data Warehouse and Analytics, S/4HANA migration). Strong communication skills in English (Dutch is a plus), with the ability to convey complex data topics to business-centric audiences. Strong analytical, problem-solving, and strategic thinking skills. Ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear and concise business language. Proven experience in conducting blueprint workshops and documenting business requirements Experience with SAP Activate methodology. Strong project management and organizational skills. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Ability to travel as required. Bonus points if you have SAP Data related certification or other formal SAP certifications. Hands on experience with either: SAP MDG or similar master data governance tools; or SAP BW/4HANA, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Datasphere or embedded analytics; or SAP S/4HANA data migration and transformation Knowledge of S/4HANA business case development, selective data transition methodologies, or advanced analytics frameworks. Previous leadership experience—project oversight, stakeholder engagement, or people management—that can drive transformative outcomes. What’s in it for you? Accenture is a global company with a world-class brand and reputation. Our people are Change Makers, and to support them as an employer, we make five promises: Work with flexibility, in a culture based on trust Embrace a sustainable growth mindset Live a human-led culture of Inclusion & Diversity by caring deeply for all our people. Lead and transform our clients with technology Connect with our global network of 700.000 colleagues These promises are reflected through our excellent employee benefits, including: Unlimited learning: access to thousands of free courses, classes and workshops. Flexible working hours (4x9) & work location and FlexDay; a flexible day you can take off to celebrate what matters to you – from EidalFitr to a birthday. 26 vacation days per year Paid transport (budget for electric bicycle or car, NS-Business card) A laptop, iPhone, and home office setup allowance What We Believe Accenture is an equal opportunity employer. Our leaders are committed to building a better, stronger and more equitable company for future generations to create positive, long-lasting change. Inclusion and diversity are fundamental to our culture and core values. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Our rich diversity makes us more innovative and creative, which helps us better serve our clients and our communities. Our position as partner to many of the world’s leading businesses, organizations and governments affords us both an extraordinary opportunity and a tremendous responsibility to make a difference. Sustainability is one of our greatest responsibilities, which we embed it into everything we do and for everyone we work with. Accenture is committed to providing equal employment opportunities for all. title: SAP S/4HANA Roadmap & Business Transformation Advisor (all levels)

In this role, you will be instrumental in developing comprehensive S/4HANA roadmaps, aligning business objectives with technology solutions, and ensuring successful transitions. You will be a trusted advisor, leveraging your deep understanding of SAP S/4HANA and industry best practices to drive strategic decision-making. You will also play a crucial role in our clients' S/4HANA transformation journeys. Conduct thorough assessments of existing SAP landscapes and business processes. Analyze business requirements and translate them into strategic S/4HANA implementation plans. Facilitate workshops and presentations to communicate roadmap strategies to stakeholders. Provide expert advice on S/4HANA best practices, industry trends, and emerging technologies. Identify and mitigate potential risks and challenges associated with S/4HANA implementations. Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including technical architects, functional consultants, and project managers. title: AI Tech Consultant (ERP/SAP) - Amsterdam | BCG Platinion
description: Who We Are At BCG Platinion, we close the gap between strategy and technology. We design and build AI tech strategies, AI solutions, and next-generation platform architectures that turn ambition into execution, turning buzzwords into a true business engine and real competitive advantage. Working at the intersection of boardroom strategy and hands-on tech delivery, we help leading organizations tackle their toughest challenges, with access to C-suite leaders and the unmatched resources of BCG. About BCG Platinion BCG Platinion's presence spans across the globe, with offices in Asia, Europe, and South and North America. We achieve digital excellence for clients with sustained solutions to the most complex and time-sensitive challenge. We guide clients into the future to push the status quo, overcome tech limitations, and enable our clients to go further in their digital journeys than what has ever been possible in the past. At BCG Platinion, we deliver business value through the innovative use of technology at a rapid pace. We roll up our sleeves to transform business, revolutionize approaches, satisfy customers, and change the game through Architecture, Cybersecurity, Digital Transformation, Enterprise Application and Risk functions. We balance vision with a pragmatic path to change transforming strategies into leading-edge tech platforms, at scale. Practice Area BCG Platinion launched in Germany in 2000 to add deep technical expertise to the Boston Consulting Group’s existing capabilities. Today, our presence spans across the globe, with offices in Asia, Europe, and South and North America. Our New York-based North American team began in 2014 and in 2017 acquired MAYA Design, a Pittsburgh-based digital design and innovation lab, to grow our capabilities around technology and design. We support our clients’ total digital transformation through technology, design, cybersecurity, and risk management & financial engineering capabilities. And together with BCG, BCG Platinion’s interdisciplinary team of technical experts enable customized technical solutions and accelerate delivery value through new business platforms, application consolidations, and major system implementations. What You'll Do We are looking for a driven consultant to support the delivery of ERP-enabled business transformation programs. This role is ideal for candidates with functional SAP expertise and early experience in client-facing delivery who want to build strategic consulting skills. Key Responsibilities Delivery Support: Contribute to ERP program delivery across requirements gathering, design, testing, cutover, and go-live. Methodology Application: Work within SAP Activate frameworks to ensure efficient, high-quality delivery. Strategic Exposure: Support business case development, PMO functions, and change management activities. Cross-Process Learning: Gain exposure to multiple SAP modules and value streams (e.g., Purchase-to-Pay, Order-to-Cash). Client Interaction: Participate in workshops and client meetings to align solution scope with business priorities. What You'll Bring We’re Looking For Exceptional Talent With Experience Of Working In The Consulting Industry To Join Us. You Will Typically Have 3 to 6 years of experience in ERP systems Consulting or SI background with functional SAP experience in at least one module Own discrete workstreams or modules under supervision Contribute to rapid assessment / as-is analysis Familiarity with AI-enabled productivity tools (e.g., generative AI) to support research, documentation, analysis, and delivery efficiency Translate business requirements into SAP functional designs Client-facing experience with exposure to PMO, delivery, or change management Understanding of ERP delivery phases and programme structures Familiarity with SAP Activate or traditional ERP delivery methodologies A university degree in a STEM-related field or business management; advanced degrees (MBA, MSc) are advantageous Additional info We make sure you never stop growing. You'll tackle exciting, high-impact challenges every day, supported by a passionate team of talented colleagues who share your drive for excellence and keep connect long after your case wraps up. Our collaborative, open culture gives you the freedom to explore and perfect your strengths, backed by individual learning opportunities and recognized certifications. #BCGPlatinion Boston Consulting Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws. BCG is an E - Verify Employer. Click here for more information on E-Verify. title: SAP HANA Cloud developer - 6 Months Contract
description: We are seeking an experienced SAP HANA Cloud Developer to design, develop, and optimize cloud-native applications and database solutions on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). The ideal candidate should have strong expertise in SAP HANA Cloud, SQLScript, CAP, and cloud application development, with experience integrating SAP and non-SAP systems. Requirements Design, develop, and maintain applications using SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). Develop database artifacts including tables, views, procedures, functions, calculation views, and SQLScript. Build cloud-native applications using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). Develop RESTful APIs and integrate SAP HANA Cloud with SAP and third-party applications. Optimize database performance through indexing, partitioning, and query tuning. Implement data modeling, security, authorization, and role management. Develop and maintain ETL/data integration processes using SAP Integration Suite or SDI/SDA. Participate in Agile ceremonies including sprint planning, code reviews, and testing. Collaborate with functional consultants, architects, and business stakeholders to deliver scalable solutions. Troubleshoot production issues and provide ongoing application support. Required Skills: 5+ years of SAP HANA development experience. Strong experience with SAP HANA Cloud. Expertise in SQL, SQLScript, CDS Views, and database modeling. Experience with SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). Hands-on experience with SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model). Strong knowledge of Node.js or Java for CAP development. Experience with SAP Fiori/UI5 (preferred). Experience with Git, CI/CD, and DevOps practices. Knowledge of REST APIs, OData services, and JSON/XML. Understanding of cloud security and authentication (OAuth, XSUAA). Preferred Skills: Experience with SAP Integration Suite (CPI). Knowledge of SAP Datasphere or SAP Analytics Cloud. Familiarity with SAP S/4HANA integration. Experience with Docker, Kubernetes, or Cloud Foundry. Exposure to SAP Build Process Automation or SAP Build Apps. Understanding of Agile/Scrum methodologies. Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field. description: Contract Opportunity: SAP S/4 HANA Senior Consultant / Manager - Procure to Pay (PTP) Belmont Lavan Ltd is seeking an experienced SAP S/4 HANA Senior Consultant or Manager with expertise in Procure to Pay (PTP) processes. In this role, you will lead complex SAP S/4 HANA implementation and optimization projects, focusing on streamlining procurement and payment operations to increase efficiency and support organizational goals. Main Responsibilities: Lead end-to-end SAP S/4 HANA PTP implementations, including requirement gathering, process design, configuration, testing, and go-live support Analyze and optimize procurement and accounts payable business processes within SAP S/4 HANA Collaborate with cross-functional teams and stakeholders to ensure seamless integration across modules such as MM, FI, and others Provide thought leadership and guidance on best practices, industry standards, and SAP innovations related to PTP Manage project timelines, deliverables, and resources effectively to ensure successful project outcomes Coach and mentor junior consultants and team members Requirements Qualifications and Experience: Minimum of 7 years of SAP experience with a strong focus on Procure to Pay (PTP) processes Extensive expertise in SAP S/4 HANA implementations, upgrades, and migrations Deep understanding of SAP MM, FI, and related modules supporting the PTP cycle Proven experience in managing SAP projects including stakeholder communication and change management Strong analytical skills and ability to design process improvements aligned with business needs Excellent communication skills in English; additional European languages (Dutch, German, etc.) are a plus Experience working in Agile/Scrum environments is advantageous Benefits What We Offer Competitive salary and benefits package. Opportunities for professional growth and Microsoft certification support. Collaborative, innovative, and inclusive work environment. Flexible working arrangements (Hybrid/Remote depending on role) Show more Show less Seniority level Mid-Senior level Employment type Full-time Job function Information Technology Industries IT Services and IT Consulting Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Belmont Lavan by 2x See who you know

title: SAP INHOUSE CONSULTANT (M/F/D)
description: We are a community open to everyone. One global team where everyone counts. Join the Canyon team and work at the cutting edge of the bike industry, with a job as versatile and exciting as the bikes we develop. All in an open, international atmosphere where teamwork, collaboration, true innovation, and a dynamic workplace are all part of our everyday. Ready to ride with us? THE ROLE We are expanding our team and are looking for SAP Inhouse Consultants for various modules! As an SAP Inhouse Consultant, you support our internal customers in generating new business requirements as part of the development process - from concept to implementation within the respective module. In doing so, you act as the bridge between the business departments and IT and take on end-to-end functional responsibility for projects. Your module focus will be on one (or more) of the following modules: SD with focus in Logistics YOUR JOB AS PART OF THE CANYON CREW Process Analysis & IT Solutions: Identify and analyze process requirements and the associated IT solutions within your module focus (including cross-module topics). Requirements Engineering & Customization: Work closely with business departments on requirements and process analysis, as well as the technical implementation of change requests and customization. Feasibility & Methodology: Gather and analyze requirements using structured, methodological approaches and assess technical feasibility. SAP Core Module Development: Support and continuously develop your SAP core module and its interfaces to connected modules and systems. Stakeholder & Vendor Collaboration: Collaborate closely with business units and external service providers. Process Testing & Documentation: Conduct and document process tests. User Training & Support: Deliver internal training sessions and act as the first point of contact for users and key users. Release & Change Management: Support release management, including transport and change management. How You Become Part Of The Race You have a degree in (Business) Informatics, Engineering, Business Administration, Information Management, or a comparable qualification Alternatively, completed vocational training in a related field Several years of professional experience as a consultant, inhouse consultant, or process/project manager in one of the listed modules (SD with focus in Logistics, SD with focus in Carrier Management and Dangerous Goods, TSM (Technical Service), MM and PP (Materials Management, Production Planning)) Strong knowledge (including customization) in at least one SAP module and in process analysis Excellent project management skills Affinity for new technologies and a strong service-oriented mindset High process affinity and strong analytical skills Excellent communication skills, with a structured and solution-oriented way of working Very good English skills, German skills are a nice to have Please note: Applications must be submitted in English. HIGHLIGHTS ON YOUR CANYON TOUR The Canyon Crew & Culture: Combine energy and transparency in a global team. We promote a strong feedback culture, unique Canyon events, and inspiring crew rides. Your voice matters in creating an inclusive workplace where well-being is a priority. Feel Canyon (Product Perks): Unlock your passion for cycling! Enjoy exclusive discounts on bikes, gear, and apparel as part of our commitment to fueling your journey. Compensation & Rewards: Experience a rewarding career with a competitive market-based salary and generous paid time off. Your satisfaction is our priority. Life-Work Integration: Join us for a career that prioritizes balance through flexible and hybrid work options in a family-friendly environment. Impact & Development: Shape the future of mobility. We offer diverse career paths across operational, strategic, creative, or technical roles, where innovation and sustainability consistently challenge and inspire you. title: Value Engineer
description: Complexio is the intelligence layer for enterprise AI. Our platform builds a connected understanding of how businesses actually operate — across people, processes, and systems. Enterprises have invested heavily in AI platforms, copilots, and data infrastructure — yet daily work still runs on manual hand-offs and thousands of unseen tasks buried in emails, chats, and spreadsheets. Complexio closes that automation gap. Our Event Knowledge Graph ingests structured and unstructured data to create a living map of how an organisation truly works — every task, interaction, and dependency. Our Context Broker unifies this knowledge across enterprise systems in real time, enabling our Orchestration layer and Enterprise Automator to discover, design, and execute workflows autonomously. Stevie, our conversational AI assistant, gives teams a natural way to query and act on that intelligence. Complexio deploys securely within your infrastructure and integrates with the enterprise platforms you already run on — turning organisational knowledge into measurable business outcomes. A joint venture between Hafnia and Símbolo, backed by leading maritime partners including Marfin Management, C Transport Maritime, Trans Sea Transport, and BW Epic Kosan. Born in maritime, now scaling rapidly across industries. We are now onboarding enterprise customers globally, with a platform built to handle the pace and complexity of real business operations. Enterprise organisations do not buy software — they fund outcomes. The Value Engineer exists to make the connection between Complexio's platform capabilities and the specific, quantified business outcomes each customer cares about, at every stage of the relationship. Value engineering as a discipline has become a defining commercial capability at the world's leading enterprise software companies. ServiceNow built a global Value Engineering practice to justify platform ROI at scale. Celonis pioneered value engineering in the process mining space, making quantified process improvement the centrepiece of every enterprise deal. SAP's Value Advisors and Salesforce's Business Value Consultants have made the ability to prove financial return a standard expectation in enterprise software procurement. Complexio is building this capability now — at the stage where it will have the most impact on commercial outcomes. This role sits at the intersection of business consulting, commercial strategy, and deep operational understanding. You are not a sales support function. You are an independent, credible business advisor who earns the trust of customer CFOs, COOs, and operational leadership by doing rigorous work — identifying where value is being lost, modelling what recovery is worth, and demonstrating with evidence what Complexio has actually delivered. Pre-sale, you build the business case that justifies the investment decision. Post-sale, you track and prove the value that was promised. Across the full lifecycle, you ensure that every Complexio customer can articulate — internally and externally — exactly what the platform is worth to their organisation. You will work alongside the CRO, Client Directors, and Solutions Consultants in customer-facing engagements, and you will work internally with Product and Engineering to ensure that value realisation data feeds back into how Complexio builds and prioritises. This is a high-influence role at a company where the ability to quantify and communicate business value is a direct commercial differentiator. Requirements Value Discovery & Business Case Development Lead structured value discovery engagements with prospective customers — working with operational, financial, and technology stakeholders to identify and quantify the cost of their current automation gap Map customer operational workflows to Complexio's platform capabilities, identifying the specific processes, hand-offs, and inefficiencies where the EKG, Context Broker, Enterprise Automator, and Stevie create measurable impact Build rigorous, defensible business cases for Complexio deployments — quantifying value across dimensions including: ○ Labour time recovered from manual task execution and coordination overhead ○ Error and exception rates reduced through automated workflow execution ○ Decision cycle times shortened through real-time operational intelligence ○ Revenue at risk protected through earlier identification of operational exceptions ○ Cost of delay reduced across procurement, chartering, scheduling, and operational workflows ○ Compliance and audit cost reduced through automated logging and traceability Translate business case outputs into executive-ready materials — CFO-grade financial models, board-level summaries, and ROI one-pagers that withstand scrutiny from finance and procurement stakeholders Validate assumptions with customers through structured interviews, data analysis, and process observation — never building a business case on hypotheticals that will be challenged at approval Pre-Sales Value Engagement Partner with Sales, Client Directors, and Solutions Consultants in enterprise sales cycles — joining customer engagements specifically to lead the value and business case workstream Run executive value workshops with customer C-suite and operational leadership — structured sessions that move from pain identification through to quantified opportunity in a single engagement Produce deal-specific value narratives and ROI models that are calibrated to each customer's operational context, financial metrics, and internal approval process Equip the sales team with reusable value frameworks, benchmark data, and industry-specific ROI models that accelerate deal cycles across similar customer profiles Support commercial proposal development by providing the financial and business value content that underpins pricing, contract structure, and deal terms Post-Sale Value Realisation Own the value realisation framework for Complexio's customer base — defining how value is tracked, measured, and reported from deployment through to steady-state operation Establish baseline metrics with customers at the point of deployment — agreeing the operational and financial KPIs against which Complexio's impact will be measured Conduct regular value reviews with customers — producing clear, evidence-based reports that document what has been delivered against what was committed Work with Client Directors to use value realisation data as the foundation for renewal and expansion conversations — replacing opinion-based selling with evidence-based commercial discussions Identify accounts where realised value is below expectation and work with Delivery, Product, and Customer Success to understand and address the root cause Value Benchmarking & Market Intelligence Build and maintain Complexio's value benchmark library — a structured, continuously updated repository of outcome data across customers, sectors, use cases, and platform capabilities Develop sector-specific value models for Complexio's primary markets — maritime operations, energy trading, industrial workflow management — grounded in real customer data and validated against industry benchmarks Track and analyse how value accrues over time across the customer base — identifying which deployment patterns, use cases, and adoption sequences drive the fastest and largest outcomes Monitor competitive value positioning — understanding how Complexio's ROI profile compares to alternatives including Microsoft Copilot, Celonis, and incumbent manual processes Internal Value Enablement Build and maintain a value engineering toolkit accessible to the broader commercial team — including value discovery frameworks, ROI calculators, benchmark data packs, and executive presentation templates Train and coach Client Directors and Sales on value-based selling techniques — shifting conversations from feature discussion to business outcome quantification Partner with Product and Engineering to ensure that Complexio's platform instrumentation supports value measurement — advocating for the telemetry, reporting, and analytics capabilities that make value realisation visible to customers Feed value data and customer outcome evidence back into Complexio's product prioritisation process — ensuring the roadmap is guided by where the platform creates the most demonstrable impact Executive & C-Suite Engagement Engage directly with CFOs, COOs, and operational leadership as a credible, independent business advisor — not as a vendor representative Lead or co-lead executive value presentations and business case reviews at board and C-suite level — with the financial and operational rigour to withstand challenge from sophisticated buyers Shadow Co-CEOs and the CRO in strategic customer engagements as required — providing the value and ROI narrative in high-stakes commercial discussions Build long-term advisory relationships with senior customer stakeholders that extend beyond individual deals and create durable commercial anchors Essential What We're Looking For 6+ years in a value engineering, value advisory, business value consulting, or management consulting role — ideally with direct experience in enterprise software, process automation, or AI/data platform contexts Demonstrable experience building and presenting rigorous business cases and ROI models to C-suite and finance stakeholders — models that have withstood scrutiny and influenced major investment decisions Strong financial modelling capability — comfortable building NPV, IRR, and payback analyses from first principles and translating operational data into financial outcomes Deep understanding of how large enterprises make investment decisions — approval processes, financial hurdle rates, competing priorities, and the politics of internal sponsorship Excellent communication and executive presence — able to present complex financial and operational analysis to a CFO or COO with clarity, confidence, and credibility Experience conducting operational process analysis — understanding where time, cost, and risk accumulate in complex workflows and quantifying the improvement opportunity Commercially aware — you understand how value engineering contributes to deal velocity, win rates, expansion, and retention, and you operate with that commercial consciousness Desirable Experience in the maritime, shipping, energy, or industrial sectors — operational familiarity with voyage management, fleet operations, chartering, trading, or supply chain workflows Background in a value engineering function at a recognised enterprise software company — the Value Engineer role at ServiceNow, the Value Engineer function at Celonis (which pioneered value engineering in the process mining space), the Value Advisor discipline at SAP, the Business Value Consultant practice at Salesforce, or equivalent programmes at enterprise software vendors where value quantification is a defined commercial discipline Experience with process mining, workflow analytics, or operational intelligence tools — and the ability to use data outputs to construct value narratives Familiarity with AI automation economics — understanding how to model labour displacement, error reduction, and decision acceleration in organisations adopting AI-driven workflows Experience building value benchmark databases and sector-specific ROI frameworks from scratch Who You Are Rigorous and credible — your business cases are built on real data, sound assumptions, and transparent methodology; you do not overstate value and you do not produce numbers that collapse under scrutiny A skilled interviewer and listener — you can run a discovery session with a COO or a fleet manager and extract the operational detail you need to build a credible model, without making them feel interrogated Commercially motivated — you understand that your work directly enables revenue and you take that seriously; you are not an academic analyst, you are a commercial contributor Independently minded — you are willing to tell a customer or a sales team that a use case does not meet the value threshold, because your credibility depends on honesty, not optimism A strong writer and presenter — you produce executive materials that are clear, well-structured, and visually compelling; you do not hide weak analysis behind impressive slides Intellectually curious about how industries operate — you find genuine interest in understanding how a maritime shipping company makes money, where it loses it, and what operational changes move the needle A builder — Complexio does not yet have a mature value engineering function; you are excited by the opportunity to create the frameworks, tools, and benchmarks that will define how the company communicates value at scale Benefits A foundational role — you will build Complexio's value engineering function from the ground up, creating the frameworks, benchmarks, and tools that will define how the company communicates and proves value across its customer base Direct commercial impact — your work will be in the room for the most important deals Complexio closes Competitive compensation with a performance-related component tied to commercial outcomes Equity participation aligned to Complexio's growth trajectory Direct access to CRO and Co-CEO leadership, and close collaboration with Product and Engineering A technically ambitious, operationally serious environment — the customers are sophisticated, the problems are real, and the value opportunity is large Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Full-time Job function Engineering Industries Technology, Information and Internet Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Complexio by 2x See who you title: Head of IT Ecommerce
description: About Us At Dyson, we demand the highest standard of performance from the technologies we engineer. Our people expect the same from the technology that supports them. We are a community that appreciates and advocates better engineering. A community of pioneers. In the same way that Dyson engineers’ better technology to solve problems others ignore, Global IT transforms the way Dyson does business through world class and pioneering IT services and systems. About The Role You will be responsible for: Providing overall IT Business partnership and Product ownership for the eCommerce business function. Accountable in providing secure & sustainable IT service delivery and continuity underpinned by operational excellence to those critical functions. Work closely with the Global IT Director, to strategically partner with the most senior members of those functions and their organisation to drive their Digital Transformation agenda by clearly articulating and defining business needs and benefits (value realization) through well documented Business Cases and developing required IT Digital and Strategy roadmaps. Take accountability for the digital and technology transformation strategy and roadmaps for the relevant applications in those functions to support their requirements and partnering closely with other IT functions to influence and deliver broader Global IT strategy within the agreed Enterprise Architecture standards and governance. Ensuring business stakeholders have clear visibility of roadmaps and plans to meet their demands, working with the wider global IT teams to ensure any new requirements are well defined and efficiently captured. Providing Digital and technology insights into the business leadership teams on technology trends, benchmarking and innovation and how these can be leveraged to drive business growth and new operating models to achieve the expected goals and ambitions of their functions. Providing leadership to develop AI literacy within eCommerce, translating business problems into AI solutions, developing monitoring capabilities and ensuring we measure/report on AI ROI. About You With experience in a similarly dynamic, international role and complex organisation, you’ll have a proven working knowledge of leading a core global team within a large IT function. We're Looking For Someone With Experience in supporting or leading transformational programmes; managing the technical, changing operational processes, business models and supporting your people. Experience and up-to-date knowledge of AI and use within eCommerce. Experience leading teams of business analysts, solution architects, development managers, as well as project managers to develop clear architectural roadmaps and associated competencies Deep product knowledge in SAP Hybris, Adobe Commerce or Magento is advantageous. Have implemented E-Commerce solutions at scale, understanding the overall architecture and business capabilities. Good knowledge of integration and security products around eCommerce supporting ERP, CRM, PIM, OMS and more. Understanding of Cyber Risk, Data Privacy and Fraud technologies. Experience in architecting and understanding different vendor solutions in this space is advantageous Have knowledge of microservices and scalability/modular, SAAS options. Have experience building teams in a true DevOps manner to handle E-Commerce changes in an agile and cost-effective manner and ability to support 24/7, peak and troughs of an E-Commerce business. Ability to set and effectively communicate IT strategies in multi-national organizations. First class leadership and management skills. Customer focused and driven, focused on service and operational excellence. Sound commercial understanding, drive and experience of a KPI/Service culture. An ability to derive business benefits from technology and to recognise opportunities. A professional who can provide examples of transforming ways of working through technology within a global company. Flexible with the ability to embrace change, as well as drive and lead change. Ability to influence across all sectors to gain alignment, agreement and commitments. Strong financial understanding. You Will Be Self-motivated, dynamic and results driven. Inquisitive, proactive and innovative. Dynamic and enthusiastic, combining flexibility and creativity. A strong leader with ethical and personal integrity. Willing to be flexible in tasks and activities taken on. Sensitive to cultural differences across Dyson Worldwide. We're Looking For Someone That Has Significant track record of establishing and maintaining high performing teams. High attention to detail without losing bigger picture. Outstanding interpersonal communication skills and ability to communicate effectively at senior leadership team levels. The ability to thrive on change. The ability to influence outside of direct control at a senior leadership team level. The ability to take a holistic view, thinking regionally and acting globally. The ability to offer solutions to various I.T. related problems, at times when under pressure. Reward At Dyson, Reward goes beyond just salary and bonuses. Through a comprehensive package of Financial, Lifestyle, and Health Benefits, we provide support tailored to every stage of life and the moments that matter most. Working Policy Our campus culture is built on connection—collaboration in person, shared creativity, and hands-on learning. That’s why we don’t offer a hybrid arrangement. Working side by side powers our best ideas, fuels belonging, and keeps us pushing boundaries together. 1 year fixed-term contract At Dyson in the Netherlands, all new joiners start on a fixed-term contract, with the intention to move to a permanent contract after the first year. Dyson is an equal opportunity employer. We know that great minds don’t think alike, and it takes all kinds of minds to make our technology so unique. We welcome applications from all backgrounds and employment decisions are made without regard to race, colour, religion, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, protected veteran status or other any other dimension of diversity. Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Contract Job function Information Technology Industries Appliances, Electrical, and Electronics Manufacturing Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Dyson by 2x See who you know

title: Sales Controller, Continent Europe (m/f/d)
description: Job Description WHAT YOU’LL DO Step into a role where your insights drive the business forward. As a Continent Sales Controller, you will play a key role in shaping performance across our markets by turning data into action and securing the best possible sales outcomes. You will: Drive and co-lead the end-to-end QCP process, coordinating cross-functional inputs and creating clear, aligned outputs for the continent. Translate financial targets and budget frames into data-driven commercial plans, balancing global priorities with local commercial realities. Monitor in-season performance against QCP, manage variances, and support corrective actions to strengthen follow-through and delivery. Work closely with continent leadership, Sales Market leadership teams, and connected global stakeholders to challenge, validate and prioritize inputs for review and decision-making. Use forecasting, analytics, and hindsight learnings to improve planning accuracy, validate commercial assumptions, and continuously strengthen the quality of planning. WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH You will report to the Head of Controlling (Continent) and be part of the Continent team for Europe in Amsterdam. The team works across diverse markets to drive sustainable, profitable growth by connecting global strategy with local execution and enabling Sales Markets to perform through strong insights, collaboration and financial discipline. In this role, you will work closely with the new leadership team for Europe, Sales Market leadership teams, and cross-functional stakeholders across controlling, planning and finance, helping create alignment, clarity and effective execution across the continent. WHO YOU ARE We are looking for people with… A degree in Finance, Business Administration, Economics, or a related field, or equivalent work experience. Proven experience in financial controlling, commercial planning, or performance management. At least 2 years of experience in a leadership role. A strong track record within controlling, ideally from roles such as Business Controller, Central Controlling, or Logistics Controller. Strong understanding of integrated business planning processes and cross-functional collaboration. Experience working in a matrix organization across regional and global levels. Proficiency in SAP, Power BI, or other planning and reporting tools. Strong analytical, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Fluency in English. And people who are… Clear and structured in the way they work. Comfortable creating clarity and direction in a complex environment. Confident working closely with senior stakeholders and challenging constructively. Collaborative and able to work across functions and interfaces. Motivated by turning analysis into action and improving business performance through strong follow-through. WHO WE ARE​ H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​ We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​ WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. An example of our benefits: 20% Staff discount on all our H&M Group brands, both in stores and online (H&M, COS, Weekday, Monki, H&M HOME, & Other Stories and ARKET). H&M Incentive Program – HIP. Learn more about the program here. With a presence in markets around the world, we offer extensive career development and international mobility. JOIN US This is a full-time position on a local contract. The role is based in Amsterdam, Netherlands, and follows a hybrid working model. If you think your experience, skills, and ambitions align with this role, please send your application, including CV in English as soon as possible. Interviews will be held continuously. We look forward to receiving your application. Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it's our people who make us who we are. Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here. *We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.
