title: Technology Transformation Manager
description: Technology Transformation Manager - Fluent in German Senior Consultant - Principal London About Us Do you want to boost your career and collaborate with expert, talented colleagues to solve and deliver against our clients' most important challenges? We are growing and are looking for people to join our team. You will be part of an entrepreneurial, high-growth environment of 300.000 employees. Our dynamic organization allows you to work across functional business pillars, contributing your ideas, experiences, diverse thinking, and a strong mindset. Are you ready? About Your Role As a Technology Transformation Manager at Infosys Consulting, you will help our clients navigate complex change by shaping and delivering next‑generation technology transformations. Embedded in our Technology Transformation Practice—and specifically within the Execution & Value Realization team—you will combine strategic thinking with hands‑on delivery expertise to drive measurable outcomes across large enterprise programs. Working in a highly collaborative, international environment with a high degree of technical complexity, you will guide clients from vision to execution while ensuring tangible business value is realized. This role is ideal for consultants who excel in dynamic environments and are passionate about transforming how organizations operate and scale in the digital age. Key Competencies And Responsibilities Coordinate and manage large-scale IT transformation programs across complex, multinational environments Drive execution excellence within the Execution & Value Realization subgroup, ensuring measurable outcomes and value generation Provide deep technical leadership in one or more domains: Cloud & Infrastructure, Cyber Security & Resilience, Digital Operations, ITSM, SIAM, ServiceNow, Data & Analytics Design and implement IT Operating Models, governance structures, and cross-functional delivery frameworks Identify automation and optimization opportunities and translate them into IT business cases with clear ROI Demonstrate thought leadership across the strategy-to-delivery lifecycle, influencing stakeholders and shaping transformation roadmaps Assess value potential, cost drivers, and performance metrics of IT transformations; develop KPIs, value frameworks, and benefit realization plans Work closely with offshore and nearshore teams to ensure efficiency, delivery quality, and proper knowledge transfer Collaborate with enterprise platform teams (e.g., ServiceNow, Adobe, Salesforce, SAP) to drive end-to-end transformation outcomes Engage with senior client stakeholders and act as a trusted advisor throughout the transformation journey Requirements Qualifications Required Minimum of 7 years of experience in IT consulting with a proven track record in technology transformation initiatives Strong understanding of enterprise technology landscapes and architecture concepts Hands-on experience with Agile and/or Scaled Agile frameworks Demonstrated ability to design IT Operating Models and governance structures Strong analytical capabilities in evaluating automation potential, optimization levers, and IT business cases Operational experience in integration of AI usecases to optimize business / it processes (utilizing ML, GenAI and LLMs) Experience collaborating with distributed delivery models, including offshore and nearshore teams Excellent leadership, team management, and stakeholder communication skills High level of proficiency in English You must be Fluent in German (at Least B1) for the role Bachelor's or master's degree in computer science, Information Systems, Engineering, Business, or a related field Qualifications Preferred Experience with enterprise platforms such as ServiceNow, Adobe Experience Cloud, Salesforce, and SAP from a transformation and integration perspective. Experience on Mergers Acquisitions respectively Carve Out scenarios Background in defining value‑realization frameworks, cost models, or mid‑/long‑term technology investment strategies. Certifications in Agile, SAFe, ITIL, or cloud/security technologies. KPI / SLA definition and monitoring Proven experience in advisory roles involving senior client stakeholders or C‑level audiences. Experience in shaping large‑scale, multi‑year transformation roadmaps. Prior involvement in transformation programs using hybrid delivery models (onsite/offshore/nearshore) Benefits About your team As part of our "Technology Transformation" practice, you will join a highly integrated ecosystem of expertise structured into seven core capability groups, ranging from Enterprise Strategy & Architecture to Execution and Value Realization. Our framework ensures that platforms, data, cloud, and security operate as one coherent system to make complex digital transformations both executable and resilient. You will collaborate across specialized domains such as Cyber & Resilience, Cloud & infrastructure and Data & Analytics to build the high-performing technical foundations that drive sustainable business value. By modernizing legacy estates in Digital Ops or unifying operations via ServiceNow, you will be key to delivering the speed, reliability, and measurable success our clients depend on. About Infosys Consulting Be part of a globally renowned management consulting firm on the front-line of industry disruption and at the cutting edge of technology. We work with market leading brands across sectors. Our culture is inclusive and entrepreneurial. Being a mid-size consultancy within the scale of Infosys gives us the global reach to partner with our clients throughout their transformation journey. Our core values, IC-LIFE, form a common code that helps us move forward. IC-LIFE stands for Inclusion, Equity and Diversity, Client, Leadership, Integrity, Fairness, and Excellence. To learn more about Infosys Consulting and our values, please visit our careers page. Within Europe, we are recognized as one of the UK's top firms by the Financial Times and Forbes due to our client innovations, our cultural diversity and dedicated training and career paths. Infosys is on the Germany's top employers list for 2023. Management Consulting Magazine named us on their list of Best Firms to Work for. Furthermore, Infosys has been recognized by the Top Employers Institute, a global certification company, for its exceptional standards in employee conditions across Europe for five years in a row. We offer industry-leading compensation and benefits, along with top training and development opportunities so that you can grow your career and achieve your personal ambitions. Curious to learn more? We'd love to hear from you.... Apply today! title: Senior Accountant
description: Our client is an international manufacturing company based in Amsterdam, operating across global supply chains with a strong focus on sustainability and innovation. They are looking for an Senior Accountant to join their finance team and take ownership of a broad range of accounting responsibilities. Your responsibilities Handle day-to-day accounting operations in SAP, including General Ledger, Accounts Receivable, Accounts Payable, Asset Accounting, Travel Expenses, and daily bank reconciliations Support month-end and year-end closing processes within tight deadlines Oversee cash management, including supplier payment preparation and monitoring of customer collections Assist with the preparation and submission of local tax filings, including VAT and Intrastat Ensure compliance with corporate tax requirements and local regulations Provide ad hoc financial analyses to support business decision-making Identify and implement process improvements with a focus on automation and digitalisation Manage payroll processing and ensure accuracy and timeliness Act as a key point of contact for external auditors and regulatory authorities Your profile Degree in Accounting, Finance, or Business (Bachelor's or Master's) Relevant experience in general accounting, ideally in an industrial setting or Big Four firm Experience with statutory accounts preparation and external audit support Solid knowledge of Dutch VAT, Corporate Tax, and Payroll regulations Strong understanding of Dutch GAAP and IFRS Comfortable handling confidential data with discretion Strong analytical and numerical skills Fluency in Dutch is required for this position What's on offer Autonomy and flexibility in your day-to-day work Company pension scheme Ongoing training and development opportunities Permanent contract with competitive compensation A supportive team with flat hierarchies and an open culture A stable, mid-sized company in a future-proof industry Show more Show less Seniority level Associate Employment type Full-time Job function Accounting/Auditing Industries Manufacturing

title: Global Maintenance Strategy and Planning Lead
description: Manage an engineering team with crucial engineering processes and contribute to strategic decisions. Job Description Global Maintenance Strategy and Planning Lead at a glance ... As the Maintenance Strategy and Planning Lead, you will architect operational efficiency and reliability for over 60 facilities worldwide. In this high visibility role, your strategic leadership will drive maintenance excellence and embed a culture of continuous improvement across our global footprint. By defining standards and spearheading asset management, you will optimize safety and performance on a global scale. As our central Subject Matter Expert (SME), you will empower plants and markets to achieve world class reliability, sustainability, and cost effectiveness. Key Ingredients The delivery of a maintenance strategy program will be your primary responsibility. You’re constantly looking for improvement activities within the maintenance field. Maintenance Global Strategy and Policies Implement the global Asset Management strategy. (Sets the foundation) Review existing maintenance policies to align with industry best practices, strategic goals, and regulatory requirements, while monitoring the effectiveness of maintenance policies globally. Develop and implement new maintenance policies for MRO, asset management, and PM transactions to address emerging challenges. Communicate maintenance policies effectively to all personnel involved in maintenance activities. Lead the Reliability Centered Maintenance (RCM) program to maximize equipment uptime and optimize costs. Conduct RCM assessments for critical equipment and assets. Support the development of RCM-based maintenance plans. Develop the strategy and focus on the annual maintenance budgeting process. Revise KPIs and targets to support the annual routine setting process. Support the analysis, evaluation, and approval of capital projects for asset replacement. Identify and develop new digital solutions to support CMMS transactions across all sites. Define MRE team structures and propose adaptations based on site size and asset volume. Perform field checks, coaching, forums, and workshops to develop site-level teams. Provide technical support for large-scale emergency failures at sites. Support the KHMS MRE pillar initiatives. Recipe for Success: Apply if this sounds like you! Hard Skills Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, Industrial) or Operations Management. 8+ years in manufacturing operations with a proven track record of scaling global maintenance and reliability programs. Asset Management Mastery: Advanced knowledge of ISO 55000 standards and Reliability Centered Maintenance (RCM) methodologies. CMPR / CRL Certification Technical Systems: User level in SAP CMMS/PM (Project Management & Maintenance modules). Experience in Opex/Capex budgeting and life cycle cost analysis for large scale asset replacement. Certified expertise in Lean Six Sigma, Kaizen, or similar systematic improvement frameworks. Demonstrated ability to manage and supervise cross functional global teams Ability to translate complex data sets into actionable maintenance strategies. Excellent communication skills to bridge the gap between shop floor technical teams and executive leadership. Personal Attributes – habit of work, mindsets, and approaches that are keys to success in the role About The Position Decisiveness: You are biased towards action even when faced with uncertainty. You will make multiple decisions every day, from what to communicate, what actions to follow-up on, what can be deferred, and what to escalate. Being assertive in day-to-day activities is key to effectively executing plans and achieving your goals. When it comes to decisions within your projects, you will need to identify and obtain critical information, and gauge when enough detail has been collected in relation to the potential outcomes. Passion for Learning: You will constantly absorb lessons from past projects. Remembering what worked or didn’t in the past is cumulative, letting you avoid pitfalls you’ve seen before. You should ask questions and remain open for new ideas. You do not just repeat behaviors because it was the way things were done historically. You will embrace your curiosity and try out new methods. Problem Solving: You will need to solve a variety of problems throughout all stages of a project. Being able to think on your feet and address disputes and problems is key to ensuring that launch ambitions are met. By thinking in original and imaginative ways you will encourage your project teams to find the best solution and outcome. You will always be on your toes thinking of a Plan B & ready to execute when needed. Clear Communication: You will need to think with clarity, express ideas, and share information with a multitude of audiences. This means having the ability to handle rapid flows of information and being able to outline complex ideas and scenarios into easy-to-understand overviews. Being able to efficiently communicate with your project team will ensure everyone is of the same understanding regarding project expectations and status. Location(s) Amsterdam Kraft Heinz is an Equal Opportunity Employer – Underrepresented Ethnic Minority Groups/Women/Veterans/Individuals with Disabilities/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity and other protected classes . title: Product and Supply Chain Business Analyst (f/m/d)
description: Patagonia is an outdoor apparel company founded in 1973. At a time when all life on earth is under threat of extinction, we aim to use the resources we have—our business, our investments, our voice and our imagination—to influence changes needed to protect our planet. A registered B Corporation and founding member of 1% for the Planet, Patagonia is recognised internationally for its commitment to product quality and environmental activism, contributing over $145 million in grants and in-kind donations to thousands of grassroots environmental groups around the world. Your role: We’re in business to save our home planet. The Product & Supply Chain Business Analyst strengthens Patagonia EMEA’s operational excellence by improving the processes and systems that power the Product function, ensuring alignment with regional priorities and global strategic frameworks. The role translates business needs into scalable systems, data models and workflows that enhance accuracy, efficiency and collaboration across Product, Supply Chain and cross regional partners. The position acts as an advisor to the product leadership team and plays a critical role in strengthening organizational agility, reducing operational risk, and building robust, future-ready ways of working across Product and Supply Chain. Main tasks: Product process expertise Maintains a deep understanding of Product and Supply Chain processes across EMEA and identifies opportunities to streamline, standardize and improve workflows including identifying process risks, dependencies and improvement opportunities. Ensures that regional practices operate effectively within Patagonia’s global frameworks while maintaining the autonomy needed for EMEA operational realities. Business requirements & system design Translates business needs into user stories, functional specifications and process flows for systems supporting the Product function and design the EMEA Roadmap for the system capabilities over time. Collaborates with US-based teams, EMEA stakeholders and technical partners to ensure system enhancements reflect operational priorities and long-term scalability. Cross functional collaboration & project partnership Leads the Product function’s contribution to cross-functional EMEA initiatives and global projects by facilitating scope definition, mapping dependencies and coordinating alignment with Planning, Logistics, Merchandising and IT teams. Acts as an advisor to the product leadership team and ensures that regional considerations are well represented in global system and process decisions. Testing, change management & adoption Coordinates testing cycles for system improvements—including regression, UAT and release readiness—ensuring functionality aligns with business expectations. Leads change management for system and process transformations, by conducting impact analysis, developing documentation and facilitating training to embed updated ways of working across the region. Operational data integrity & reporting Oversees key operational processes such as the 7th online import to D365, inventory related data flows and reporting dashboards for the Product team. Ensures high levels of data accuracy and provides insights that enable effective inventory, replenishment and order related decisions across EMEA. What we’re looking for: An inclusive mindset In depth expertise in business analysis & process design Applies advanced analytical and process mapping techniques to translate functional needs into system requirements and workflows that strengthen Product and Supply Chain operations across EMEA. In depth expertise in project coordination & stakeholder alignmentCan lead cross-functional collaboration, manages timelines and clarifies dependencies between Product, Planning, Logistics, Merchandising, and global system teams to ensure coherent and timely project delivery. Full understanding of testing & implementation practicesExecutes structured testing cycles, validates system functionality and ensures readiness before deployment across regional Product processes and systems. Full understanding of data integrity & operational reporting Ensures accuracy of product and inventory data, builds reporting structures in systems such as Power BI and identifies data driven opportunities to improve replenishment, allocation and operational performance. Full understanding of change management & communicationDelivers clear documentation, facilitates training and drives adoption of new tools and processes by ensuring teams understand impacts, benefits and required behavioral shifts. A love of the outdoors An interest in saving our home planet and making a positive impact through your work Other important details: This is a Full-time position based in Amsterdam We have a hybrid work model, working from home for maximum 3 days per week We strive to make unbiased decisions and to offer a great experience to every person who applies for a role at Patagonia. Our interviews include standardised questions that focus on the aspects that are important for success in the role. To find out more about what we offer our employees click here: https://eu.patagonia.com/nl/en/what-we-offer/ At Patagonia, we commit to becoming an anti-racist company that leads by example. This means doing the work to be inclusive and equitable, across all aspects of our business. Every person who applies to join Patagonia receives equal employment opportunities, and we value a welcoming environment where each member of our EMEA team feels included, respected and empowered, regardless of their race, colour, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, civil status, national origin, disability or age. At Patagonia, we strive to have inclusive representation within our organisation and embrace a diverse mix of talent to contribute to our culture. We actively seek applications from Black and People of Colour (BPOC) and other underrepresented groups. As we are in business to save our home planet, we can only do this together. Please make sure that your CV and cover letter are in English. title: Senior Quality Officer
description: Senior Quality Officer Preparation of QP batch release for Products (Quality records, Batch recordreview, release documentation), overall QMS compliance relating to QA Operational tasks. You play a key role in executing structured simple Root Cause Analyses (RCA), tracking CAPA effectiveness, and supporting audit readiness. Your work contributes to maintaining product quality, reducing recurring issues, and ensuring continuous process improvements within the organization. Responsibilities Batch Release Batch record review Preparation for QP batch release (intermediates, Drug Substances, DrugProducts and Finished Products) Supervise QA Officers (or junior personnel in general ) in Communication with OMCL Physical release in the product Warehouse QMS Assessment and review, follow-up and timely closure of minor/major/(Critical if SME) deviations Assist extended Root Cause Analyse (RCA) with known techniques. Assessment and review, follow-up and timely closure of OOS / OOL /OOT records. Assist Quality engineers in Investigation and timely closure of Product Quality Complaints Handling of returned goods Review, follow-up and timely closure of Corrective and Preventive actions (CAPA’s) Execute deliveries from change controls Validation support Review & Approval of routine Equipment validations Inspections SME (Subject Matter Expert) during Self inspection as auditee, and customer audit. Back office in regulatory audit. Support presentation of process in regulatory audits Training On the Job of QA Ops staff in Deviations, CAPA’s the related systems Compliance review Writing of GMP-procedures review and approval of GMP-documents (e.g. GMP-procedures, master batch records) Documentation and reporting Deliver quality raw data for monthly Metrics and Quality Management Review and for Product Quality Review. QA on the shopfloor Perform shopfloor visits People management/ leadership (example) Coach junior member team. Possible project leader if SME. Stakeholder Management (example) Support Quality lead in stakeholder management GxP Takes GxP requirements into account during preparation, execution and reporting of GxP activities. Is fully aware of key SOPs such as procedures for Events, OOS and good documentation practice. Follows mandatory GxP training and maintains an appropriate training record. Identifies and escalates GxP issues. Your Profile: Bachelor/ Academic (master’s degree) in Life Sciences/ Pharmacy HBO > 2yrs experience or young Academic (MSc) Basic knowledge of the GMP-guidelines and experience with biological intermediates, drug substances and aseptic manufacturing preferred. Experience with Trackwise/ Mastercontrol/ SAP or similar. Dutch and English speaking (work proficiency) English writing skills. You naturally take a proactive approach are an analytical thinker and clear communicator What we offer: A market-rate salary A budget of 16,66% (holiday allowance and year-end bonus) of your gross salary, yours to spend or to save monthly A fulltime employment of 40 hours per week Flexible working hours after deliberation Your own place in a close-knit and expert team with an employer that stimulates growth and personal development Good accessibility by public transport and we provide private parking; Sound pension provision. title: Process Analyst
description: Sia is a next-generation, global management consulting group. Founded in 1999, we were born digital. Today, our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity, and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change, helping clients initiate, navigate, and benefit from transformation. Optimism is our force multiplier, mitigating downside and maximizing opportunity. With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our +3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results; our optimism transforms outcomes. Job Description Contributing to client success through optimized business processes and systems. That's what drives you and where your expertise lies. Finishing a project after delivering a consultancy report or design? Absolutely not. You want to deliver tangible results and make a lasting impact with your in-depth knowledge. Are you eager to tackle complex issues with us and further develop your process knowledge? Read more about this role here! What you'll be doing at the client's site: Understanding, analyzing, and designing business processes that align with our clients' needs and strategic pillars: these are your core responsibilities as our Process Analyst. In collaboration with our multidisciplinary team of consultants, you'll evaluate current processes, identify potential gaps, and identify opportunities. You'll be involved in all phases of the project, from gathering and validating requirements and process modeling to implementing new workflows and monitoring results. Curious about a project like this? In addition: You'll work according to GRIPP principles, our in-house methodology for accurately and efficiently analyzing our clients' processes. You'll analyze and document existing business processes, including identifying inefficiencies, bottlenecks, and opportunities for improvement. You'll design streamlined and efficient workflows. You'll use process modeling tools and techniques to create visual representations of processes, such as Adonis, flowcharts, and process diagrams. Advise your clients on best practices for process design and optimization. Provide training and support to users. Qualifications This is you You're naturally curious, enjoy a good laugh, but are also deadly serious when it comes to the content. Sound familiar? Great, that's what we appreciate in a colleague. Furthermore, in this role, we'd like you to: Enjoy your work! Enjoy collaborating with colleagues and clients on interesting issues. In fact, that's a key part of your job. You want to develop yourself every day with your positive attitude and drive. You have strong communication skills and, above all, understand the importance of listening. To the client, colleagues, and of course, yourself! You don't do the same thing every day; you prefer diverse and complex projects. You do what you promise. You're accurate and ensure your work is completed on time, without compromising on quality. You're a true team player. At Sia you can always count on the support of your colleagues. Achievements are the result of successful collaboration, and you celebrate them together! We value this culture highly. You have completed your Master’s Degree. You have 0 to 2 years’ experience. Fluent Dutch and English communication and presentation skills are mandatory. If you speak German, that's a plus! What you can expect from us Your development is our priority. Whether it’s through challenging projects, inspiring colleagues or personal growth, we invest in your success, every step of the way. We offer: An attractive salary that grows with your experience and performance. A yearly bonus based on your individual and team results. Sustainable and practical mobility options , whether you prefer to travel by train, bike, or your own vehicle. A solid pension plan and additional benefits to support your well-being. Freedom in where you work, from home, at the client’s office, or in one of our inspiring hubs in Amsterdam or Maastricht, including a home working allowance to make remote work comfortable. 26 days of holiday per year , with the opportunity to earn extra days as your seniority grows. Plenty of learning opportunities , from mentoring and training programs to international projects that broaden your perspective. A strong community , where connection matters – we organize regular social and sports activities to celebrate successes together. Do you have the drive and enthusiasm to help build our great company, and does this position sound like a perfect fit for you? We'd love to get to know you! Additional Information We are unable to provide a work permit for this position Sia is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or discipline, are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, or business needs. Sia is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or discipline, are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, or business needs. title: Supply Planner
description: Functieomschrijving Als Supply Planner speel je een sleutelrol in de beschikbaarheid van onze producten. Jij zorgt ervoor dat verpakkingsmaterialen en eindproducten op het juiste moment, in de juiste hoeveelheid. Daarmee lever je een directe bijdrage aan een efficiënte supply chain en een optimale service voor onze klanten. De functie is afwisselend doordat je werkt met een internationaal netwerk van materiaal- en productleveranciers. Je hebt dagelijks contact met leveranciers, bewaakt de voortgang van orders en stemt af met interne afdelingen zoals Customer Service en Commercie. Daarbij signaleer je knelpunten, los je operationele vraagstukken op en draag je bij aan verbeteringen in het planningsproces. Samen met de Sourcing Manager vorm je het aanspreekpunt voor onze leveranciers. Je onderhoudt de dagelijkse contacten en zorgt voor een goede afstemming tussen Lucas Bols en haar leveranciers. Supply Chain Team Het Supply Chain team is verantwoordelijk voor de inkoop (sourcen) van het volledige portfolio bij diverse distilleerderijen, voornamelijk binnen Europa. Daarnaast verzorgt het team het transport naar het centrale magazijn in Zoetermeer (of rechtstreeks naar de markt) en de wereldwijde distributie vanuit het magazijn naar klanten. Gezien het premium karakter van het portfolio hanteert Lucas Bols hoge standaarden op het gebied van productkwaliteit en service. Ook het Customer Service Team (CST), waar alle orders worden ontvangen en verwerkt, maakt onderdeel uit van Supply Chain. Daarnaast vallen het Sales & Operations Planning-proces (S&OP) en de implementatie van projecten met supply chain-impact, zoals new product introductions, binnen de verantwoordelijkheid van het team. Het Supply Chain team bestaat uit circa tien medewerkers en is gevestigd in Amsterdam, Zoetermeer en New York. Verantwoordelijkheden Op basis van voorraden en verwachte verkopen wordt een (materiaal)behoefteplan opgesteld, dat wordt vertaald naar Purchase Orders richting leveranciers. In nauwe samenwerking met collega’s en leveranciers wordt bepaald welke producten op welk moment geleverd dienen te worden. Intern komen veel vragen vanuit ons Customer Service Team over (aanstaande) beschikbaarheid van producten. Het accuraat en volledig informeren van stakeholders en het meedenken in scenario’s om planningen waar nodig aan te passen, vormt een belangrijk onderdeel van de functie. De scope is afwisselend en dat maakt het leuk: naast Final Products (bijvoorbeeld een doos met zes flessen Bols Blue Curaçao voor een markt in Azië) vallen ook diverse materialen binnen de verantwoordelijkheid van de Supply Planner, zoals de import van lege Delftsblauwe kruiken en huisjes. Lucas Bols werkt met Oracle maar al onze leveranciers hebben hun eigen ERP systemen, variërend van bijna handmatige Excel files tot geavanceerde SAP systemen. Iedere relatie is weer anders en heeft zo zijn eigenaardigheden en voor – en nadelen. Communicatie tussen Lucas Bols en onze partners is een hoofdtaak van de Supply Planner. De internationale spreiding van partners, onder andere in Nederland, Frankrijk en Canada, vraagt om een flexibele en interculturele benadering. Profiel HBO werk- & kennisniveau, bij voorkeur in logistiek, supply chain of bedrijfskunde 0-3 jaar werkervaring Uitstekende beheersing van Nederlands & Engels in woord en geschrift is vereist Goede beheersing van Excel en bij voorkeur ervaring met ERP-systemen Affiniteit met productieprocessen, waardoor je de impact van keuzes en de haalbaarheid van verzoeken goed kunt beoordelen en toelichten Analytisch sterk, accuraat en gestructureerd, met een proactieve en probleemoplossende instelling Communicatief vaardig, zelfstandig en standvastig, met oog voor samenwerking en het opbouwen van goede relaties Wij bieden Een uitdagende en afwisselende functie binnen een internationaal opererend bedrijf met iconische merken Een werkplek op een unieke locatie aan het Museumplein in Amsterdam of op ons goed bereikbare kantoor in Zoetermeer Een klein en hecht team waarin je veel verantwoordelijkheid krijgt, zelfstandig kunt werken en de ruimte hebt om met eigen ideeën en initiatieven te komen Uitstekende arbeidsvoorwaarden, waaronder 30 vakantiedagen Een dagelijkse verzorgde lunch op kantoor Bedrijfsinformatie Lucas Bols is een toonaangevende speler op de internationale markt voor gedistilleerde dranken voor cocktails, met een uniek erfgoed dat teruggaat tot 1575 in Amsterdam. Ons merk Bols is daarmee het oudste gedistilleerde merk ter wereld. In 2023 realiseerde Lucas Bols een omzet van circa €100 miljoen. Wij zijn actief in meer dan 110 landen met een portfolio van premium- en superpremiummerken, waaronder Bols Liqueurs, Passoã, Pallini en Tequila Partida. Daarnaast beschikken wij over een sterk portfolio van regionale jenever-, gin-, vodka- en likeurmerken, waaronder in Nederland Bols Jenever, Bokma, Hoppe, Pisang Ambon en Coebergh. Al 450 jaar innoveren wij voor en met bartenders. Die focus heeft ons gebracht tot waar we vandaag staan: de nummer één likeurenrange wereldwijd (met uitzondering van de VS) en wereldwijd marktleider in het jeneversegment. Sterke merken worden gebouwd door sterke mensen. Daarom zijn wij altijd op zoek naar nieuw talent en frisse inzichten. Werken bij Lucas Bols betekent onderdeel zijn van een ambitieus en gedreven team dat intensief samenwerkt met één gezamenlijk doel: Lucas Bols positioneren als dé wereldwijde autoriteit op het gebied van cocktails. Voor meer informatie over onze organisatie verwijzen wij naar onze website: www.lucasbols.com Show more Show less Seniority level Mid-Senior level Employment type Full-time Job function Management and Manufacturing Industries Beverage Manufacturing Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at The Lucas Bols Company by 2x See who you know

title: SAP Finance Consultant – Amsterdam – Freelance
description: SAP Finance Consultant – Amsterdam – Freelance Leading consultancy require a SAP Finance Consultant on a freelance basis. As a pivotal member of the SAP practice. Your expertise will be the compass guiding organizations oward more efficient and secure collaboration. Imagine collaborating closely with clients and colleagues, sharing your insights, and contributing to a future where innovation and excellence thrive. Join us in making a real impact as we journey through the realms of digital possibility. As a Consultant SAP Finance, you'll be a driving force behind digital transformation and client projects. Your expertise will be the compass supporting organizations towards a digital enterprise and collaboration supported by SAP S/4HANA Finance solutions both on-premise and in the cloud. You are the type of consultant who naturally enthuses others with your ideas and clear vision. As our Consultant SAP Finance you are involved in implementation projects and support. Bring minimal 5 years of hands-on experience in SAP S/4HANA Finance (Fi, Co, AA, AP/AR, etc) implementation and support Experience in public sector is a pre. Experience with Vendor Invoice Management (VIM) is a pre. Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. Communicate fluently in Dutch and English Capability to support our commercial staff with presales activities SAP S/4HANA certifications This is a freelance position based in Amsterdam offering 600 euro per day for an initial 6 month engagement. title: Director of Merchandising
description: For A Better You At iHerb, we believe that living a healthy and balanced life should be easy and accessible to everyone. As a team member, we’ll empower you to live this promise each day as you make a truly global impact in your career. We constantly strive for innovation, while transforming and improving the online shopping experience for our customers. We believe that individually we are incredible, but when we come together, our growth is infinite. In an industry that is constantly evolving, we are on a mission to make an impact on the global market, and the individual and collaborative efforts of our people are paramount to helping us succeed. Whether you work in one of our logistics centers, technology hubs, corporate offices or even from home, your role at iHerb will take you beyond what’s expected, turning challenge into change. If you're ready for it, we want you to join our team. Get started now. Job Summary The Regional Senior Buyer is responsible for leading the evaluating, curating, and expanding the product assortment for the European region. This includes identifying high-potential brands, developing vendor partnerships, securing competitive commercial terms, and using data-driven insights to drive category and revenue growth. The role requires strong commercial judgement and the ability to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution. The Regional Senior Buyer sources and manages brands that meet customer needs, comply with regional requirements, and align with market trends within Europe. Job Expectations Work closely with the Senior Director Merchandising and collaborate with global merchandising teams as needed, to align on business strategies, priorities, and category direction Partner with in-market leadership, including Region Leaders (dotted line), to build and maintain an assortment relevant to the market Own key category metrics (KPIs) for new and existing brands, including pricing, gross margin, product mix, promotions and full product lifecycle, while driving continuous improvement Understand and navigate Europe-specific compliance, regulatory and supply-chain requirements to ensure proper and compliant buying practices Partner with Compliance, Regulatory teams to manage catalog additions, documentation, and approvals for the region Procure products directly from manufacturers or authorized distributors, following company policies and regional sourcing standards Negotiate competitive commercial terms, including everyday pricing, marketing budgets, rebates, accruals, delivery schedules, and volume agreements Build and maintain strong vendor relationships to support innovation, margin improvement, and consistent communication on capabilities and expectations Manage vendor contracts, product evaluation, and issue resolution in collaboration with cross-functional partners Drive new product and brand onboarding to ensure Pan-European Experience Preferred Education Requirements: Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Merchandising or related field preferred, or a combination of education and equivalent work experience. title: Regional Inventory & Planning Specialist - EU
description: For A Better You At iHerb, we believe that living a healthy and balanced life should be easy and accessible to everyone. As a team member, we’ll empower you to live this promise each day as you make a truly global impact in your career. We constantly strive for innovation, while transforming and improving the online shopping experience for our customers. We believe that individually we are incredible, but when we come together, our growth is infinite. In an industry that is constantly evolving, we are on a mission to make an impact on the global market, and the individual and collaborative efforts of our people are paramount to helping us succeed. Whether you work in one of our logistics centers, technology hubs, corporate offices or even from home, your role at iHerb will take you beyond what’s expected, turning challenge into change. If you're ready for it, we want you to join our team. Get started now. At iHerb, we are growing our European business and looking for people who enjoy building, improving, and making an impact in a fast-moving environment. As Regional Inventory & Planning Specialist, you will help shape how inventory is planned and managed across Europe. Working closely with our Global Inventory & Planning team, you will support the growth of the European business by ensuring the right products are available at the right time while balancing inventory efficiency and customer experience. Your work will directly support sales performance, product launches, promotions, and regional growth initiatives. You will collaborate across Merchandising, Marketing, Operations, Supply Planning, and external suppliers to turn data and market insights into effective inventory and replenishment plans. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving problems, improving processes, and taking ownership in a collaborative international environment where there is real opportunity to contribute, grow, and help scale the business. What You’ll Do Build and maintain demand forecasts to support inventory planning, product availability, and regional growth Partner with cross-functional teams across Merchandising, Marketing, Operations, and Supply Planning to align inventory, promotions, launches, and replenishment plans Monitor inventory health and forecast performance, proactively identifying risks, opportunities, and improvement areas Analyze sales trends, demand patterns, and business performance to improve forecast accuracy and support data-driven decisions Collaborate with suppliers and internal stakeholders to support smooth purchasing, replenishment, and day-to-day execution Drive continuous improvements in planning tools, reporting, automation, and ways of working to support scalability and efficiency What We’re Looking For Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to turn data into clear actions and business insights A proactive and hands-on mindset, with an interest in improving processes and finding smarter ways of working Comfortable working in a fast-moving and evolving environment Strong communication and collaboration skills across international and cross-functional teams Attention to detail combined with commercial and operational awareness Experience & Skills 7+ years of experience in demand planning, forecasting, inventory management, or related supply chain roles Experience in e-commerce, retail, or other fast-paced environments is a plus Strong Excel skills required; SQL knowledge is an advantage Experience with planning or inventory management systems (SAP IBP preferred) Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain, Business, or a related field — or equivalent practical experience What You Can Expect A collaborative and international team environment The opportunity to help shape and grow iHerb’s European operations Meaningful ownership and the ability to make a visible impact A business that values ideas, continuous improvement, and initiative Hybrid working model (3 days per week in the office) Regional travel within Europe (approximately 10–20%) Why Join iHerb? We are building for the long term in Europe and looking for people who want to grow with us. You’ll have the chance to contribute ideas, improve processes, and help shape how we operate as we continue to expand. We welcome different perspectives, backgrounds, and ways of thinking — and we know that strong teams are built through diversity and collaboration. If you like wild growth and working with happy, enthusiastic over-achievers, you'll enjoy your career with us! Staffing Agency Submission Notice iHerb does not accept unsolicited 3rd party ("Agency") candidates. If you are an Agency, please send any requests to be considered as a supplier in our Vendor Management System to staffingvendors@iherb.com. Do not contact iHerb employees directly. If requested to work on a role, any Agency candidates would be presented through the internal recruiting organization. iHerb is an Equal Opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status. iHerb provides equal employment opportunities to all applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment. Garanta que cada vendedor e comprador do Mercado Livre receba e entregue seus produtos o mais rápido possível, impactando diretamente na experiência de milhões de usuários. A posição é presencial em Araçariguama - São Paulo. Os turnos variam conforme a operação e são detalhados durante o processo seletivo. Montagem de pedidos: localizar os produtos que nossos usuários compraram na plataforma e prepará-los para envio com agilidade. Ter disposição para o trabalho em equipe, atitude positiva e curiosidade genuína para realizar cada tarefa da melhor forma. Não é necessária experiência prévia. Esta posição é uma oportunidade de primeiro emprego. Saiba mais sobre nossa cultura e nosso negócio no nosso site . O melhor está chegando! title: Data Analyst
description: Job Title: Data Analyst – Supply Chain & Logistics Company: Aaratech Inc. Experience: 2–4 years Compensation: $65,000 – $80,000 per year Work Authorization At this time, we are only able to consider candidates who are authorized to work in the United States without sponsorship. Job Summary Aaratech Inc. is seeking a Data Analyst – Supply Chain & Logistics to support supply chain operations through data-driven insights. In this role, you will analyze logistics, inventory, and operational data to improve efficiency, optimize supply chain performance, and support strategic business decisions. Key Responsibilities Collect, clean, and analyze supply chain and logistics data from multiple sources. Develop dashboards and reports to monitor inventory, transportation, and operational performance. Identify trends, inefficiencies, and opportunities for process improvement. Collaborate with supply chain, operations, procurement, and business teams to support data-driven decision-making. Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and reporting quality. Present analytical findings and recommendations to stakeholders. Qualifications Bachelor's degree in Data Science, Information Technology, Computer Science, Supply Chain Management, Business Analytics, or a related field. 2–4 years of experience as a Data Analyst, preferably in supply chain, logistics, or operations. Strong experience with SQL, Microsoft Excel, and data visualization tools. Experience working with supply chain, inventory, or logistics data. Strong analytical, communication, and problem-solving skills. Preferred Skills Experience with Power BI, Tableau, or similar Business Intelligence tools. Knowledge of supply chain analytics, inventory management, and logistics reporting. Basic knowledge of Python or R for data analysis. Experience working with ERP systems such as SAP or Oracle is a plus. Any certifications such as Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst Associate, Tableau Desktop Specialist, or Google Data Analytics Professional Certificate are a plus. About Aaratech Inc. Aaratech Inc. is a technology-driven organization focused on delivering innovative solutions across cybersecurity, healthcare, and enterprise domains. We are committed to helping organizations optimize supply chain operations, improve business performance, and drive informed decision-making through advanced analytics and innovative technology solutions.

title: SAP Architect
description: SAP FICO Solution Architect Permanent | Germany - Bad Homburg (Hybrid) - 550-750p/d - 6 month rolling contract Shape the Future of Enterprise Finance Transformation We're partnering with a leading global organisation to recruit an experienced SAP FICO Solution Architect to play a key role in the design, integration and delivery of enterprise-wide SAP S/4HANA solutions. This is an exciting opportunity for an accomplished SAP Financials professional to lead large-scale finance transformation initiatives, working closely with senior business stakeholders to define technology strategy, optimise financial processes and deliver innovative SAP solutions across a complex global landscape. You'll act as the technical authority for SAP Financials, ensuring seamless integration across finance and business modules while influencing the future ERP roadmap. The Role As the SAP FICO Solution Architect, you'll work closely with Finance, Technology and Business leadership to understand strategic objectives and translate business requirements into scalable SAP solutions. You'll be responsible for defining end-to-end solution architecture, overseeing SAP Financials integration, providing technical leadership across projects and ensuring best practice is embedded throughout the delivery lifecycle. This role combines architecture, stakeholder engagement and hands-on SAP expertise, making it ideal for someone who enjoys influencing strategy while remaining close to solution delivery. Key Responsibilities Solution Architecture & Design Design enterprise-wide SAP FICO solutions within an SAP S/4HANA environment. Define architecture across Finance and Accounting processes, ensuring scalability and best practice. Act as the technical owner for SAP Financials integration across multiple business functions. Develop architecture standards and contribute to the long-term ERP strategy. SAP Financials & Cross-Module Integration Lead the design and configuration of SAP FICO modules including: General Ledger Accounts Payable Accounts Receivable Asset Accounting Controlling Tax Treasury Cost Centre Accounting Internal Orders Lease Management Project Costing Profitability Analysis Revenue Accounting & Recognition (RAR) Ensure seamless integration across SAP SD, MM, BW, BPC, Procurement-to-Pay (P2P) and Order-to-Cash (O2C) processes. Collaborate with technical teams to optimise cross-functional SAP solutions. Project Delivery Lead and support large-scale SAP S/4HANA implementation and transformation programmes. Work closely with project managers, technical teams and business stakeholders throughout the project lifecycle. Manage technical risks, dependencies and solution quality. Support system upgrades, enhancements and future SAP releases. Drive successful testing, deployment and production readiness activities. Stakeholder Engagement Partner with senior business leaders to understand strategic priorities and technology requirements. Translate complex business challenges into practical SAP solutions. Present solution designs and recommendations to executive stakeholders. Mentor technical teams and support knowledge sharing across the organisation. Continuous Improvement Identify opportunities to improve financial processes through technology. Contribute to the SAP technology roadmap and future-state architecture. Promote best practice across solution design, governance and delivery. What We're Looking For You'll be an experienced SAP Solution Architect with a strong background in SAP Financials and enterprise transformation. You'll ideally have: Proven experience as an SAP FICO Architect or SAP Finance Solution Architect. Extensive hands-on experience with SAP S/4HANA . Strong knowledge of: SAP FICO SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) SAP BPC SAP Fiori SAP BW Deep understanding of financial processes including General Ledger, AP, AR, Tax, Treasury, Asset Accounting and Controlling. Strong knowledge of SAP SD and MM integration. Experience delivering at least three full lifecycle SAP implementations. Excellent stakeholder management skills, with the ability to engage senior executives and business leaders. Experience producing functional specifications and supporting development teams. Strong communication, leadership and mentoring capabilities. Desirable SAP certifications. Experience working within multinational organisations. Knowledge of Finance Transformation programmes. Experience supporting global ERP deployments. Background in Accounting or Finance. Why Join? This is an opportunity to join a global organisation investing heavily in its enterprise technology landscape. You'll benefit from: Working on large-scale SAP S/4HANA transformation programmes. Exposure to complex international business environments. Significant stakeholder engagement at Director and Executive level. A collaborative, high-performing technology function. Opportunities to influence enterprise architecture and future technology strategy. Hybrid working and excellent long-term career progression. Interested? If you're an experienced SAP FICO Solution Architect looking to play a strategic role in major enterprise transformation programmes, we'd love to hear from you. Apply today for a confidential discussion. title: Sr. SAP Engineer - Platform
description: About Protera Welcome to Protera Technologies, where we're reimagining how SAP-centric organizations work in the cloud. Since 1998, we've been pioneers in bringing SAP and related applications to the cloud—think Microsoft Azure and AWS. Our global crew, spanning the US with offices in Chicago (HQ), Athens, Greece, Barcelona, Spain and Mumbai, India, is on a mission to make IT smoother, faster, and more fun for our clients. But here's the thing: we're not your typical "serious tech company." While we're obsessed with delivering top-notch IT solutions, we're all about keeping it real, approachable, and enjoyable. We work hard, but we also play hard—whether we're collaborating on cloud optimizations, enhancing security, or just hanging out as a team. At Protera, we believe in empowering SAP-centric organizations with the best tools and tech to drive growth, and we love what we do. Our values? Simple: stay curious, stay authentic, and make meaningful connections. We celebrate wins big and small, whether it's a successful project or a fun team event. If you're looking for a place where tech and fun collide, come join us and see what makes Protera a great place to work. What You'll Do The Senior SAP Engineer - Platform will be responsible for designing, configuring, operating, and optimizing SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) environments that support large‑scale enterprise SAP landscapes. You will ensure seamless integration between BTP and core SAP systems such as S/4HANA and ECC, while maintaining a secure, reliable, and well‑governed platform foundation for product teams, engineering groups, and business applications. You will work across the following areas: Security & Identity Design and maintain Platform Trust Configuration, including certificate‑based trust setups and secure token flows Configure and operate SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS/IPS) for authentication, provisioning, lifecycle management, and federation Implement principal propagation, enabling secure identity flow across BTP ↔ S/4HANA/ECC and cloud interfaces Design and support Single Sign‑On (SSO) architectures, including corporate IdP integrations, proxies, and hybrid identity landscapes Govern platform‑wide role, role collection, and authorization models BTP Services & Provisioning Manage entitlements, subaccount planning, and environment segmentation Provision and maintain service instances and service keys across development, test, and production environments Architect and support the following key services: Job Scheduler SAP HANA Cloud Cloud Transport Management Service (CTMS) Cloud Logging Service Connectivity & Integration Configure and maintain Destinations for system‑to‑system connectivity, API consumption, and secure service access Install, configure, and maintain Cloud Connector instances to enable hybrid connectivity between BTP and on‑prem SAP systems Ensure high‑availability, secure tunnels, and proper resource access governance Platform Management & Operations Operate and optimize the Cloud Management Service, monitoring subaccount health, resource utilization, and operational stability Use BTP CLI and CF CLI for platform operations, automation, and scripted environment management Enforce platform governance standards, naming conventions, and environment consistency Support troubleshooting across the entire platform stack—including identity, services, connectivity, and application runtime issues DevOps & Automation Automate platform provisioning and service configuration using Terraform for BTP Build and support CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, integrating transports, quality checks, and deployment orchestration Manage Cloud Transport Management Service (CTMS) pipelines for multi‑environment delivery Implement operational automation and ensure repeatable, compliant, auditable infrastructure patterns You will serve as a platform SME, collaborating with application teams, security teams, architects, and integration specialists to ensure SAP BTP is delivered as a secure, scalable, and high‑performing enterprise platform. Requirements What You Bring Requirements A proven track record of configuring and maintaining SAP BTP environments with integrations to external systems as well as SAP products such as S/4HANA and ECC , including: Security Platform Trust Configuration SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS/IPS) Principal Propagation Single Sign‑On (SSO) integrations Corporate Identity Provider (IdP) proxies User provisioning and lifecycle management XSUAA service BTP Services Entitlements & quota management Service instance provisioning Service key creation and lifecycle handling Role and role collection configuration Specific Services Job Scheduler SAP HANA Cloud Cloud Transport Management Service Cloud Logging Service SAP DMS Redis Store Object Store Connectivity Destinations (OAuth2, Basic Auth, Principal Propagation, etc.) Cloud Connector setup & maintenance Platform Management Cloud Management Service BTP CLI CF CLI DevOps BTP Terraform GitHub Actions Cloud Transport Management Service integration with CI/CD Why Join Protera? Work with global enterprise clients Learn fast with mentorship, certifications, and career growth Make a real impact in a company where ideas are welcome Flexible work setup (remote/hybrid) People-first culture with a global mindset Ready to Make the Move? We'd love to meet you. Click "Apply Now" and tell us why you're the one. Not sure yet? Check us out at www.protera.com or connect with our team on LinkedIn. Protera is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Benefits In addition to a competitive compensation package, Protera offers a company meal card, private health insurance, and an office full of complimentary snacks and coffee Show more Show less Seniority level Mid-Senior level Employment type Full-time Job function Information Technology Industries IT Services and IT Consulting Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Protera by 2x See who you know

title: Gerente de Projetos SAP Pleno/Sênior
description: Será responsável por planejar, coordenar e executar projetos SAP, garantindo que sejam concluídos dentro dos prazos, escopo e orçamento definidos. Irá atuar na gestão de projetos de implementação, transformação digital, migração para SAP S/4HANA e rollouts, servindo como principal contato entre clientes, equipes de projeto e liderança executiva. O profissional deverá assegurar a entrega dos projetos conforme padrões de qualidade, prazo, escopo e orçamento, promovendo excelência operacional, satisfação dos clientes e geração de valor para o negócio. Procuramos alguém com forte liderança, visão estratégica, habilidade de relacionamento com clientes e experiência comprovada em projetos SAP complexos, tanto nacionais quanto internacionais. Responsabilidades Planejar, coordenar e gerenciar projetos SAP de ponta a ponta, desde a iniciação até o encerramento Liderar projetos de implementação SAP Greenfield, incluindo desenho de processos, configuração, testes e Go-Live Conduzir projetos de conversão SAP S/4HANA Brownfield, garantindo a continuidade operacional e mitigação de riscos Atuar em projetos Bluefield (Selective Data Transition), quando aplicável Gerenciar projetos de rollout nacional e internacional, upgrades, melhorias evolutivas e outros Elaborar e acompanhar cronogramas, orçamento, escopo, qualidade, riscos e indicadores de desempenho Conduzir reuniões executivas e operacionais com clientes, patrocinadores e stakeholders Liderar equipes multidisciplinares compostas por consultores funcionais, ABAP, BASIS, Integração, Segurança e demais especialistas SAP Garantir a aplicação das metodologias SAP Activate, PMBOK, Agile e melhores práticas de governança Gerenciar riscos, mudanças, dependências e impedimentos dos projetos Promover alinhamento entre áreas de negócio e tecnologia Elaborar apresentações executivas, dashboards e relatórios gerenciais para clientes e diretoria Promover a melhoria contínua dos processos, metodologias e práticas de gestão de projetos. Requisitos Experiência comprovada na liderança de projetos SAP de ponta a ponta (End-to-End) Participação em projetos de implementação SAP Greenfield Participação em projetos de conversão SAP S/4HANA Brownfield Experiência em projetos de rollout, upgrade, transformação digital e AMS Vivência em projetos envolvendo múltiplos módulos SAP (FI, CO, MM, SD, PP, EWM, HCM, RE-FX, entre outros) Experiência na gestão simultânea de múltiplos projetos Experiência na gestão de orçamento, cronograma, escopo, qualidade, riscos e recursos Vivência em consultorias SAP ou grandes programas corporativos de transformação Experiência na gestão de equipes multidisciplinares e distribuídas geograficamente Experiência em ambientes multiculturais e relacionamento com clientes internacionais Benefícios 5 dias de ausência programada Parceria movida, aluguel de carro Subsídio de inglês Programa de certificação Wellhub Crédio livre cartão Caju Conexa Saúde (Plataforma de promoção a saúde com 2 consultas por mês por conta da Tec,sendo uma de telepsicologia e outra de telemedicina) Programa de indicação Show more Show less Seniority level Mid-Senior level Employment type Full-time Job function Project Management and Information Technology Industries Computer and Network Security Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Tec2Cloud by 2x See who you know

description: Estamos em busca de um Consultor SAP SD com experiência em projetos e conhecimento na localização Brasil, para atuar na análise de requisitos, definição de arquitetura e implementação de soluções dentro do ecossistema SAP. title: Coordenador(a) Distribuição
description: #SomosKOF e Sabemos Que Nosso Talento é a Chave Do Sucesso. Por Isso, Convidamos Você a Conhecer Esta Nova Oportunidade De Desenvolvimento COORDENADOR(A) DISTRIBUIÇÃO Sobre a Coca-Cola FEMSA Brasil Somos uma das maiores fabricantes do mundo em volume de vendas dos produtos The Coca-Cola Company. Atuamos no Brasil há mais de 20 anos, com mais de 25 mil colaboradores diretos, 11 fábricas e 47 centros de distribuição, 470 mil pontos de venda e mais de 93 milhões de consumidores. Operamos em 19% do território brasileiro, atendendo 52% da população, atuando nos estados de São Paulo, Paraná, Santa Catarina, Rio Grande do Sul, Minas Gerais, Mato Grosso do Sul, Rio de Janeiro e Goiás. Somos responsáveis pela produção, comercialização e distribuição de mais de 169 marcas em nosso portfólio, divididas nas seguintes categorias: bebidas (refrigerantes, hidrotônicos e isotônicos, energéticos, águas, sucos, chás, bebidas vegetais, cervejas, sprits e alcoólicos prontos para beber) e multicategorias (balas e doces). Para obter mais informações, visite www.coca-colafemsa.com Como Será o Seu Dia a Dia Realizar a gestão da equipe e do ambiente de trabalho; Controlar ativos em uso na área de Distribuição (veículos, celulares, carrinhos, etc.); Realizar visitas aos clientes e acompanhar equipe de Distribuição em rota, preenchendo o check list, a fim de identificar oportunidades de melhorias; Acompanhar, treinar e avaliar o desempenho da equipe de entrega; Supervisionar e orientar as atividades executadas pela equipe, realizar o acompanhamento das operações na entrega; Visitar os clientes prontificando-se a atuar na melhoria contínua e eficiência nas entregas; Realizar reuniões de alinhamento com equipe Comercial, a fim de regularizar os principais gap's nos clientes; Demais atividades inerentes ao cargo. Requisitos e Qualificações Formação: Ensino Superior Completo em Logística, Administração ou áreas afins CNH: B Experiência desejável Necessário Ter atuado na área de Transporte e Distribuição; Necessária vivência com Liderança de equipes, habilidade de gestão de pessoas com foco em resultados Conhecimentos Técnicos: Necessário Conhecimento em Pacote Office Intermediário e SAP Necessário CNH Categoria B e prática de direção; Competências Interpessoais: Planejamento, organização, resiliência, agilidade, senso de dono, senso de urgência. Necessário Disponibilidade Total de Horário = Benefícios e qualidade de vida: Vale-alimentação | Vale Refeição| Vale-transporte ou estacionamento | Assistência médica e odontológica | Auxílio farmácia | Desconto em produtos | Cooperativa de crédito | Seguro de vida | Participação nos lucros | Auxílio creche | Kit escolar e de aniversário | Vale brinquedo | Cesta de Natal, entre outros. Diversidade, Equidade e Inclusão Na Cola-Cola FEMSA estamos comprometidos em oferecer as mesmas oportunidades de desenvolvimento para todas as pessoas, independente de origem, raça, idade, orientação sexual, identidade de gênero, deficiência, nacionalidade, status migratório ou qualquer outra característica. Valorizamos a diversidade que nos faz mais fortes. Se você cumpre com os requisitos e seu objetivo é aprender, crescer e desenvolver uma carreira exitosa, te convidamos a enviar sua candidatura. Em KOF, estamos comprometidos em oferecer as mesmas oportunidades de desenvolvimento para todos os candidatos sem origem, raça, estado civil, idade, opinião política, gênero, credo, associação a uma cultura ou filiação a um sindicato, classe social ou econômica, situação familiar. Gravidez, língua, orientação sexual, identidade de gênero, deficiência, doença, nacionalidade ou status migratório e/ou qualquer outro motivo. Valorizamos a diversidade já que nos faz mais fortes. title: Gerente Operações Jr.
description: #SomosKOF e Sabemos Que Nosso Talento é a Chave Do Sucesso. Por Isso, Convidamos Você a Conhecer Esta Nova Oportunidade De Desenvolvimento GERENTE OPERAÇÕES Sobre a Coca-Cola FEMSA Brasil Somos uma das maiores fabricantes do mundo em volume de vendas dos produtos The Coca-Cola Company. Atuamos no Brasil há mais de 20 anos, com mais de 25 mil colaboradores diretos, 11 fábricas e 47 centros de distribuição, 470 mil pontos de venda e mais de 93 milhões de consumidores. Operamos em 19% do território brasileiro, atendendo 52% da população, atuando nos estados de São Paulo, Paraná, Santa Catarina, Rio Grande do Sul, Minas Gerais, Mato Grosso do Sul, Rio de Janeiro e Goiás. Somos responsáveis pela produção, comercialização e distribuição de mais de 169 marcas em nosso portfólio, divididas nas seguintes categorias: bebidas (refrigerantes, hidrotônicos e isotônicos, energéticos, águas, sucos, chás, bebidas vegetais, cervejas, sprits e alcoólicos prontos para beber) e multicategorias (balas e doces). Para obter mais informações, visite www.coca-colafemsa.com Como Será o Seu Dia a Dia Controlar a armazenagem e movimentação de produtos acabados, dentro das normas estabelecidas pela empresa, para assegurar a qualidade do produto e sua adequada movimentação; manter a organização do pátio, facilitando a carga e descarga de caminhões, a movimentação de produtos e a segurança do estoque. Coordenar a supervisão da montagem das cargas, que deverá ocorrer orientada pelas ondas de saída, utilizando-se das sumulas de conferência e tecnologia Optimal Picking para assegurar a correta expedição das cargas; coordenar e garantir o envio de vasilhames, chapatex e pallets, orientando-se pelo planejamento diário de demandas das Plantas; Coordenar a classificação de vasilhames do retorno de rota que posteriormente serão enviados às Plantas. Controle de inventários. Melhoria contínua nos processos da Operação, garantindo produtividade e eficiência para a empresa; coordenar a supervisão do processo de conferência de carga e descarga de caminhões e carretas, orientando e distribuindo os procedimentos, para assegurar o controle correto do estoque. Apoiar a equipe de abastecimento quanto a garantia da disponibilidade de produtos demandados conforme o fechamento diário de vendas. Coordenar a supervisão do processo de emissão de notas fiscais e registros de movimentação de mercadorias (perda, consumo etc.), orientando os procedimentos conforme o caso de forma a atender a legislação e os controles internos de estoque, garantir a utilização do WMS, demais atividades vinculadas ao cargo. Requisitos e Qualificações Formação: Ensino Superior Completo em Logística, Administração ou áreas afins CNH: B Conhecimentos Técnicos: Necessário conhecimento em Pacote Office Intermediário e SAP; conhecimento básico em estatística; Necessário ter atuado na área de Armazém, Logística, Operações; Necessário vivência com Liderança de equipes, habilidade de gestão de pessoas com foco em resultados; Diferencial, trabalhar com métodos PDCA, Kaizen, ferramentas de controle, diagnóstico das oportunidades, análises e ação; CNH: Categoria B Disponibilidade Total de Horário Competências Interpessoais: Planejamento, organização, agilidade, senso de dono, senso de urgência. title: Business Analyst
description: At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture and technology to become the best version of you. And we’re counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better, too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all. Business Analyst, Supervising Associate The opportunity We are seeking a skilled Business Analyst with expertise in SAP SuccessFactors and Data Integration to drive Talent projects forward. The successful candidate will play a crucial role in gathering requirements, analyzing business processes, and ensuring the seamless integration of SAP SuccessFactors with other systems and data sources, contributing to the success of our Talent initiatives. Your Key Responsibilities Requirement Gathering: Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand and document detailed requirements for Talent projects, including SAP SuccessFactors modules and data integration needs Process Analysis: Analyze existing Talent processes, identify areas for improvement, and recommend solutions that leverage SAP SuccessFactors and other technologies to enhance efficiency and effectiveness System Integration: Work closely with technical teams to design and implement data integration solutions, ensuring the accurate and secure flow of data between SAP SuccessFactors and other systems Documentation: Create comprehensive and clear business requirement documents, process maps, use cases, and other relevant documentation to guide project implementation Testing and Validation: Develop test plans, scenarios, and conduct testing to ensure the functionality and integration of SAP SuccessFactors meet business needs and quality standards Stakeholder Communication: Facilitate effective communication between business stakeholders, project teams, and technical experts to ensure a shared understanding of project objectives and progress Change Management: Collaborate with change management teams to support end-users in adapting to new systems and processes resulting from SAP SuccessFactors implementations Issue Resolution: Identify and address project issues and risks related to SAP SuccessFactors and data integration, escalating when necessary to ensure timely resolution Continuous Improvement: Participate in process improvement initiatives, suggesting enhancements to business processes and system functionality Skills And Attributes For Success Solid understanding of Talent processes and system functionalities, particularly within the context of SAP SuccessFactors Proficiency in data integration methodologies, including data mapping, ETL processes, and API integrations Excellent analytical skills, with the ability to translate business needs into actionable technical requirements Strong documentation skills, including the creation of requirement documents, process flows, and test plans Effective communication skills, both written and verbal, to facilitate collaboration among diverse project teams and stakeholders Proficiency in project management software tools and business analysis techniques Detail-oriented with a focus on delivering high-quality, accurate results Positive attitude, adaptability, and willingness to learn and grow within the role Ability to work virtually with global teams To qualify for this role Bachelor's degree in a relevant field; additional certifications in business analysis or SAP SuccessFactors preferred Proven experience as a Business Analyst with a focus on Talent systems, SAP SuccessFactors, and data integration As a Business Analyst, 5 to 8 years of industry experience preferably in Talent What We Offer Dynamic teamwork: You’ll work with brilliant and innovative professionals who are devoted to building a better working world Continuous learning: You’ll develop the mindset and skills to navigate whatever comes next Success as defined by you: We’ll provide the tools and flexibility, so you can make a meaningful impact, your way Transformative leadership: We’ll give the insights, coaching and confidence to be the leader the world needs Diverse and inclusive culture: You’ll be embraced for who you are and empowered to use your voice to help others find theirs. EY | Building a better working world EY exists to build a better working world, helping to create long-term value for clients, people and society and build trust in the capital markets. Enabled by data and technology, diverse EY teams in over 150 countries provide trust through assurance and help clients grow, transform and operate. Working across assurance, consulting, law, strategy, tax and transactions, EY teams ask better questions to find new answers for the complex issues facing our world today. title: Accountant
description: Job Description WHAT YOU'LL DO As the Accountant you will be responsible for monthly and yearly closing, finance projects, online and marketplace accounting. You will be communicating with auditors and stakeholders internal and external for the responsible work areas. Key Responsibilities: Accounts management Support in preparation of Financial statements in compliance with Local and IFRS. Primary SPOC for Revenue Audits and certification, tax audit etc. Ensuring TDS Compliance and timely reporting and filing Ensure monthly and year end activities are done on a timely basis Initiate and carry out general ledger account analysis, bank reconciliations etc. Liaison with Banking, Audit and other external agencies. Fixed Assets Management Set routines for fixed asset tracking and depreciation calculations Ensure FAR is maintained as per the local statutory laws and as per the group guidelines Communicate with internal stakeholders for FA related queries Responsible for FA Audit Ensure physical verification of fixed assets as per the Companies Act requirements Finance Projects & Online Accounting Act as the SPOC for finance function in various automation and transformation projects, new business platform, store project launches, etc. Coordination with Global teams and business partners for communicating finance requirement in structured way to get the right solution delivered. Drive the finance conversations and communicate clearly the accounting/compliance landscape and keep the project team updated with relevant developments in micro/macro-economic environments. Please click here for complete role description WHO YOU'LL WORK WITH You drive the goals forward by collaborating with the Head of Finance & Accounting, relevant business stakeholders, and other colleagues in the Finance & Accounting team. WHO YOU ARE To be successful as Senior Accountant we are looking for people with .... Strong Analytical ability 2 to 6 years of relevant experience in fixed asset accounting, preferably CA qualified Ambitious and goal driven Strong team player Open minded & willing to learn Getting motivated by results and seek business opportunities SAP Experience - Preferably FICO+ Material Management Advanced MS Office specially Excel and Power point. Power BI is an added advantage. Experience of Stakeholder Management roles, both Internal and External (Auditors/Banks etc.) WHO WE ARE ​​ H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​ ​ We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​ ​ WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE ​​ At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. ​​ You will receive 25% off H&M Group brands; H&M, COS, Monki, Weekdays, Arket, & Other Stories We are an inclusive company where you are encouraged to be yourself at work Additional Information This is a full-time position, reporting to Head of Finance & Accounting. Apply by sending in your CV in English as soon as possible, but no later than July 15th, 2026 . Due to data policies, we only accept applications through career page.
