title: Analista de Custos e Orçamento JR
description: Descrição da vaga Na Stefanini, acreditamos no poder da colaboração. Co-criamos soluções inovadoras em parceria com nossos clientes, combinando tecnologia de ponta, inteligência artificial e a criatividade humana. Estamos na vanguarda da resolução de problemas de negócios, proporcionando impacto real em escala global. Ao se juntar à Stefanini, você se torna parte de uma jornada global de transformação. Estamos empenhados em criar impacto positivo não apenas nos negócios, mas também na vida de nossos colaboradores. Se você procura uma oportunidade de crescimento profissional em uma empresa que valoriza inovação, respeito, autonomia e parceria, você encontra aqui! Junte-se a nós e seja parte da mudança! Responsabilidades e atribuições O que esperamos que você realize: Contato com fornecedores; Controle da entrada de notas fiscais e medições de serviços; Validação e acompanhamento dos faturamentos mensais, com análise de desvios; Resolução e acompanhamento de divergências junto às áreas Financeira, Fiscal e Contas a Pagar. Requisitos e qualificações Requisitos: Graduação em Administração, Economia, Contabilidade ou áreas correlatas; Conhecimento em Excel avançado; Conhecimento em sistemas de gestão financeira. Diferenciais: Experiência prévia na área de custos e orçamento; Conhecimento em SAP. Informações adicionais 🍛 Vale-alimentação ou vale-refeição; 👨🏼‍🎓 Desconto em cursos, universidades e instituições de idiomas; 📚 Academia Stefanini — plataforma com cursos on-line, gratuitos, atualizados e com certificado; 🗣 Mentoring; 💉 Clube de vantagens para consultas e exames; 🏥 Assistência médica; 🦷 Assistência odontológica; 💰 Clube de vantagens e descontos nos melhores estabelecimentos; 🛫 Clube de viagens; 🐶 Convênio para pets. Etapas do processo Cadastro Mapeamento Comportamental Entrevista Comportamental Entrevista Cliente Contratação Stefanini: Acreditar para Cocriar 🌟 Na Stefanini, a inovação não é apenas uma meta; é uma jornada que trilhamos juntos, onde a colaboração se torna a essência da transformação. 🤝 Com 37 anos de história e uma presença global em mais de 40 países, contamos com uma equipe de 38 mil talentos apaixonados, prontos para transformar desafios em oportunidades por meio da tecnologia. 💡 Acreditamos que cada projeto é uma oportunidade e que, juntos, podemos moldar um futuro mais ágil, eficiente e conectado. Nossa missão vai além de oferecer soluções: buscamos cocriar com nossos clientes, integrando inteligência artificial e tecnologias emergentes para revolucionar o cotidiano das empresas. 🌍✨ Aqui, cada voz conta e cada ideia tem o poder de impactar. Valorizamos a humildade, o respeito, o comprometimento e a coragem, construindo uma cultura onde a inovação floresce. Aqui, liderar é dar o exemplo, e é por isso que inspiramos um ambiente dinâmico onde tecnologia e talento se complementam. Se você sonha grande, tem coragem de desafiar o convencional e quer fazer parte de uma equipe que acredita no poder da cocriação, junte-se a nós! Vamos, juntos, acreditar e cocriar um futuro brilhante, onde cada passo representa uma nova conquista!🚀✨ Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Full-time Job function Finance and Sales Industries Business Consulting and Services Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Stefanini Brasil by 2x See who you know

title: ANALISTA PLENO DE CONTRATOS & PAGAMENTOS - STAFF
description: Somos a Tahto, uma empresa com uma nova mentalidade de negócios, que usa a tecnologia não apenas para criar soluções de atendimento ao cliente, mas para manter o ser humano no centro de tudo o que fazemos. Somos mais de 12 mil colaboradores espalhados em 4 estados do Brasil, pessoas de todas as diversidades, culturas, raças, gêneros, e que são valorizadas pela sua história. Para compor ainda mais esse time de sucesso, buscamos talentos que nos ajudem a ser uma empresa protagonista em transformar a vida de pessoas com soluções em tecnologia e inovações, sempre com muito tato, empatia e qualidade. Se você tem esse perfil e é movido por desafios e novas conquistas, venha fazer parte do nosso time! A Tahto valoriza o potencial dos seus colaboradores e por isso conta com um programa de oportunidades internas, voltado para quem busca crescimento e desenvolvimento proMedidas jurídicas e administrativas para regularização imobiliária.fissional. Você pode ser um deles!" Contamos com o seu calor humano e a sua atitude imaginativa para juntos escrevermos uma nova história. A Tahto é tecnologia, calor humano, criatividade. A Tahto somos nós! #soutahto Responsabilidades e atribuições Análise documental e registral de imóveis; Apoio técnico em processos de desapropriação por parte do poder público; Atuação na baixa de indisponibilidades e possíveis outros gravames na matrícula; Atos para incorporação de bens ao patrimônio da companhia; Elaboração de esclarecimento e manifestações em procedimentos cartorários; Acompanhamento e suscitação de dúvidas junto a cartórios de registro de imóveis; Medidas jurídicas e administrativas para regularização imobiliária; Executar demais atividades correlatas com a função. title: Funcional de Sistema SAP Junior
description: Nossa cultura é sobre colocar a mão na massa, ter autonomia, colaborar e crescer rápido. Estamos construindo um ambiente onde as pessoas se desenvolvem junto com a empresa e esperamos o mesmo de quem chega por aqui. Se você tem sentimento de dono, sede de aprendizado, gosta de resolver problemas, se desafiar e quer uma carreira acelerada, vem com a gente! Na nossa página de carreiras você encontra tudo sobre nossa Cultura e dicas do Processo. Não deixe de conferir: clicando aqui Precisa de ajuda com o currículo? Para facilitar sua candidatura, você pode baixar seu currículo do LinkedIn ou preencher o modelo da V360 como inspiração, garantindo que nenhuma informação importante seja esquecida. Caso opte pelo nosso modelo, faça uma cópia e edite com seus dados : clicando aqui Agora, conheça agora um pouco sobre a oportunidade: Nosso time de Analista Funcional de Sistemas tem como principal função fazer a tradução das necessidades de processo para os sistemas, apoiando tanto o time do cliente, quanto o time técnico V360 por meio de especificações e mapeamentos. Esse time também é responsável por, eventualmente, realizar configurações dentro da plataforma e configurar variáveis referentes ao conector V360 x SAP. Responsabilidades e Atribuições: Participar de reuniões com Stakeholders e clientes V360 a fim de compreender e interpretar requisitos de negócio e traduzi-los em configurações suportados pelo sistema V360; Participar em discussões que envolvem o SAP e propor soluções que resolvam o problema levando em conta as especificidades SAP e V360; Criar Especificações Funcionais de maneira a resolver problemas e cenários de integração entre o SAP e o V360; Realizar testes de aderência dos cenários do projeto entre o conector V360 x SAP; Se aprofundar no produto V360 para garantir o alinhamento de expectativas entre cliente e time técnico; Levantar especificações de configurações e eventualmente colocá-las em prática; Apoiar o time na resolução de problemas, diagnóstico e investigações de problemas e erros relacionados ao SAP. Requisitos e Qualificações: Graduação completa em Análise e Desenvolvimento de Sistemas, Sistemas de Informação, Engenharia de Software, Ciência da Computação, Engenharia de Produção, Administração de Empresas ou áreas correlatas de tecnologia e gestão de sistemas. Ter experiência com Projetos de Implantação de Sistemas; Conhecimento em Sistemas e Softwares; Conhecimento básico em linguagens de programação ou scripting, com habilidade em escrever scripts ou pequenas personalizações que podem ser necessárias para ajustes mais específicos; Capacidade de realizar consultas e modificações em bancos de dados, utilizando SQL ou outras linguagens de consulta. Diferenciais: Vivência prática em projetos de implementação, configuração e suporte de sistemas empresariais, preferencialmente com foco em parametrização; Academia SAP e/ou Certificações SAP; Ter conhecimento no Módulo SAP SD; Ter conhecimento no Módulo SAP FI; Vivência prática com requisitos de negócios. Benefícios: Temos experiência junto às maiores empresas do Brasil e levamos isso como bagagem na construção de soluções eficientes e escaláveis. A colaboração está no nosso DNA. Aqui, trabalhamos lado a lado (na prática, em home office ), e em pouco tempo você estará não só aprendendo, mas também trocando experiências e contribuindo ativamente. Benefícios: Remuneração de R$ 4.000,00 Possibilidade de ganhar PLR anual por desempenho (até 7 salários) Auxílio Home Office no valor de R$300 Refeição R$42,00 ao dia útil Convênio saúde Bradesco sem coparticipação Day off de aniversário Temos programa de Stock Options Seguro de vida ‍♀️ Totalpass Auxílio creche para até 5 anos da criança no valor de até R$638,13 Mentoria e Plano de Carreira Contratação CLT ⏲ Horário flexível dentro do período comercial Autonomia, aprendizado e trocas constantes ️ Trabalho 100% remoto, com encontros presenciais pontuais no RJ para integração do time e, eventualmente, com clientes em todo o Brasil Etapas do Processo: Saiba como será sua jornada no nosso processo seletivo: Candidatura Bate-Papo com Gente e Cultura Bate-Papo com a Liderança Teste de Integridade Contratação As etapas de triagem acontecem exclusivamente pela plataforma InHire , enquanto as etapas síncronas são realizadas pelo Google Meet . Todas as fases do processo são eliminatórias. Nas etapas iniciais, nossa comunicação será feita por e-mail , então fique de olho na sua caixa de entrada (e no spam, por precaução) para não perder nenhum prazo ou instrução importante. title: Maintenance Coordinator
description: Facility Coordinator / Maintenance Coordinator - new site near Breda Want to help shape the facilities set-up of a brand-new logistics site near Breda? As Facility Coordinator, you’ll be the person keeping the site safe, reliable and running smoothly — coordinating maintenance, suppliers, building services and improvement actions from day one. If you enjoy coordinating multiple stakeholders, improving ways of working, and making a visible impact on day-to-day operations, this is a great opportunity to grow with us. What you’ll be doing Coordinate daily and planned maintenance to ensure work is completed accurately, safely and on time Partner with approved vendors to maintain warehouse equipment and building systems to a high standard Track performance against agreed targets, analyse results and follow up on corrective actions Maintain accurate records and produce required reports (e.g., maintenance logs, vendor documentation and compliance records) Deliver excellent service to internal stakeholders and customers by responding quickly and communicating clearly Apply and promote company policies, safety procedures and relevant legal requirements to ensure full compliance Work safely in line with GXO Health & Safety training, standards and procedures, and proactively contribute to a safer environment for colleagues and visitors Support initiatives that reduce our environmental impact (energy, water and fuel use, waste and emissions) and improve overall site performance Contribute to continuous improvement by identifying opportunities, sharing ideas and helping implement better ways of working What you’ll bring MBO Level 4 or HBO in Facility Management (or a comparable qualification/experience) 3+ years’ experience in a similar facilities/maintenance coordination role, ideally within Logistics, warehousing, manufacturing, warehousing or another operational environment Technical knowledge with air conditioning units and/or electrical engineering is desirable - You don’t need to be a hands-on engineer, but you should be comfortable coordinating technical maintenance topics, asking the right questions and working with specialist vendors Strong English communication skills are important; Dutch is a plus, but not essential Familiarity with LEAN ways of working in a warehouse or operational environment What we offer you A competitive salary plus benefits You’ll help shape a new site, not just maintain an existing one Monday–Friday stability Autonomy and ownership Exposure to multiple stakeholders Career development via GXO University A visible role where your work has daily impact Unlock the potential of your future! Your work environment Working at GXO Logistics means being part of a dynamic, international organisation where no two days are the same. You’ll have room to take ownership, share ideas and keep developing—supported by a team that wants you to succeed. GXO Logistics delivers advanced supply chain solutions for many of the world’s most successful companies across industries such as e-commerce, food & retail and industrial multinationals. In the BeNe region (The Netherlands and Belgium), GXO operates from 30+ locations, with the headquarters in Eindhoven (NL). Want to know more about GXO Logistics? Watch the video below or visit our website www.gxo.com https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3P7NC8gl3bg In short, this is the next place you want to work! Interested? Apply via the button and upload your most recent CV—we’d love to hear from you. For more details, please contact Sharon Bird, Corporate Recruiter. She can be reached via email at Sharon.bird1@gxo.com Acquisition in response to this vacancy is not appreciated. title: Accounts Payable Specialist
description: Job Description Accounts Payable Specialist Location: Breda Salary: Competitive + Benefits To strengthen our AP Team we are looking for an Accounts Payable Specialist to join the team in Breda (NL) You will be responsible for all transactions and payments to suppliers, consignors and Customers for one or more Ritchie Bros. companies. Within our group we have several companies that have local entities in multiple countries. Building extensive knowledge about the entity that is under your supervision is essential to ensure smooth operating of our company. Your main task is to verify and process all documents, following our SOX controls and proactively communicate with your stakeholders and make sure that all the payments are done on time. Key Responsibilities Assess, verify and process invoices, credit notes, purchase orders, refunds, Advanced payments and expense reports from various offices and sale sites in the EMEA regions Input data/invoices into Basware and Oracle ERP system Maintain accurate and complete information on AP subledgers Prepare invoices for payment accurately and in due time Follow up on unpaid and late invoices Investigate and resolve discrepancies independently and/or in collaboration with sale site staff by exercising ethical and objective judgment skills Ensure appropriate authorizations in accordance with the company’s contractual obligations Actively contribute to the success of Accounts Payable month/quarter/year end accounting closing and reporting Work close to Project team to improve and simplify our processes Support the other team members when it’s needed by providing trainings/guidance, helping with the workload, anticipating issues and making sure our processes are accurate Here Is What You Bring It goes without saying that you have strong attention to detail, you are proactive, accountable, organized and have an excellent communication skill. A first-time right approach enables you to process tasks swiftly and contributes to the service level that you want to maintain to both your colleagues and our clients. If you have a proven commitment to providing fantastic customer service, we would love to have you on board. Experience in making process improvements in an operating department is a plus. Furthermore, you have: Accounts Payable and accounting experience (2 years or more) Professional level English Experience with Oracle (advantageous) Intermediate/advanced level of Excel and PowerPoint Residence and work permit for the Netherlands What We Offer Our success is built on the drive and dedication of our people. As we grow around the globe, there will be many opportunities to advance your career – we have proven this throughout our 65+ years in business. And, we will take care of you along the way. Here is how: Hybrid flexibility Pension Plan with company match Appealing Employee share purchase plan Comprehensive health benefits Gym sponsorship Who are we? Ritchie Bros. has grown from a small-town auctioneer to a global asset management and disposition company. We help our customers buy and sell a wide range of used and unused equipment for construction, mining, transportation, agriculture, oil & gas, lifting & material handling, forestry & other industries. title: Senior SAP Platform Architect (m/w/d)
description: ENERCON gehört seit 40 Jahren zu den Technologieführern im Bereich der Windenergie. Als erster Hersteller von Windenergieanlagen setzte das Unternehmen ein getriebeloses Antriebskonzept ein, das ein Merkmal aller ENERCON Windenergieanlagen ist. Auch in anderen Bereichen, wie z.B. Rotorblattdesign, Steuerungstechnik und Netzanschlusstechnik ist ENERCON führend und beweist mit einer Vielzahl von technologischen Neuentwicklungen immer wieder seine Innovationskraft. Senior SAP Platform Architect (m/w/d) Innovative Ideen sind das Markenzeichen unserer Erfolge und treiben uns an. Wir setzen uns mit Leidenschaft dafür ein, Windenergieprojekte weltweit zu realisieren und die energietechnischen Herausforderungen von morgen zu meistern. Du kannst mit deinem Engagement dazu beitragen, die Zukunft der erneuerbaren Energien zu gestalten. Die Position ist mit einer Gehaltsspanne von 74.400 € bis 81.200 € brutto jährlich ausgestattet – abhängig von Qualifikation und relevanter Berufserfahrung. Darüber hinaus profitierst Du von zusätzlichen Sonderzahlungen in Form von Urlaubs- und Weihnachtsgeld, gemäß Betriebszugehörigkeit, die sich gestaffelt entwickeln: nach sechs Monaten 20%, nach neun Monaten 30%, nach zwanzig Monaten 40%, nach zweiunddreißig Monaten 50% des Monatsgehalts. Deine Aufgaben: Sicherstellung des stabilen, sicheren und performanten Betriebes unserer SAP-Systemlandschaft (On-Premise und BTP) Troubleshooting sowie Durchführung von Root-Cause-Analysen bei Störungen Verantwortung für Monitoring- und Alerting-Lösungen Betrieb und Administration von SAP BTP Services Konzeption von System- und Integrationsarchitekturen, auch in Zusammenarbeit mit anderen Bereichen der IT, wie z. title: Consultant SAP Digital Manufacturing (all genders)
description: Wir gehören zu den Top-Adressen in der IT-Welt und fokussieren uns auf die Optimierung der Kerngeschäfts­prozesse unserer Kunden. Durch den smarten Einsatz maßgeschneiderter IT-Lösungen unterstützen wir unsere Kunden dabei, das Beste aus ihrem Business und aktuellen Technologien herauszuholen. Unseren Erfolg aber erreichen wir nur durch eins: die Menschen bei adesso! Sie bringen Ideen, fachliches Know how und Technologien zusammen. Gemeinsam programmieren wir (deine) Zukunft bei adesso. DEINE ROLLE Produktionsanalysen: Du führst Produktionsanalysen (auch auf dem Shopfloor) durch, erhebst Anforderungen und gestaltest Digitalisierungs-Use-Cases. Strategische Beratung: Du berätst unsere Kunden zu dem sinnvollen Einsatz von Software in der Produktion und hilfst ihnen dabei die richtige Lösung für ihre Herausforderungen zu finden. Fachkonzepte: Du entwickelst Fachkonzepte für die SAP Digital Manufacturing und setzt diese in Projekten um. Projektleitung: Du leitest Projekte und begeisterst Stakeholder für den Erfolg der digitalen Transformation. Wissenstransfer: Du teilst dein Wissen durch Blogbeiträge, Vorträge und Schulungen und stärkst das interne Know-how. Sales Support: Du unterstützt das Sales-Team bei der Akquise spannender, neuer Projekte. DEIN PROFIL Abschluss: Du hast einen Abschluss (Studium oder Ausbildung) im Bereich (Wirtschafts-) Informatik, (Wirtschafts-) Ingenieurwesen, Produktionstechnik, Maschinenbau, Elektrotechnik oder vergleichbare Kenntnisse. SAP-Erfahrung: Du bringst 5 Jahre Erfahrungen mit der Implementierung von SAP ME / SAP DM mit. Maschinendatenerfassung: Du kennst Standards wie OPC-UA und hast erste Erfahrungen mit SAP PCo oder dem Production Connector. Moderation: Du kannst Workshops leiten, Präsentationen in PowerPoint erstellen und Kalkulationen in Excel durchführen. Projektleitung: Du hast bereits (Teil-)Projekte erfolgreich geführt und Stakeholder überzeugt. KI Kompetenz: Du setzt KI-Tools in deinem Fachgebiet souverän ein, bewertest die Ergebnisse kritisch und unterstützt Kunden und adessi dabei, Potenziale von KI zu nutzen. Sprachkenntnisse: Deine sehr guten Deutsch- und guten Englischkenntnisse ermöglichen dir eine sichere Kommunikation. Unser Versprechen: Du wirst dich bei uns wohlfühlen! Kollegial, familiär und auf Augenhöhe – wir leben Austausch, Teamgeist und einen respektvollen Umgang miteinander. Diversität und verschiedene Blickwinkel werden bei uns genauso geschätzt wie du als Mensch. Das und viel mehr steht für unser ganz besonderes Wir-Gefühl. Für das es sogar ein Wort gibt: adessi. Unsere Kultur und das Zusammenarbeiten sind geprägt von gegenseitiger Wertschätzung, Anerkennung und Unterstützung. Das verbindet uns hierarchieübergreifend – auch im Home-Office. So belegen wir zum wiederholten Mal den 1. Platz beim Wettbewerb „Deutschlands Beste Arbeitgeber in der ITK“!. Unser umfangreiches Trainingsangebot und ein transparentes Laufbahnstufenmodell für alle sorgen dafür, dass deine Entwicklung bei uns nicht stillsteht. Denn gemeinsam zu wachsen und Chancengeber zu sein, das ist programmiert in unserer DNA. title: SAP Manager
description: Responsibilities Kforce has a client in Broomfield, CO that is seeking an experienced SAP Manager to lead the strategy, implementation, enhancement, and support of enterprise SAP applications. This role will manage a team of SAP professionals, oversee ERP projects, drive process improvements, and ensure the SAP environment remains secure, scalable, and aligned with business objectives. Key Responsibilities: Lead and develop a team of SAP administrators, architects, consultants, and external partners Define the SAP roadmap in collaboration with business and technology stakeholders Oversee SAP implementations, upgrades, enhancements, and ongoing support Manage system performance, security, compliance, patches, and overall platform stability Improve agile delivery, change management, and support processes Manage vendor relationships, budgets, contracts, and team performance metrics Mentor team members and foster continuous improvement Requirements Bachelor's degree required 8+ years of SAP/ERP implementation and support experience, including leadership of SAP teams and projects 5+ years supporting SAP modules such as FI/CO, MM, SD, PP, and PLM Strong understanding of manufacturing, finance, supply chain, or HR business processes Excellent communication, leadership, organizational, and problem-solving skills Must be authorized to work in the U.S. as required by government contract regulations Preferred Qualifications Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field SAP certifications Hands-on enterprise SAP architecture, implementation, and support experience Experience leading technology transformations, process improvements, or organizational change Startup or high-growth company experience is a plus The pay range is the lowest to highest compensation we reasonably in good faith believe we would pay at posting for this role. We may ultimately pay more or less than this range. Employee pay is based on factors like relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, location, performance, union contract and business needs. This range may be modified in the future. We offer comprehensive benefits including medical/dental/vision insurance, HSA, FSA, 401(k), and life, disability & ADD insurance to eligible employees. Salaried personnel receive paid time off. Hourly employees are not eligible for paid time off unless required by law. Hourly employees on a Service Contract Act project are eligible for paid sick leave. Note: Pay is not considered compensation until it is earned, vested and determinable. The amount and availability of any compensation remains in Kforce's sole discretion unless and until paid and may be modified in its discretion consistent with the law. This job is not eligible for bonuses, incentives or commissions. Kforce is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, protected veteran status, or disability status. By clicking “Apply Today” you agree to receive calls, AI-generated calls, text messages or emails from Kforce and its affiliates, and service providers. Note that if you choose to communicate with Kforce via text messaging the frequency may vary, and message and data rates may apply. Carriers are not liable for delayed or undelivered messages. You will always have the right to cease communicating via text by using key words such as STOP. title: SAP / BTP Development Specialist
description: Nyrstar is an international producer of critical minerals and metals essential for a low carbon future. With a market leading position in zinc and lead, Nyrstar has mining, smelting and other operations located in Europe and Australia and employs 3,000+ people. Its Corporate Office is based in Budel-Dorplein, the Netherlands. The company’s operations are located close to key customers and major transport hubs to facilitate reliable and efficient delivery of raw materials and distribution of finished products. Nyrstar’s operating business is wholly owned by Trafigura, one of the world’s leading independent commodity trading and supply chain logistics companies. Join our team as a SAP / BTP Development Specialist , where you’ll design smart SAP and BTP solutions that strengthen our custom applications and power key business processes across Nyrstar. In This Role You Will Develop, maintain, and enhance Nyrstar’s key SAP and BTP applications, including Metal Accounting, Exposure Management, and F&A Pricing. Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues to ensure system reliability and continuous operations. Modernize and optimize custom code in line with SAP best practices and Nyrstar’s digital roadmap. Build strong technical and functional expertise across core business processes and act as an internal subject matter expert. Translate business requirements into scalable, high-quality technical solutions and guide offshore development partners. Lead and contribute to system upgrades, integrations, and SAP BTP / S4HANA transformation projects. Create and maintain technical documentation, sharing knowledge to strengthen internal capabilities and continuity. Bachelors / masters degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering or relevant field. SAP ABAP certified. SAP BTP, BW certified preferrable. 3-5+ years of experience in SAP ABAP development. Prior exposure to custom SAP ABAP development in finance, logistics and/or commercial processes. Experience with the SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) components (integration, process automation, UX and Mobile Apps, GenAI) is a plus. Experience supporting mission?critical systems and processes in a manufacturing or commodity-driven environment is a plus. Knowledge of SAP integration technologies (OData, APIs, IDocs, RFCs). Strong foundation in SAP technologies, ideally with exposure to finance-, commercial, or production-related modules. Experience with SAPUI5/Fiori is an advantage. Proficiency in English is essential, Dutch, French or German is a big plus. Applicants must be based in the Netherlands to apply. At Nyrstar, We Recognize That The Strength Of Our Organization Lies In The Enthusiasm And Dedication Of Our Employees. Our Team Members Are The Driving Force Behind Our Success, And We Are Committed To Providing An Environment Where They Can Thrive Both Professionally And Personally. Here's What You Can Expect When You Become Part Of The Nyrstar Family A Supportive Foundation for Excellence: We equip our employees with the resources and tools necessary to excel in their roles, ensuring that every member of our team feels confident and prepared to perform their duties to the best of their abilities. Meaningful Work That Matters: Our employees find their work not only useful but also meaningful. We foster a sense of purpose that resonates deeply, as every individual understands their contributions and feels truly valued. At Nyrstar, you are more than just an employee; you are a vital part of a mission that impacts the world. Pride in Our Collective Identity: As a member of our team, you'll be proud to talk about Nyrstar with others. Our reputation as a leader in producing critical materials essential for everyday life is something every employee can take pride in. You'll know that you're part of a legacy of excellence and innovation. Clear Direction and Autonomy: Employees at Nyrstar have a clear understanding of their roles and the tasks at hand. We promote autonomy and provide a clear roadmap, so you know exactly what's expected and have the freedom to approach your work creatively. Satisfaction and Engagement: We take pride in the high level of satisfaction our employees express regarding their work content and our organizational culture. At Nyrstar, you'll enjoy a workplace environment that is both dynamic and supportive. Involvement in Shaping the Future: Your voice matters. We encourage our employees to be involved in decision-making within their work scope, ensuring that everyone has the opportunity to contribute to our collective success. Why Nyrstar? International Exposure: Embrace a diverse office culture with colleagues from 28 different nationalities, offering a rich exchange of perspectives and experiences. A Role with Impact: Be part of an organization at the forefront of producing materials that are crucial for modern living, where your contributions make a significant difference. Competitive Compensation: Enjoy a competitive salary and benefits package that recognizes your hard work and dedication to our mission. Flexibility for Work-Life Balance: We understand the importance of balancing professional and personal life, which is why we offer flexible working arrangements to accommodate the unique needs of our employees. Happiness that’s built to last: Recognized by WorkL as one of the happiest workplaces in the Netherlands — measured across six key pillars: well-being, job satisfaction, reward and recognition, information provision, empowerment and pride. Join Nyrstar and become part of a community where passion meets purpose. We're not just offering a job; we're offering a career that makes a difference. Are you ready to be part of something bigger? title: SAP INDUSTRIAL PROCESS DEVELOPMENT SENIOR SPECIALIST
PROCUREMENT LEAD Interior & Exterior
description: INEOS Automotive - Built For More Story so far Since we started our journey to build a no nonsense 4X4, we've come a long way. We've established INEOS Automotive as a serious contender in 4X4 development and manufacturing. We've partnered with some of the biggest names in the automotive industry. We've made our way from vision to prototype. We've secured a world-class production facility and delivery team. We've delivered Grenadiers to customers all around the world. We're building our double cab pick up, the Quartermaster. People and Culture Easy never changed anything. Not in manufacturing, science, engineering or energy - and certainly not elite sport. At INEOS we tackle every challenge head-on, and we help extraordinary athletes achieve extraordinary things. When it comes to making positive change, we know nothing gets done without grit, rigor and humor. Our Automotive team consist of around 1500 employees, 10 locations and 44 nationalities, we're an international team working together to challenge the ordinary. If this sounds like you, let's talk. Your role: To own a Procurement Category in Interior & Exterior end-to-end, including leadership and development of the procurement strategy, using your experience to finally ensure the right parts are in the right place at the right time, allowing purchasing to achieve the best possible commercial terms and conditions in line with the business group strategy, and to own the supplier relationship for the suppliers related to those categories. Responsibilities include (but are not limited to): Coordinate all procurement activities related to the selected category Interior & Exterior Identify and source the components and systems with the best technical and commercial fit to the vehicle and operations requirements Prepare, co-ordinate and execute sourcing decisions for selected commodities and full category - all sourcing decisions are cross functional aligned between engineering, manufacturing, quality, supply chain and procurement Negotiate with suppliers to secure best balanced commercial terms considering technical feasibility, volume demands, quality and manufacturing requirements Fulfil all relevant project targets regarding material and tooling cost as well as timeline Constant observation of the global supply market regarding changes that affects our business Develop and align in a cross-functional manner category strategies considering global supply market developments as well as internal requirements Identify, evaluate and countermeasure risks associated with the supply chain of the selected category Manage and co-ordinate raw material fluctuations with the suppliers and commodities across the whole category Establish and secure reliable supply chains during serial production Support the Procurement Manager in developing and delivering the transition strategy for procurement from project to operation Help drive and deliver all procurement activities of the project phase e.g. through close cooperation with the cost engineers Work closely with the Engineering, Commercial and Industrialisation to agree future demand plans, materials and equipment Network with the wider INEOS organisation to maximise group leverage possibilities Requirements Ideally 8-10 years' experience gained at either a Tier 1/2 automotive supplier or automotive OEM Previous direct knowledge of automotive procurement from concept-through-industrialisation-to-operation, with focus on Interior & Exterior Significant automotive supply chain / procurement know-how gained over a long career in automobile manufacturing Excellent project management skills, with a track record of delivery Able to demonstrate experiences in building and maintaining senior supplier relationships Demonstrable strategic procurement experience and or NPI (New Product Introduction) Ability to prioritise tasks and have effective time management and analytical skills Excellent communication and interpersonal skills There will be significant travel involved in the role Good capability with Microsoft Office software Experience with SAP is desirable, training will be available English language is mandatory German language would be beneficial Benefits Responsible tasks Attractive compensation 30 days of vacation per year Flexible working hours Company pension plan Corporate benefits Excellent transportation links by car or train, plus a company subsidy for public transportation passes Opportunities for remote work If the role sounds of interest and you want to learn more, please apply. Contact:: Benedikt Schmidt, Head of Direct Procurement We welcome all applications, regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic background, or sexual identity. If your application is successful one of our team members will be in touch to arrange an initial conversation! title: Auxiliar de Logística
description: Faça história na democratização do comércio e dos serviços financeiros, transformando a vida de milhões de pessoas na América Latina com o Mercado Livre! Garanta que cada vendedor e comprador do Mercado Livre receba e entregue seus produtos o mais rápido possível, impactando diretamente na experiência de milhões de usuários. A posição é presencial em Cajamar - São Paulo. Os turnos variam conforme a operação e são detalhados durante o processo seletivo. Benefícios: Auxílio-creche Vale-alimentação Fretado Plano médico Plano odontológico Alimentação no local Desafios: Recebimento e organização estratégica de mercadoria: descarregar os produtos que chegam e organizá-los de forma inteligente no armazém. Montagem de pedidos: localizar os produtos que nossos usuários compraram na plataforma e prepará-los para envio com agilidade. Controle de qualidade: verificar que cada caixa e produto estejam impecáveis para o envio. Experiência e conhecimentos: Ter 18 anos ou mais, ensino médio completo e documentação pessoal completa e em dia. Ter disposição para o trabalho em equipe, atitude positiva e curiosidade genuína para realizar cada tarefa da melhor forma. Não é necessária experiência prévia. Esta posição é uma oportunidade de primeiro emprego. Saiba mais sobre nossa cultura e nosso negócio no nosso site . O melhor está chegando! title: Arquiteto de dados | SAP
description: CLT Local: Campinas SP Modalidade: presencial Sobre Nós Sonhar grande está no nosso DNA. É quem somos como empresa. É a nossa cultura. É a nossa herança. E, mais do que nunca, é o nosso futuro. Um futuro onde estamos sempre olhando para frente. Sempre oferecendo novas maneiras de vivenciar os momentos da vida. Um futuro onde continuamos a sonhar cada vez mais alto. Buscamos pessoas com paixão, talento e curiosidade, e oferecemos a elas colegas de equipe, recursos e oportunidades para que liberem todo o seu potencial. O poder que criamos juntos – quando unimos suas forças às nossas – é imparável. Você está pronto para se juntar a uma equipe que sonha tão grande quanto você? Seu papel Você será a principal referência técnica para decisões de plataforma, discussões de arquitetura, novos casos de uso, estabilidade operacional, governança e integração entre ecossistemas SAP e não-SAP. A função exige estreita colaboração com equipes globais, equipes de produto, arquitetos, engenheiros, stakeholders de análise de dados e liderança, apoiando tanto a evolução estratégica quanto a tomada de decisões técnicas do dia a dia. Principais Responsabilidades • Atuar como o principal ponto focal técnico para a Plataforma de Integração Aurora, definindo padrões de arquitetura, direção técnica e melhores práticas de integração. • Liderar soluções de integração ponta a ponta em ambientes SAP e não SAP, abrangendo cenários de entrada, saída e SAP para SAP. • Impulsionar a evolução técnica da pilha de integração Aurora, especialmente a transição do PI/PO para o SAP Integration Suite, alinhada ao roadmap da Aurora e à estratégia da SAP. • Definir e governar padrões de integração baseados em API usando o Azure APIM e o SAP Integration Suite, incluindo segurança, ciclo de vida, monitoramento e exposição controlada de serviços. • Apoiar a estabilidade da plataforma e a excelência operacional por meio de solução de problemas, monitoramento, tratamento de erros, estratégias de reprocessamento e suporte técnico de nível 3/4. • Colaborar com arquitetos, equipes de produto, desenvolvedores e stakeholders de negócios para traduzir requisitos de integração em soluções técnicas escaláveis e governadas. O que esperamos •Formação acadêmica concluída em nível de Bacharel ou Técnologo. • Pensamento crítico e visão estratégica de negócios. • Excelente domínio da comunicação entre áreas e pares. • Forte experiência em arquitetura de integração, integração de sistemas corporativos e integração de negócios em ambientes SAP e não SAP complexos. • Sólida experiência prática com SAP Integration Suite/CPI e conhecimento de SAP PI/PO, incluindo cenários de migração, coexistência ou modernização para o Integration Suite. • Experiência prática com Azure API Management (APIM), incluindo gateway de API, políticas, produtos, chaves de assinatura, segurança e monitoramento. • Conhecimento de SAP BTP e seus serviços relacionados à integração, como Integration Suite, Cloud Foundry, Cloud ALM e outros serviços do BTP. • Sólido conhecimento de padrões de integração REST, SOAP, IDoc, RFC e OData. • Experiência em integração do SAP S/4HANA com sistemas externos, microsserviços e ambientes empresariais híbridos. • Forte capacidade de resolução de problemas de integração entre sistemas, incluindo conectividade, autenticação, erros de tempo de execução e falhas de mensagens. • Inglês avançado para liderar reuniões, discussões técnicas e comunicação com stakeholders em um ambiente global. (mandatório) • Conhecimento de integração orientada a eventos e arquitetura assíncrona. • Experiência com governança de API, gerenciamento de ciclo de vida, limitação de requisições, versionamento e exposição segura de serviços corporativos. • Disponibilidade para trabalhar presencialmente no escritório da AB-InBev em Campinas. Conhecimentos desejáveis • Senso de liderança. • Experiência com recursos adicionais de integração do SAP BTP, como API Management, Event Mesh, Open Connectors ou serviços relacionados. • Experiência em programas de transformação global e programas de ERP em diferentes regiões. • Conhecimento de cenários de integração que permitem soluções de IA, agentes ou copilotos por meio de APIs governadas e serviços do SAP BTP. • Espanhol é um diferencial. Pacote de benefícios 40 dias de home office por ano; Vale refeição ou alimentação ifood; Vale transporte; Bônus; Plano de saúde e Telemedicina; Convênio odontológico; Convênio Farmácia; Seguro de vida; Instituto Ambev de Previdência Privada; Auxílio Material Escolar; Cesta de Natal e Kit Congelado; Brinquedos de Natal; Empresa cidadã (licença maternidade e paternidade) e presente do bebê; Desconto mensal em produtos AMBEV; Wellhub (Gympass); Fretado; Abono anual referente a um salário. title: Expression of Interest - SAP Consultants
description: At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence. We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go. Join EY and help to build a better working world. Expression of Interest – SAP Technology Consultant The opportunity We are seeking Expressions of Interest from experienced SAP professionals to support current and future client engagements across a range of industries. These opportunities sit within a consulting environment and span technical, functional, and leadership roles, supporting some of the most complex business and technology transformations underway today. This Expression of Interest is suited to candidates operating at Senior Consultant, Manager, Senior Manager, or Director level, with capability across one or more SAP domains and a passion for delivering value-led, client-focused outcomes. As an SAP Technology Consultant, you will work as part of multidisciplinary consulting teams advising clients through the case for change, design, implementation, and value optimisation of SAP solutions. Engagements may range from large-scale, end-to-end SAP transformations complex, multi-stakeholder environments to targeted discreet consulting pieces of work. This is a hybrid position based in Naarm – Melbourne / Canberra - Ngambri / Warrang – Sydney. Our roles can potentially be adjusted to work flexibly with reduced hours. Please speak with us about potential options. Your Key Responsibilities Depending on your level and experience, responsibilities may include: Contributing to or leading end-to-end SAP implementations, upgrades, and transformation programs Advising clients on SAP-enabled business transformation and technology strategy Translating complex business requirements into scalable SAP functional and technical solutions Designing, configuring, and optimising SAP solutions in line with industry best practice Partnering with senior client stakeholders to shape solution direction and drive adoption Providing thought leadership on SAP S/4HANA, cloud, data, analytics, and integration Supporting testing, cutover, deployment, and hypercare activities Identifying opportunities for continuous improvement, automation, and innovation Coaching and mentoring team members to build capability and future leaders At Manager level and above, supporting engagement management, commercial outcomes, and business development activities Skills And Attributes For Success We welcome interest from consultants with experience across one or more of the following areas: SAP S/4HANA (greenfield, brownfield, bluefield) implementations SAP solution and Integration architecture ERP Consulting strategy and value realization Finance and Performance Management (FI/CO) Supply Chain and Operations (MM, SD, PP, EWM, TM) Enterprise Asset Management (EAM / PM) Human Capital Management and SuccessFactors SAP Ariba, Concur, and broader SAP cloud platforms Technical development including ABAP, RAP, and SAP BTP Integration and middleware (SAP Integration Suite, CPI, PI/PO) Data, analytics, and reporting (BW/4HANA, SAC) SAP security, roles, authorisations, and governance Ideally, you’ll also have the skills and attributes below but don’t worry if you don’t tick all the boxes. We’re interested in your aptitude, attitude and willingness to learn. You will bring strong consulting experience and a proven track record of delivering SAP solutions within complex client environments. Depending on your level, you will demonstrate depth in your SAP domain, the ability to navigate ambiguity, and the confidence to advise senior stakeholders. Demonstrated consulting experience, including time spent in Big 4 or comparable professional services organisations Experience delivering complex, large-scale business and technology transformation programs Strong understanding of SAP best practices and end-to-end business processes Ability to engage, influence, and communicate effectively with senior business and technology stakeholders Experience working across multiple phases of the SAP delivery lifecycle A collaborative, outcomes-focused mindset with a commitment to quality delivery For senior roles, experience leading teams, managing client relationships, and contributing to sales and proposal activities A passion for coaching, mentoring, and developing high-performing consulting teams What We Offer You At EY, we’ll fuel you and your extraordinary talents in a diverse and inclusive culture of globally connected teams. We’re proud to be recognised as the #1 WORK180 Endorsed Employer in the Top 101 Employers for Women 2026. Learn more. Career development: At EY, your career is yours to shape! We’ll develop you with future-focused skills and equip you with world-class experiences ey.com/au/careerdevelopment Flexible work arrangements: Our flexible work policies empower you to balance your professional and personal life, fostering a culture of trust and autonomy. A comprehensive benefits package: From a yearly wellness incentive, to access to additional 8 weeks of flex leave per year, and family-friendly policies, including up to 26 weeks of gender-neutral paid parental leave, we cater to your diverse needs to help you thrive both personally and professionally www.ey.com/au/benefits Acknowledgement of Country EY acknowledges the Traditional Owners and Custodians of the lands on which EY offices are located around Australia. We pay our respects to their cultures, and to their Elders — past, present, and emerging. Find out more about our vision for reconciliation at ey.com/en_au/careers/indigenous Inclusiveness is core to who we are and how we work together, driving value for our people and for our business. We encourage applications from people of all ages, nationalities, abilities, cultures, sexual orientations, and gender identities and are committed to providing an equitable and barrier free recruitment experience for all. We encourage you to share any support and adjustments you need to be your best and participate equitably in our recruitment process. We understand sharing your needs with us can be daunting, so if you have questions before or during your application, we welcome you to get in touch at contactrecruitment@au.ey.com or +61 3 8650 7788 (option 2). Anything you tell us will be kept completely confidential. Are you ready to shape your future with confidence? Apply today. EY | Building a better working world Our preferred applicant will be required to undertake employment screening by EY or our external third-party provider. © 2026 Ernst & Young Australia. A member firm of Ernst & Young Global Limited. All Rights Reserved. title: SAP Manufacturing Lead
description: Innovation Starts With You Propel your career at Belden, where innovation creates possibilities—for our people, our customers, and the communities we serve. We connect people, information, and ideas to solve the world’s most complex connectivity challenges, turning curiosity into meaningful impact. Here, you’ll take on work that challenges you, supports your growth, and empowers you to shape what’s next. You’ll collaborate with global teams, gain diverse perspectives, and contribute to solutions that extend beyond our business—creating value in the world around us. Together, we are shaping the future of digitization and paving the way for the next generation of innovation. Job Summary Belden is seeking a highly skilled and motivated SAP Manufacturing Lead to support S/4 HANA solutions. Ideal candidate is responsible for supporting, maintaining, and enhancing Belden's SAP S/4HANA manufacturing domain. As part of the Application Management Services (AMS) organization, this role leads production support, continuous improvement, and business engagement across Manufacturing functions, including Production Planning (PP), Quality Management (QM), and Plant Maintenance (PM). Responsibilities Production Support & Service Delivery Own AMS support activities for SAP Manufacturing, including PP, QM, and EAM processes. Lead incident resolution, problem management, and root cause analysis for production and shop floor issues. Monitor system performance, interfaces, background jobs, and integrations to ensure operational stability. Drive SLA compliance and support governance across internal teams, offshore resources, and external partners. Ensure continuity of manufacturing operations by prioritizing and resolving business-critical issues. System Configuration & Integration Configure and support SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing processes, including: Production Planning (MPS, MRP, capacity planning) Production execution and shop floor control Production orders, process orders, and confirmations Configure and support manufacturing master data, including Bills of Material (BOM) Routings, Work centers, Production versions. Configure and support Quality Management (QM), including inspection planning, quality notifications, and compliance processes. Support Plant Maintenance (PM), including maintenance orders, preventive maintenance, and asset tracking. Ensure integration between manufacturing and related SAP modules, including MM, EWM, SD, TM, and Finance. Monitor and troubleshoot interfaces, IDocs, and middleware integrations impacting shop floor execution and production processes. Collaborate with external systems such as MES, shop floor automation, IoT platforms, and data collection systems. Contribute to and guide solution design decisions, ensuring alignment with SAP standards and global manufacturing templates. Maintain configuration, integration documentation, and process flows for AMS and knowledge management. Application Management & Enhancements Analyze business requirements and deliver enhancements aligned with SAP S/4HANA best practices. Own prioritization of the Manufacturing support backlog and coordinate activities across onshore and offshore teams. Support system releases, upgrades, testing cycles (unit, integration, UAT), and change management processes. Ensure consistent integration and data flow across manufacturing and supply chain processes. Business Partnership & Continuous Improvement Partner with plant operations, manufacturing leadership, and supply chain teams to improve operational efficiency and drive adoption of SAP solutions. Act as the primary IT point of contact for manufacturing stakeholders across global sites. Identify opportunities for standardization, automation, and optimization of manufacturing processes. Translate business needs into functional and technical solutions. Provide functional leadership, user guidance, and training to business users. Lead continuous improvement initiatives and promote SAP standard capabilities. Stay current with SAP S/4HANA manufacturing trends and recommend strategic improvements. Required Skills & Experience Bachelor’s degree or MBA in Computer Science, Information Systems, Supply Chain, or related field. 8+ years of SAP experience with strong expertise in Manufacturing domains. Deep functional expertise in: Production Planning (PP) Quality Management (QM) EAM/Plant Maintenance (PM) Strong understanding of manufacturing execution, shop floor control, and production processes. Strong knowledge of manufacturing master data (BOMs, routings, work centers, production versions). Experience with SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing configuration and integration with MM, EWM, SD, TM, and Finance. Experience supporting production environments within an AMS or support organization. Experience with integrations (IDocs, middleware, MES/shop floor systems). Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and problem-solving skills. Experience managing incidents, enhancements, releases, and change control processes. Proven ability to lead cross-functional initiatives and coordinate global teams. Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and leadership skills. Preferred Qualifications SAP certifications in PP, QM, or PM. Experience with MES integration, shop floor automation and EAM. Familiarity with SAP EWM and supply chain integration. Experience working with global system integrators and distributed teams. Knowledge of ITIL or service management frameworks. Applicants can expect a base compensation range of $101,400 - $152,100 plus benefits and additional incentives based on the level of the role. This is the reasonable estimate that Belden believes it might pay for this job based on applicable circumstances at the time of posting. Belden may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range as permitted by law, and commensurate with the applicant’s experience, qualifications, and geographical location. Belden also offers hybrid and remote work practices where feasible and provides employees with benefits that could include health/dental/vision, long term/short term disability, life insurance, HSA/FSA, matching retirement plans, paid vacation, parental leave, employee stock purchase plan, paid leave for volunteer work in your community, training opportunities, professional talent management and succession planning, corporate health well-being initiatives and a work culture which includes commitment to diversity, equity, inclusion and sustainability! Let’s Create Possibilities Together. Join a global community shaping the future of intelligent connectivity. At Belden, you’ll help push the boundaries of technology and write the next chapter of innovation, creating possibilities for your career, your future, and the world around you. These statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work involved for this job. It is not an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties and skills required of this job. title: Expert SAP BC (F/H)
description: Lieu : Castres, France Construisons ensemble un avenir de confiance Thales est un leader mondial des hautes technologies spécialisé dans trois secteurs d’activité : Défense & Sécurité, Aéronautique & Spatial, et Cyber & Digital. Il développe des produits et solutions qui contribuent à un monde plus sûr, plus respectueux de l’environnement et plus inclusif. Le Groupe investit près de 4,5 milliards d’euros par an en Recherche & Développement, notamment dans des domaines clés de l’innovation tels que l’IA, la cybersécurité, le quantique, les technologies du cloud et la 6G. Thales compte près de 85 000 collaborateurs dans 65 pays. Nos engagements, vos avantages Une réussite portée par notre excellence technologique, votre expérience et notre ambition partagée Un package de rémunération attractif Un développement des compétences en continu : parcours de formation, académies et communautés internes Un environnement inclusif, bienveillant et respectant l’équilibre des collaborateurs Un engagement sociétal et environnemental reconnu Votre quotidien Nos équipes de l’activité Systèmes d’information critiques et cybersécurité fournissent des services et des solutions globales optimisant la performance, la résilience et la sécurité des systèmes d’information afin de faire face aux ruptures technologiques et aux cybermenaces. Au sein de la Squad ERP de 20 personnes, vous intervenez principalement dans le cadre d’un projet autour des applications SAP. En tant qu'expert(e) SAP, vous aurez pour missions de : Maintenir et optimiser les applications SAP existantes et futures Piloter le maintien en condition opérationnelles et en condition de sécurité des systèmes SAP Assister et conseiller les équipes métiers et clientes autour des aspects techniques de SAP Effectuer les architectures SAP et les déployer suivant les recommandations de l’éditeur Automatiser les processus de déploiement, de configuration et de gestion de l’application SAP Prendre part aux migrations vers des solutions CLOUD Votre profil Vous êtes diplômé(e) d'un Master ou d'une école d'Ingénieur et justifiez d'une expérience significative sur un poste similaire. Vous avez de fortes connaissances de SAP Basics Components. Vous possédez des compétences en base de données HANA. Vous êtes familier(ère) des systèmes Linux et Windows. Vous connaissez les outils DEVOPS (Ansible-DEVOPS). Vous êtes autonome, innovant(e) et orienté(e) solutions ? Alors ce poste est fait pour vous ! Thales, entreprise Handi-Engagée, reconnait tous les talents. La diversité est notre meilleur atout. Postulez et rejoignez nous ! Le poste pouvant nécessiter d'accéder à des informations relevant du secret de la défense nationale, la personne retenue fera l'objet d'une procédure d’habilitation, conformément aux dispositions des articles R.2311-1 et suivants du Code de la défense et de l’IGI 1300 SGDSN/PSE du 09 août 2021. title: Associate Staff Accountant - Accounts Payable
description: Application and Interview Impersonation Notice : Impersonating another individual when applying for employment, and/or participating in an interview process to assist another individual in obtaining employment, with Precisely Software Incorporated (“Precisely”) is unlawful. If Precisely identifies such fraudulent conduct, then as applicable and to the extent permitted by law, the application will be rejected, an offer (if made) will be rescinded, or the employment will be terminated, and legal action may be taken against the impersonators. Precisely is the leader in data integrity. We empower businesses to make more confident decisions based on trusted data through a unique combination of software, data enrichment products and strategic services. What does this mean to you? For starters, it means joining a company focused on delivering outstanding innovation and support that helps customers increase revenue, lower costs and reduce risk. In fact, Precisely powers better decisions for more than 12,000 global organizations, including 95 of the Fortune 100. Precisely's 2500 employees are unified by four company core values that are central to who we are and how we operate: Openness, Determination, Individuality, and Collaboration. We are committed to career development for our employees and offer opportunities for growth, learning and building community. With a "work from anywhere" culture, we celebrate diversity in a distributed environment with a presence in 30 countries as well as 20 offices in over 5 continents. Learn more about why it's an exciting time to join Precisely! Precisely is an AI-first organization. All employees are expected to demonstrate proficiency in applying AI tools to accelerate their work, improve output quality, and eliminate low-value tasks. Candidates should be comfortable using generative AI tools (e.g., Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT) in their day-to-day workflows, able to evaluate AI-generated outputs critically, and open to continuously adopting new AI capabilities as they emerge. Overview: This role will perform activities to ensure accurate and timely processing of invoices and making payments for Global Precisely. This role has daily interaction with other departments across the company therefore it requires advanced verbal and communication skills. What you will do: Manage end-to-end Accounts Payable operations for global entities, ensuring accuracy and compliance with company policies. Assist with tax payments, customer refunds, and ad hoc requests related to audits, legal, and compliance requirements. Process AP invoices after general checks complying with Accounts Payable policies and procedures. Coordinate purchase order approvals and monitor workflow to maintain timely processing. Execute weekly payment runs and handle urgent payment requests as needed. Respond to vendor inquiries promptly and resolve discrepancies to maintain strong supplier relationships. Review and reconcile aged payable reports, addressing overdue items and ensuring timely resolution. Support month-end close activities, including accruals, reconciliations, and reporting. Perform corporate card reconciliations and ensure proper documentation for expense compliance. Other duties may be assigned. What we are looking for: Required: Minimum a Commerce graduate or above. Atleast 3 years of experience in Accounts Payable. Sound knowledge of Accounts Payable processes and accounting principles. Exposure to Coupa, NetSuite is required. Able to handle internal and external users queries independently. AI Skills/Knowledge: Proficient in using AI tools such as Microsoft Copilot to draft communications, summarize information, and improve productivity.. Ability to leverage AI for data analysis, reporting, and process automation. Preferred Skills (a plus but not required): Advance knowledge of MS Office (Excel). Analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to identify process efficiencies. Experience working in a shared services or global finance environment. This role requires the incumbent to work in a hybrid model from our Chandigarh office. The personal data that you provide as a part of this job application will be handled in accordance with relevant laws. For more information about how Precisely handles the personal data of job applicants, please see the Precisely Candidate Privacy Notice

title: SAP Analyst
description: Our client, a water technology provider, is seeking an SAP Source-to-Pay (STP) Functional Analyst for a 5 month contract opportunity located in Charlotte, NC. This role is hybrid, onsite 3 days per week. Position Summary The SAP Source-to-Pay (STP) Functional Analyst is responsible for supporting, enhancing, and optimizing SAP procurement solutions across the Source-to-Pay lifecycle. This role partners closely with Procurement, Supply Chain, Finance, IT, and business stakeholders to analyze business requirements, design functional solutions, configure SAP applications, and support system enhancements. The analyst serves as the functional expert for procurement processes including sourcing, purchasing, supplier management, inventory integration, invoice processing, and related SAP modules, including integration with Coupa. Key Responsibilities Analyze business requirements and translate them into SAP functional solutions. Configure and maintain SAP procurement functionality supporting Source-to-Pay business processes. Collaborate with Procurement, Supply Chain, Finance, and business users to improve operational efficiency. Support end-to-end procurement processes including: Requisition Management Purchase Orders Goods Receipts Invoice Verification Vendor Management Contract Management Sourcing Events Support and maintain SAP integration with Coupa for procurement and Source-to-Pay processes. Perform business process analysis and recommend improvements aligned with SAP best practices. Create functional specifications for enhancements, reports, interfaces, conversions, forms, and workflows. Work closely with SAP technical developers and integration teams to implement custom solutions. Participate in SAP implementation, upgrade, migration, and enhancement projects. Conduct system testing including: Functional Unit Testing (FUT) System Integration Testing (SIT) User Acceptance Testing (UAT) Support production issues by troubleshooting functional problems and coordinating resolution across SAP and Coupa. Develop training materials and provide end-user support. Ensure system changes follow change management and governance processes. Maintain functional documentation, configuration documentation, and business process procedures. Support integration with other SAP modules including Finance, Warehouse Management, Sales, Manufacturing, and external procurement platforms. Experience integrating SAP with Coupa or other procurement platforms. Experience gathering and documenting business requirements. Experience with SAP testing methodologies. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. Preferred Qualifications SAP S/4HANA implementation experience. Experience implementing or supporting Coupa Procure-to-Pay. Experience with SAP Fiori applications. Knowledge of SAP Business Workflow. Experience integrating SAP with third-party procurement applications. SAP certification in SAP MM or SAP S/4HANA Procurement preferred. title: Functional Lead
description: Job Summary Serve as a functional lead for complex SAP utilities solutions combining deep expertise in waste management customer care services service contracts work management energy data management device management billing and generative AI. Enable hybrid delivery for global utilities and oil and gas clients ensuring stable day shift operations and impactful business transformation. Responsibilities Lead end to end functional design across SAP waste management customer care services service contracts work management energy data management device management and billing to deliver integrated utilities processes that run reliably and efficiently for clients in a hybrid work environment. Drive requirement discovery sessions with business stakeholders to translate complex utilities and oil and gas needs into detailed SAP functional specifications that are feasible scalable and aligned with industry regulations and sustainability goals. Coordinate with technical teams to ensure that configured SAP solutions and enhancements follow best practices meet performance expectations and support secure handling of high volume customer and metering data. Guide the configuration of SAP customer care services and billing to optimize customer journeys reduce billing errors improve call center efficiency and support new digital self service capabilities. Oversee the functional setup of SAP work management and device management to streamline field operations improve asset visibility reduce service disruptions and support safe and compliant maintenance activities. Shape the functional design of SAP energy data management to support advanced metering time of use rates load profiling and analytics that help clients manage energy demand and integrate renewable generation. Apply generative AI capabilities in collaboration with data and AI teams to automate documentation accelerate impact analysis improve knowledge search and enhance user assistance for SAP utilities users. Validate end to end business process flows through system integration testing and user acceptance testing ensuring that defect triage and resolution are handled promptly and with clear root cause documentation. Prepare detailed functional documentation covering configuration decisions process flows test scenarios and user guides so that support teams and client stakeholders can operate the solution confidently over the long term. Collaborate with project managers to estimate functional effort manage risks track progress and support on time delivery within agreed scope and quality for global utilities and oil and gas clients. Mentor junior functional consultants by reviewing deliverables sharing industry insights and encouraging structured problem solving that improves the overall capability of the project team. Engage with client business process owners on a regular cadence to gather feedback propose continuous improvements and demonstrate how SAP innovations and generative AI can deliver measurable business value. Ensure that all delivered processes comply with relevant data privacy cyber security and audit requirements while supporting the company purpose of enabling safe reliable and sustainable energy and resource management for society. Qualifications Possess eight to twelve years of progressive functional experience in SAP utilities or related industries with substantial exposure to cross functional processes in large or multinational organizations. Demonstrate strong hands on configuration experience in SAP waste management customer care services service contracts work management energy data management device management and billing modules for complex business scenarios. Exhibit practical understanding of utilities and oil and gas domain processes such as metering service connections field operations contract management billing cycles and revenue assurance in a regulated environment. Show proven ability to collaborate with technical teams on integration enhancements and data migration topics while articulating functional requirements clearly and comprehensively. Display familiarity with generative AI concepts and their application in enterprise environments such as document generation impact analysis assistance knowledge retrieval and conversational support for end users. Demonstrate strong communication and stakeholder management skills with the ability to facilitate workshops explain complex topics in simple terms and build trust with diverse business and technology partners. Show readiness to work in a hybrid model during day shifts while collaborating effectively with distributed teams across locations and time zones using modern collaboration tools and structured ways of working. Certifications Required Preferred certifications include SAP Certified Associate Utilities SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management and a recognized generative AI or AI practitioner certification. title: Junior Executive - Procurement
description: Junior Executive - Procurement Gradiant India Private Limited Chennai, India 7/8/26 Procurement Gradiant is a Different Kind of Water Company. With a full suite of differentiated and proprietary end-to-end solutions for advanced water and wastewater treatment powered by the top minds in water, the company serves its clients’ mission-critical operations in the world’s essential industries, including semiconductors, pharmaceuticals, food & beverage, lithium and critical minerals, and renewable energy. Gradiant’s innovative solutions reduce water used and wastewater discharged, reclaim valuable resources, and renew wastewater into freshwater. The Boston-headquartered company was founded at MIT and has over 1,400 employees worldwide. Discover us at www.gradiant.com. Job Overview Gradiant is looking for a proactive, detail-oriented and motivated Procurement Administrator to support the day-to-day procurement operations of our global business. This role is ideal for someone with a positive attitude, strong organizational skills, and a willingness to learn. Prior experience in procurement or exposure to SAP/ERP systems is advantageous but not essential, as training will be provided to the right candidate. Responsibilities Procure-to-Pay (P2P): Review purchase requisitions, convert them into official purchase orders (PO), and handle requests for quotations (RFQ). Master Data Management: Update and maintain accurate Vendor Master Data and Material Master Data (e.g., pricing, storage, units). Issue Resolution: Troubleshoot discrepancies in delivery or billing and communicate with vendors and internal stakeholders to resolve delays. Compliance and Standards: Ensure procurement processes comply with company policies and regulatory standards. Records Maintenance: Maintain accurate procurement records, delivery documentation, and reports for audits and analysis. Others: Perform all other duties and tasks as assigned by supervisor/manager/management. Requirements Diploma in Supply Chain Management, Procurement, Business Administration, or a related discipline; equivalent qualifications will also be considered. Fresh graduates are encouraged to apply. Candidates with 1–3 years of experience in procurement, purchasing, supply chain, or a related field are welcome. Positive attitude with a strong willingness to learn and develop professionally. Excellent attention to detail and strong organizational skills. Good communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across teams. Ability to work independently while contributing as a team player. Proficient in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel, Outlook, and Word. Strong multitasking skills with the ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced environment. Highly dependable, proactive, and demonstrates a strong sense of ownership and accountability. Able to prioritize tasks effectively and consistently meet deadlines. Comfortable working in a dynamic and rapidly changing business environment. Enthusiastic about continuous learning, process improvement, and professional growth. Preferred Qualifications (Advantageous But Not Mandatory) Exposure to SAP or other ERP systems. Prior experience in procurement, purchasing, supply chain, or related operational functions. Gradiant is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, religion, creed, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, national origin, age, veteran status, disability, or any other protected class. title: SAP Signavio ( Business Process ) Lead
description: We are seeking a highly skilled SAP Signavio (Business Process) Lead to support the administration, optimization, and continuous improvement of business processes across the organization. The role will be responsible for SAP Signavio platform administration, process mining, process insights, process architecture, maturity assessments, and development of process management solutions leveraging Microsoft technologies. Key Responsibilities Signavio Administration Manage and administer the SAP Signavio platform. Ensure platform availability, performance, and governance. Support user access management, system configurations, and platform enhancements. Collaborate with SAP Basis and technical teams to maintain operational excellence. Process Insights Administration Perform SAP Note implementations and service pack upgrades. Monitor RFCs, background jobs, and system integrations. Troubleshoot Process Performance Indicators (PPI) configurations and related issues. Coordinate with SAP Basis teams to resolve technical and infrastructure challenges. Maintain Process Insights configurations and reporting frameworks. Process Mining & Data Engineering Conduct process discovery, process analysis, and conformance checking activities. Identify bottlenecks, inefficiencies, and root causes within business processes. Define, monitor, and track process KPIs and improvement opportunities. Design and manage event log data models for process mining initiatives. Develop SQL queries to support data extraction and analysis. Build and maintain ETL pipelines and data integration processes. Ensure data quality, consistency, and integrity across multiple source systems. Develop and maintain SAP Signavio Process Intelligence dashboards and reports. Write and optimize SIGNAL queries for advanced process analytics. Process Architecture & Maturity Assessment Solutions Design and deliver business applications using Microsoft Power Apps. Develop and support enterprise web applications leveraging ASP.NET MVC, Web API, C#, and MS SQL Server. Build scalable and secure authentication solutions using OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, Azure AD (Entra ID), OWIN, and cookie-based authentication mechanisms. Integrate applications with Microsoft Graph APIs and MSAL for identity and access management. Support LDAP, directory services, and identity management solutions. Develop responsive user interfaces using AJAX, DOM manipulation, and modern web technologies. Automate reporting and data processing using Excel automation frameworks such as ClosedXML, OpenXML, and Microsoft Interop. Implement secure secrets and credentials management practices using solutions such as AWS Secrets Manager. Required Qualifications Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or related field. Strong experience with SAP Signavio administration and process management solutions. Hands-on experience with Process Mining, Process Intelligence, and Process Insights. Proficiency in SQL, ETL development, and data engineering concepts. Experience developing solutions using Microsoft Power Platform. Strong programming skills in C#, ASP.NET MVC, Web API, and MS SQL Server. Experience with authentication and identity technologies including Azure AD/Entra ID, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, SAML SSO, and Microsoft Graph APIs. Knowledge of cloud security, secrets management, and enterprise-grade application development. Preferred Qualifications SAP Signavio certifications preferred Experience with SAP landscapes and SAP Basis coordination. Exposure to enterprise process transformation initiatives. Experience working in global and cross-functional teams. Strong analytical, problem-solving, and stakeholder management skills. Skills SAP Signavio Administration Process Insights Administration Process Mining & Process Intelligence SQL & Data Engineering ETL Development Microsoft Power Apps ASP.NET MVC & Web API C# MS SQL Server OAuth 2.0 & OpenID Connect Azure AD / Entra ID Microsoft Graph API LDAP & Identity Management AJAX & Responsive UI Development Excel Automation (ClosedXML, OpenXML, Interop) AWS Secrets Manager Business Process Optimization Process Architecture & Maturity Assessment Show more Show less Seniority level Mid-Senior level Employment type Full-time Job function Information Technology Industries Computer Hardware Manufacturing, Software Development, and IT Services and IT Consulting Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at HP by 2x See who you know
