title: Associate Document Controller
description: What’s The Role Technical Asset Support (TAS) team aims to develop central, competitive capabilities, delivering end-to-end value through the AMS work processes as an integral part of our Downstream Manufacturing (DS-M), Integrated Gas & New Energies (IGNE) and Upstream (UP) asset teams. We support the business worldwide and often do so together with the deep capability of our Project & Technology (P&T), Information Technology (IT) and Finance & Data Operations (FO-Data) partners. You are going to play a vital role in the delivery of continued efficiency and effectiveness improvement by bringing cross-business learning on key processes. The Data Document Management Engineer is expected to perform document management of technical data, procedures, and engineering drawings through functional mailbox management, metadata management, and high-level quality checks. The role will be responsible in the maintenance, assurance, technical support, and standardization of Shell’s Upstream, IG and Downstream assets technical data. The role will steer the Document Management of an asset’s technical drawings, vendor/OEM correspondences, procedures, and other engineering drawings. What You’ll Be Doing Work in CMMS (SAP) and EDMS tools such as either AIM-EDMS / ASSAI / ACCONEX / UDOCs (Doc Management tool) for Maintenance, assurance, technical support and standardization of technical data and documents of the manufacturing sites, chemical plants, supply chain and distribution locations. With support roles on site engagement & technical & tools forums. Analyze data and business processes which includes data cleansing, data mining and profiling, benchmarking, reporting projects for site related equipment and maintenance related activities. Monitors functional mailbox for Document Turnovers on daily basis and prioritizes the completion of these document turnovers based on agreed criteria and in line with asset standards. Understands data related maintenance reports, equipment drawings, circuit diagrams, P&IDs, operation, and maintenance manuals of OEMs for Downstream (Refineries and Chemical plants) operations. Understanding the functions of the various equipment’s /instruments and knowledge of reading/ interpreting the engineering drawings (P&IDs, PEFS, PFDs) is an advantage. Opens each drawing or document and performs quality check based on the Asset’s Document Management standards - returns document turnovers to the originator that don’t meet baseline quality standards. Sends required notifications and closes document turnover per instruction when complete. Loads documents to EDMS, adds metadata and links to FLOC per instruction. Looks at opportunities to streamline processes in the future. Shift work following operating hours of the Downstream operation units across the globe. Virtual working with diverse stakeholders and time zones for day to day operations Effective initiative to identify and help resolve business and technical issues within areas of responsibility. A self-starter and reliable deliverer, with good verbal and written skills in English. The role primarily involves the remote support to the downstream operating units/sites in the drawing management & updates. What You Bring University / Technical/Professional / Engineering in Mechanical / Instrumentation / Electrical / Industrial / Chemical Engineering. Preferably 2 to 3+ yrs. post degree in engineering / technical jobs from one or more of the following: Experience handling technical documentation in the Oil and Gas or related industry. Experience working in an ERT or Computerized Maintenance Management Systems (CMMS) such as SAP/Blueprint. Experience working in an Engineering Document Management Systems (EDMS.) such as AIM-EDMS / ASSAI / AHA. Experience in applying document management standards (such as Document Numbering schemes), metadata management, and working in an established document management workflow. Preferable someone with strong communication and can easily grasp information. Demonstrates collaboration and contributes positively to team dynamics. Ability to prioritize and ensure delivery of priorities for the area of responsibility. Flexible and adaptable to change, with track record of demonstrating initiative, analytical capabilities and problem solving. Technical Documentation in the following: Project Drawings – IFR/IFC’s, Level Sketches, P&IDs, PEFs, etc., Vendor/OEM Manual, Equipment Diagrams. Experience in AIM-EDMS, ASSAI, and Primavera. Experience in Engineering Projects as part of project controls team. Experience in Reporting and Analysis and use of tools such as MS Access, Advanced Excel, Spotfire, Power BI, and other similar tools. Experience in Engineering Continuous Improvement practices, Failure Mode and Effects Analysis, Fault Tree Analysis, and Total Quality Management. What We Offer You bring your skills and experience to Shell and in return you work with talented, committed people on one of the most important challenges facing our planet. You’ll have the opportunity to develop the skills you need to grow in an environment where we value honesty, integrity, and respect for one another. You’ll be able to balance your priorities as you become the best version of yourself. Progress as a person as we work on the energy transition together. Continuously grow the transferable skills you need to get ahead. Work at the forefront of technology, trends, and practices. Collaborate with experienced colleagues with unique expertise. Achieve your balance in a values-led culture that encourages you to be the best version of yourself. Join an organisation working to become one of the most diverse and inclusive in the world. We strongly encourage applicants of all genders, ages, ethnicities, cultures, abilities, sexual orientation, and life experiences to apply. Grow as you progress through diverse career opportunities in national and international teams. Gain access to a wide range of training and development programmes. title: NEOM- Ingénieur Méthodes F/H
description: VINCI Construction est l'un des principaux acteurs mondiaux de la construction, dans le domaine des infrastructures de transport, des bâtiments, des réseaux et des aménagements urbains. VINCI Construction s'appuie sur un réseau d'entreprises de proximité, des réseaux de spécialité et une expertise spécifique sur les grands projets d'infrastructures. Présentes dans plus de 100 pays, ses 1 300 entreprises emploient plus de 119 000 collaborateurs et ont réalisé un chiffre d'affaires de 31,5 milliards d'euros, NEOM, spécialisée dans les travaux de déconstruction, exerce son savoir-faire dans les domaines du désamiantage, du déplombage et du curage. Fort de l’expérience de ses 300 collaborateurs formés et expérimentés, elle intervient sur des projets variés : bureaux, commerces, gares, bâtiments industriels, établissements scolaires… NEOM s’inscrit au sein de la Délégation des Ouvrages Fonctionnels Réhabilités et des Métiers de Spécialités. Avec un chiffre d’affaires de 834 M€ et plus de 1850 collaborateurs, la délégation est aujourd’hui leader de la réhabilitation. Elle Est Accompagnée Par Des Marques Reconnues PETIT, DUMEZ IDF, CBC Service, DUMEZ Service, Dumez concept, PLENDI, DEGAINE, FACAL. Les équipes allient la proximité d’entités à taille humaine à la puissance et aux expertises du groupe VINCI Construction. Notre service Méthodes est constitué d’une équipe de 7 personnes. Notre principale mission est d’accompagner les études de prix dans la conception de la réponse à l’appel d’offre et les équipes travaux dans la préparation et le suivi de l’opération. Notre principale force est le lien que nous assurons entre les études de prix, les chantiers et la Prévention, permettant des retours d’expérience bénéfiques à tous. Votre quotidien en tant que Chargée. Méthodes En mode gestion de projet, vous apportez votre aide, vos conseils à nos équipes sur le terrain ainsi qu’au bureau d’études de prix et vivez ainsi un projet de la faisabilité jusqu’à la réception du chantier. Vos Missions Seront Les Suivantes Définir la conception et l'organisation des méthodes pour assurer la bonne réalisation de l’opération, notamment par la réalisation des fiches Safe et des modes opératoires avec les équipes travaux et la prévention, Suivre l'avancement des travaux en veillant au respect de la qualité et de la sécurité des chantiers, Trouver et proposer des solutions innovantes et d'optimisations, tant en phase étude que travaux. Le poste sera basé à Chevilly-Larue jusqu’à fin juin 2026, avant un déménagement vers Vélizy prévu début juillet 2026. title: Accounting Operations- 6 Month Contract (100% Remote US)
description: About The Role Hopper is hiring an Accounting Operations (Contractor) to own our monthly partner billing cycle end-to-end and provide broader finance operations support across a 6-month engagement, with potential to extend. This is a hands-on, execution-focused role sitting at the intersection of partner relationships, financial operations, and data integrity. If you're someone who thrives on process ownership and running a tight billing cycle without being managed step-by-step, this role is built for you. What Would Your Day-to-day Look Like Run the full monthly partner billing cycle — gathering partner-specific inputs, generating reports using existing tooling, and coordinating internal review and approval through to close Own partner-facing communication, including reviewing and sending billing emails, and responding to partner inquiries directly Triage and investigate data discrepancies, resolving what you can independently and escalating pipeline issues with clear documentation Create, issue, and track partner invoices in NetSuite, match POs to invoices, and confirm remittances are received and applied Complete vendor and customer setup for new partner onboarding, including collecting required documentation, tax forms, and banking details Support month-end close through account reconciliations and journal entry preparation Maintain process documentation and assist with external audit requests as needed Take on ad hoc accounting and finance operations projects as they come up An ideal candidate has 3+ years in billing, accounts receivable, or finance operations, with a track record of running recurring cycles independently Hands-on NetSuite AR and billing experience Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage a deadline-driven monthly cycle without day-to-day oversight Clear, professional written communication — you'll be a direct point of contact for external partners A methodical reconciliation mindset, comfortable chasing down discrepancies across multiple data sources SQL or BigQuery familiarity to investigate data questions before escalating (nice to have) Experience at a high-growth technology, marketplace, or travel company (nice to have) Familiarity with revenue share, commission, or variable partner billing structures (nice to have) Perks And Benefits Of Working With Us Well-funded and proven startup with large ambitions, competitive salary and the upsides of pre-IPO equity packages. Unlimited PTO. Entrepreneurial culture where pushing limits and taking risks is everyday business. Open communication with management and company leadership. Small, dynamic teams = massive impact. Contractors will submit invoices and be paid on a monthly basis through Coupa. Please be aware that because this is an independent contractor engagement, the position is not eligible for standard HTS employee perks, healthcare benefits, or equity. More About Hopper At Hopper, we are on a mission to become the leading travel platform globally – powering Hopper’s mobile app, website and our B2B business, HTS (Hopper Technology Solutions). By leveraging massive amounts of data and advanced machine learning algorithms, Hopper combines its world-class travel agency offering with proprietary fintech products to bring transparency, flexibility and savings to travelers globally. We have developed several unique fintech solutions that address everything from pricing volatility to trip disruptions – helping people travel better and save more on their trips. The Hopper platform serves hundreds of millions of travelers globally and continues to capture market share around the world. The Hopper app has been downloaded over 120 million times and has become largely popular among younger travelers – with 70% of its users being Gen Z and millennials. While everyone knows us as the Gen Z and Millennial travel app, Hopper has evolved to become much more than that. In recent years, we’ve grown into a travel fintech provider, commerce platform, and global travel agency that powers some of the world’s largest brands. Through HTS, our B2B division, the company supercharges its partners’ direct channels by integrating our fintech products on their sites or powering end-to-end travel portals. Today, our partners include leading travel brands like Capital One, Nubank, Air Canada, and many more. Here are just a few stats that demonstrate the company’s recent growth: Billions of dollars worth of travel and travel fintech are sold through Hopper and HTS’ channels every year. Our fintech products – including Cancel for Any Reason and Flight Disruption Assistance – have exceptionally strong CSAT because the terms are always clear, and customers receive instant, no-questions-asked resolutions. Almost 30% of our app customers purchase at least one fintech product when making a booking; and consumers are 1.6x more likely to repurchase if they add fintech to their booking vs if they booked just travel. Given the success of its fintech products, Hopper launched a B2B initiative, HTS (Hopper Technology Solutions), which represents more than 75% of the business. Through HTS, any travel provider (airlines, hotels, banks, travel agencies, etc.) can integrate and seamlessly distribute Hopper’s fintech or travel inventory on their direct channels. As its first HTS partnership, the company partnered with Capital One to co-develop Capital One Travel, a new travel portal designed specifically for cardholders. Other HTS partners include Air Canada, Uber, CommBank, Nubank, Flair Airlines and many more. Hopper has been named the #1 most innovative company in travel by Fast Company. We have raised over $750 million USD of private capital and is backed by some of the largest institutional investors and banks in the world. HTS is primed to continue its growth as the leading travel ecommerce provider in a $1 trillion online shopping category. The Hopper app and website will also continue to be the preferred travel provider for Gen Z and Millennials. Come take off with us! At Hopper, we leverage AI-driven tools to assist our team in efficiently reviewing applications. These tools help us assess qualifications fairly and objectively. By submitting your application, you acknowledge the use of these automated screening technologies. title: Sr. SAP Architect - Product
description: About Protera Welcome to Protera Technologies, where we're reimagining how SAP-centric organizations work in the cloud. Since 1998, we've been pioneers in bringing SAP and related applications to the cloud—think Microsoft Azure and AWS. Our global crew, spanning the US with offices in Chicago (HQ), Athens, Greece, Barcelona, Spain and Mumbai, India, is on a mission to make IT smoother, faster, and more fun for our clients. But here's the thing: we're not your typical "serious tech company." While we're obsessed with delivering top-notch IT solutions, we're all about keeping it real, approachable, and enjoyable. We work hard, but we also play hard—whether we're collaborating on cloud optimizations, enhancing security, or just hanging out as a team. At Protera, we believe in empowering SAP-centric organizations with the best tools and tech to drive growth, and we love what we do. Our values? Simple: stay curious, stay authentic, and make meaningful connections. We celebrate wins big and small, whether it's a successful project or a fun team event. If you're looking for a place where tech and fun collide, come join us and see what makes Protera a great place to work. What You'll Do The Senior SAP Architect will lead the end-to-end architectural design, process, and technical strategy for enterprise-grade SAP products built on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) . This role will drive blueprinting, modernization, and scalable integration of Multi-Target Applications (MTAs) using the Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model and other cloud business solutions. Critical to This Role: ERP & Business Process Expertise This is not purely a technical architecture role. The Architect must bring strong ERP experience and business process expertise , with the ability to: Lead conversations around core enterprise processes (Finance, Order-to-Cash, Procure-to-Pay, etc.) Translate functional requirements into scalable, well-architected technical solutions Bridge business and engineering teams with confidence and clarity We are specifically looking for either: A functional SAP professional with deep ERP process knowledge who is passionate about custom applications and architecture OR A technical SAP professional who wants to expand into functional design and business architecture The ideal candidate can discuss business process challenges and immediately formulate the corresponding technical solution architecture. Architectural Accountability You will design highly available, secure, and performant platform components across: TypeScript-based NodeJS CAP service architectures Enterprise CDS domain modeling HANA and PostgreSQL data architecture Identity & Access architecture (XSUAA, IAS/IPS, OAuth2, SAML, JWT) SAP Destination & Connectivity framework design React-based UI architecture You will also guide strategy across: SAPUI5 modernization approaches Cloud-native architecture principles (eventing, microservices, containerization) End-to-end DevOps architecture ECC → S/4HANA transition strategy You will serve as the technical and process authority across engineering, product, SAP ABAP teams, and business stakeholders. Requirements What You Bring You will act as the primary decision-maker for solution design, ERP alignment, and architectural governance. Core Requirements Strong ERP experience with deep business process expertise Proven ability to lead business process discussions and convert them into technical solutions Experience operating in one of the following profiles: Functional-first SAP professional expanding into architecture Technical-first SAP professional moving into functional/business architecture Demonstrated ability to design end-to-end ERP-aligned extension architectures on SAP BTP Technical Requirements Expert-level NodeJS architecture experience Expert-level TypeScript proficiency Deep experience with SAP BTP platform architecture Mastery of MTA and CAP frameworks Strong cross-platform integration expertise Advanced experience with React in SAP BTP ecosystems Extensive SAP ecosystem knowledge (S/4, ECC, OData, RFC, IDoc) Optional Qualifications Experience with SAP FI/CO Experience with SAP SD ABAP extensibility and integration pattern experience SAPUI5/UI5 enterprise architecture experience Experience in global/distributed SAP landscapes Why Join Protera? Work with global enterprise clients Learn fast with mentorship, certifications, and career growth Make a real impact in a company where ideas are welcome Flexible work setup (remote/hybrid) People-first culture with a global mindset Ready to Make the Move? We'd love to meet you. Click "Apply Now" and tell us why you're the one. Not sure yet? Check us out at www.protera.com or connect with our team on LinkedIn. Protera is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. title: Engineering Support Specialist II
description: Job Description You will play a critical role in optimizing engineering workflows and driving efficiency through the effective use of SAP, directly contributing to the success of our NPI engineering team and the overall Honeywell Aerospace Technologies business supporting the delivery of new product introduction hardware to our Customers. Role In this role, you will directly contribute towards the success of the engineering design and manufacturing operations by providing critical technical support that includes, but is not limited to: Production demand management Parts inventory management Work order management Production parts tracking through proper SAP updates (from incoming to factory floor to shipping) Documenting product completion status through all phases of manufacturing You are also expected to collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify and implement process improvements, ensuring the effective use of SAP in engineering operations. Key Responsibilities Provide technical support and expertise to the engineering team in the use of SAP systems and processes Manage and provide status on engineering production parts inventory on factory floor Manage and provide status on product shipping and receiving related to NPI engineering needs Troubleshoot and resolve SAP-related issues, ensuring the smooth operation of engineering workflows Optimize engineering processes and workflows through the effective use of SAP Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify and implement process improvements Train and support engineering team members in the use of SAP Qualifications YOU MUST HAVE Associate’s degree from an accredited institution in technical discipline 2 years of technical and troubleshooting skills within a manufacturing environment Communication and collaboration skills WE VALUE Knowledge of engineering processes and workflows Experience working with SAP systems and processes Experience in engineering support roles SAP certification or training Continuous improvement mindset About Honeywell Aerospace Honeywell Aerospace (Nasdaq: HONA) invents and commercializes technologies that address some of the world’s most critical demands around energy, safety, security, air travel, productivity, and global urbanization. We are a leading software-industrial company dedicated to introducing state-of-the-art technology solutions to improve efficiency, productivity, sustainability, and safety in high-growth businesses in broad-based, attractive industrial end markets. Our products and solutions enable a safer, more comfortable, and more productive world, enhancing the quality of life of people around the globe. Honeywell Aerospace is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will be considered without regard to age, race, creed, color, national origin, ancestry, marital status, care or sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, nationality, sex, religion, or veteran status. Benefits Of Working For Honeywell Aerospace In addition to a performance-driven salary, cutting-edge work, and developing solutions side-by-side with dedicated experts in their fields, Honeywell Aerospace employees are eligible for a comprehensive benefits package. This package includes employer-subsidized Medical, Dental, Vision, and Life Insurance; Short-Term and Long-Term Disability; 401(k) match, Flexible Spending Accounts, Health Savings Accounts, EAP, and Educational Assistance; Parental Leave, Paid Time Off (for vacation, personal business, sick time, and parental leave), and Paid Holidays. For more information visit: Benefits At a Glance | Honeywell Aerospace Benefits The application period for the job is estimated to be 40 days from the job posting date; however, this may be shortened or extended depending on business needs and the availability of qualified candidates. Job Posting Date: July 14, 2026 U.S. CITIZENSHIP REQUIREMENT Must be a US Citizen due to contractual requirements. title: Procurement Specialist
description: Foods you love. Brands you trust. And a career that empowers you to grow. At Nestlé USA, we’re all working towards the same goal – to delight and deliver for our consumers. With a rich portfolio of beloved brands, including DiGiorno, Toll House, and Coffee mate, in 97% of U.S. households, we have a unique opportunity – and responsibility – to be there for every moment in our consumers’ lives. Joining Nestlé means becoming part of an inclusive workplace that inspires innovation, encourages strategic thinking and creativity, and celebrates your achievements. No matter where you work within the organization, you are empowered to challenge the status quo, embrace risk-taking, and pioneer new ideas. Our supportive and collaborative environment encourages bold ambitions and continuous learning so that everyone can grow and thrive. This position is not eligible for Visa Sponsorship. POSITION SUMMARY: The Factory Buyer role is a critical purchasing network position, with functional responsibility for all indirect services and materials to include maintenance spare parts, reporting in to the Financial Controller. The Factory Buyer’s prime focus is the area of indirect materials and services, with a key focus on negotiating contracts and agreements, working with Corporate Purchasing and the ION Network on new initiatives, implementing and maintaining compliance strategies within the purchasing area, creating cost savings projects and initiatives within services and indirect materials, and maintaining supplier relationships. JOB DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES: Oversee the coordination of purchases of all supplies, equipment, and services used by all departments to ensure lowest cost is obtained. Review purchase requisitions for accuracy and completeness, designate approved supplier (with support from corporate purchasing), assign correct account information and assign requisitions to staff for processing. Facilitate team and functional meetings to drive decision making, information sharing and consensus building in support of an optimized S&IM spend. Ensure inventory accuracy through monitoring and reporting effective cycle counts performed by Engineering. Ensure corrective actions are taken if accuracy falls below acceptable threshold. Copilot w Engineering to monitor inventory stock levels and turns. Ensure factory is compliant with all Nestle Purchasing Policies and perform periodic audits of processes. Negotiate contracts and agreements for factory for localized S&IM purchases. Engage the plant with key, strategic suppliers to identify cost savings initiatives and projects. Develop and maintain good relationships with all S&IM suppliers. Work closely with Corporate Purchasing supporting National initiatives. Support all departments with any special projects. EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS: Bachelor’s degree preferred; degrees in business area such as Finance or other analytical field a plus. EXPERIENCE & SKILLS REQUIREMENTS: Minimum 2 years finance, manufacturing, purchasing, or supply chain experience required. Basic accounting Negotiation skills Strong Proactive Cooperation skills high performance team player Results Oriented Excellent attention to detail Excellent analytical and computer skills (SAP & excel) Ability to visit Gemba to work with teams, audit inventories The approximate pay range for this position is $65,382 to $88,323 base salary. Please note that the pay range provided is a good faith estimate for the position at the time of posting. Final compensation may vary based on factors including but not limited to knowledge, skills and abilities as well as geographic location. Nestlé offers performance-based incentives and a competitive total rewards package, which includes a 401k with company match, healthcare coverage and a broad range of other benefits. Incentives and/or benefits packages may vary depending on the position. Learn more at: About Us | Nestlé Careers It is our business imperative to remain a very inclusive workplace. To our veterans and separated service members, you're at the forefront of our minds as we recruit top talent to join Nestlé. The skills you've gained while serving our country, such as flexibility, agility, and leadership, are much like the skills that will make you successful in this role. In addition, with our commitment to an inclusive work environment, we recognize the exceptional engagement and innovation displayed by individuals with disabilities. Nestlé seeks such skilled and qualified individuals to share our mission where you’ll join a cohort of others who have chosen to call Nestlé home. The Nestlé Companies are equal employment opportunity employers. All applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or veteran status or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. Prior to the next step in the recruiting process, we welcome you to inform us confidentially if you may require any special accommodations in order to participate fully in our recruitment experience. Contact us at accommodations@nestle.com or please dial 711 and provide this number to the operator: 1-800-321-6467. This position is not eligible for Visa Sponsorship. Review our applicant privacy notice before applying at https://www.nestlejobs.com/privacy. title: Sr Manager - SAP AMS
description: Visteon is advancing mobility through innovative technology solutions that enable a software-defined future. Our state-of-the-art product portfolio merges digital cockpit innovations, advanced displays, AI-enhanced software solutions, and integrated EV architecture solutions. With expertise spanning passenger vehicles, commercial transportation, and two-wheelers, Visteon partners with global automakers to create safer, cleaner, and more connected journeys. Headquartered in Van Buren Township, Michigan, Visteon operates in 17 countries, employing a global network of innovation centers and manufacturing facilities. Key Accountabilities / How You Will Spend Your Days Operational Support Leadership Own end-to-end SAP AMS operations across all modules (FI/CO, OTC, PTP, EWM, PP/DS, GRC, Master Data, CPI/EDI, Reporting). Manage daily service delivery, ensuring incidents, service requests, and small enhancements are handled within SLA. Monitor system performance, integration flows, batch jobs, and error queues; ensure quick recovery and proactive prevention. Oversee L1, L2, and L3 support teams, including partner resources and internal SME involvement. Governance & Vendor Management Establish strong governance with AMS partner(s), ensuring quality, accountability, and measurable outcomes. Conduct weekly/monthly service review meetings, KPIs reporting, workload tracking, and performance escalations. Align ticket routing, prioritization, SLA adherence, capacity planning, and knowledge transfer processes. Continuous Improvement & Stabilization Identify recurring issues and lead systemic fixes, not just symptom resolution. Drive small enhancements and optimization backlog aligned with business priorities and IT leadership direction. Improve process documentation, runbooks, knowledge articles (KBA), and standard operating procedures. Cross-Functional Engagement Partner with CoE leaders, Site Leaders, IT Directors, and central process owners to ensure business continuity and improvement. Support change impact analysis, regression testing, authorization updates, and deployment of small enhancements. Coordinate with project teams for rollouts, upgrades, and new functionality transition into AMS. Security, Compliance & Audit Readiness Ensure AMS activities comply with ICFR, ITGC, audit findings, and segregation of duties requirements. Maintain proper documentation, transport controls, access reviews, and evidence for audit cycles. Team & Capability Development Build, mentor, and grow the internal AMS capability for long-term sustainability. Drive knowledge transfer from implementation partners to VISTEON IT team. Develop skill matrices, role clarity, onboarding guides, and competency development plans. Key Performance Indicators Operational Performance KPIs SLA adherence for incident and service request resolution. Average ticket resolution time and backlog levels. System uptime, job runtime success, and interface reliability. Reduction in repeat incidents and functional defects. Quality & Stability KPIs Hypercare performance score for each go-live or rollout transition. Number of recurring issues eliminated permanently. Regression defects avoided during changes and minor releases. Financial & Vendor KPIs Cost per ticket and optimization of AMS spend. Partner performance score (SLA, quality, skill depth, governance adherence). Budget adherence for AMS operations and enhancements. Stakeholder Satisfaction KPIs Satisfaction rating from GBPOs, Site Leaders, and business super-users. Internal IT satisfaction score on partner performance. User experience metrics for system stability and responsiveness. Capability Building KPIs Number of KBAs, SOPs, and runbooks created or updated. Internal team capability uplift scores. Reduction in dependency on external partners over time. title: SAP BTP-Manager
description: At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence. We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go. Join EY and help to build a better working world. Service line: SAP - Position Title: BTP Developer – Manager. At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence. We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go. Join EY and help to build a better working world. About Global Delivery Services Global Delivery Services refers to EY's worldwide network of service delivery centers. The GDS team plays an important role in EY’s strategy by ensuring effective support to EY’s growth agenda. Our journey started in 2002 with approximately 200 people. Today we stand at 80,000+ professionals in ten locations around the world. We operate in Argentina, China, Hungary, India, Philippines, Poland, Sri Lanka, Mexico, Spain and the United Kingdom. Client service is focused on providing Consulting, Assurance, Tax, Strategy & Transactions, and Knowledge support to our clients around the world. The teams enable account teams worldwide to provide seamless, high-quality, value-added support, helping deliver exceptional client service. Enablement Services provides cost-effective, high-skilled, and innovative services to support EY’s global and local enablement teams. Markets, BMC, AWS, Finance and Accounting, Risk Management, Procurement, People Shared Services, IT Service Delivery and IT Global Infrastructure services, are among the services offered by Enablement Services. Our innovation specialists serve the GDS Client Service and Enablement Services teams, along with Service Lines, Core Business Services and Sectors. The team brings the desired environment, technologies and skilled teams together for facilitation, rapid prototyping and innovative thinking. The competencies offered include analytics, digital, user experience, mobile technology, infrastructure, Microsoft technologies and open innovation. The Opportunity We are seeking an experienced SAP Developer with 7 to 11 years of hands-on expertise in SAP technologies, particularly SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), Core Data Services (CDS), ABAP, and SAPUI5/Fiori. In this role, you will be responsible for building, enhancing, and maintaining SAP applications leveraging these technologies. Your Key Responsibilities Lead decision-making processes related to SAP application development and optimization. Manage and optimize resources effectively to ensure timely and quality delivery of tasks. Oversee team management including personnel allocation, task assignment, and performance evaluation. Ensure the team is equipped with the necessary tools and support to achieve organizational objectives. Drive process improvements and foster a collaborative team environment. Skills And Attributes For Success Proficient in ABAP, Object-Oriented ABAP, and BAdI implementations. Experience with SAP Gateway and HANA Cloud Connector. Strong knowledge of data provisioning and testing OData services. Familiarity with SAP Workflow. Understanding of SAP Basis and Authorization concepts. Ideal Candidate Profile Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (BE/BTech), MCA, or equivalent with 7-11 years of relevant industry experience. Hands-on experience in SAPUI5 and Fiori elements-based app development and front-end extensions. Skilled in developing Core Data Services (CDS). Expertise in developing and consuming RESTful services using SAP BTP. Experience in extending standard Fiori applications. Basic understanding of SAP BTP and its services. Good to have experience in S/4 key user extensions, developer extensions, and Side-by-Side extensions using SAP BTP. Strong analytical skills, problem-solving capability, and attention to detail. Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage stakeholders at all levels. Proven ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team. SAP certifications in relevant technologies (e.g., SAP Certified Development Associate) are preferred but not mandatory. What We Offer You At EY, we’ll develop you with future-focused skills and equip you with world-class experiences. We’ll empower you in a flexible environment, and fuel you and your extraordinary talents in a diverse and inclusive culture of globally connected teams. Learn more. Are you ready to shape your future with confidence? Apply today. To help create an equitable and inclusive experience during the recruitment process, please inform us as soon as possible about any disability-related adjustments or accommodations you may need. Our Commitment : As a commitment, we persistently endeavour to embody our values, fulfil our purpose, and champion inclusiveness. Our dedication is to cultivate EY into an environment where diverse perspectives are celebrated, creating a supportive atmosphere for individuals to authentically be themselves and contribute their utmost. Professional Development : From entry-level employees to senior leaders, we believe in continuous learning. We offer opportunities to build new skills, take on leadership roles, and connect and grow through mentorship. People and Culture : In our dynamic workplace, diversity, equity, and inclusiveness are ingrained in our culture. We're united by a commitment to create an environment where every individual's differences are valued, practices are equitable, fostering a sense of belonging. Our shared values include integrity, respect, teaming, inclusiveness, energy, enthusiasm, courage to lead, and building relationships based on doing the right thing. Benefits : Embark on a transformative career journey with us and indulge in a suite of premium benefits, encompassing exclusive health and wellness packages, enticing rewards, and cutting-edge learning opportunities that empower you to continually grow and excel in your professional and personal development. How to Apply: If you are passionate to join us and are aligned with our commitment to building a better working world, we invite you to apply by completing our user-friendly form with personal and professional information and by providing your consent to data privacy. Successful candidates advance to a competency-based interview. If mutual interest persists, a job offer awaits. “We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to Diversity, Equity & Inclusion”. EY | Building a better working world EY is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets. Enabled by data, AI and advanced technology, EY teams help clients shape the future with confidence and develop answers for the most pressing issues of today and tomorrow. EY teams work across a full spectrum of services in assurance, consulting, tax, strategy and transactions. Fueled by sector insights, a globally connected, multi-disciplinary network and diverse ecosystem partners, EY teams can provide services in more than 150 countries and territories. title: (Junior) IT Support Specialist - SAP (w/m/d)
description: Als (Junior) IT Support Specialist im Bereich SAP übernimmst du Schritt für Schritt Verantwortung für den stabilen und qualitätsgesicherten Betrieb unserer SAP-Applikationen. Du bearbeitest Incidents und Service Requests im 2nd Level und wächst in die Rolle als fachliche Schnittstelle zwischen Service Desk, SAP-Provider und Fachbereichen hinein. Was du bei uns bewegst Incident Management: Du analysierst und bearbeitest SAP Incidents im produktiven Betrieb und lernst, nachhaltige Fehlerbehebungen zu entwickeln. Dazu gehören Ursachenanalysen bei wiederkehrenden Störungen, Dokumentation und die Steuerung der Übergabe komplexerer Themen an externe Dienstleister Service Request & Application Support: Du bist Ansprechpartner:in für unsere SAP Application Management Services und unterstützt die Zusammenarbeit mit externen Dienstleistern, dem SAP Support sowie internen IT-Einheiten. Du bearbeitest Serviceanfragen, stellst die Qualität und Nachvollziehbarkeit von Tickets sicher und bringst dich in die kontinuierliche Verbesserung unserer Support-Prozesse ein Rollen-, Berechtigungs- & Auditmanagement: Du arbeitest eng mit unseren Business-Teams sowie dem IAM-Team zusammen, um SAP IAS ordnungsgemäß zu integrieren und unsere Sicherheits- und Berechtigungsvorgaben umzusetzen. Zudem unterstützt du bei Systemaudits Release- & Transport-Management: Du unterstützt die Durchführung von Releases und Transporten in Zusammenarbeit mit internen und externen Stakeholdern Dokumentation & Standardisierung: Du sorgst für eine strukturierte Ticket- und Betriebsdokumentation und hilfst mit, Wissensartikel und SOPs weiterzuentwickeln Was du mitbringst Du hast ein abgeschlossenes Studium der (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, eine IT-Ausbildung oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation – oder relevante praktische Erfahrung, z. B. aus einem Quereinstieg Du bringst erste Erfahrung im SAP Application Support mit (z. B. durch Praktika, Werkstudententätigkeit, Ausbildung oder erste Berufsjahre) – ein tiefes SAP-Expertenwissen wird nicht vorausgesetzt Du interessierst dich für SAP-Applikationslogik, Geschäftsprozesse und das Zusammenspiel verschiedener SAP-Komponenten und bist bereit, dich hier weiterzuentwickeln Erste Berührungspunkte mit IT-Service-Management (ITSM)-Prozessen wie Incident-, Service-Request- oder Problem-Management sind von Vorteil, aber kein Muss Du arbeitest analytisch und strukturiert und hast Freude daran, komplexe Sachverhalte systematisch zu durchdringen und verständlich zu dokumentieren Du besitzt ein hohes Qualitäts- und Servicebewusstsein und behältst auch in anspruchsvollen Situationen einen kühlen Kopf Du kommunizierst sicher auf Deutsch und gut auf Englisch (schriftlich wie mündlich) Eigeninitiative und die Bereitschaft, Themen nachzuverfolgen und dich in neue Fragestellungen einzuarbeiten, runden dein Profil ab Die Position richtet sich auch an Berufseinsteigende mit erster SAP-Erfahrung als auch an Quereinsteigende, die sich mit unserer Unterstützung in dieses Umfeld einarbeiten möchten. Wir begleiten dich mit gezieltem Onboarding, Schulungen und einem erfahrenen Team dabei, in die Rolle hineinzuwachsen. Wir freuen uns auf deine Bewerbung! Was wir dir bieten Womit wir dir einen echten Mehrwert bieten: Ein attraktives und leistungsorientiertes Vergütungspaket Ein vielfältiges und spannendes Aufgabenspektrum mit hoher Eigenverantwortung Ein professionelles und kollegiales Arbeitsumfeld mit flachen Hierarchien und kurzen Entscheidungswegen Ein umfangreiches Angebot an Weiterbildungs- und Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten Flexible Arbeitszeiten und Homeoffice-Optionen Zusätzliche Benefits wie betriebliche Altersvorsorge, Gesundheitsförderung, etc. Was für uns selbstverständlich ist und Ausdruck unserer WIR-Kultur: Mobiles und flexibles Arbeiten sorgt für deine optimale Work-Life Integration Regelmäßige Aus- und Weiterbildungen und Förderung individueller Zertifizierungen bringen dich weiter 30 Tage Urlaub, Sonderurlaub und individuelle Sabbatical Regelungen bieten dir Auszeit und Entspannung Die Hardware kannst du auch für private Zwecke nutzen Softgetränke sowie Kaffee und Tee kriegst du bei uns kostenlos Großartige Firmenevents machen unsere Kultur für dich erlebbar Über uns Vielfalt, Innovation und Teamgeist – das ist die TIMETOACT GROUP. Mit mehr als 1.700 Kolleginnen und Kollegen arbeiten wir in 25 Städten in Deutschland, Österreich und der Schweiz sowie in Niederlassungen in Italien, den Niederlanden, Spanien und den USA . Ergänzt wird unser Netzwerk durch Service Hubs in Mittelamerika, Mittel- und Osteuropa sowie Südostasien . 2025 haben wir gemeinsam einen Umsatz von über 460 Millionen Euro erwirtschaftet und wachsen weiter. Als führender IT-Dienstleister gestalten wir für Unternehmen, Konzerne und öffentliche Einrichtungen die digitale Transformation. Wir beraten zu IT-Strategien, beschleunigen die Cloud-Transformation und setzen auf AI, Data, Software- und System-Engineering , um zukunftsfähige Lösungen zu entwickeln. Gemeinsam mit starken Partnern wie AWS, Atlassian, Google, Microsoft, IBM und SAP helfen wir unseren Kunden, das volle Potenzial ihrer Cloud-Plattformen auszuschöpfen. Bei uns arbeitest du gruppenübergreifend in einem starken Netzwerk aus spezialisierten Unternehmen. Unser WIR ist unsere Stärke – und deine Chance, dich einzubringen, weiterzuentwickeln und die digitale Welt aktiv mitzugestalten. * Geschlecht, Herkunft, sexuelle Orientierung, Alter und religiöse Ausrichtung spielen bei uns keine Rolle – wichtig sind uns stattdessen das funktionierende Team, gegenseitiger Respekt, Wertschätzung, sowie Motivation und Spaß an der Arbeit. title: Consultant(e) - SAP - SAP Excellence Center - Toulouse
description: Description De L'entreprise À propos de Sopra Steria Sopra Steria, acteur majeur de la Tech en Europe, avec 51 000 collaborateurs dans près de 30 pays, est reconnu pour ses activités de conseil, de services et solutions numériques. Il aide ses clients à mener leur transformation digitale et à obtenir des bénéfices concrets et durables. Le Groupe apporte une réponse globale aux enjeux de compétitivité des grandes entreprises et organisations, en combinant une connaissance approfondie des secteurs d’activité et des technologies à une approche collaborative. Sopra Steria place l’humain au cœur de son action et s’engage auprès de ses clients à tirer le meilleur parti du numérique pour construire un avenir positif. En 2025, le Groupe a réalisé un chiffre d’affaires de 5,6 milliards d’euros. The world is how we shape it Description Du Poste Sopra Steria a été choisi par un acteur majeur du monde de l'aéronautique, pour prendre en charge la maintenance des applications SAP. Notre périmètre d'intervention démarre au recueil du besoin jusqu'à l'implémentation dans le système SAP. Le poste envisagé consiste à intégrer une équipe Agile distribuée en charge de la maintenance applicatives des processus de Supply chain : Achat (MM), Vente (SD), Logistique (WM) et Finance (FI/CO) et de Manufacturing : Production (PP), Gestion de Projets (PS) et Qualité(QM). Vous intégrez une équipe dynamique, composée d'un chef de projet et d'une vingtaine de consultants expérimentés. Une équipe multifonctionnelle repartie entre la France et l'Inde. Dans le cadre de cette mission, vous êtes intégré(e) à l'équipe en tant que Consultant(e) Technique SAP technique du delivery Sopra Steria. Vos missions sont les suivantes : Vous définissez le design général, en ligne avec l'architecture définie pour le projet ; Vous expliquez le design global à l'équipe ; Vous supportez l'équipe de développeurs dans les phases de design détaillé ; Vous validez les designs détaillés ; Vous supportez l'équipe de développeurs dans les phases de développement et de test unitaires (donc doit maitriser parfaitement ces 2 activités) ; Vous assurez les phases d'entrer en service du produit délivré ; Vous garantissez la qualité des livrables contractuels ; Vous assurez le déploiement des activités vers notre équipe offshore ; La méthodologie Agile est utilisée pour délivrer le projet. #TOULOUSE Qualifications Vous maitrisez SAP ABAP / ABAP OO, SAPUI5. Vous connaissez les outils HP ALM et Jira. Une expérience en conception fonctionnelle SAP sur le domaine Achat, Vente, Qualité, Logistique, dans le secteur aéronautique serait un plus. Vous êtes polyvalent(e), autonome, avez un bon esprit de synthèse. La maîtrise de l'anglais est indispensable. Informations supplémentaires Un accord télétravail pour télétravailler jusqu’à 2 jours par semaine selon vos missions. Un package avantages intéressant : une mutuelle, un CSE, des titres restaurants, un accord d’intéressement, des primes vacances et cooptation. Des opportunités de carrières multiples : plus de 30 familles de métiers, autant de passerelles à imaginer ensemble. Plusieurs centaines de formations accessibles en toute autonomie avec Sopra Steria Academy. L'opportunité de rejoindre le collectif Tech'Me UP (formations, conférences, veille, et bien plus encore…). Employeur inclusif et engagé, notre société œuvre chaque jour pour lutter contre toute forme de discrimination et favoriser un environnement de travail respectueux. title: Accounts Payable Specialist
description: Accounts Payable Specialist (Contract) Location: Charlotte, NC Position Type: Contract (Minimum 6 Months) Schedule: Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM–5:00 PM Work Arrangement: Onsite Position Overview We are seeking an experienced Accounts Payable Specialist to join a busy accounting team on a contract basis. This role will support day-to-day accounts payable operations, processing a high volume of invoices while ensuring accuracy, timely payments, and excellent vendor communication. The ideal candidate has strong AP experience, thrives in a fast-paced environment, and is comfortable handling invoice processing from receipt through payment. Key Responsibilities Process approximately 50 invoices per day with a high degree of accuracy. Review, code, and enter vendor invoices into the accounting system. Perform three-way matching of invoices, purchase orders, and receiving documentation. Process receiving transactions and ensure supporting documentation is complete. Serve as the primary point of contact for vendor inquiries related to invoices and payments. Research and resolve invoice discrepancies and payment issues. Maintain accurate vendor records and supporting documentation. Utilize Microsoft Excel to organize, reconcile, and analyze accounts payable data. Collaborate with internal departments to ensure timely invoice approvals and payment processing. Support additional accounts payable and accounting functions as needed. Required Qualifications 3+ years of dedicated Accounts Payable experience. Experience processing high-volume invoices in a fast-paced environment. Strong understanding of three-way matching. Experience working directly with vendors to resolve invoice and payment issues. Proficiency with Microsoft Excel. Strong attention to detail, organizational skills, and accuracy. Excellent communication and problem-solving abilities. Preferred Qualifications Experience using Acumatica ERP . Manufacturing, distribution, or industrial industry experience is a plus.

title: Consultant-e SAP EWM/WM F/H
description: Safran est un groupe international de haute technologie opérant dans les domaines de l'aéronautique (propulsion, équipements et intérieurs), de l'espace et de la défense. Sa mission : contribuer durablement à un monde plus sûr, où le transport aérien devient toujours plus respectueux de l'environnement, plus confortable et plus accessible. Implanté sur tous les continents, le Groupe emploie 100 000 collaborateurs pour un chiffre d'affaires de 27,3 milliards d'euros en 2024, et occupe, seul ou en partenariat, des positions de premier plan mondial ou européen sur ses marchés. Safran est la 2ème entreprise du secteur aéronautique et défense du classement « World's Best Companies 2024 » du magazine TIME. Safran Aircraft Engines conçoit, produit et commercialise, seul ou en coopération, des moteurs aéronautiques civils et militaires aux meilleurs niveaux de performance, fiabilité et respect de l'environnement. La société est notamment, à travers CFM International*, le leader mondial de la propulsion d'avions commerciaux courts et moyen-courriers. * CFM International est une société commune 50/50 de Safran Aircraft Engines et GE Aerospace. Parce que nous sommes persuadés que chaque talent compte, nous valorisons et encourageons les candidatures de personnes en situation de handicap pour nos opportunités d'emploi. Vous voyez un avion ? C'est simple, le groupe Safran fait tout dans l'avion, sauf l'avion ! Et nous, Safran Aircraft Engines, notre mission c'est de concevoir, produire et commercialiser nos moteurs tout en assurant leur support depuis plus de 110 ans ! La transformation digitale est un tremplin pour atteindre les challenges de la société et la DSI a une ambition : rendre tout cela possible. Composée de plus de 500 collaborateurs la DSI est en pleine transformation pour soutenir sa croissance et relever les défis technologiques de demain. Envie d'embarquer au sein de la Direction du Système d'Information ? C'est par ici ! Au sein de la Direction du Système d'Information de Safran Aircraft Engines composé de 380 collaborateurs, vous intégrerez la division ERP, Supply Chain et Chain. Cette division assure de nombreuses missions : 1.Piloter le programme de remplacement de nos ERP historiques vers une solution SAP sur tous nos sites dans le cadre de notre Transformation Digitale, 2.Assurer le Maintien en Conditions Opérationnelles des solutions ERP & des Satellites : ·Garantir la performance et la disponibilité des systèmes ·Garantir la construction et mise en place des roadmaps SI ; ·Garantir la pérennité dans le temps (roadmap obsolescence) ; ·Mettre en place et assurer le fonctionnement des centres de compétences. 3.Accompagner nos clients dans la réalisation de nouveaux projets au sein de la société. Pendant la réalisation du projet SAP au sein de Safran Aircraft Engines, le candidat contribuera, en binôme avec le Responsable Domaine (Domain Leader), à la conception détaillée et à l'intégration de la solution SAP sur le domaine fonctionnel Logistique. Pour ce faire, il/elle devra : * S'assurer du bon déroulement de la mise en oeuvre de la solution fonctionnelle et technique par l'intégrateur, sur le/les domaines fonctionnels attribués. * Contribue, vis-à-vis de l'intégrateur, à garantir la cohérence et l'exhaustivité de la conception, du domaine fonctionnel attribué, concernant : o La couverture des exigences fonctionnelles, o L'identification des interfaces et évolutions des SI externes o L'identification des données à cataloguer / migrer o Les spécificités liées au déploiement sur les différents sites/organisations * Conseille le Responsable Domaine (Domain Leader), sur les solutions à mettre en place (SAP / Legacy) et challenge l'intégrateur * Valide l'intégration, du domaine fonctionnel attribué, dans la solution * Contribue à la recette/qualification et à la mise en service, du domaine fonctionnel attribué, de la solution * Exerce son devoir d'alerte sur son périmètre et est force de proposition pour anticiper sur les difficultés à venir du programme. Soft Skills : * Capacité à conseiller et guider le métier en s'appuyant sur son expertise, * Capacité à travailler en équipe et en mode projet, * Bonne communication et écoute, * Sens de la relation client, * Assertif(ve) et empathique. Compétences Techniques : * Bonne connaissance de ERP SAP ECC et S/4 HANA * Expérience de conception ou de déploiement de solutions sur ECC 6 ou S/4HANA * Cadrage ou conception générale impliquant la refonte des processus logistique et la gestion des entrepôts * Expertise fonctionnelle et technique autour des processus * Vous avez plusieurs années d'expérience soit dans des cabinets de conseil, soit au sein du centre de compétence SAP d'un client utilisateur final. * Due au contexte international de nos projets, la maitrise de l'anglais est nécessaire. title: Software Technician
description: Job Summary This role is responsible for ensuring accurate, auditable, and reliable data flow and transactions across the data systems related to the Corvallis and Singapore wafer fabs. The primary data systems are Workstream, SAP S4, Holmes, RDMA, and related platforms. This position plays a critical role in maintaining fab financial integrity, production continuity, and audit readiness, and requires deep understanding of manufacturing transaction semantics and cross‑system dependencies. Key Responsibilities Manufacturing Systems Integration & Data Integrity, Operations Support Own end‑to‑end transaction integrity across Workstream, SAP S4, Holmes, and supporting systems. Design, implement, and validate logic for transactions such as scrap/unscrap and inventory adjustments. Identify, analyze, and resolve discrepancies between physical inventory, Workstream records, and S4 financial data, often under time‑critical conditions. Lead and support Holmes–Workstream synchronization testing, including wafer, panel, and device‑level transaction scenarios. Ensure material usage, cost center assignment, and quantity accounting are correctly executed for: Production and engineering materials Indirect materials (e.g., gold wire, adhesives, flex) Non‑standard and exception manufacturing flows Produce and validate calculations required for quarter‑end corrections, audits, and fab financial reviews. Provide direct support to manufacturing, inventory control, engineering, and finance teams. Respond to urgent operational issues where incorrect system behavior could impact production, inventory valuation, or financial reporting. Develop and maintain custom utilities and scripts that enable: Engineering‑to‑production lot conversions Accurate cost and quantity tracking across systems Automated reconciliation and scrap processing Document and explain complex system behavior to stakeholders to reduce operational risk and process errors. Required Qualifications Demonstrated expertise in manufacturing transactions, inventory accounting, and financial impacts of system behavior. Experience working with SAP S4, Workstream, or similar enterprise manufacturing platforms. Ability to analyze complex data flows and resolve issues spanning multiple systems. Preferred Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Information Systems, or equivalent practical experience. Experience supporting fab or high‑mix manufacturing environments. Scripting or development experience (e.g., Perl, Python) used for system integration or utilities. Experience supporting audits, quarter‑end close, or financial reconciliation in manufacturing environments. Responsibilities Leads day-to-day design activities from concept through manufacturing and/or production, and pays detailed attention to design execution from concept through delivery. Creates wireframes, user interface (UI) layouts, mockups, prototypes, visual artifacts, icons, and design specifications that are ready to be implemented by the rest of the team. Leads design thinking sessions in collaboration with designers, program managers, and engineers, and applies design thinking methods to address requirements. Influences and drives changes across disciplines to optimize the usage of design thinking within the portfolio. Drives and manages the development of deliverables through to publishing and presentation against engagement deadlines. Leads conversations with administrators from large organizations to refine designs and incorporate best practices within the organization. Acts as the project management administrator to ensure all required project information and documentation are provided clearly on drawing sets. Successfully tracks record leading initiatives and interdisciplinary teams with a design and strategic vision. Reacts quickly to new information, opinions, and direction to strategically pivot the designs and strategies when necessary. Provides guidance, mentoring, and training to individual contributors in lower-level roles, and contributes to their growth in the organization. The pay range for this position is $73,800 to $100,350 USD per hour (applies to United States of America candidates only). Pay varies by work location, job-related Benefits knowledge, skills, and experience. HP offers a comprehensive benefits package for this position, including: Health insurance Dental insurance Vision insurance Long term/short term disability insurance Employee assistance program Flexible spending account Life insurance Generous time off policies, including; 4-12 weeks fully paid parental leave based on tenure 13 paid holidays 15 days paid time off (US benefits overview [https://hpbenefits.ce.alight.com/]) The compensation and benefits information is accurate as of the date of this posting. The Company reserves the right to modify this information at any time, with or without notice, subject to applicable law. title: SAP Service Owner (m/w/d)
description: Thiemstrasse 111 Berufserfahrung IT Vollzeit Die Medizinische Universität Lausitz – Carl Thiem ist eine junge Universitätsmedizin und der größte Arbeitgeber in Cottbus mit rund 1.200 Betten und knapp 3.000 Mitarbeitenden aus mehr als 40 Ländern. Als bedeutendes Leitkrankenhaus und Universität gestalten wir die Zukunft der Gesundheitsversorgung in der Modellregion Gesundheit Lausitz. Wir bieten umfassende Maximalversorgung mit 22 Kliniken und 4 Instituten für jährlich über 150.000 Patienten/innen sowie ein inspirierendes Umfeld für Forschung und Lehre. Die MUL – CT vereint Forschung, Lehre und Krankenversorgung in einem innovativen Rahmen und bietet vielfältige Karrierechancen, erstklassige Weiterbildungsprogramme und eine hochmoderne Arbeitsumgebung im Herzen von Cottbus. Gestalte die Zukunft der Medizin bei uns! Wir suchen Dich als SAP Service Owner (m/w/d) zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt. Gemeinsam gestalten wir die Zukunft der medizinischen Universität Lausitz durch moderne, effiziente SAP-Lösungen. Wenn Du Freude daran hast, komplexe Prozesse zu steuern und innovative IT-Services zu gestalten, freuen wir uns auf Deine Bewerbung! Dein Verantwortungsbereich Serviceverantwortung: Steuerung und Bereitstellung aller SAP-Services im SAP Competence Center Qualitätssicherung: Koordination und Überwachung der Servicequalität sowie Sicherstellung der Einhaltung von Leistungskennzahlen Zusammenarbeit: Enge Abstimmung mit technischen und fachlichen Applikationsbetreuern zur Gewährleistung einer reibungslosen Systemfunktionalität und kontinuierlichen Weiterentwicklung Implementierung & Weiterentwicklung: Unterstützung bei der Einführung und Optimierung der SAP S/4HANA Applikation (SAP ERP Cloud Private), weiterer SAP Services auf Basis der SAP Technology Platform (BTP) sowie der SAP-Schnittstellen Prozesssteuerung: Mitwirkung bei der Planung und Steuerung der Service- und Supportprozesse im Rahmen des SAP S/4HANA Projekts und des anschließenden Betriebs Dein Profil Ausbildung: Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Wirtschaftsinformatik, Informatik, Betriebswirtschaft oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation Berufserfahrung: Mehrjährige Erfahrung im Bereich SAP-Services, idealerweise im Umfeld von SAP S/4HANA Kommunikationsstärke: Ausgeprägte Fähigkeit im Umgang mit Stakeholdern und in der zielgerichteten Kommunikation Sprache: Deutschkenntnisse mindestens auf Niveau B2 Arbeitsweise: Selbstständige, strukturierte und teamorientierte Arbeitsweise Verantwortungsbereitschaft: Motivation, in einem dynamischen Umfeld Verantwortung zu übernehmen Das bieten wir Dir Ein unbefristetes Arbeitsverhältnis in Vollzeitbeschäftigung Umfassende Entwicklungschancen durch vielfältige Weiterbildungsmaßnahmen und Karriereplanung sowie der kostenfreien Nutzung des Wissenskompetenzzentrums an der MUL – CT Vereinbarkeit von Beruf & Familie durch moderne digitale Arbeitsplätze sowie mobiles Arbeiten, familienfreundliche Arbeitszeitenregelung und flexible Arbeitszeitmodelle sowie Unterstützung bei der Unterbringung in einer betriebsnahen Kindertagesstätte Ein Entgelt nach TV MUL Eine Jahressonderzahlung i.H.v. 80% 30 Tage Urlaub Attraktive Zusatzleistungen, eine krankenhauseigene Cafeteria für Mitarbeitende, Zugang zu Corporate Benefits und zur hauseigenen Apotheke mit attraktiven Rabatten sowie betriebliche Altersvorsorge Ein ausgeprägtes Betriebliches Gesundheitsmanagement mit vielen Angeboten und Maßnahmen zur Betrieblichen Gesundheitsförderung Mitarbeiterveranstaltungen *Alle personenbezogenen Formulierungen in dieser Stellenanzeige sind geschlechtsneutral zu betrachten. Kontakt Wenn Du dir einen zukunftssicheren Beruf wünschst, dir Teamarbeit eine Herzensangelegenheit ist und Du dich persönlich sowie fachlich weiterentwickeln willst, dann bewirb Dich jetzt bei uns über unser Bewerberformular unter der Referenz-Nr.: JW_2050 . Wir freuen uns auf Dich! Erste Fragen zum Bewerbungsverfahren beantworten wir Dir gern unter Tel: 0355 46-3250 oder unter bewerbungen@mul-ct.de . Deine persönliche Ansprechpartnerin ist Julia Wilk. Nimm bei Fragen auch gern Kontakt per WhatsApp mit uns unter 0160 96518789 auf. Für fachliche Fragen steht Dir Sven Kleemann (Leiter IT) gern unter der Rufnummer Tel: 0355 46-1449 zur Verfügung. Weitere Informationen zum Universitätsklinikum findest Du auf unserer Homepage www.mul-ct.de Medizinische Universität Lausitz – Carl Thiem ÷ Recruiting-Team der MUL – CT ÷ Thiemstraße 111, 03048 Cottbus ÷ Julia Wilk Referentin Arbeitgebermarketing und Recruiting 0355/ 463250 Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Full-time Job function Other Industries Higher Education Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Medizinische Universität Lausitz – Carl Thiem by 2x See who you know

title: SAP S/4HANA Consultant - Remote Work
description: At BairesDev®, we've been leading the way in technology projects for over 15 years. We deliver cutting-edge solutions to giants like Google and the most innovative startups in Silicon Valley. Our diverse 4,000+ team, composed of the world's Top 1% of tech talent, works remotely on roles that drive significant impact worldwide. When you apply for this position, you're taking the first step in a process that goes beyond the ordinary. We aim to align your passions and skills with our vacancies, setting you on a path to exceptional career development and success. SAP S/4HANA Consultant at BairesDev As an SAP S/4HANA Consultant, you will lead the implementation and migration of organizations to the next-generation ERP suite. You will play a key role in transforming business operations by redesigning processes and ensuring seamless data transitions to the S/4HANA platform. What You'll Do: - Design and implement end-to-end SAP S/4HANA solutions tailored to specific business requirements. - Lead complex data migration strategies from legacy systems to SAP S/4HANA environments. - Redesign and optimize business processes to leverage the advanced capabilities of the S/4HANA suite. - Develop and configure intuitive user interfaces using SAP Fiori to enhance the user experience. - Provide expert guidance on S/4HANA best practices, including system architecture and landscape transformation. - Collaborate with stakeholders to ensure successful project delivery and alignment with enterprise goals. What we are looking for: - 8+ years of experience in SAP Consulting, ERP Implementation, or Business Process Redesign. - Proven expertise in implementing and migrating organizations to SAP S/4HANA. - Proficiency in SAP Fiori and designing modern user experiences within the SAP ecosystem. - Hands-on experience with complex data migration strategies and business process optimization. - Strong background in architecting enterprise-grade SAP solutions and managing full-lifecycle deployments. - Advanced proficiency in English. How we do make your work (and your life) easier: - 100% remote work (from anywhere). - Excellent compensation in USD or your local currency if preferred - Hardware and software setup for you to work from home. - Flexible hours: create your own schedule. - Paid parental leaves, vacations, and national holidays. - Innovative and multicultural work environment: collaborate and learn from the global Top 1% of talent. - Supportive environment with mentorship, promotions, skill development, and diverse growth opportunities. Apply now and become part of a global team where your unique talents can truly thrive! title: Analista Administrativo Jr - Relacionamento Corporativo
description: Somos a Unimed Cuiabá! Com 49 anos no mercado de Saúde Suplementar, a Unimed Cuiabá faz parte da maior cooperativa médica do mundo. Nascemos com a vocação do cuidado e isso nos move. Somos mais de mil colaboradores movidos por um mesmo ideal: o cuidado com a saúde. Nossa rede própria é composta por Hospital, Pronto Atendimento Adulto e Infantil, Distribuidora de Medicamentos, Farmácias, Núcleo de Vacinação, Centro de Feridas, Centro de Infusão de Biológicos, Núcleo de Medicina Preventiva – Viver Bem, Fisioterapia, Clínicas de Terapias Especiais, Consultórios, Ambulatório Fácil, Telemedicina, Rede de Cuidados Continuados e Sede Administrativa. Valorizamos a diversidade, a inclusão e o respeito, pois acreditamos que isso promove inovação, favorece o ambiente de trabalho e gera resultados excepcionais. Aqui, todos são bem-vindos! Quer fazer parte deste time? Verifique se alguma de nossas oportunidades se alinha ao seu perfil e junte-se a nós nessa jornada. Responsabilidades e atribuições Um breve resumo de como será o seu dia dia: Executar rotinas administrativas de pós-venda relacionadas à carteira de clientes empresariais; Executar atividades de apoio à gestão da carteira, conforme orientação; Atender solicitações administrativas de clientes empresariais; Manusear contratos, aditivos e documentos sob orientação; Registrar movimentações contratuais e cadastrais em sistemas corporativos; Organizar arquivos físicos e digitais da carteira, manter dados atualizados; Acompanhar prazos contratuais e demandas administrativas; Cumprir normas, políticas internas e procedimentos estabelecidos; Reportar ocorrências e informações ao superior imediato; Executar contatos administrativos preventivos com clientes, conforme direcionamento; Apoiar ações de manutenção da carteira ativa, por meio do acompanhamento de pendências administrativas; Interagir com áreas internas para encaminhamento de solicitações operacionais. Requisitos e qualificações O que você precisa ter: Ensino superior completo e/ou em andamento; Noções básicas do pacote Office; Conhecimento em regulamentação da ANS, contratos, rotinas administrativas e gestão de carteira de clientes. Informações adicionais Valorizamos nossos colaboradores que são a força motriz da instituição. Atuamos no bem-estar, ofertando benefícios e promovendo condições plenas de atuação. Confira alguns dos benefícios disponíveis: Plano de saúde Unimed Fácil, com cobertura para cônjuges e filhos (com coparticipação); Plano de odontológico que contempla cônjuges e filhos; Vale alimentação; Refeições em nossos restaurantes parceiros; Auxílio creche conforme elegibilidade; Seguro de vida; Vale transporte; Mimo aniversário; Asfunimed - Associação dos colaboradores oferece vôlei e aula funcional, além de parcerias com instituições de ensino e salões de beleza; Estacionamento para o colaborador; Se você atende aos pré-requisitos da vaga, não hesite em se candidatar e esteja atento às seguintes orientações: Durante o processo de seleção, toda a comunicação será realizada por e-mail ou telefone. Certifique-se de manter seus dados atualizados e verifique regularmente sua caixa de entrada, incluindo a pasta de spam. Em caso de feedback negativo, mantenha-se motivado e atento a outras oportunidades que possam surgir. 🌟 Junte-se à equipe Unimed Cuiabá e faça parte do desafio de transformar a saúde suplementar em MT! 🌟 Buscamos profissionais comprometidos para enfrentar o maior desafio de uma operadora de saúde: proporcionar o melhor atendimento possível aos nossos beneficiários, em um cenário dinâmico e desafiador. Em um mundo onde as demandas por cuidados de saúde estão em constante crescimento, é essencial que nos destaquemos na excelência do atendimento, na eficiência operacional e na inovação contínua. A Unimed Cuiabá cuida de forma integral de seus clientes para que eles possam aproveitar a vida. Nossos serviços e atendimentos são pautados pela segurança, humanização e promoção de qualidade de vida. Como parte da nossa equipe, você terá a oportunidade de viver o jeito de cuidar Unimed, colaborando para encontrar soluções criativas e eficazes, e contribuindo para o bem-estar e a qualidade de vida da nossa comunidade. Se você é apaixonado por saúde e busca fazer a diferença na vida das pessoas, venha fazer parte da nossa equipe! Confira as vagas disponíveis e junte-se a nós! title: Analista Financeiro Jr.
description: Somos o CoE, o Centro de Excelência Votorantim. Acreditamos na evolução contínua: tudo muda, se transforma e se reinventa. Somos pessoas focadas em resultado, relações, aprendizado e ordem. Se você se identifica com nossos atributos, venha fazer parte desse time! Buscamos por um analista para integrar o time do Centro de Excelência Votorantim na área de Inadimplência . Nesta frente, atuamos com a gestão e acompanhamento das pendências financeiras do Grupo Votorantim, garantindo direcionamento adequado dos casos e suporte às áreas envolvidas. Trabalhamos com tratativas administrativas e jurídicas, sempre com foco na qualidade, eficiência e melhoria contínua dos processos. Os desafios dessa pessoa serão na frente de Inadimplência, atuando com: Atuar na gestão e acompanhamento de pendências financeiras, garantindo análise assertiva dos casos e direcionamento adequado para as áreas responsáveis. Monitorar indicadores de inadimplência, identificando causas, tendências e oportunidades de mitigação de riscos financeiros. Conduzir tratativas administrativas junto a clientes internos e externos, buscando soluções eficientes para regularização de valores em aberto. Realizar interface com áreas jurídicas, financeiras e de negócio para acompanhamento de processos e definição das melhores estratégias de atuação. Garantir qualidade e confiabilidade das informações por meio de análises críticas, controles internos e acompanhamento de indicadores. Atuar em ambiente dinâmico, conciliando múltiplas demandas, prazos e prioridades com foco em resultados e excelência operacional. É importante que você conheça sobre: Processos e rotinas financeiras. Familiaridade com sistemas ERP (SAP, Oracle ou similares) e ferramentas de gestão financeira. Excel intermediário. Perfil organizado, orientado a resultados, aprendizado contínuo e melhoria de processos. Nossos escritórios estão localizados em São Paulo e Curitiba. Atuamos com suporte administrativo para as empresas investidas do Grupo Votorantim. Nossa estrutura é formada pelos Centros de Serviços Compartilhados com soluções nas áreas de Finanças, Contabilidade e Tributos e Recursos Humanos; Soluções Imobiliárias e de Tecnologia da Informação. Nascemos com o objetivo de gerar valor por meio da busca incessante por excelência operacional, intimidade com o cliente e inovação. Acreditamos que profissionais talentosos estão em todos os lugares e por isso as nossas vagas estão abertas a todos, independente de cor, faixa etária, gênero, orientação sexual, identidade de gênero e/ou deficiências. Acesse https://votorantimcoe.gupy.io/ e conheça mais sobre o CoE.
