title: SAP Basis Administrator
description: About Us At ExxonMobil, our vision is to lead in energy innovations that advance modern living and a net-zero future. As one of the world’s largest publicly traded energy and chemical companies, we are powered by a unique and diverse workforce fueled by the pride in what we do and what we stand for. The success of our Upstream, Product Solutions and Low Carbon Solutions businesses is the result of the talent, curiosity and drive of our people. They bring solutions every day to optimize our strategy in energy, chemicals, lubricants and lower-emissions technologies. We invite you to bring your ideas to ExxonMobil to help create sustainable solutions that improve quality of life and meet society’s evolving needs. Learn more about our What and our Why and how we can work together . More About Us Come be part of the EMIT Department of ExxonMobil Business Support Center Brasil Ltda. at our Global Business Center (GBC) in Curitiba, with more than 21 years of presence in the country and a workforce of ~1800 employees, that provides support to an extensive portfolio of businesses across the globe. Our offices are located in Brazil, USA, Europe and Asia. What Role You Will Play In Our Team We are looking for a professional to join our global, distributed team who will be responsible for the design, implementation, and support of foundational platform components within a large and complex SAP landscape, ensuring adherence to established standards while supporting mission‑critical SAP systems. What You Will Do As part of a global, distributed team, you will design, implement, and support the foundational platform components of a large and complex SAP landscape, ensuring adherence to established standards while helping maintain the stability, scalability, and reliability of mission‑critical SAP systems. About You Skills and Qualifications: Provide Follow‑the‑Sun (FTS) incident management support for SAP systems, ensuring rapid service restoration. Maintain system integrity, availability, and operational controls across SAP RISE landscapes. Coordinate and execute cross‑functional technical activities, including outages, upgrades, and infrastructure changes (DB, OS, HW). Lead and support SAP Basis administration activities, including system builds, configurations, monitoring, and housekeeping. Implement and manage system monitoring, alerting, proactive health checks, and capacity monitoring (CPU, memory, disk, spool). Plan and execute system upgrades, enhancements, DR, and cyber recovery tests within SAP RISE. Analyze and resolve performance and technical issues, including EarlyWatch findings and problem management. Produce and maintain SAP Basis standards and technical documentation. Build and test Proof of Concept (PoC) systems following SAP RISE best practices. Provide stakeholder reporting, KPIs, and operational metrics. Collaborate in an Agile/Scrum environment, participating in sprint planning, stand‑ups, retrospectives, and backlog management. Deliver on‑call rotational support during off‑hours and weekends as required. Preferred Qualifications/Experience: Experience with SAP S/4HANA and SAP HANA Database administration. Experience with SAP NetWeaver ABAP and Java stacks (ECC, S/4HANA). Experience with SAP Solution Manager, Technical Monitoring, CALM. SAP RISE. SAP system monitoring, performance tuning, and troubleshooting. Operating system and database-level support for SAP environments. Scripting and automation skills (PowerShell, Shell scripting). Strong incident, problem, and change management experience. Strong experience with SAP Basis experience in enterprise-scale environments. Your Benefits An ExxonMobil career is one designed to last. Our commitment to you runs deep: our employees grow personally and professionally, with benefits built on our core categories of health, security, finance, and life. We offer you: PAE – Programa de Assistência aos Empregados Seguro de Vida Creche e babá Tickets alimentação Assistência funeral Auxilio Filho Excepcional Plano de pensões Among others. Please note benefits may be changed from time to time without notice, subject to applicable law. Stay connected with us Learn more at our website Follow us on Linkedin Like us on Facebook Subscribe our channel at YouTube Alternate Location: Nothing herein is intended to override the corporate separateness of local entities. Working relationships discussed herein do not necessarily represent a reporting connection, but may reflect a functional guidance, stewardship, or service relationship. Exxon Mobil Corporation has numerous affiliates, many with names that include ExxonMobil, Exxon, Esso and Mobil. For convenience and simplicity, those terms and terms like corporation, company, our, we and its are sometimes used as abbreviated references to specific affiliates or affiliate groups. Abbreviated references describing global or regional operational organizations and global or regional business lines are also sometimes used for convenience and simplicity. Similarly, ExxonMobil has business relationships with thousands of customers, suppliers, governments, and others. Acting as a central point of coordination, you will collaborate closely with various IT support teams to plan, manage, and implement changes efficiently, ensuring minimal disruption while maintaining high system performance. title: SAP Order-to-Cash (OTC) System Analyst
description: About Us At ExxonMobil, our vision is to lead in energy innovations that advance modern living while reducing emissions. As one of the world’s largest publicly traded energy and chemical companies, we are powered by a unique and diverse workforce fueled by the pride in what we do and what we stand for. The success of our Upstream, Product Solutions and Low Carbon Solutions businesses is the result of the talent, curiosity and drive of our people. They bring solutions every day to optimize our strategy in energy, chemicals, lubricants and lower-emissions technologies. We invite you to bring your ideas to ExxonMobil to help create sustainable solutions that improve quality of life and meet society’s evolving needs. Learn more about our What and our Why and how we can work together . More About Us Come be part of the EMIT Department of ExxonMobil Business Support Center Brasil Ltda. at our Global Business Center (GBC) in Curitiba, with more than 21 years of presence in the country and a workforce of ~1800 employees, that provides support to an extensive portfolio of businesses across the globe. Our offices are located in Brazil, USA, Europe and Asia. What Role You Will Play In Our Team As an SAP Order-to-Cash (OTC) System Analyst, you will play a key role in driving the efficiency and effectiveness of our end-to-end OTC processes across SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA environments. You will act as a vital bridge between business needs and technical solutions, ensuring that order management, billing, and cash application processes run smoothly and are continuously optimized. In this role, you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, including Finance, Sales, and IT, to design, configure, and enhance SAP solutions that support business growth and operational excellence. Working within an Agile delivery framework, you will have the opportunity to contribute to continuous improvement initiatives, troubleshoot complex issues, and implement scalable solutions that deliver real value to the organization. What You Will Do Analyze business requirements and design OTC solutions within SAP (SD/FI modules and related integrations) Configure and maintain SAP Order-to-Cash functionalities, including: Order Management Pricing Billing & Invoicing Credit Management Accounts Receivable Collaborate with Finance, Logistics, and Procurement teams to ensure end-to-end process alignment Provide system support, troubleshoot issues, and implement enhancements to improve performance and usability Prepare functional specifications and coordinate with technical teams for development and integration Conduct testing, deliver end-user training, and maintain clear documentation Provide go-live and hyper-care support during SAP implementations and rollouts Participate actively in Agile ceremonies (daily stand-ups, sprint planning, retrospectives) and deliver solutions iteratively Identify and drive continuous improvement initiatives and automation opportunities across OTC processes About You Skills and Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in information systems, Business Administration, or related field. Experience with SAP OTC configuration and support. Familiarity with integration points between OTC and FI/MM modules. Strong understanding of SAP SD processes and data structures. Ability to perform basic ABAP debugging and work with technical teams. Excellent analytical and communication skills. Fluent in English (written and spoken). Experience working in Agile frameworks (Scrum or SAFe) Preferred Qualifications/Experience: SAP ECC experience SAP S/4HANA experience. SAP certification in SD or OTC modules. Knowledge of EDI, IDoc processing, and third-party system integrations. Strong problem-solving and organizational skills. Ability to work in a global, cross-functional team environment. Customer-focused mindset. Your Benefits An ExxonMobil career is one designed to last. Our commitment to you runs deep: our employees grow personally and professionally, with benefits built on our core categories of health, security, finance, and life. title: Especialista SAP
description: Líder no Brasil e uma das maiores empresas do segmento no mundo, a Neodent faz parte do Grupo Straumann, líder global em odontologia . Aqui no Brasil, onde a Neodent foi criada há mais de 30 anos, representamos as marcas Neodent, Straumann, ClearCorrect e Yller. Nosso propósito é criar novos sorrisos todos os dias e fazemos isso guiados por uma jornada cultural e mentalidade de Protagonista & Aprendiz, que tornam os nossos colaboradores um time de alta performance. Oportunidade para atuação no Batel. O que você pode esperar do seu dia com a gente? Atuar como especialista funcional nos módulos SAP que impactam Warehouse to Deliver, Procure to Pay, e Manufacturing & Quality. Liderar análises técnicas e funcionais para implementação de melhorias e novos processos. Levantar, analisar, validar e documentar requisitos de negócio junto às áreas usuárias. Definir soluções aderentes às melhores práticas SAP e às diretrizes corporativas. Elaborar especificações funcionais para desenvolvimentos e integrações. Planejar, coordenar e executar testes funcionais e integrados. Apoiar Business Process Owners, usuários-chave e áreas operacionais na definição e evolução dos processos. Atuar como interface entre áreas de negócio, equipes técnicas, fornecedores e times globais. Participar de projetos de implantação, migração, rollout e atualização SAP. Conduzir reuniões de alinhamento e apresentações técnicas. Ministrar treinamentos e apoiar a disseminação do conhecimento para usuários e equipes internas. Garantir a estabilidade, disponibilidade e evolução contínua das soluções SAP. Avaliar impactos de mudanças, releases e upgrades, assegurando aderência aos processos locais e globais. Apoiar iniciativas de melhoria contínua, inovação e transformação digital. Propor otimizações de processos. Garantir conformidade com normas internas, requisitos regulatórios e boas práticas de governança. O que você precisa ter ou saber? Ensino superior completo em áreas relacionadas; Experiência sólida em implementação, sustentação e evolução em soluções SAP (ECC e S4/HANA). Experiência em projetos de implantação rollout, migração ou upgrade SAP. Vivência em levantamento de requisitos, desenho de soluções e relacionamento com áreas de negócio. Conhecimento de processos de negócio relacionados aos módulos SAP de atuação. Experiência em metodologias de projetos e gestão de demandas. Inglês avançado é mandatório. Espanhol intermediário mandatório, avançado é desejável. O que você vai encontrar aqui? Vale-alimentação e Vale refeição; Plano de saúde com cobertura nacional extensivo aos dependentes; Plano Odontológico; WellHub (Gympass); Vale-transporte ou subsídio estacionamento; Participação por Resultados (PPR); Programas internos de desenvolvimento; Descontos nas nossas linhas de produtos; Oportunidades de crescimento. A Neodent acredita na diversidade, por isso as nossas vagas estão disponíveis para todas as pessoas, independente de sexo, raça, orientação sexual, religião, nacionalidade, idade ou deficiência. Vem com a gente fazer o mundo sorrir! title: SR DIRECTOR SAP
description: Join the largest manufacturer of tile and natural stone in the United States and watch your career stand out with Dal-Tile, a subsidiary of Mohawk Industries. Our close-knit team of dedicated professionals has made us the success we are today. In exchange for their hard work, we support our people with a family-friendly work environment, a commitment to promoting from within, unique benefits that go beyond just medical and dental, and a belief that every employee deserves a productive life outside of work. If this sounds outstanding to you, take the first step forward and explore a career with Dal-Tile. This Sr SAP Director role is responsible for setting and executing the enterprise ERP strategy (SAP) to enable growth, efficiency, and high-quality decision support in the functional areas of Finance, Supply Chain, Transportation & Logistics, Order to Cash & Warehouse Mgmt. This role ensures ERP and other functional applications, platforms, data, and processes align with business priorities while delivering reliable, secure, and scalable capabilities. This role does NOT provide current or future sponorship assisstance Please note that we will NOT provide relocation assistance (Candidates are expected to relocate on own 3 weeks from time of offer acceptance) Employees are required to report in office 4 days a week / 7834 CF Hawn Freeway | Dallas, Texas 75217 (NO NEGOTIATION) Duties And Responsibilities Of The Position Partners with executive leadership team to define the enterprise ERP and supporting platforms strategy and multi-year roadmap, aligning technology support and solutions with corporate and business unit goals. Partnering with the Dal-Tile Business Architect, oversees SAP architecture, integrations, and data design to ensure scalability, performance, and interoperability. Develop reliable and predictable solutions by leading ERP build capabilities including ABAP and RPG developers Partners with functional leaders to translate business needs into ERP solutions that drive measurable value Directs major SAP programs and releases (e.g., S/4HANA updates, template rollouts), ensuring on-time, on-budget delivery and adoption. Establishes technology governance, standards, and controls, including change management, data quality, and information security requirements. Develop and manage the application management services agreement and corresponding vendor management. Defines KPIs and dashboards to track system health, process performance, and business outcomes; drives corrective actions. Optimizes total cost of ownership through platform rationalization, licensing strategy, and vendor negotiations. Champions best practices in process design and harmonization across impacted business functions. Builds, coaches, and retains a high-performing team; develops succession plans and career paths. Coordinates cutover, hypercare, and stabilization plans to protect operations and minimize business disruption. Serves as executive liaison with SAP and strategic partners to influence product direction and unlock co-innovation opportunities. Collaborates with the executive team to develop and implement strategic plans that align with the company's vision and goals. Provides visionary leadership to drive growth and innovation. Identifies and pursues new business opportunities, partnerships, and market expansions. Leads initiatives to enhance the company's market presence and competitiveness. Provides strong leadership to department heads and teams. Fosters a collaborative and high-performance culture. Identifies and assesses risks to the company's success. Implements effective risk management strategies. Drives a culture of innovation and continuous improvement. Leads initiatives to enhance operational efficiency and effectiveness. Builds and maintain relationships with key customers, partners, and stakeholders. Ensures high levels of customer satisfaction and retention. Performs other duties as needed. Required Experience And Education Bachelors degree in a related field or equivalent education and/or experience. 15+ years relevant experience or equivalent education and/or experience. 10+ years of management experience. Competencies Clear expertise as a strategic leader. Experience with organizational design for transformational technology change. Experience with deploying and maintaining SAP S/4 HANA and/or SAP ECC Experience with pricing and EDI solutions preferred Knowledge of business processes for Finance, Supply Chain, Order to Cash and Warehouse Mgmt preferred Requires the ability to influence others in sensitive situations while preserving relationships. Focused on, and responsible for, the functions productivity and collective impact. Excellent communication, problem solving, and organizational skills. Able to multitask, prioritize, delegate, and manage time effectively. High level of integrity and discretion in handling sensitive and confidential data. High degree of professionalism, judgment, maturity, and resourcefulness in the absence of formalized guidelines and procedures. title: Senior Business Analyst
description: We are seeking a highly articulate and analytical Business Analyst to serve as the critical bridge between our business operations and technical teams. The ideal candidate will possess exceptional communication skills, a proven ability to translate complex business needs into actionable technical requirements, and functional familiarity with SAP environments . This role requires a proactive problem-solver who is comfortable collaborating with cross-functional stakeholders and is available to travel as project needs dictate. Key Responsibilities Requirement Gathering & Translation: Lead workshops and interviews with stakeholders to elicit, analyze, and document comprehensive business requirements. Translate these needs into clear functional specifications, user stories, and process maps. SAP System Alignment: Collaborate with technical and functional teams to ensure business requirements align with SAP capabilities, standard processes, and best practices. Stakeholder Management: Act as the primary point of contact between business units and IT, delivering crystal-clear presentations and progress updates to both technical teams and executive leadership. Process Optimization: Analyze existing business processes, identify inefficiencies, and propose data-driven solutions to maximize operational performance. Testing & Deployment Support: Assist in developing test scenarios, coordinating User Acceptance Testing (UAT), and supporting end-user training to ensure seamless system adoption. Qualifications & Skills Education: Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Information Technology, Finance, or a related field. Experience: 4+ years of experience as a Business Analyst, preferably working within enterprise software environments. Communication Master: Outstanding verbal, written, and interpersonal communication skills. Must be highly adept at active listening, facilitation, and presenting technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders. SAP Familiarity: Hands-on experience or a strong functional understanding of SAP modules (e.g., SAP ERP, S/4HANA, FI/CO, SD, or MM) is strongly preferred. Analytical Tools: Proficiency in data analysis, process mapping tools (e.g., Visio, Lucidchart), and Agile project management tools (e.g., Jira, Azure DevOps). Travel Requirements Mobility: Ability and willingness to travel on an as-needed basis to corporate offices, client sites, or regional facilities to facilitate workshops or support critical project deployments (estimated travel: 15–25%). title: SR DIRECTOR SAP (Dallas, Texas, United States, 75217)
description: Join the largest manufacturer of tile and natural stone in the United States and watch your career stand out with Dal-Tile, a subsidiary of Mohawk Industries. Our close-knit team of dedicated professionals has made us the success we are today. In exchange for their hard work, we support our people with a family-friendly work environment, a commitment to promoting from within, unique benefits that go beyond just medical and dental, and a belief that every employee deserves a productive life outside of work. If this sounds outstanding to you, take the first step forward and explore a career with Dal-Tile. This Sr SAP Director role is responsible for setting and executing the enterprise ERP strategy (SAP) to enable growth, efficiency, and high-quality decision support in the functional areas of Finance, Supply Chain, Transportation & Logistics, Order to Cash & Warehouse Mgmt. This role ensures ERP and other functional applications, platforms, data, and processes align with business priorities while delivering reliable, secure, and scalable capabilities. This role does NOT provide current or future sponorship assisstance Please note that we will NOT provide relocation assistance (Candidates are expected to relocate on own 3 weeks from time of offer acceptance) Employees are required to report in office 4 days a week / 7834 CF Hawn Freeway | Dallas, Texas 75217 (NO NEGOTIATION) Duties And Responsibilities Of The Position Partners with executive leadership team to define the enterprise ERP and supporting platforms strategy and multi-year roadmap, aligning technology support and solutions with corporate and business unit goals. Partnering with the Dal-Tile Business Architect, oversees SAP architecture, integrations, and data design to ensure scalability, performance, and interoperability. Develop reliable and predictable solutions by leading ERP build capabilities including ABAP and RPG developers Partners with functional leaders to translate business needs into ERP solutions that drive measurable value Directs major SAP programs and releases (e.g., S/4HANA updates, template rollouts), ensuring on-time, on-budget delivery and adoption. Establishes technology governance, standards, and controls, including change management, data quality, and information security requirements. Develop and manage the application management services agreement and corresponding vendor management. Defines KPIs and dashboards to track system health, process performance, and business outcomes; drives corrective actions. Optimizes total cost of ownership through platform rationalization, licensing strategy, and vendor negotiations. Champions best practices in process design and harmonization across impacted business functions. Builds, coaches, and retains a high-performing team; develops succession plans and career paths. Coordinates cutover, hypercare, and stabilization plans to protect operations and minimize business disruption. Serves as executive liaison with SAP and strategic partners to influence product direction and unlock co-innovation opportunities. Collaborates with the executive team to develop and implement strategic plans that align with the company's vision and goals. Provides visionary leadership to drive growth and innovation. Identifies and pursues new business opportunities, partnerships, and market expansions. Leads initiatives to enhance the company's market presence and competitiveness. Provides strong leadership to department heads and teams. Fosters a collaborative and high-performance culture. Identifies and assesses risks to the company's success. Implements effective risk management strategies. Drives a culture of innovation and continuous improvement. Leads initiatives to enhance operational efficiency and effectiveness. Builds and maintain relationships with key customers, partners, and stakeholders. Ensures high levels of customer satisfaction and retention. Performs other duties as needed. Required Experience And Education Bachelor’s degree in a related field or equivalent education and/or experience. 15+ years relevant experience or equivalent education and/or experience. 10+ years of management experience. Competencies Clear expertise as a strategic leader. Experience with organizational design for transformational technology change. Experience with deploying and maintaining SAP S/4 HANA and/or SAP ECC Experience with pricing and EDI solutions preferred Knowledge of business processes for Finance, Supply Chain, Order to Cash and Warehouse Mgmt preferred Requires the ability to influence others in sensitive situations while preserving relationships. Focused on, and responsible for, the function’s productivity and collective impact. Excellent communication, problem solving, and organizational skills. Able to multitask, prioritize, delegate, and manage time effectively. High level of integrity and discretion in handling sensitive and confidential data. High degree of professionalism, judgment, maturity, and resourcefulness in the absence of formalized guidelines and procedures. title: SAP Functional Software Engineer
description: We made history and now we work to transform the future – for our customers, our communities and our families. You'll see your work on the road every day, helping people move freely and pursue their dreams. At Ford, you can build more than vehicles. Come build what matters. Enterprise Technology plays a critical part in shaping the future of mobility. If you’re looking for the chance to leverage advanced technology to redefine the transportation landscape, enhance customer experience and improve people’s lives, this is the opportunity for you. Join us and challenge your IT expertise and analytical skills to help create vehicles that are as smart as you are. In this position... Ford Motor Company is seeking a highly experienced SAP Functional Software Engineer (expert in SAP S4 HANA Sales and Distribution (SD) & Vehicle Management System (VMS)) for the Vehicle Invoicing and Drafting Application (VI&D). You must have 10+ years of solid technology experience with demonstrated technical leadership capability. Vehicle Invoicing and Drafting (VI&D) is a Mainframe/COBOL Legacy application which we are modernizing/transitioning to SAP, with the help of a Consulting Firm/System Integrator. Based in Dearborn, MI, this is a hybrid position with a required four-day onsite presence each week. Relocation assistance may be available for qualified candidates. What You'll Do... Lead/Guide the SAP Modernization effort of VI&D utilizing your strong expertise in SAP S4 HANA Sales and Distribution (SD) & SAP Vehicle Management System (VMS) with Tax knowledge Lead/Guide the transition to SAP including all of the following: validate the solutioning of the System Integrators (design, build, test, & deployment), navigate the hyper-care phase, and facilitate the long-term support activities including future enhancements and upgrades Easily assimilate into the existing Global Team and will help to shape the modernization efforts of the Application. Maintain accurate systems related documentation and adhere to Systems coding/security standards in accordance with Ford Guidelines/Controls Effectively engage in technical discussions within the Application Team, other iERP PDOs, and with other interfacing Applications. Strong ability to navigate new, complex, and challenging SAP technical situations with creative solutions Ability to bridge Legacy Mainframe Systems with SAP/S4 Hana leading-edge technology Analyze complex problems/gaps and develop/identify the most viable and efficient SAP solutions Superior capability in analyzing complex business requirements, documenting /updating Functional Specs and can easily guide the Technical Teams in SAP Customizations Strong functional experience in supporting SAP upgrades, support, and maintenance of Sales and Distribution (SD) & SAP Vehicle Management System (VMS) modules Superior ability to quickly build relationships with people, including Software Engineers and Product Owners Demonstrated ability to develop technical solutions, lead, and guide small teams through complex technical situations (Solid Technical Leadership abilities) You'll have... Bachelor of Computer Science, Information Systems, Systems Programming or equivalent combination of relevant education and experience 10+ years of IT experience 7+ years of functional experience with SAP/S4 HANA with a keen ability to use SAP SD/Sales & Distribution, Vehicle Management System (VMS), and Invoicing Processes to modernize a complex legacy application in a fast-paced environment, using Agile Methodology 5+ years designing, analyzing requirements, documenting functional specs, integration testing, implementing, trouble-shooting (within SLA Guidelines), and deploying SAP ERP solutions for sales and business transactions using SAP Sales and Distribution/Vehicle Management System (involving Pricing, billing, and Customer Service functions) 5+ years of experience with the Agile Methodology or PDO Framework. Even better, you may have... Master’s degree Formal SAP Training and/or SAP CertificationsFormal SAP Training and/or SAP Certifications Extensive experience with large complex global SAP Implementations (from Requirements Gathering, Functional Spec Definition, Testing, and Deployment/Support) Capability and willingness to share knowledge, mentor, and train others that are new to the SAP journey Experience with COBOL or large complex legacy applications Experience with automotive or OEM domains, or similar experience Superior documentation and communication skills Strong ability to drive requirements gathering sessions with Business Partners Superior technical competency with great initiative and strong technical leadership capability Strong can-do attitude with a natural ability to understand a complex Application and interconnections with upstream and downstream Applications Demonstrated ability to quickly learn and adapt to a wide variety of advanced technologies Logical/Critical Thinker Empowered, Independent, Problem Solver, Highly Analytical, Responsible and Friendly Team Player with an ability to share and train other team members Highly capable in the midst of ambiguity Exceptional communication Skills (Both verbal and written) Experienced in facilitating requirements definition discussions with Product Owners Process and Detail Oriented (with a keen understanding of the importance of security and controls) Creative (Out of the Box) Thinker Experience with Jira and Agile Methodology Previous automotive industry experience and an understanding of the vehicle life cycle is highly desired You may not check every box, or your experience may look a little different from what we've outlined, but if you think you can bring value to Ford Motor Company, we encourage you to apply! title: SAP Platform Architect
description: Ford is transforming its Enterprise Technology landscape through the implementation of SAP S/4HANA on SAP RISE. This transformation will reduce technical footprint complexity, standardize global business processes, and enable differentiated capabilities across the enterprise. The SAP Platform Architect, Tooling will play a key role in designing, engineering, and governing the SAP platform ecosystem that supports this transformation. Platform Architecture & Ecosystem Design: Define, design, and maintain high-availability, global reference architectures for the SAP ecosystem, spanning SAP S/4HANA, Business Technology Platform (BTP), and other data products. Platform Engineering & Developer Enablement: Engineer core platform tools, reusable templates, and enablers to accelerate delivery. Establish best practices and modernization patterns to guide application teams transitioning legacy technologies to S/4HANA and BTP. Hybrid Cloud Integration & Security: Design and implement secure, robust connectivity and API patterns bridging on-premises SAP environments, SAP cloud services, and GCP-based containerized applications (such as Cloud Run) using modern identity federation and security standards. Observability & Performance Engineering: Establish patterns and reference implementations for enterprise-grade observability, telemetry, and monitoring frameworks for BTP and GCP-hosted applications. Partner with application teams and SAP to proactively diagnose and resolve complex platform performance issues. AI Integration & Emerging Tech Evaluation: Lead Proof of Concepts (POCs) and establish architectural standards for AI-driven capabilities, including SAP Joule, SAP Business AI, and third-party AI models hosted on GCP. Lifecycle Management & Technology Renewal: Plan, govern, and execute regular technology renewal and upgrade cycles across SAP platform components to ensure alignment with vendor lifecycles and modern security standards. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner closely with SAP Basis, Security, Data Management, System Integrators, product and business teams to translate complex enterprise requirements into platform-level engineering solutions in a fast-paced environment. Qualifications Bachelor's in computer science or engineering or related fields or equivalent experience. 6+ years of relevant industry experience implementing SAP and other software. In depth technical knowledge of the S/4HANA eco-system, including RISE cloud, BTP cloud, and GRC. Hands on experience in SAP Basis and SAP BTP Administration. Strong communication skills both verbal and written. Experience with Hyperscalers with Google Cloud Platform being the most desired. Preferred Qualifications Technical knowledge and experience one or more of these technologies: Informatica MDM, HANA Database, Big Query, Linux operating systems, and SAP Joule/AI. Project Management experience You may not check every box, or your experience may look a little different from what we've outlined, but if you think you can bring value to Ford Motor Company, we encourage you to apply! Experience in Automotive / Manufacturing industry Experienced in at least 2 full life cycle of S/4 HANA and Fiori implementation. title: SAP developer
description: Jouw energie in de plus Als SAP developer bij Stedin ontwikkel je de kernprocessen die ons energienet realtime laten draaien en bouw je SAP‑oplossingen die de energietransitie technisch mogelijk maken. Waarom Werken Bij Stedin Jou Energie Oplevert Werken aan complexe SAP‑vraagstukken die direct impact hebben op het energienet. Hybride werken met veel ruimte om thuis te werken. Flexibele werktijden die je werk‑privébalans versterken. Werk om trots op te zijn Als SAP developer bij Stedin bouw je mee aan de systemen die ons energienet draaiende houden. Je ontwikkelt en verbetert SAP‑processen die essentieel zijn voor een betrouwbaar, toekomstbestendig elektriciteitsnet. Met jouw werk help je Nederland klaar te maken voor een energiesysteem dat steeds slimmer, digitaler en duurzamer wordt. Een Dag Als SAP Developer Je start de dag met de daily stand‑up, waar je met je DevOps‑team de prioriteiten bepaalt. Daarna werk je aan je user stories: van ABAP‑ontwikkeling tot Fiori/UI5‑apps en SAP‑integraties. Tijdens de lunch praat je bij met collega’s en spar je met de senior developer die jou begeleidt over de functionaliteit waar je aan werkt. Na de lunch doe je de refinement voor de volgende sprint en denk je mee over de beste technische aanpak. Je sluit af met afstemming met de Epic Owner en pakt nog wat beheerissues op, waarna je zo naar station Delft Campus wandelt. Hier laad jij van op Alle financiële arbeidsvoorwaarden op een rij, weet je meteen waar je aan toe bent: Een bruto maandsalaris van maximaal € 5.512,37 (bij 40 uur) (afhankelijk van jouw kennis en ervaring), 24,5 vakantiedagen en pensioenopbouw bij ABP waarbij wij 70% van de premie betalen. Bovenop je salaris ontvang je een persoonlijk budget van 24% (inclusief 8% vakantiegeld). Jij kiest wat je met het budget doet: bijvoorbeeld extra salaris of (tot max. 35,5) verlofdagen. In onze rekentool zie je hoe het werkt. Een laptop en telefoon, of € 30,- netto per maand als je je eigen telefoon gebruikt. Daar Komt Dit Nog Bovenop Moderne thuiswerkfaciliteiten en een thuiswerkvergoeding van € 2,40 per dag. Bij Stedin werken we 50% thuis en 50% op kantoor. Reizen naar kantoor doe je met de ov-vrijregeling. Dat is een ov-kaart die je ook in je vrije tijd kan gebruiken. Of met de flexibele reisregeling, waarbij je zelf kiest, combineert en varieert hoe je naar je werk reist en een extra vergoeding krijgt als je met de fiets komt. Jouw ontwikkeling staat centraal: events bezoeken met collega’s en trainingen en opleidingen volgen. Zo haal jij het beste uit jezelf. Vol energie vooruit met team SAP DevOps Je komt terecht in een jong en hecht team van zes collega’s in Delft, waar iedereen elkaar echt kent. Je deelt interesses, van rugby tot muziek, en na een Big Room Planning wordt er gezellig geborreld. Tijdens de lunch schuif je samen aan, wordt er gelachen en spar je makkelijk met collega’s. Op maandag werken jullie samen op kantoor, met humor, whiteboard‑sessies en iemand die altijd te vroeg is voor de stand‑up. Een team waar je je snel thuis voelt en volop ruimte krijgt om te groeien. Met deze kwaliteiten groei jij hier verder Je werkt graag samen om processen slimmer te maken en schakelt moeiteloos tussen collega’s en stakeholders. Je duikt met plezier in nieuwe technologieën en blijft nieuwsgierig naar betere oplossingen. Met jouw analytische blik en probleemoplossend vermogen pak je complexe SAP‑vraagstukken gestructureerd en nauwkeurig aan. Verder Heb Je Een afgeronde hbo‑ of wo‑opleiding in (Bedrijfskundige) Informatica, Software Engineering of een vergelijkbare richting. Minimaal 2 jaar aantoonbare ervaring als SAP Developer in een grote complexe organisatie. Basiskennis van SAP‑technologieën zoals SAP ABAP, SAP Integration Suite, SAP Fiori/UI5 of SAP Build. Minimaal C2-niveau beheersing van de Nederlandse taal Zet de stap naar jouw volgende uitdaging We kijken ernaar uit je te ontmoeten. Dit Is Nog Goed Om Te Weten Je mag een motivatiebrief meesturen, dat hoeft niet. Je werkt in principe 40 uur per week. 32 of 36 uur werken is ook een optie. description: Additional Information Job Number 26078864 Job Category Finance & Accounting Location Asset Area 13 - Hospitality District, New Delhi, New Delhi, India, 110037 VIEW ON MAP Schedule Full Time Located Remotely? N Position Type Non-Management Position Summary Check figures, postings, and documents for accuracy. Organize, secure, and maintain all files, records, cash and cash equivalents in accordance with policies and procedures. Record, store, access, and/or analyze computerized financial information. Classify, code, and summarize numerical and financial data to compile and keep financial records, using journals, ledgers, and/or computers. Prepare, maintain, audit, and distribute statistical, financial, accounting, auditing, or payroll reports and tables. Complete period-end closing procedures and reports as specified. Prepare, review, reconcile, and issue bills, invoices, and account statements according to company procedures. Follow-up and resolve past due accounts and vendor invoices until payment in full is received or resolved. Coordinate tasks and work with other departments; serve as a departmental role model or mentor; assign and ensure work tasks are completed on time and that they meet appropriate quality standards. Report work related accidents, or other injuries immediately upon occurrence to manager/supervisor. Follow all company policies and procedures; ensure uniform and personal appearance are clean and professional; maintain confidentiality of proprietary information; protect company assets. Speak with others using clear and professional language; prepare and review written documents accurately and completely; answer telephones using appropriate etiquette. Develop and maintain positive working relationships with others; support team to reach common goals; listen and respond appropriately to the concerns of other employees. Move, lift, carry, push, pull, and place objects weighing less than or equal to 10 pounds without assistance. Perform other reasonable job duties as requested by Supervisors. title: SAP Manufacturing - Public Cloud
description: Als Manufacturing Consultant in Public Cloud ben jij de drijvende kracht achter de optimalisatie en implementatie van SAP-projecten voor onze klanten. Je bent hun steun en toeverlaat bij het verbeteren van processen en de snelste weg naar oplossingen gedurende het hele traject. Dankzij de dynamiek in je projecten is geen dag hetzelfde. Voor uitdaging en variatie ben je bij deze functie aan het juiste adres! Jouw toekomstige baan! Ben je een ervaren professional en klaar voor de volgende stap in je carrière? Overweeg dan om bij delaware te starten. Je krijgt hier alle kansen om verantwoordelijkheid te nemen op impactvolle projecten en je vaardigheden naar een hoger niveau te tillen. Je draagt verantwoordelijkheid op projecten en bewaakt de planning en scope binnen jouw domein. Je maakt impact door bij te dragen aan pre-sales cases en bent een betrouwbare sparringpartner voor je klant dankzij jouw expertise en ervaring. Daarnaast denk je mee over het trainen en opleiden van key-users en ben je een mentor voor starters, waarbij je graag je expertise deelt. In jouw toekomstige baan zet je SAP Best Practices en je ervaring in om onze greenfield implementaties en optimalisatieprojecten binnen het Manufacutring-domeinn voor public cloud succesvol af te ronden. Je werkt aan twee projecten tegelijk, waardoor je job dynamisch blijft en je elke dag verschillende collega’s ziet. Je verdiept je expertise door te leren van je collega’s en je kennis met hen te delen. In de SAP-wereld ben je immers nooit uitgeleerd! Bovendien zie je niet alleen verbeteringsmogelijkheden bij de klant, maar ook binnen je eigen werk. Dit doe je door goed op te letten wat er buiten jouw domein gebeurt. Kan jij onderstaande vragen met "ja" beantwoorden? Je hebt ervaring met SAP-implementaties in public cloud. Pluspunten als je de greenfield approach al in de vingers hebt! Je hebt een hbo- of wo diploma op zak. Je wilt je graag focussen op Sales/Procurement. Je beheerst Nederlands en Engels uitstekend in woord en geschrift. Je bent klaar om een van ons te worden en kan jezelf vinden in onze kernwaarden: Care, Commitment, Entrepreneurship, Respect en Team Spirit. #peopleofdelaware Waarin Maken wij het verschil? We care about our people! Samen werken we aan elkaars succes. Best possible you programma! Je staat aan het roer van je eigen carrière en krijgt van ons alle ruimte om aan je persoonlijke ontwikkeling te werken. Wij investeren in opleidingsmogelijkheden; zoals in diverse trainingen, certificeringen of coaching trajecten. Een passend salaris dat op jouw level wordt afgestemd; Leaseauto naar keuze of mobiliteitsbudget Nieuwste laptop en telefoon (Samsung /Iphone) Samenwerking met OpenUp. We vinden het bij delaware erg belangrijk om te investeren in onze mensen en hun welzijn. Een informele en familiare werksfeer waarbij teamwork en collegialiteit hoog in het vaandel staat; Een levendige activiteitencommisie; Er worden regelmatig activiteiten door en voor onze collega’s georganiseerd. Zoals ons jaarlijkse wintersportuitje, pubquiz, borrels, kerst- en zomerfeesten. Jij krijgt van ons voldoende vrijheid om je eigen werkweek in te delen. Wie wordt jouw nieuwe werkgever? De afgelopen 20 jaar heeft delaware veel ervaring opgebouwd in de wereld van ERP, Enterprise Resource Planning. Op dit moment werken we met ongeveer 4600 mensen in 19 verschillende landen. In 2013 zijn wij met succes in Nederland gestart en snel gegroeid naar de huidige 250 medewerkers verspreid over 2 locaties; ’s-Hertogenbosch en Naarden. Zeg je ja tegen delaware! Dan kom je terecht in een warm team met ruim 20 Dynamics collega’s die samen Microsoft Dynamics als nieuw ecosysteem willen toevoegen aan delaware Nederland. Allemaal hebben we hetzelfde doel; er een groot succes van maken! Onze collega’s beschrijven delaware Nederland als een plek waar je veel vrijheid krijgt om te werken aan het gezamenlijke en jouw eigen succes! We zijn géén 100% business Consultants maar ook géén technische consultants, we zijn het beide. We vinden de balans tussen het begeleiden van onze klanten in hun business en digitale transformaties met behulp van IT-oplossingen. Juist deze combinatie maakt consultancy bij delaware net even anders. Het is het delaware-DNA: de ene helft van het DNA bestaat uit consultancy, de andere helft uit technologie. We willen het ook niet anders! Een ding is zeker: onze consultants zijn getalenteerde mensen, klaar om uitdagingen te tackelen met de kracht van technologie. Wij maken het mogelijk. Je kunt op ons vertrouwen. Wij maken het mogelijk. Je kunt op ons vertrouwen. we commit. we deliver. Nieuwsgierig en wil je meer weten? Ga vandaag nog het gesprek aan met onze Recruiter Maxime. Zij vertelt je graag meer over de functie en de organisatie. Maxime is bereikbaar via dlw-nl-recruitment@delaware.pro 088-606 9100 of via WhatsApp +31 620385013 Acquisitie naar aanleiding van deze vacature wordt niet op prijs gesteld. title: Consultant, Functional Transformation SAP
description: Our Deloitte Enterprise Performance team is at the forefront of enterprise technology, working across finance, supply chain, and IT operations to deliver holistic performance improvement and digital transformation. Join our team of strategic advisers and architects, differentiated by our industry depth to collaborate with leading solution providers and leverage your experience in strategy, process design, technology enablement, and operational services to enable heart-of-the-business solutions. Recruiting for this role ends on 08/31/2026. Work you'll do As a Consultant, Functional Transformation on the GPS SAP business transformation team, you will be responsible for supporting SAP-enabled transformation initiatives across the implementation lifecycle. Support SAP implementation and modernization efforts, including SAP S/4HANA programs and ECC-to-S/4HANA transformation activities. Configure SAP functionality to support business process requirements and fit-to-standard design decisions. Document requirements, assumptions, risks, issues, and decisions in collaboration with business, technical, integration, and data teams. Support process design workshops, integrated testing, and deployment activities, including unit testing, system integration testing, user acceptance testing, cutover, and stabilization. Develop, maintain, and govern implementation deliverables, including solution design documents, configuration rationale, test scripts, cutover inputs, deployment readiness materials, and status updates. A successful candidate would possess these skills: Ability to work independently and collaborate as part of a team Effective written and verbal communication skills Meticulous attention to detail and quality of work product Ability to build and sustain professional relationships Ability to lead projects or workstreams Ability to manage and prioritize multiple tasks in a fast-paced and dynamic environment Strong interpersonal skills and professional demeanor Ability to meet deadlines Ability to provide clear guidance to others The team Deloitte's Government & Public Services (GPS) practice - our people, ideas, technology and outcomes - is designed for impact. Serving federal, state, & local government clients as well as public higher education institutions, our team of professionals brings fresh perspective to help clients anticipate disruption, reimagine the possible, and fulfill their mission promise. Our SAP business transformation services drive to improve performance and value delivered by the full suite of SAP solutions. Qualifications Required: Bachelor's degree and ability to obtain and maintain a Secret Clearance 2+ years of experience working in regulated environments with enterprise resource planning controls, audit readiness, and compliance requirements 2+ years of experience producing or governing implementation deliverables, including solution design documentation, configuration rationale, test artifacts, cutover plans, and deployment readiness materials 1+ years of experience delivering SAP implementations, including SAP S/4HANA transformation work 1+ years of experience configuring SAP S/4HANA and/or SAP ECC Ability to travel 25%, on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sectors you serve. Must be legally authorized to work in the United States without the need for employer sponsorship, now or at any time in the future. Preferred: Active Secret Clearance Experience supporting Department of Defense and/or U.S. Federal civilian agency enterprise resource planning modernization programs The wage range for this role takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; licensure and certifications; and other business and organizational needs. The disclosed range estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the position may be filled. At Deloitte, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is $82,600 to $162,800. You may also be eligible to participate in a discretionary annual incentive program, subject to the rules governing the program, whereby an award, if any, depends on various factors, including, without limitation, individual and organizational performance. title: Indirect Procurement
description: About The Business Tata Electronics Private Limited (TEPL) is a greenfield venture of the Tata Group with expertise in manufacturing precision components. Tata Electronics (a wholly owned subsidiary of Tata Sons Pvt. Ltd.) is building India’s first AI-enabled state-of-the-art Semiconductor Foundry. This facility will produce chips for applications such as power management IC, display drivers, microcontrollers (MCU) and high-performance computing logic, addressing the growing demand in markets such as automotive, computing and data storage, wireless communications and artificial intelligence. Tata Electronics is a subsidiary of the Tata group. The Tata Group operates in more than 100 countries across six continents, with the mission 'To improve the quality of life of the communities we serve globally, through long term stakeholder value creation based on leadership with Trust.’ Job Responsibilities Manage end-to-end Procure-to-Pay (P2P) operations, including requisition validation, purchase order processing, invoice coordination, payment follow-ups, and compliance adherence. Leverage SAP MM and SAP Ariba to drive process efficiency through automation, spend analytics, and effective PR, PO, and invoice management. Drive cost control, supplier performance tracking, and close coordination with internal teams for smooth operations. Create and process purchase orders while ensuring timely approvals in line with organizational policies. Ensure prompt PO acknowledgements from suppliers and maintain accurate delivery schedules within SAP. Proactively engage suppliers to identify and mitigate risks impacting on‑time delivery. Expedite critical orders and manage procurement exceptions to minimize supply disruptions. Collaborate with Logistics and Warehouse teams for timely material receipts and inventory availability. title: Warehouse Specialist
description: Vacancy – Earthmoving - Warehouse Keeper (Netherlands) About us Zoomlion is a leading global manufacturer of construction and industrial machinery, active in more than 100 countries. With a strong presence in Europe and a subsidiary in the Netherlands, we provide innovative solutions in forklifts, concrete pumps, tractors and other heavy equipment. Job Responsibilities (1) Warehouse operations execution Be responsible for the receiving, inspection, shelving, picking, in&outbound, verification and packaging of spare parts. Strictly follow the standard operating procedures (SOP) to ensure the accuracy and efficiency of operations. Assist in conducting inventory checks to ensure consistency between the accounts and the actual situation. Operate the warehouse management system (WMS and LTC) to handle order and inventory data. Be responsible for the inbound and outbound management of the earthmoving machines and perform corresponding operations on the system based on the actual business situation. (2) Site management and maintenance Maintain the 5S standards in the warehouse, keep the environment clean and tidy, and arrange items in order. Operate common warehouse equipment (such as forklifts, pallet trucks, etc.) to ensure safe operation. Work with the logistics specialist to sign for incoming goods, hand over goods for outgoing goods and prepare for shipment. (3) Collaboration and Support Assist the warehouse supervisor in optimizing warehouse layout and picking routes. Participate in warehouse process improvement and training activities to enhance the overall operational level. Qualifications Educational Background: High school education or above, logistics, warehousing related major preferred. Work experience: More than 1 year of warehouse work experience, with experience in construction machinery or parts warehousing preferred. Professional skills: Familiar with warehouse operation processes, familiar with warehousing systems, with basic equipment operation certificates. Language proficiency: Basic English communication skills (required). Local language proficiency preferred. Overall Quality: Hardworking, careful, responsible, teamwork and safety awareness. Kindly email your CV to: sedef.demirag@zoomlion-zitc.com Subject: Application for Earthmoving Warehouse Keeper (Netherlands) vacancy. title: Junior SAP Integration Developer (m/w/d)
description: Cloud Integration | SAP BTP | Berufseinstieg Standort: Dortmund | Vollzeit Über KPS KPS zählt zu den führenden Beratungen im DACH-Raum, wenn es um digitale Transformation und Prozessoptimierung geht. Als spezialisierte Management- und Technologieberatung gestalten wir zukunftssichere Lösungen – mit klarem Fokus auf Unternehmen im Handel und Konsumgüterbereich. Mit unserer Kombination aus strategischer Beratung, tiefgreifender SAP-Expertise und eigenen KPS-Assets unterstützen wir Unternehmen dabei, Transformationen schneller und strukturierter umzusetzen. Unsere erprobten Methoden und Accelerators helfen dabei, komplexe SAP-Programme effizient zu gestalten – von der Strategie bis zur erfolgreichen Umsetzung. Unsere rund 800 Mitarbeitenden sind in Deutschland, Spanien, Großbritannien, Belgien und Skandinavien aktiv. In Deutschland findest du uns an unseren Standorten in München (HQ), Hamburg und Dortmund. Gestalte die Zukunft der SAP-Integration mit uns. Du möchtest nach deinem Studium nicht nur Code schreiben, sondern verstehen, wie moderne Unternehmen ihre SAP-, Cloud- und Drittsysteme miteinander verbinden? Dann bist du bei uns genau richtig. Wir bauen unser Kompetenzteam für SAP Integration & Cloud Technologies weiter aus und suchen motivierte Berufseinsteiger:innen, die gemeinsam mit erfahrenen Consultants in ein zukunftsweisendes Themenfeld einsteigen möchten. Dabei arbeitest du mit der SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration/CPI) , APIs und modernen Cloud-Technologien. Gleichzeitig erhältst du spannende Einblicke in den Einsatz von KI innerhalb moderner Integrationsarchitekturen , beispielsweise durch SAP AI Core. Von Anfang an arbeitest du an echten Kundenprojekten, übernimmst Verantwortung und entwickelst dich Schritt für Schritt zum SAP Integration Consultant. title: SAP Technology Architect (all genders)

title: Senior SAP Analyst
description: Job descriptions may display in multiple languages based on your language selection. What We Offer At Magna, you can expect an engaging and dynamic environment where you can help to develop industry-leading automotive technologies. We invest in our employees, providing them with the support and resources they need to succeed. As a member of our global team, you can expect exciting, varied responsibilities as well as a wide range of development prospects. Because we believe that your career path should be as unique as you are. Group Summary The Mechatronics, Mirrors and Lighting group specializes in automotive technologies that are driving the future of mobility. Combining a deep systems knowledge to develop unique vehicle access experiences, intelligent visions systems and advanced automotive lighting technologies, MML's expertise light the path to innovation, safety and styling. Job Responsibilities Scope of Position Manages all aspects of SAP integration, systems development, process improvements, and process development to support business requirements, operational efficiency, and continuous improvement initiatives. Minimum Requirements Knowledge of SAP Production Planning and Inventory Management and Warehouse Management. Knowledge of SAP Materials Management and Quality Management and Sales and Distribution Knowledge of SAP Financial Accounting and Controlling and Asset Accounting Knowledge of SAP Basis and EDI Knowledge of SAP P2P and B2B and workflow Knowledge of SAP reporting capabilities and background data tables. Experience with Business Warehouse. Associates Degree preferred Job Specific Responsibilities Take the lead for ERP on delivering the material control project. Utilize SAP RF functionality and deliver material requirements to the assembly point of need. Work with project team to ensure that SAP has been updated with all data required and that new processes are documented and trained to SAP users Manage integrated project management systems interfaces with SAP Work with the CAPP (Computer Aided Planning Process) and MES (Manufacturing Execution Systems) project leads, manage the linkages required with SAP Take the lead for ERP on delivering inbound and outbound transportation strategy SAP tracking of internal deliveries from vendors and outbound shipments to customers. Work with project team and ERP Center of Competence, ensure SAP has been updated with all data required and that new processes are documented and trained to SAP users. Act as MRP Controller Super User Provide SAP and process training to end users. Ensure all relevant updates are passed to the MRPC community Provide cross functional SAP reporting capabilities. Utilizing existing Business Warehouse reporting tools and acting as the power user, create reports, provide the business with the method of understanding its current position and monitoring ERP compliance. Lead SAP transfer request among SAP Landscapes Coordinate EDI mapping with Magna B2B Team Vendor Master data creation and maintenance Responsible for Employee creation as vendor Coordinate creation and change of BOM, Rate Routing, activity price, production version, and cost collector Coordinate and support quality inspection plan creation and dynamic modification rule Support materials and production team on HUs and Serial numbers Maintain SAP users according to Magna IT policies Support shipping team with sales and EDI requirements Responsible to post Scrap in SAP Support purchase team on purchase requisitions, purchase orders and schedule agreements Coordinate Annual physical inventory in SAP Support Finance on costing analyses, accounting analyses and standard price Support Accounts payable and receivable Global Leadership Competencies Leading Self Think Strategically: Sees and considers different sides of an issue, examining the full range of options and outcomes, and building intellectual frameworks to guide analysis and action. Act Decisively: Uses critical thinking, good business sense and Magna’s values to make decisions involving risk and ambiguity. Champion Change: Recognizes the need for change, be open to new ideas and methods, and champion transformational change within Magna and beyond. Lead the Team: Assumes leadership role in helping others achieve excellent results. Inspire Others: Energizes and inspires others to strive for excellence and commit to common goals and purposes, creating a sense of self-efficacy, resilience, and persistence in followers. Think Top and Bottom Line – Understanding the Business: Understands how Magna makes money and what drives profitability. Prioritize Achievement: Focuses efforts on achieving high quality results consistent with Magna’s standards. Be Agile and Adapt: Adapts and thrives in volatile, ambiguous and changing environments by anticipating and responding to changes. General Responsibilities Actively and positively support the continued practice of Magna’s Core Values. Must be flexible in fulfilling job responsibilities. Must be able to work overtime and weekends, when required. Initiate and embrace company change. Comply and participate with the Company’s Continuous Improvement program. Health and Safety Responsibilities Observe standards for safe working conditions as outlined in the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) as well as rules and regulations outlined in the employee handbook. According to OSHA Section 1960 (10) (a) (b) (c) (d): Each employee shall comply with the standards, rules, regulations, and orders issued by his/her agency in accordance with section 19 of the Act, Executive Order 12196, and this part which are applicable to his/her own actions and conduct. Employees shall use safety equipment, personal protective equipment, and other devices and procedures provided or directed by the agency and necessary for their protection. Employees shall have the right to report unsafe and unhealthful working conditions to appropriate officials. Employees shall be authorized official time to participate in the activities provided for in section 19 of the Act, Executive Order 12196, this part, and the agency occupational safety and health program. Physical Demands Normal amount of sitting or standing, average mobility to move around an office and plant environment, able to conduct normal amount of work at a computer, requires travel. Training Orientation New Employee Human Resources Orientation New Employee Health Safety & Environmental Orientation Compliance Level 2 Introduction to Ergonomics Introduction to CNC Machining / MasterTask Quality Systems and Gaging Mirrors Benefits Orientation Mafact Modules 1-8 Operational Development 5S Workshop: Workplace Organization & Standardization Dawn Raid Overview of Automotive Manufacturing SAP Training Value Stream Mapping (VSM) Awareness, Unity, Empowerment At Magna, we believe that a diverse workforce is critical to our success. That’s why we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We hire on the basis of experience and qualifications, and in consideration of job requirements, regardless of, in particular, color, ancestry, religion, gender, origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability or gender identity. Magna takes the privacy of your personal information seriously. We discourage you from sending applications via email or traditional mail to comply with GDPR requirements and your local Data Privacy Law. AI-Assisted Screening Disclosure As part of our commitment to a fair, consistent, and efficient recruitment process, we may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to assist in the initial screening of applications submitted through our Workday system. These tools help identify qualifications and experience that align with the role requirements. Please note that AI is used solely to support our recruiters. Final decisions are always made by the hiring manager and the hiring team. Importantly, no applicant data is shared externally through these AI tools. All information remains securely within our systems and is handled in accordance with our privacy and data protection policies. Under conditions defined by applicable law, you may have the right to request an explanation of how AI is used to support decision-making. If you have any questions or concerns about this process, feel free to contact our Talent Attraction team. Worker Type Regular / Permanent Group Magna Mechatronics, Mirrors & Lighting Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Full-time Job function Information Technology Industries Motor Vehicle Manufacturing Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Magna International by 2x See who you know

description: 👋 Você busca um lugar para aprender e contribuir? Venha fazer parte do time Leo Madeiras. 🏢 A Leo Madeiras é uma das maiores distribuidoras de produtos e serviços para marcenaria do Brasil, que valoriza as pessoas e oferece oportunidades de aprendizado, independentemente de sua condição física, mental, social, idade, etnia, identidade de gênero, credo ou orientação sexual. Para nós, o mais importante é que seu perfil esteja alinhado aos nossos princípios e cultura. 🚀 O Perfil que Buscamos: Buscamos profissionais comprometidos, proativos e com vontade de fazer acontecer no dia a dia da operação. Responsabilidades e atribuições Realizar o descarregamento de mercadorias seguindo os procedimentos estabelecidos; Conferir materiais recebidos de fornecedores internos e externos, comparando notas fiscais e produtos físicos; Armazenar, separar e organizar produtos, garantindo a integridade dos itens em estoque; Apoiar na movimentação e armazenagem de ferragens, MDF, MDP, máquinas, químicos e demais produtos; Participar de inventários e contagens de estoque; Sinalizar não conformidades encontradas nos processos de entrada, armazenagem, saída e inventário; Realizar reposição de produtos nos endereços de picking (autosserviço); Efetuar entrada de notas fiscais no sistema SAP; Seguir normas, procedimentos e instruções de trabalho definidos pela empresa. Requisitos e qualificações Ensino Médio Completo; Conhecimento básico em Pacote Office; Experiência anterior na função; Perfil organizado, responsável e atento aos detalhes; Facilidade para trabalhar em equipe; Proatividade e disposição para atuar em rotina operacional. Informações adicionais Aqui na Leo, acreditamos que cuidar das pessoas faz parte do nosso jeito de crescer juntos. Por isso, oferecemos um pacote de benefícios pensado para proporcionar mais qualidade de vida, bem-estar e desenvolvimento para você e sua família. 💚 🚀 Participação nos Resultados (PLR) Quando a Leo cresce, você cresce junto! Nosso programa de remuneração variável é baseado no Lucro Líquido da empresa e na sua performance individual. 🍽️ Alimentação & Refeição Restaurante Corporativo | Vale Refeição: Varia de acordo com as políticas internas de acordo com área/setor. Vale Alimentação: Via cartão 🩺 Saúde em primeiro lugar Convênio Médico: A Leo custeia: 100% do plano do colaborador, 100% do plano para filhos de até 16 anos Inclusão de cônjuge/parceiro(a) e dependentes a partir de 17 anos mediante coparticipação mensal. Convênio Odontológico: Com possibilidade de inclusão de dependentes e agregados. 🛡️ Seguro de Vida Mais tranquilidade e proteção para você e sua família. 💊 Convênio Farmácia Medicamentos com desconto e praticidade direto na folha de pagamento. 🏋️ Wellhub Acesso a academias e atividades de bem-estar a partir do 2º mês de empresa. 🚗 Mobilidade Vale Transporte / Estacionamento 📚 Desenvolvimento Contínuo Acesso à plataforma de cursos online para apoiar seu crescimento profissional e pessoal. 🎁 Extras que fazem a diferença! Aqui, valorizamos os momentos importantes da vida 💛 📚 Kit Material Escolar Para filhos de colaboradores entre 4 e 16 anos. 👶 Kit Maternidade Um carinho especial para celebrar a chegada de um novo membro da família. 🎄 Kit Natal Cesta especial para a família e brinquedos para crianças de até 12 anos. ✈️ Parcerias & Descontos Convênios com instituições de ensino, lazer e viagens. title: Senior SAP Finance Consultant (FI/CO Rollout)
description: About Us Gramian Consultancy is a boutique consultancy specializing in IT professional services and engineering talent solutions. With a strong background in engineering and leadership, we help companies build high-performing teams by matching them with professionals who truly fit their needs. About The Role We are looking for a senior SAP FI/CO Consultant to support the preparation of an SAP finance rollout in Germany. The role will focus on scoping, Fit/Gap analysis, business workshops, Core Model adaptation, SAP FI/CO customizing, testing support, and rollout planning . This position is suited for an experienced SAP finance consultant with strong knowledge of SAP ECC6 , international template rollouts, FI/CO configuration, intercompany processes, financial reporting, and German accounting requirements. CONTRACT: Freelance / contractor assignment LOCATIONS: Hybrid, Düsseldorf region, Germany NOTES: German accounting knowledge is important. English is mandatory; German is a strong advantage. Responsibilities Analyze financial processes at the German manufacturing site Identify gaps between local requirements and the existing SAP ECC6 Core Model Prepare and facilitate SAP FI/CO business workshops Define required adaptations for the Core Model rollout Perform SAP FI/CO customizing across relevant finance areas Support testing, validation, and rollout preparation activities Participate in scoping, estimation, and deployment planning Contribute to the commercial proposal and rollout strategy Ensure German accounting, statutory, localization, and reporting requirements are addressed Requirements 8+ years of experience as an SAP FI/CO Consultant Strong expertise in SAP FI/CO, including FI-GL, AP, AR, AA, and CO Hands-on experience with SAP FI/CO customizing and functional analysis Proven experience with SAP ECC6 Experience with international rollouts, Core Model, or template deployments Experience with Fit/Gap analysis and business workshop facilitation Good understanding of German accounting, statutory reporting, or localization requirements Show more Show less Seniority level Mid-Senior level Employment type Contract Job function Other Industries IT Services and IT Consulting Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Gramian Consulting by 2x See who you know

description: Intetics Inc., a leading American technology company specializing in custom software application development, distributed professional teams creation, software product quality assessment, and "all-things-digital" solutions, is looking for a SAP BW/4HANA Senior Consultant to join its innovative team. title: Master Data Specialist
description: Master Data Specialist – Onsite East Longmeadow, MA, USA Come build something that matters. It takes great people to achieve greatness. People with a sense of purpose and integrity. People with a relentless pursuit of excellence. People who care about making things better For Those Who Make The World™. Sound like you? Join our top-notch team of approximately 43,500 diverse and high-performing professionals globally who are making their mark on some of the world’s most beloved brands, including DEWALT®, BLACK+DECKER®, CRAFTSMAN®, STANLEY®, CUB CADET®, and HUSTLER®. The Job: As a Master Data Specialist, you’ll be part of our East Longmeadow, MA team working as an on-site employee. You’ll get to: Responsible for administrating plant-level engineering changes and updating the manufacturing ERP system (SAP) to accommodate those changes. Requires interaction with multiple functions within a manufacturing environment to establish and implement master data critical to day to day running of the business. Be a hands-on expert in SAP and proficient with SAP functions such as: Create Material Masters. Change, extend, and obsolete part numbers using Change Masters. Create Bills of Materials (BOMs) and Alternate BOMs. Calculate BOM quantities. Maintain BOM yields per instructions from Finance. Create and maintain BOMs for OSP/SUBC (Outside Processing). Create and maintain Production Versions (PVs). Create and maintain Routes as well as alternate routes. Work with finance and manufacturing engineering to establish and maintain work centers. Manage part discontinuation (NOD). Maintain documents to support the building of parts and BOMs. Maintain documents to support SAP process rates. Monitor plant-level “Temporary Change” documents for potential changes to processes and process rates. Work with Manufacturing Engineers to conduct audits on processes to capture changed process rates. Administer plant-level Engineering Changes (LECNs) for the East Longmeadow (ELM) plant. LECNs control ANY plant-level changes made to the product or process. LECNs are initiated by product changes that arise from a SBD Corporate Change Notice (CN) or any other product or process change that is initiated by a Product Engineer, Manufacturing Engineer, or Product Manager via the plant-level Engineering Change Request (LECR) system. Develop and implement an alternative way to manage LECR/LECN document workflow. A manual system via email is a possible option. The Person: You love to learn and grow and be acknowledged for your valuable contributions. You’re not intimidated by innovation. Wouldn’t it be great if you could do your job and do a world of good? In fact, you embrace it. You also have: Bachelor’s degree preferred. More than 3 years’ experience working with SAP in a factory floor environment. Excellent mathematical skills. Experience doing Time Studies and setting Work Standards is a plus. Microsoft Office proficiency. Good English written and oral communication skills. The Details: You’ll receive a competitive salary and a great benefits plan, including: Medical, dental, life, vision, disability, 401(k), Employee Stock Purchase Plan, paid time off, and tuition reimbursement in addition to programs & benefits in support of your well-being. Discounts on Stanley Black & Decker tools and other partner programs. And More: We want our company to be a place you’ll want to be – and stay. Being part of our team means you’ll get to: Grow: Be part of our global company with 20+ brands to grow and develop your skills along multiple career paths. Learn: Have access to a wealth of learning resources, including our digital learning portal. Belong: Experience an awesome place to work, where we have mutual respect and a great appreciation for a wide range of perspectives and experiences. Give Back: Help us continue to make positive changes locally and globally through volunteerism, giving back, and sustainable business practices. What’s more, you’ll get that pride that comes from empowering makers, doers, protectors, and everyday heroes all over the world. We’re more than the #1 tools company. More than a driving force in outdoor power equipment. More than a global leader in industrial. We’re visionaries and innovators. As successful as we’ve been in the past, we have so much further to go. That’s where you come in. Join us! This role is not eligible for visa sponsorship (H1B, OPT/CPT, TN etc). The Salary range for this position is $62,200.00 - $100,200.00 This is the lowest to highest annual full-time salary range we would pay for this role at the time of this posting. The salary offered within the range may vary depending on multiple factors including job level, geographic location, job-related knowledge, skills, qualifications, experience and in compliance with local wage requirements. Please note that salary is only one component of total rewards at Stanley Black & Decker. The salary range listed in this job posting reflects the range for the primary location specified. The salary range may vary for other locations. The successful candidate may be eligible for annual merit increases. Medical, dental, life, vision, disability,401(k), Employee Stock Purchase Plan, paid time off, and tuition reimbursement in addition to programs & benefits in support of your well-being. Discounts on Stanley Black & Decker tool sand other partner programs. We Don’t Just Build The World, We Build Innovative Technology Too. Joining the Stanley Black & Decker team means working in an innovative, tech-driven and highly collaborative team environment supported by over 43,500 professionals in 60 countries across the globe. Here, you’ll get the unique chance to impact some of the world’s most iconic brands including STANLEY TOOLS, DEWALT, CRAFTSMAN, MAC TOOLS and Black + Decker. Your ideas and solutions have the potential to reach millions of customers as we work together to write the next chapter in our history. Come build with us and take your career to new heights. Who We Are We’re the World’s largest tool company. We’re industry visionaries. We’re solving problems and advancing the manufacturing trade through innovative technology and our Industry 4.0 Initiative. We are committed to ensuring our state-of-the-art “smart factory” products and services provide greater quality to our customers & greater environmental and social value to our planet. We are unique in that we have a rich and storied history dating back to 1843, but that hasn't stopped us from evolving into a vibrant, diverse, global growth company. What You’ll Also Get Career Opportunity: Career paths aren’t linear here. Being part of our global company with 60+ brands gives you the chance to grow and develop your skills along multiple career paths. Benefits & Perks You’ll get a competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits plan that includes medical, dental, life, vision, wellness program, disability, retirement benefits, Employee Stock Purchase Plan, Paid Time Off, including paid vacation, holidays & personal days, and tuition reimbursement. And, of course, discounts on Stanley Black & Decker tools and products and well as discount programs for many other vendors and partners. Learning & Development: Our lifelong learning philosophy means you’ll have access to a wealth of state-of-the-art learning resources, including our Lean Academy and online university (where you can get certificates and specializations from renowned colleges and universities). Diverse & Inclusive Culture: We pride ourselves on being an awesome place to work. We respect and embrace differences because that’s how the best work gets done. You’ll find we like to have fun here, too. Purpose-Driven Company: You’ll help us continue to make positive changes in the local communities where we work and live as well as in the broader world through volunteerism, giving back and sustainable business practices. EEO Statement: All qualified applicants to Stanley Black & Decker are considered for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran’s status or any other protected characteristic.
