title: Senior SAP Technology Consultant - Banking (f/m/d)
description: Über SAP Fioneer Innovation ist und bleibt das Herzstück von SAP Fioneer - und genau deshalb wurden wir aus SAP ausgegründet: für mehr Agilität, Innovationskraft und konsequente Umsetzung. Wir bauen auf einer starken technologischen Basis und einem tiefen Verständnis für die Bedürfnisse von Unternehmen und Endkund:innen. Unser Ziel ist klar: Wir bringen Finanzdienstleistungen mit innovativen Softwarelösungen und Plattformen auf das nächste Level. Wir helfen Unternehmen der Finanzbranche, Geschwindigkeit, Skalierbarkeit und Kosteneffizienz zu erreichen - durch digitale Innovation, Cloud-Technologien und Lösungen, die Bank- und Versicherungsprozesse ganzheitlich abdecken. Als global wachsendes Unternehmen mit innovativen Menschen und schlanken Strukturen ist SAP Fioneer ein Ort, an dem du deine Zukunft beschleunigen kannst! Über die Rolle Wir suchen dich als SAP Technology Consultant (m/w/d) mit praktischer Erfahrung im SAP-Technologie-Stack - entweder in der ABAP-/Backend- oder in der Fiori-/Frontend-Entwicklung. Wenn du Lust hast, dein Fachwissen zu erweitern und in unsere Banking-Lösungen einzutauchen - z. B. Transactional Banking, Payments Centralization oder Virtual Account Management - bist du bei uns genau richtig. Egal, ob du lieber Lösungen entwickelst oder technische Architekturen entwirfst und Kund:innen berätst - diese Rolle bietet dir die Flexibilität für beides. Mit deiner fundierten technischen Basis unterstützt du unsere Kund:innen dabei, unsere Lösungen erfolgreich einzuführen und zu nutzen. Als SAP Technology Consultant bei SAP Fioneer spielst du eine Schlüsselrolle dabei, moderne Bankanwendungen mit SAP-Technologien zu gestalten, umzusetzen und zu optimieren. Deine Aufgaben Du entwirfst Lösungsarchitekturen, erstellst Aufwandsabschätzungen und technische Blueprints Du präsentierst und erklärst verschiedene Designoptionen und empfiehlst die beste Vorgehensweise gemäß unserer Architektur- und Produktstandards Du konfigurierst unsere Produkte entsprechend den Kundenanforderungen, indem du Geschäftsprozesse auf unsere Software abbildest Du arbeitest eng mit unseren Produktentwicklungsteams und Kund:innen zusammen, um neue Funktionen und Lösungen zu entwickeln Du unterstützt die Integration unserer Lösungen in die Systemlandschaften und technischen Umgebungen der Kund:innen Du wirkst bei der Modernisierung von Kundeninfrastrukturen mit, z. B. durch die Transformation von On-Premise-Systemen in cloudbasierte Modelle Du bleibst am Puls der Zeit, was aktuelle SAP-Technologien und -Methoden betrifft, und treibst so kontinuierlich Innovation und Verbesserung voran Requirements Du hast mindestens 5 Jahre Erfahrung in der SAP-Entwicklung, im Technologie-Consulting oder in der SAP-Technischen Architektur Du verfügst über fundierte ABAP-Kenntnisse und ein solides Verständnis funktionaler und objektorientierter Programmierparadigmen, Datenstrukturen und Algorithmen Erfahrung im Design und in der Umsetzung von Backend- und/oder Webanwendungen nach etablierten SAP-Standards Du bist vertraut mit SAP Basis und Technologie und hast idealerweise Erfahrung mit Integrationen in Tools wie SAP Solution Manager, SAP ChaRM, SAP CTS/CTS+ oder SAP Transport Management Du hast Kenntnisse moderner SAP-Technologien wie SAP BTP, RAP oder SAP Edge Integration Cell Du verstehst die Unterschiede und Einsatzmöglichkeiten von On-Premise- und Cloud-Deployment-Modellen sowie deren Integrationsszenarien Du bringst Erfahrung in der Software-Qualitätssicherung mit - von Unit Tests über Debugging bis zur Problemlösung Du sprichst fließend Deutsch und Englisch Erste Kenntnisse in der UI-/Webentwicklung mit SAPUI5, Fiori, HTML5, CSS und JavaScript sind ein Plus Benefits Du arbeitest mit einigen der klügsten Köpfe der Branche an einem ambitionierten Projekt, das den Finanzsektor von morgen prägt. Bei SAP Fioneer findest du das Beste aus zwei Welten: die Agilität, Begeisterung und Dynamik eines Start-ups - kombiniert mit der etablierten Expertise, der starken Marktpräsenz und dem umfangreichen Kundennetzwerk eines erfolgreichen Unternehmens. Wir bieten dir ein flexibles Arbeitsumfeld, das Kreativität fördert und dich ermutigt, querzudenken, neue Ideen einzubringen und den Status quo herauszufordern. Du wirst Teil eines vielfältigen, globalen Teams, auf das wir stolz sind und das kontinuierlich wächst und sich neu erfindet. Dabei hast du die Möglichkeit, deine Karriere aktiv zu gestalten und von attraktiven Vergütungspaketen sowie Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten auf Basis deiner Leistung zu profitieren. Darüber hinaus erwarten dich umfassende Benefits: eine betriebliche Altersvorsorge, Angebote zur Gesundheitsförderung, verschiedene Mobilitätsoptionen wie Bike-Leasing oder Fahrtkostenzuschüsse sowie zusätzliche Extras wie Prämien für besondere Anlässe, Essensprogramme, Jubiläumsgeschenke und Unterstützung bei Umzügen. title: SAP MM Consultant
description: Freelance SAP MM P2P Consultant (Germany-speaking - Remote with Onsite Travel) ASAP Start 6-Month Initial Contract + Extensions German + English-Speaking Project We are looking for an experienced Freelance SAP MM P2P Consultant based in Europe to join a major international SAP S/4HANA transformation programme. This role is primarily remote , with occasional onsite presence in Germany required depending on project phases and business needs. The project language is English and German . About the Role As an SAP MM P2P Consultant, you will play a key role in delivering SAP S/4HANA Source-to-Pay processes, ensuring alignment between global templates and German local business requirements. You will collaborate with cross-functional SAP teams to support procurement and accounts payable processes throughout the implementation lifecycle. Key Responsibilities Coordinate business requirements workshops, functional specifications, functional unit testing, and User Acceptance Testing (UAT). Configure and support SAP MM Procure-to-Pay processes within an SAP S/4HANA environment. Support integration with SAP Ariba and OpenText VIM. Assist with data migration, cutover planning, and hypercare activities. Ensure compliance with German VAT, e-invoicing, and audit regulations. Align the German rollout with the global SAP template while addressing local business requirements. Collaborate with multiple SAP workstreams across parallel project releases. Deliver knowledge transfer and training to business and IT teams. Requirements Must Have 10+ years of SAP consulting experience. At least two full-cycle SAP S/4HANA implementation projects within the Source-to-Pay (P2P) stream. Strong expertise in SAP MM, Procure-to-Pay processes, MM-FI integration, and Accounts Payable. Experience with SAP S/4HANA. Strong understanding of German VAT, e-invoicing (XRechnung/ZUGFeRD), and regulatory compliance. Fluent English, both spoken and written. Based in Europe and able to travel onsite when required. Ability to work independently in a fast-paced international project environment. Nice to Have Experience with SAP Ariba and OpenText VIM. Knowledge of SAP Workflow and SAP Fiori applications for Accounts Payable. Experience with Central Finance or multi-entity rollout projects. Knowledge of OCR/ICR solutions integrated with OpenText VIM. German language skills are mandatory. SAP certification in relevant modules. Education Bachelor's or Master's degree in IT, Finance, Business, or a related field. Contract Details Type: Freelance / Contractor Duration: 6-month initial contract Extension: High likelihood of extension Location: Remote, with occasional onsite travel in Germany Language: English and German Start Date: ASAP What We Offer Long-term SAP S/4HANA transformation programme. Flexible remote working with limited onsite travel. Opportunity to work on a high-profile international project. Collaborative environment with global SAP experts. Flexible freelance engagement with excellent extension potential. If you're a Germany-speaking SAP MM P2P Consultant looking for your next freelance opportunity, we'd love to hear from you. title: Senior SAP S/4HANA Consultant MM (m/w/d)
description: Über uns gicom steht seit über 25 Jahren für exzellente Lösungen im SAP-Umfeld. Als langjähriger Partner der SAP entwickeln wir innovative Software und begleiten unsere Kunden aus dem Handel und der Konsumgüterindustrie bei der digitalen Transformation. Mit dem Ausbau unserer Beratungskompetenz im SAP Standard, insbesondere im Bereich SAP S/4HANA , bieten wir Berater:innen die Möglichkeit, aktiv mitzugestalten und schnell Verantwortung zu übernehmen – in einem wachsenden, agilen Team mit viel Gestaltungsspielraum. Klingt spannend? Dann kommen Sie zu uns als: Senior SAP S/4HANA Consultant MM (m/w/d) in Teil- oder Vollzeit IHRE ROLLE Als SAP S/4HANA Consultant MM beraten Sie unsere nationalen und internationalen Kunden bei der Optimierung ihrer Einkaufs- und Vertriebsprozesse. Sie bringen Ihr Know-how und Ihre Ideen ein, gestalten innovative Lösungen und entwickeln unsere Kunden – und uns weiter. Ihre Aufgaben im Überblick: Beratung und Betreuung unserer Kunden aus Retail, Wholesale, Consumer Products & Industry Analyse und Optimierung bestehender Prozesse sowie Ableitung passender SAP-Lösungen Implementierung und Customizing von SAP S/4HANA MM Begleitung der Projekte bis zum Go-Live – und darüber hinaus Ihr Profil Das bringen Sie mit: Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung (mind. 4 Jahre) als SAP Consultant – inhouse oder in der Beratung Sehr gute Kenntnisse im SAP MM inklusive Customizing Ausgeprägte analytische Fähigkeiten, Eigeninitiative und Spaß an kreativen Lösungen Reisebereitschaft – national und international Fließende Deutschkenntnisse (mind. C2) und sehr gutes Englisch Und ganz wichtig: eine Portion Humor und Teamgeist! Warum wir? Weil wir mehr bieten als spannende Projekte: Homeoffice deutschlandweit – flexibel und vertrauensbasiert Attraktives Jahreszieleinkommen: 65.000 € – 100.000 € (brutto, je nach Erfahrung) Firmenwagen-Option ab dem ersten Tag (Elektro oder Verbrenner) Bewährtes Onboarding mit individuellem Einarbeitungsprogramm Bis zu 42 Tage Urlaub Weltweite Workation (ohne Limitierung) & Sabbatical-Möglichkeiten Job-Rad Leasing Pluxee Benefit Pass mit 40 € steuerfreiem Sachbezug monatlich Regelmäßige Events & echtes Teamgefühl Enge Zusammenarbeit mit der SAP SE seit über zwei Jahrzehnten Neugierig geworden? Dann lassen Sie uns sprechen! Laden Sie einfach Ihren Lebenslauf über unser kurzes Bewerbungsformular hoch oder greifen Sie direkt zum Hörer – wir freuen uns auf den Austausch mit Ihnen! Über uns Mit bahnbrechenden SAP-Lösungen revolutionieren wir Verhandlungsprozesse und optimieren SAP-Systeme. Wir sind ein Zuhause für Veränderer, Vorausdenker und Anpacker (m/w/d). Viele spannenden Infos zu uns gibt's auf unserer Homepage und auf unserer Karriereseite! title: Trainee SAP Logistics / Junior Consultant SAP Logistics (w/m/d)
description: Die leogistics GmbH ist führender und global agierender Partner in der Konzeption und Einführung digitaler Softwarelösungen für Logistik und Supply Chain Management. Dabei weisen wir eine besonders hohe Expertise in den Bereichen Transportmanagement, Werks- & Bahnlogistik sowie Lagerlogistik auf. Unsere anerkannte Beratungskompetenz in der Logistik, gepaart mit dem Angebot eigener, innovativer Lösungen, ermöglicht es unseren Kunden, ihre logistischen Prozesse neu zu denken und den Weg hin zu einer vernetzten, digitalisierten Logistik erfolgreich zu gestalten. Du hast Dein Studium erfolgreich abgeschlossen und möchtest nun in der (SAP) Beraterwelt den ersten Schritt auf der Karriereleiter nehmen? Du willst im Rahmen von Kundenprojekten bei namhaften Unternehmen schnell Verantwortung übernehmen und Prozesse gestalten? Dir fällt es leicht, Dich auf unterschiedlichen Unternehmensebenen (bis hin zum C-Level) zu bewegen? Dann ist unser Traineeprogramm in Kooperation mit unserer Muttergesellschaft cbs genau das Richtige für Dich! Wir suchen zum 01.10.2026 für die Verstärkung unseres Beratungsgeschäfts engagierte und hochqualifizierte (Master)-Absolventen (m/w/d) für unsere SAP Extended Warehouse Management (SAP EWM) und SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM) Communities. Du nimmst Vollzeit am viermonatigen Traineeprogramm am Standort Heidelberg teil. Im Anschluss kannst du mit deinem Homeoffice-Arbeitsvertrag dort arbeiten, wo du dich am wohlsten fühlst (freie Standortwahl). Aufgaben Du nimmst am viermonatigen Traineeprogramm am Standort Heidelberg teil. Du begleitest Projekte von der Analyse der Geschäftsprozesse über Implementierung und Schulung bis hin zur Betreuung im Rahmen des Go-Live. Du übernimmst Systemeinstellungen, konzipierst kundenindividuelle Entwicklungen und realisierst diese in Abstimmung mit unserem Entwicklungsteam. Du nimmst an Testphasen teil, einschließlich Systemtests und Integrationstests. Du schulst Endanwender:innen in der Nutzung von SAP TM oder SAP EWM und erstellst Dokumentationen für implementierte Lösungen. Du wirkst aktiv an der Gestaltung von Unternehmens- und Lösungsprozessen mit. Qualifikation Du hast einen Master oder ein Diplom in Wirtschaftsingenieurwesen, -informatik, -wissenschaften, Naturwissenschaften mit Erfolg abgeschlossen und bringst idealerweise Kenntnisse einem der folgenden Fachgebiete mit: Supply Chain Management, Produktion oder Logistik. Du hast eine ausgeprägte Affinität zu Logistik- und IT-Themen. Du hast vielleicht schon erste Erfahrungen im Umgang mit SAP-Lösungen (EWM/WM/TM/ERP) oder Digitalisierung sammeln können. Deine Stärken verortest du in den Bereichen analytischer Fähigkeiten, Kreativität, Eigeninitiative und Kommunikations-/Präsentationsfähigkeit. Du verfügst über Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift. Du bist bereit national und international zu Reisen. Benefits Du arbeitest so, wie es zu Deinem Leben passt, überwiegend im Homeoffice, flexibel und mit der Möglichkeit, unsere Standorte in ganz Deutschland zu nutzen. Du profitierst von flexiblen Arbeitszeiten und einer vernetzten Unternehmensstruktur, die zu einem modernen Projektalltag passt. Du triffst auf ein Team, das Wissen teilt, sich gegenseitig unterstützt und Zusammenarbeit wirklich lebt. Du entwickelst Dich fachlich und persönlich weiter, langfristig, individuell und im Austausch mit erfahrenen Kolleg:innen sowie unseren Expert Communities. Du übernimmst früh Verantwortung und bewegst Dich in einem Umfeld mit kurzen Entscheidungswegen und echtem Gestaltungsspielraum. Du bekommst das Beste aus zwei Welten, die Agilität eines dynamischen Unternehmens und die Sicherheit einer starken Muttergesellschaft. Du profitierst von attraktiven Benefits wie einem monatlichen Mobilitätsbudget, das Du flexibel für Deine private Mobilität nutzen kannst, Fitnessangeboten, Fahrrad und Hardware Leasing sowie Mitarbeiter:innenrabatten. Du erlebst ein Miteinander, das auch außerhalb des Projektalltags verbindet, bei Sommerfesten, Weihnachtsfeiern, Radtouren und weiteren gemeinsamen Events. title: Assistant Manager - Accounts Payable
description: Overview Build The Future At McGraw Hill, our Finance & Accounting teams play a critical role in driving business operations through strong financial controls, accurate reporting, and efficient accounting processes. We are looking for a detail-oriented and hands-on finance professional to join our Finance & Accounts team and support key accounting operations, compliance activities, and financial processes. How can you make an impact? As our new Assistant Manager – Accounts Payable , you will be responsible for managing core finance operations including Accounts Payable, General Ledger accounting, reconciliations, taxation compliance, month-end close activities, and audit support. You will work closely with internal stakeholders, finance leadership, vendors, service providers, and banking partners to ensure accurate accounting, timely financial operations, and strong process controls. What you will be doing: Accounts Payable & Vendor Management You will manage end-to-end Accounts Payable operations including invoice processing, vendor reconciliation, payment coordination, and resolution of accounting discrepancies. You will ensure accurate application of GST, TDS, and statutory requirements while maintaining compliance with payment timelines. General Ledger & Accounting Operations You will support general accounting activities including journal entries, accruals, provisions, prepayments, and monthly ledger reviews. You will perform balance sheet reconciliations and ensure financial records remain accurate and audit-ready. Tax Compliance & Controls You will support TDS compliance, tax reconciliations, statutory requirements, and audit documentation. You will work closely with internal teams to ensure timely closure of tax-related activities. Month-End Close & Audit Support You will support monthly, quarterly, and annual financial closing activities by ensuring accurate recording of expenses, preparing reconciliations, and supporting statutory, tax, and internal audits. Stakeholder Management & Process Improvement You will collaborate with internal teams, outsourced finance partners, vendors, and banking stakeholders to resolve issues, improve processes, and drive operational efficiency. We're looking for someone with: Bachelor’s degree in Commerce, Finance, Accounting, or related field CA Inter / ICWA Inter / MBA Finance / M.Com qualification preferred 7–10 years of experience in Finance & Accounting operations Strong experience in Accounts Payable, General Ledger, reconciliations, and month-end close processes Good understanding of Indian taxation including TDS and GST compliance Experience handling vendor reconciliations and payment processes Experience supporting audits and financial reporting activities Strong knowledge of accounting principles and financial controls Experience working with ERP systems such as Oracle, IP1, SAP, or similar platforms Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel Strong analytical, problem-solving, and stakeholder management skills Key Skills: Accounts Payable (AP) General Ledger (GL) Accounting Month-End Closing Vendor Reconciliation Balance Sheet Reconciliation TDS & GST Compliance Audit Support Journal Entries Accruals & Provisions ERP Finance Systems Oracle ERP / Accounting Systems Financial Controls Process Improvement Preferred Experience: Experience in publishing, manufacturing, FMCG, shared services, or structured corporate environments Exposure to multi-currency accounting Experience managing ERP-driven finance operations Experience coordinating with outsourced finance service providers Why McGraw Hill? There has never been a better time to join McGraw Hill. In our culture of curiosity, collaboration, and innovation, you will have the opportunity to strengthen finance operations, improve processes, and contribute to building the future of learning. 50859 Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Full-time Job function Finance Industries Education Administration Programs, E-Learning Providers, and Book and Periodical Publishing Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at McGraw Hill by 2x See who you know

title: SAP-consultant
description: Are you looking for an opportunity where you can combine your SAP knowledge with an international organisation? Do you enjoy working closely with business stakeholders while helping to improve SAP processes in a modern SAP S/4HANA environment? Then this could be the perfect next step in your career. About the role As a SAP Consultant , you will become part of a close-knit SAP team responsible for supporting different business domains, including Finance, Logistics and Warehousing. Your main focus will be SAP FICO. You will work closely with stakeholders, understand their processes and translate business requirements into practical SAP solutions. Rather than acting as an external consultant, you'll become a trusted partner within the organization and help further develop the SAP landscape. The company is continuously investing in innovation and operates in a modern SAP S/4HANA environment, offering plenty of opportunities to learn and grow. What will you do? Support and optimize SAP processes Work closely with stakeholders and key users Translate business requirements into SAP solutions Support users with functional questions Improve and optimize existing processes Participate in ERP improvement and implementation projects Collaborate with colleagues across Finance, Logistics and IT Help drive continuous improvement within the SAP environment Who are you? You enjoy working with both people and technology. You understand finance processes, communicate easily with stakeholders and like finding practical solutions. Ideally, you have: Experience working with SAP Finance, SAP FI/CO or SAP FICO Experience working in an SAP environment Strong communication skills A proactive and collaborative mindset Fluent in English Experience with SAP S/4HANA is a plus, but candidates with a willingness to learn are also encouraged to apply. About the company You will join an international organization that is investing heavily in digital transformation and innovation. The SAP team works closely with different business departments and plays a key role in improving business processes. You'll be working in a modern SAP environment alongside experienced colleagues in an informal and collaborative team, with plenty of opportunities for personal and professional growth. What is offered? Salary until €65.000,- 32-40 hour workweek Modern SAP S/4HANA environment Long-term career opportunity Challenging ERP and digital transformation projects Personal development and training opportunities Collaborative and international working environment Excellent secondary benefits Interested? Are you a SAP Finance professional looking for a long-term role where you can make an impact on both Finance and IT? We'd love to hear from you. Send us your CV and we'll be happy to discuss the opportunity with you. title: Pessoa Assistente Administrativa
description: Na busca contínua por mentes criativas e apaixonadas por inovação, estamos à procura de indivíduos que desafiem os limites, que abracem o novo e tracem caminhos inexplorados rumo a um futuro promissor. Com mais de 30 anos de expertise em Consultoria e Gestão de Projetos, somos uma referência nacional em Engenharia Consultiva, em constante expansão global. Com DNA de inovação, nos reinventamos e evoluímos. Nossa equipe, formada por mais de 2 mil colaboradores, trabalha para impulsionar nossa empresa rumo a um futuro ainda mais relevante, global e digital até 2030. Se você se identifica com nosso propósito, vamos juntos imaginar um futuro e engenhá-lo? Responsabilidades e atribuições Descrição sumária: Atuar no suporte administrativo, financeiro e de controle contratual, realizando a conferência e validação das evidências de medição, elaboração e organização dos processos de faturamento, registro de boletins de medição, controle de informações financeiras e administrativas do contrato, elaboração de relatórios gerenciais e apresentações de resultados, garantindo a conformidade dos processos e o suporte às rotinas de gerenciamento contratual da Timenow. Descrição detalhada: Realizar a conferência detalhada das evidências e documentos que compõem os processos de medição do contrato; Analisar e validar informações quantitativas e qualitativas apresentadas nas medições, assegurando aderência aos requisitos contratuais; Elaborar, organizar e manter atualizados os Books de Medição para envio aos clientes e áreas responsáveis; Abrir e acompanhar requisições de compra, monitorando o fluxo de aprovação até a emissão dos pedidos; Realizar o controle de receitas, despesas, provisões e demais informações financeiras relacionadas ao contrato; Apoiar o acompanhamento orçamentário, contribuindo para análises de custos e desempenho financeiro; Controlar movimentações de pessoal do contrato, incluindo admissões, desligamentos, transferências e atualização de registros; Elaborar relatórios semanais e mensais de desempenho operacional, financeiro e contratual; Consolidar informações e indicadores para apresentação de resultados à gestão e ao cliente; Garantir a organização documental física e eletrônica dos registros do contrato; Apoiar auditorias internas e externas, disponibilizando documentação e evidências quando solicitado; Executar outras atividades correlatas e inerentes à função. Requisitos e qualificações Como requisito básico, você deve ter: Ensino médio completo; Experiência em rotinas de medição de contratos, controle administrativo ou financeiro; Vivência com processos de faturamento, controle de custos e gestão documental; Conhecimento em elaboração de relatórios gerenciais e apresentações executivas; Familiaridade com controles financeiros, orçamentários e indicadores de desempenho; Facilidade para trabalhar com informações e controles numéricos; Capacidade de planejamento e cumprimento de prazos; Organização e atenção aos detalhes; Conhecimento intermediário ou avançado em Microsoft Excel; Você se destaca se tiver: Curso Técnico Completo em Administração ou áreas correlatas; Cursando ensino superior em Administração, Engenharia, Ciências Contábeis, Economia ou áreas afins. Experiência com sistema SAP, especialmente para registro de medições e requisições de compra; Carteira Nacional de Habilitação permanente, categoria B; Informações adicionais Características da vaga: Regime de contratação: Regular Office (Presencial) Local de atuação: Governador Valadares - MG Horário de trabalho: segunda - quinta-feira de 07h às 17h19 e sexta-feira 07h às 16h19, com 1h de intervalo de almoço Benefícios contemplados na vaga: Plano de saúde 💳 Plano Odontológico 🦷 Seguro de vida ⛑️ Transporte 🚌 Alimentação 🥖 Convênio com Totalpass 🏋️‍♀️ Descontos e parcerias com diversas instituições de ensino 📚 Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Full-time Job function Other Industries Industrial Machinery Manufacturing Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Timenow by 2x See who you know

title: SAP Service Contract Administrator
description: Service Contract Administrator is a critical role responsible for ensuring that customer contracts, warranties, and functional locations are accurately established, maintained, and governed within SAP S/4HANA. This position ensures effective Service Business execution through proper contract and warranty determination, cost control, resource alignment, and service readiness. The role works closely with Project Teams, Customer Support, Field Service, Renewals, Finance, Shared Services and Delivery Organizations to ensure contractual compliance, financial accuracy, and high-quality customer service delivery. We offer: What We Offer: Career Development Competitive Compensation and Benefits Pay Transparency Global Opportunities Learn More Here : https://www.dematic.com/en-us/about/careers/what-we-offer/ Dematic provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation and training. The base pay range for this role is estimated to be $65,000 to $140,000 an hour at the time of posting. Final compensation will be determined by various factors such as work location, education, experience, knowledge, and skills. Tasks and Qualifications: What You Will Do In This Role: Review and validate service contracts, including structure, billing, dates, and system linkage. Coordinate intercompany contract requirements with global teams and renewal groups. Ensure contracts are properly created, activated, and governed in SAP S/4HANA. Manage third-party vendor contract alignment and support service order creation when needed. Ensure warranties are correctly linked to Functional Locations and applied in service processes. Review warranty-related service orders for eligibility and cost accuracy. Align warranty coverage with service delivery methods and support reassignment decisions when required. Determine if cost is accurately charged to the warranty and proper functional location. Utilize Service Cloud (C4) and SAP S/4HANA to manage service tickets, contracts, service orders, and cost postings. Monitor and apply financial controls such as stop-order criteria. Support monthly financial activities, including cost settlement, forecasting, and variance analysis. Ensure contracts and service orders are properly closed, with all costs settled in SAP S/4HANA. Coordinate with Finance for accurate contract completion and reporting. What We Are Looking For: Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, Operations, or a related field, or equivalent experience. 5+ years of experience in contract management, service operations, or warranty administration Demonstrated experience working with ERP systems, preferably SAP S/4HANA. Experience collaborating with Finance, Project Teams, and Customer Service organizations. Strong knowledge of contract lifecycle and warranty management Working knowledge of SAP S/4HANA service and contract modules Ability to interpret contract entitlements and apply them to service delivery. Financial and cost management acumen Strong planning, prioritization, and problem‑solving skills. Excellent communication and cross‑functional collaboration abilities High attention to detail and process discipline Experience managing contracts. Familiarity with Service Cloud or similar CRM/ticketing platforms Exposure to intercompany service delivery models Understanding of straight‑line and completed‑revenue recognition models. title: SAP Functional Integration Lead
description: JOB TITLE: SAP Functional Integration Lead This position is part of BRP's SAP Center of Expertise (CoE), a cross-functional team of technical and business process experts. The SAP CoE drives support and continuous improvement across various domains to help the organization achieve its strategic goals and growth objectives. We're looking for experienced SAP Functional Integration Lead to support large‑scale SAP S/4HANA and ecosystem initiatives. This role is responsible for ensuring effective integration across SAP functional teams and alignment with business process stakeholders throughout the project lifecycle. The SAP Functional Integration Lead plays a critical role in driving end‑to‑end process consistency, managing cross‑functional dependencies, and ensuring SAP solutions are delivered as cohesive business solutions rather than isolated functional components. Your Key Responsibilities Ensure SAP solutions are designed and delivered end‑to‑end by aligning functional designs with approved business process intent. Act as a primary integration point across SAP functional workstreams to prevent design silos and integration gaps. Identify, assess, and manage cross‑functional and cross‑process dependencies, impacts, and risks. Facilitate cross‑functional design reviews, integration workshops, and alignment sessions. Collaborate with business stakeholders and subject matter experts (SMEs) to validate functional designs. Partner with PMO and program leadership to provide integrated input on alignment issues, risks, and delivery readiness. Support system integration testing (SIT), user acceptance testing (UAT), cutover, and go‑live activities from a functional and process integration perspective. Ensure adherence to solution standards, templates, and agreed‑upon design principles. Your Qualifications And Skills University degree or equivalent by experience 8 years+ years' experience working in large, complex SAP programs with multiple parallel workstreams Strong SAP functional expertise, with broad knowledge of SAP S/4HANA implementation approaches across multiple modules and supporting add-on solutions including EWM, TM, GTS, and IBP Proven ability to work across functional, business, and program governance teams Strong end‑to‑end and integration mindset, with the ability to translate business requirements into functional solutions and resolve issues dynamically through clear, effective communication. Comfortable operating in a multi-time zone environment and participating in escalations for critical incidents High-level experience with cutover, go-live and data migration Experience in scaled agile environment (e.g SAFe, LeSS) Ability to constantly adapt and perform in ever evolving environments and business realities You’re able to work in English (written and oral) ACKNOWLEDGING THE POWER OF DIVERSITY BRP is dedicated to nurturing a culture that invites, connects, and propels the ambitions of people of all backgrounds, profiles, beliefs and experiences. Ultimately, the diversity and uniqueness of our people fuel our ingenuity and set the course for the path ahead! For this reason, we value diversity and we strive to always push each other forward to build an inclusive workplace where every employee feels like they belong, where they can grow and find meaning. WELCOME TO BRP We’re a world leader in recreational vehicles and boats, creating innovative ways to move on snow, water, asphalt, dirt and even in the air. Headquartered in the Canadian town of Valcourt, Quebec, our company is rooted in a spirit of ingenuity and intense customer focus. Today, we operate manufacturing facilities in Canada, the United States, Mexico, Finland, Australia and Austria, with a workforce made up of close to 20,000 spirited people, all driven by the deeply held belief that at work, as with life itself, it’s not about the destination; It’s about the journey. This position is based in Campinas, Brazil. You will have the opportunity to work in hybrid mode. title: SAP Migration Data Engineer
description: We are seeking an experienced SAP Migration Data Engineer to support complex SAP transformation initiatives, including S/4HANA migrations and system consolidations. You will be responsible for end-to-end data migration delivery — from source profiling through cutover validation — working at the intersection of SAP functional knowledge, data engineering, data cleansing, and data quality practice. You'll Have The Opportunity To Design and execute data migration strategies for SAP implementations (greenfield, brownfield, or selective data transition) Build and maintain ETL/ELT pipelines to extract, transform, and load legacy data into SAP target structures Perform source system data profiling and quality assessments; document migration rules and transformation logic Design and execute data cleansing workflows — including deduplication, standardization, null handling, referential integrity checks, and business-rule-driven corrections — prior to and during migration loads Develop and maintain migration objects using SAP Data Services, LSMW, BAPI/IDOC frameworks, or MIGRATE (SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit) Leverage Dataiku DSS to orchestrate data preparation, cleansing, and transformation pipelines across migration workstreams Collaborate with SAP functional consultants to validate business rules and reconcile data mapping gaps Execute mock migration cycles, reconciliation reporting, and defect resolution ahead of go-live Define and enforce cutover data migration runbooks and sequence plans Partner with data governance and MDM teams to align migrated data to target data standards Support post-migration hypercare: triage data incidents, validate production loads, and close reconciliation gaps YOU’LL THRIVE IN THIS ROLE IF YOU HAVE THE FOLLOWING SKILLS AND QUALITIES: Required Qualifications 5+ years of experience in data migration roles on SAP projects (ECC or S/4HANA) Hands-on experience with SAP Migration Cockpit or equivalent tooling Strong SQL skills; ability to write complex transformation, cleansing, and reconciliation queries Demonstrated experience designing and executing data cleansing programs: deduplication, format standardization, referential integrity validation, and enrichment Hands-on experience with Dataiku DSS — including Flow design, recipe authoring (visual and code), and pipeline orchestration for data preparation and migration workstreams Experience with SAP data domains: Material Master, Customer/Vendor (BP), Finance (GL/AP/AR), or Plant Maintenance Understanding of SAP data structures, table relationships, and key object dependencies Proficiency in at least one scripting language (Python, Shell) for automation and reconciliation Experience executing full migration lifecycle: extract → profile → cleanse → map → load → validate Preferred Qualifications Experience with SAP MDG (Master Data Governance) or MDM integration Familiarity with Collibra or equivalent data governance/quality platforms Exposure to RISE with SAP or BTP data migration patterns Knowledge of SAP IDOC processing and error handling Dataiku DSS certification or advanced platform knowledge (custom Python/R recipes, MLflow integration, API node deployment) Experience in regulated or high-volume manufacturing, distribution, or recreational products environments Familiar or exposure to PowerBi reporting tool or equivalent What You Bring Methodical approach to data quality — you don't just move data, you improve it A cleansing-first mindset: you treat dirty data as a design problem, not a migration footnote Ability to translate between SAP functional teams and data engineering without losing fidelity on either side Comfort operating in ambiguity during active migration cycles under cutover pressure Experience using Dataiku to accelerate and democratize data preparation without sacrificing rigor ACKNOWLEDGING THE POWER OF DIVERSITY BRP is dedicated to nurturing a culture that invites, connects, and propels the ambitions of people of all backgrounds, profiles, beliefs and experiences. For this reason, we value diversity and we strive to always push each other forward to build an inclusive workplace where every employee feels like they belong, where they can grow and find meaning. title: Deployment Lead
description: Project Role : Deployment Lead Project Role Description : Plan and lead the execution of a comprehensive deployment plan, including work planning, scheduling, budgeting, metrics, training, pilots, and resources. Collaborate with all project teams to manage interdependencies, ensure alignment between all deployment-related activities, and monitor & control progress through the deployment plan. Must have skills : SAP Concur Good to have skills : NA Minimum 7.5 Year(s) Of Experience Is Required Educational Qualification : 15 years full time education Summary AI Powered Tech Talent Design, configure, and deliver enterprise SAP Concur solutions by combining strong travel, expense, and invoice management domain expertise with AI assisted delivery and integration practices. The role focuses on accelerating implementation and rollout, improving compliance and user experience, and embedding pre built AI and automation capabilities into spend management processes—without building or training AI models. Core Responsibilities Enterprise Spend Management Delivery Design, configure, and implement SAP Concur solutions for travel, expense, and invoice management. Translate finance, procurement, and business requirements into scalable Concur configurations and workflows. Support end to end delivery including enhancements, deployments, upgrades, and production support. AI Assisted Delivery & Productivity Use AI copilots and GenAI tools to accelerate requirements analysis, configuration validation, documentation, testing, and defect resolution. Apply AI assisted techniques to identify policy deviations, expense anomalies, and process inefficiencies. Leverage pre built AI capabilities within SAP Concur and adjacent platforms (e.g., intelligent auditing, automation, insights). Integration & Data Enablement Integrate SAP Concur with ERP, finance, payroll, and other enterprise systems using standard integration patterns. Collaborate with finance, IT, and analytics teams to ensure accurate, compliant, and auditable spend data. Understand AI concepts such as intelligent audit, automation, and recommendations at an applied level, without owning model design. Stakeholder Collaboration Partner with finance, procurement, and business stakeholders to align solutions with policy and compliance objectives. Communicate complex spend and system concepts clearly to non technical users and leaders. Act as a bridge between functional teams and AI native specialists when advanced AI capabilities are needed. Quality, Governance & Adoption Ensure adherence to enterprise SDLC, financial controls, security, and responsible AI guidelines. Support deployment, change management, and user adoption through training and enablement. Drive continuous improvement using feedback, metrics, and AI enabled accelerators. Primary Skills (Must Have) SAP Concur configuration and delivery expertise (Travel, Expense, Invoice, or combinations). Strong understanding of finance, expense, and compliance processes. Experience using AI assisted tools or copilots for delivery, analysis, or documentation. Ability to align spend management solutions with business efficiency and compliance outcomes. Secondary / Good to Have Skills Experience with ERP or finance system integrations. Exposure to analytics, reporting, or audit processes related to spend management. Familiarity with change management and global rollouts. Working knowledge of automation or intelligent insights in finance operations. What This Role Does Not Require No training or fine tuning of AI/ML models. No ownership of AI architectures or MLOps pipelines. Focus remains on integration, adoption, and productivity uplift, not AI engineering or research. Value Delivered Faster SAP Concur implementations and deployments through AI assisted delivery. Improved compliance, audit effectiveness, and user experience. Scalable spend management outcomes without reliance on scarce AI native talent. Nice to Have Skills & Experience - Experience with Microsoft Power Platform (Power Automate, Power Forms, etc.). - Oracle, SAP, or other asset management software experience. - Experience with data lakes or enterprise data warehousing tools such as SAP HANA or BigQuery. - Utilities, energy, construction, infrastructure, distribution, or related industry experience. - Experience supporting capital projects, capital budgets, project tracking, or operational performance reporting. - Exposure to regulatory reporting, forecasting, or financial performance analysis. Job Description Insight Global is seeking a Business Analyst I to support a natural gas utility company's contract construction operations and infrastructure groups with data analytics, reporting, and business intelligence initiatives. This is a 9-month contract-to-hire opportunity supporting construction, field operations, engineering, accounting, supply chain, and regulatory stakeholders. The analyst will build, maintain, and troubleshoot Power BI dashboards and reports; analyze project, operational, and financial data; track capital project spending and performance metrics; support forecasting activities; gather and validate data from internal teams and contractors; and provide reporting that helps leadership make informed business decisions. This role can sit in New Orleans, Baton Rouge, Shreveport, Lake Charles, Broussard, LA, or Pearl, MS. Compensation ranges from $33-$43/hour based on experience. title: SAP Functional / EPPDS
description: A Accenture ajuda as principais empresas do mundo a se reinventarem, construindo seu núcleo digital e liberando o poder da IA para gerar valor com rapidez em organizações de todos os setores. Nossa estratégia é ser o parceiro de reinvenção preferido de nossos clientes, liderar a adoção da IA de maneira segura e em escala, ser a empresa impulsionada por IA mais centrada no cliente, além de ser o melhor lugar para se trabalhar. Reunimos o talento de aproximadamente 786 mil pessoas com ativos e plataformas proprietárias, profundo conhecimento em processos e indústrias e relações líderes no ecossistema para oferecer soluções integradas e resultados mensuráveis em escala. Por meio de nossos Reinvention Services oferecemos ampla expertise em Cybersecurity, Digital Core, Finance, Industry & Enterprise, Song, Supply Chain & Engineering e Talent, com capacidades avançadas em IA e Dados, Indústria & Processos e Tecnologia. Atendemos aproximadamente 9 mil clientes e geramos cerca de US$ 70 bilhões em receita no ano fiscal de 2025. Visite a Accenture em accenture.com. Construa o futuro do SAP com a Accenture Na Accenture, você atua em um dos maiores ecossistemas SAP do mundo, organizado por SAP Domains, o que permite aprofundar seu conhecimento por indústria e gerar impacto real nos negócios dos clientes. Nossa atuação vai além da implementação: entregamos transformação digital com SAP integrado a Inteligência Artificial, automação e dados, apoiando decisões mais inteligentes e processos mais eficientes. Você trabalhará com tecnologias SAP de última geração, metodologias ágeis e times globais, em um ambiente que estimula inovação contínua, aprendizado e crescimento de carreira por meio de certificações, capacitação técnica e desafios de alta complexidade. O que você vai fazer no seu dia a dia: Atuar na implementação e configuração do SAP S/4HANA Embedded Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling (ePPDS) Analisar e desenhar processos de planejamento avançado da produção e sequenciamento detalhado Integrar soluções ePPDS com outros módulos SAP, especialmente Supply Chain Planning (SCP) Identificar oportunidades de otimização e melhoria contínua nos processos produtivos dos clientes Trabalhar em conjunto com times multidisciplinares em ambientes ágeis e projetos globais Apoiar na definição de soluções técnicas e funcionais aderentes às necessidades do negócio Realizar suporte e troubleshooting em ambientes produtivos quando necessário O que estamos procurando na pessoa que vai fazer parte do time: Perfil analítico, com forte capacidade de resolução de problemas Boa comunicação para interação com stakeholders e clientes Proatividade e senso de ownership nas entregas Capacidade de trabalhar em equipes colaborativas e ambientes dinâmicos Interesse em inovação e evolução contínua em tecnologias SAP Além disso, é desejável conhecimento: Experiência prática com SAP S/4HANA e ePPDS Conhecimento em Supply Chain Planning (SCP) Experiência prévia em projetos de implementação ou suporte SAP Ensino superior completo em área correlata Inglês desejável para atuação em projetos globais Benefícios: 🏥 Assistência Médica – title: (1485) SAP Analytics Solutions Architect
description: Join Nortal for your next big adventure. We are seeking a SAP Analytics Solutions Architect to join our team, grow with us, and teach us a few things along the way. Why join Nortal Say Goodbye to Micromanagement: We champion autonomy, open communication, and respect for diversity as our core values. Outcomes Over Hours: We believe in a work model focused on impact, providing you with the flexibility to balance your professional success with your personal well-being. Vibrant Community: Join our close-knit LATAM remote team and connect through tech talks, virtual coffee breaks, and team-building activities. Support from Day One: Our People Care team is here to support you throughout your journey, from wellness check-ins to professional growth. Our benefits ✨ Competitive USD salary. 100% remote work: While you can work from anywhere, you’re always welcome to connect with teammates and grow your network at our coworking spaces across LATAM. Paid off & national holidays: Take the time you need according to your country’s regulations while receiving your full salary. Sick leave: Focus on your health without stress. Take the necessary time to recover and feel better. Annual credit: Spend it on the perks you love to enhance your work-life balance. Team-building activities: Join us for coffee breaks, tech talks, and after-work gatherings to bond with your Nortal family and feel part of our vibrant community. Birthday off: Enjoy an extra day off during your birthday week to celebrate in style with friends and family. About The Project As a SAP Analytics Solutions Architect , you drive the delivery of analytics solutions within SAP and other software applications, including the development of in-app reports and extraction of data for business intelligence applications. You will partner cross-functionally with teams in supply chain, sales, and IT to advance analytics and reporting maturity through digital transformation initiatives. This position offers the opportunity to work in a collaborative environment focused on innovation, continuous learning, and professional growth. What you’ll do Partner with value stream teams and analysts to understand business needs and translate them into SAP-enabled analytical solutions. Design and develop embedded analytics data models and custom S/4HANA CDS views to support data extraction and custom Fiori analytical applications. Evaluate SAP-delivered Fiori analytical content, conduct demos with business users, and identify gaps requiring custom solutions. Lead the development lifecycle, testing, and deployment of custom SAP analytics content across project landscapes. Provide technical leadership to offshore analytics developers and ensure consistent quality and alignment with analytics standards. Prepare technical specifications, solution documentation, and testing evidence to support sustainment and improvement processes. Identify data sources, CDS views, and extraction pathways required to support ingestion into Databricks. Advanced analytics best practices for embedded reporting and data extraction with enterprise and manufacturing applications. Maintain compliance with training expectations as required for this position. What makes you a great fit 🎯 Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field. 7+ Years of relevant analytics, data engineering, or ERP-based reporting experience, including hands-on work designing enterprise reporting solutions. Extensive experience with SAP or another major ERP system, including a strong understanding of SAP data structures, CDS views, embedded analytics, or Fiori analytical applications. Demonstrated experience designing or developing data models, data extractions, or integrations that support enterprise reporting or analytics use cases. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to translate complex business questions into scalable technical solutions. Strong project execution and communication skills, including the ability to define projects, set stakeholder expectations, organize work streams, and deliver solutions. Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively with a variety of partners in complex projects. Advanced English Level is required for this role, as you will work with US clients. Effective communication in English is essential to deliver the best solutions to our clients and expand your horizons. Global Impact, Local Culture 🌍 For over 25 years, we’ve been shaping digital transformation, delivering complex solutions for global enterprises and public sector organizations. Today, we are at the forefront of an AI-driven transformation era, helping organizations turn data into real business value. By combining deep domain expertise with data and AI, we design solutions that create immediate impact while scaling for the future. We believe complexity doesn’t have to mean complicated, and that the best solutions are those that work in the real world, not just on paper. That’s why our clients trust us with their most ambitious, business-critical initiatives. Apply now and let’s shape the future of technology together! 🧑‍💻 By applying to this position, you authorize Nortal to collect, store, transfer, and process your personal data in accordance with our Privacy Policy. For more information, please review our Privacy Policy. title: Analista de Controles Internos Pl - São Paulo
description: Nosso sucesso é fruto da dedicação e do talento das pessoas que trabalham conosco. Construímos uma cultura forte, baseada em valores sólidos, que guiam nossas decisões, estratégias e a busca por profissionais alinhados ao nosso propósito. Acreditamos que desafios são oportunidades e buscamos talentos que compartilhem de nossos valores, contribuindo para um ambiente dinâmico e inspirador. Juntos, seguimos aprimorando nossa cultura e fortalecendo nosso compromisso de alimentar sua vida. Responsabilidades e atribuições Se você busca a oportunidade de atuar de forma estratégica, fortalecendo a governança corporativa e impulsionando uma cultura sólida de controles internos, essa vaga pode ser o próximo passo da sua carreira. Na Vigor, você terá a missão de apoiar e aprimorar o modelo de controle interno em parceria com diversas áreas de negócio, garantindo a gestão dos riscos operacionais e a efetividade dos processos que sustentam nossas operações. Essa posição exige visão analítica, organização, protagonismo e excelente capacidade de interação com diferentes times, contribuindo diretamente para a integridade dos nossos processos e para a conformidade da companhia. Gerenciar, em conjunto com as áreas de negócio, um modelo integral de controle interno, em alinhamento com o responsável da área e com a equipe central de controle interno, com foco na gestão de riscos e na atribuição de controles por meio de: Mapeamento dos processos-chave da organização, assegurando o cumprimento na execução dos controles e informando gerentes e líderes sobre a certificação e adequação dos controles. Apoio na documentação de políticas e procedimentos de todos os processos que regulamentam a companhia. Promoção e apoio a programas de capacitação relacionados à cultura de controle, bem como ao desenho e implementação de controles nos processos. Monitoramento da correta segregação de funções (SoD) nos sistemas centrais da companhia, com o objetivo de identificar conflitos entre funções e permissões atribuídas a um mesmo colaborador. Documentação das matrizes de risco de segregação de funções em nível global, além do monitoramento dos controles mitigadores e da realização de testes de efetividade desses controles pelo menos uma vez ao ano. Execução de exercícios de recertificação periódica para funções com risco em nível global, garantindo que as licenças se mantenham alinhadas ao desenho e livres de risco pelo menos uma vez ao ano. Requisitos e qualificações Ensino Superior completo em Administração, Ciências Contábeis, Economia, Engenharia, Sistemas de Informação ou áreas correlatas. É necessário possuir experiência prévia em Controles Internos, Auditoria Interna ou Externa, Governança, Gestão de Riscos ou Compliance. Conhecimento em SAP e ferramentas de controles internos. Desejável conhecimento de modelos de SoD e metodologias de avaliação de controles. Espanhol e/ou inglês será considerado diferencial. Perfil analítico, organizado, com boa comunicação e capacidade de interação com múltiplas áreas. Local de atuação: Barra Funda – São Paulo/SP. title: Accountant
description: Accountant Job Description: Are you a detail-oriented individual with a passion for numbers? We are seeking a talented Accountant to join our vibrant team. In this role, you'll play a vital part in ensuring that cost and expense documents are accurately recorded. Your meticulous attention to detail will be essential in reflecting revenue and expenses in our accounting records, adhering to applicable legislation, company regulations, circular letters, approvals, and generally accepted accounting principles. You'll also be responsible for communicating activities required by local laws to our certified public accountant and diligently monitoring compliance. Responsibilities: Timely monitor and verify revenues from our sales management, ensuring compliance with relevant laws, legislation, and regulations. Your expertise in recording these revenues accurately in our accounting records will be invaluable. Respond to requests by providing comprehensive office income, cost, financing, and financial statistical information to the Sales Manager or your immediate superior. Take charge of timely bank deposits for sales proceeds (Office+BSP+Cargo+CASS+GSA) and diligently record them. Efficiently process payments for approved expenditures, strictly following written approvals. Your keen eye for detail will ensure proper accounting records and the best interests of the company. Additionally, handle disbursements of allowances as necessary. Maintain the annual cost budget sheet and keep a close watch on costs, making sure they align with the budget limits. Take proactive steps to prevent exceeding budget portions and promptly inform your superior. Skillfully manage administrative affairs and diligently follow up on related issues. Desired Skills & Experience: Bachelor's degree or at least high school graduation. Preferably, at least one year of experience in finance, accounting, monthly close, and financial reporting. Proficient knowledge of generally accepted accounting principles, procedures, and their practical application. Familiarity and competency with MS Office products. Excellent command of English and local language(s). Valid work permit or citizenship (the company does not provide or assist with work permits). Demonstrated sense of responsibility and the ability to make accurate and effective decisions. Highly organized, detail-oriented, and skilled in analyzing various data. Experience with SAP system preferred. A collaborative team player who can also work independently when required, thriving in a fast-paced environment. Proactive problem-solving skills. We strive to bring the far close by making the world move through our wings. We are working with all our strength to offer our guests a unique experience while connecting 350 destinations around the world. We would like to see you among us in our success story to which each member of our family contributes. Now it is time to grow our family with you to achieve greater goals. title: Consultant SAP F/H
description: Up Technologies est une société de conseil pour l’industrie avec des agences sur Lyon , Grenoble , Aix-en-Provence , Toulon, Nice Sophia et Paris . Nous sommes une société de conseil spécialisée dans les métiers de l'électronique, des systèmes embarqués et de la mécatronique. Nous intervenons en assistance technique ainsi qu'au forfait à travers notre bureau d'études pour fabriquer des cartes électroniques et briques logicielles. Forts d'expériences dans des sociétés de conseil en tant que Managers et Consultants, notre ambition est de proposer une société qui associe les objectifs professionnels des Consultants et ceux d'une société de conseil en ingénierie à taille humaine. Le point commun des Consultants d'Up Technologies est d'avoir un esprit de services orienté Client, une capacité d'autonomie et une aisance relationnelle. La réussite des missions est portée par l'écoute et la transparence du trinôme Consultant, Client, Manager. Vous partagez ces principes ? Vous souhaitez vous investir et relever des challenges ? Alors rejoignez nous et venez participez à notre développement ! 🚀 Are you Up for it ? Objectif : déployer un core model SAP S/4HANA sur plusieurs sites industriels en Europe dans le cadre d’un programme stratégique d’harmonisation des processus. Vous intégrerez une organisation déjà structurée (plusieurs équipes d’une trentaine de personnes) avec des perspectives d’évolution significatives. 🎯 Vos Missions En tant que Squad Data Lead au sein d'un grand groupe international du secteur de l'énergie , vous effectuerez les missions suivantes : Analyse des processus locaux et recueil des pratiques existantes Accompagnement des sites dans la migration vers SAP S/4HANA Préparation et chargement des données (data quality, intégrité, cohérence) Organisation et animation des User Acceptance Tests Support au cut-over et au go-live Formation et accompagnement des utilisateurs finaux Compréhension des processus PEX (Production / Exécution) et du module PP ➡️Vous jouerez un rôle clé mêlant expertise SAP, gestion de la donnée et forte dimension humaine et pédagogique . 👤 Et si on parlait de vous ? Vous justifiez d’au moins 3 ans d’expérience sur des projets de migration et/ou de déploiement SAP. Vous disposez d’une solide compréhension des processus PEX (Production / Execution) et êtes particulièrement sensible aux enjeux liés aux données ainsi qu’à la qualité des informations. Vous évoluez avec aisance dans des environnements internationaux et multi-sites . Doté(e) d’excellentes qualités relationnelles, vous êtes en mesure de former les utilisateurs et d’accompagner le changement dans un contexte de transformation. Le poste s’inscrit dans un environnement international impliquant des interventions sur plusieurs sites en Europe, notamment en Europe de l’Est. Accompagnant nos clients sur des projets à dimension internationale, un niveau d'anglais professionnel est requis pour l'ensemble de nos collaborateurs. 3/ Up Technologies c’est : · Participer à des projets innovants · Intégrer une société à taille humaine · Intervenir sur des projets dans des domaines diversifiés · Rejoindre une équipe conviviale composée d’experts et participer à la vie de la communauté technique Up. Vous partagez ces principes ? Vous souhaitez vous investir et relever des challenges ? Alors rejoignez nous et venez participez à notre développement ! title: SAP主数据业务流程专家
company: 卡尔蔡司光学（广州）有限公司
description: 该职位来源于猎聘 一、岗位目标 作为ERP项目中主数据相关业务流程的核心连接人，负责统筹产品、客户、供应商、可售清单等关键主数据的业务规则、端到端流程、系统需求与数据治理要求，确保本地主数据业务需求能够准确输入Global项目团队，并在SAP S/4HANA落地过程中实现标准化、可追踪和可持续运营。 二、主要职责 主数据流程梳理与标准化：梳理产品主数据、客户主数据、供应商主数据、可售清单等端到端业务流程，识别现状差异、痛点和优化机会，推动流程固化与标准化。 业务需求与Global对接：代表本地业务与Global对应Workstream沟通主数据需求，进行需求澄清、Fit-Gap分析、差异说明、方案确认和关键决策跟进。 数据治理与规则设计：协助建立主数据维护规则、字段定义、职责分工、审批机制、质量校验规则和数据生命周期管理方法，提升主数据准确性与一致性。 系统实施支持：参与SAP相关项目调研、蓝图/流程设计、Core Solution Building、本地化验证、权限和角色设计、测试场景准备、UAT支持及缺陷跟踪。 数据迁移与切换准备：支持主数据清理、映射、迁移规则制定、数据验证、切换计划及上线前准备，跟进数据问题闭环。 知识传递与长期运营：在项目实施后沉淀主数据管理方法、流程文档和运营机制，支持后续持续优化和关键用户/业务团队能力建设。 三、任职要求 本科及以上学历，信息管理、供应链、财务、数据管理、企业管理、信息技术等相关专业优先。 具备5年以上主数据管理、业务流程管理、SAP/ERP项目、数据治理或业务流程优化相关经验；有产品、客户、供应商、物料/可售清单主数据管理经验者优先。 熟悉SAP系统，具备SAP主数据维护、主数据治理、SAP S/4HANA项目或SAP咨询经验者优先。 理解端到端业务流程，能够在业务规则、数据结构、系统逻辑和流程控制之间建立清晰关联。 具备较强的逻辑分析、问题拆解、需求澄清和文档表达能力，能够输出流程图、需求说明、问题清单、决策材料和管理汇报。 能够熟练使用英文作为书面工作语言，并具备一定商务英语沟通能力，可与Global团队进行日常沟通。 具备较强的跨部门沟通协调能力、责任心和推动力，能够在时间紧张及多方协作环境下推进任务落地。 Show more Show less

title: Financial Reporting
description: About The Opportunity Role: Financial Reporting Level: Deputy Manager Reporting To: Vice President - Finance and Accounts Location: Gurugram About The Function The Financial Reporting function is responsible for accurate consolidation of group financials and ensuring compliance with IFRS/Ind-AS and Schedule III requirements under the Companies Act, 2013. It provides stakeholders with timely and reliable financial insights to support decision-making. The function also ensures regulatory adherence, prepares statutory and annual reports (20F), coordination with auditors and leverages ERP systems and MS Office tools for efficient reporting. Strong communication and analytical skills are key to driving transparency and governance in this role. About The Role The role focuses on managing end-to-end financial reporting activities, including consolidation of group results and preparation of statutory reports. It requires applying knowledge of Ind-AS/IFRS to ensure accuracy and compliance while adhering to Schedule III guidelines . The incumbent will work closely with internal teams, auditors and stakeholders, leveraging MS Office and ERP platforms to streamline reporting processes. Strong written and verbal communication skills are essential to present insights effectively. This position plays a critical part in strengthening the organization’s financial integrity and decision-making framework. What Will You Be Doing The role involves preparing consolidated financial statements across multiple subsidiaries, including inter-company eliminations and minority interest adjustments . Requires hands-on experience in applying Ind-AS and IFRS standards for statutory reporting. Key responsibilities include quarterly/annual closings, compliance with Schedule III, and supporting audits. Proficiency in ERP systems (SAP/Oracle/MS Navision) and advanced Excel skills is preferred. Qualification & Experience Qualified Chartered Accountant (CA) with 5–6 years of post-qualification experience in Financial Closing and Reporting within medium to large organizations. Prior experience in a listed company (preferably India- or US-listed) will be an added advantage. Key Success Factors For The Role Accuracy and timeliness in financial closing and group reporting. Strong application of Ind-AS/IFRS and compliance with regulatory requirements. Effective collaboration, communication, and use of ERP/Excel for streamlined reporting. title: Procurement Operations Analyst
description: Skill required: Procurement Operations - Procurement Support Designation: Procurement Operations Analyst Qualifications: Any Graduation Years of Experience: 3 to 5 years About Accenture Accenture is a global professional services company with leading capabilities in digital, cloud and security.Combining unmatched experience and specialized skills across more than 40 industries, we offer Strategy and Consulting, Technology and Operations services, and Accenture Song— all powered by the world’s largest network of Advanced Technology and Intelligent Operations centers. Our 784,000 people deliver on the promise of technology and human ingenuity every day, serving clients in more than 120 countries. We embrace the power of change to create value and shared success for our clients, people, shareholders, partners and communities.Visit us at www.accenture.com What would you do? You will be aligned with our Procurement vertical and help us in the process of finding and agreeing to terms, and acquiring goods, services, or works from an external source, often via a tendering or competitive bidding process. Procurement is used to ensure the buyer receives goods, services, or works at the best possible price when aspects such as quality, quantity, time, and location are compared. You will be working as a part of Procurement management team which uses a systematic approach for buying all the goods and services needed for a company to stay sustainable. goods and services needed for a company to stay sustainable. They are involved in the identification of need, finding and qualifying the suppliers, requesting proposals, negotiating with suppliers, contracting, delivery and analyzing results. In Spot Buy Sourcing, you will be responsible for Spot Buy Sourcing, a custom B2B marketplace approach of placing purchase orders in minimal time with better pricing. You will be involved in Self-service requisitioning application that controls requester spending and handling multiple activities included in researching and sourcing vendors (from client approved supplier list), obtaining quotes with pricing, capabilities, turnaround times, and quality of work, and negotiating contracts. What are we looking for? goods and services needed for a company to stay sustainable. They are involved in the identification of need, finding and qualifying the suppliers, requesting proposals, negotiating with suppliers, contracting, delivery and analyzing results. In Spot Buy Sourcing, you will be responsible for Spot Buy Sourcing, a custom B2B marketplace approach of placing purchase orders in minimal time with better pricing. You will be involved in Self-service requisitioning application that controls requester spending and handling multiple activities included in researching and sourcing vendors (from client approved supplier list), obtaining quotes with pricing, capabilities, turnaround times, and quality of work, and negotiating contracts. In this role you would require to solve increasingly complex problem Accurate processing of PO/NON PO invoices in Coupa/SAP Sort Scan received invoices/send invoice to OCR Batch Determine error and resolve any interface/posting errors Assist team with GIRIR clearing and Debit balance clearing Should be well versed with GRIR account clearing, debit balance investigate variance and clearing Ensure team reconciles vendor statements on agreed timelines / resolving the open items coming in account reconciliations Generate adhoc or agreed reports from SAP within the agreed timelines Should support Internal / external audit requirements/Ensure SOX Compliance Timely updation of process documents (SOPs)/procuredures Ability to carry out Root Cause Analysis (RCA) and recommend Corrective Action Plan (CAP). Adhering to SLA / KPI / Other relevant metrics reporting Roles and Responsibilities: In this role you are required to solve routine problems, largely through precedent and referral to general guidelines Your expected interactions are within your own team and direct supervisor You will be provided detailed to moderate level of instruction on daily work tasks and detailed instruction on new assignments The decisions that you make would impact your own work You will be an individual contributor as a part of a team, with a predetermined, focused scope of work Please note that this role may require you to work in rotational shiftsIn this role you are required to do analysis and solving of lower-complexity problems Your day to day interaction is with peers within Accenture before updating supervisors In this role you may have limited exposure with clients and/or Accenture management You will be given moderate level instruction on daily work tasks and detailed instructions on new assignments The decisions you make impact your own work and may impact the work of others You will be an individual contributor as a part of a team, with a focused scope of work Please note that this role may require you to work in rotational shifts Show more Show less Seniority level Entry level Employment type Full-time Job function Project Management and Management Industries IT Services and IT Consulting Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Accenture in India by 2x See who you know

title: Procurement Executive, Global Engineering Services INT
description: Description At Amazon, we're working to be the most customer-centric company on earth. To get there, we need exceptionally talented, bright, and driven people. If you'd like to help us build the place to find and buy anything online, this is your chance to make history. We are seeking a highly effective Procurement receiver with capex and construction procurement experience to optimize and streamline the current work practices related to procurement & invoicing cycle. The person in this role will coordinate with several teams across Amazon and will interact with our key vendors to satisfy organizational requirements. This role will partner with internal business owners and key stakeholders to track and monitor CAPEX, including: material handling and conveyance, racking/storage, IT, facility construction and fit-out upgrades, compliance and other capex equipment. The position will work and partner with multiple teams to develop, analyse and identify areas of improvement and roll out new processes to drive the right input at each stage. Understanding of vendor management, invoicing & internal Amazon system workflows will be key to resolve and mitigate issues. Key job responsibilities Responsibilities The candidate is required to coordinate the project procurement and receiving related work for all formats (FCs, SCs & RCs) and will be based out of North India. The procurement receiver role will be responsible for setting up right processes on every project to meet KPI’s and metrics defined for this role. Procurement receiver has to work with all project managers and maintain P2P (purchase to pay) tracker for all projects in the region. The key responsibilities are listed below: Maintaining PR/PO tracker for all projects. Work with Projects site Security and Loss Prevention (SLP)/ PMC team to gather daily incoming Projects goods data (Capex build-out and Capex 4M besides IT and other Safety materials which Projects team may order). Establish a daily input mechanism from SLP team to ensure the goods inward data on the physical registers are converted into MS-Excel form. Ensure goods are received systemically/ correctly in Coupa based on SLP record of inward / counted goods as per defined SLA’s and SOP’s Reconcile the IOH reports shared by AP team for Projects. Analysis & dive deep into the root-causes and provide explanations for discrepancies to Finance partners for review in WBR meetings. Budget tracker for all projects in region - Budget vs Actual forecast & CAR tracking. Commercial Closure of PO and Invoice- Weekly (With Site Finance) Raising Trouble tickets, PRs & issuance of Purchase orders. Support vendor development for the region. Improve vendor payment experience by ensuring timely payments. Training to other regions receivers and new hires. Follow up for advance payments and PBGs Follow-up for supplies with the suppliers Coordinate with the AP team for payment processing Connect with finance team on queries related with taxation The role will be based in NCR with 10% of time traveling to construction sites within region. Basic Qualifications Graduates (preferably B.Com, B. Tech/B.E. background) Preferable work experience in procurement, receiving, warehouse material handling and store management. Should have experience in spend management tools i.e. Coupa, Oracle, SAP etc. regarding the buying, receiving and invoicing activities. Should have knowledge on commercial taxes & TDS Should have the competency to make dashboards, reports & trackers for all the Invoices. Good communication skills. Proficient in MS office (excel, word, power point). Preferred Qualifications Prior experience in Oracle Financial Systems – creating PO’s etc Accounting Background Commerce Degree Holder with proficiency in basic MS office tools, SQL, analytics dashboard etc. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.
