title: Supply Chain Team Lead
description: Ben jij iemand die overzicht houdt, vooruitdenkt en energie krijgt van het optimaliseren van processen? Vind je het leuk om de verbindende schakel te zijn tussen inkoop, logistiek, productie en projectmanagement? Dan is deze functie als Lead Supply Chain iets voor jou. WAT GA JE DOEN? Als Lead Supply Chain zorg jij ervoor dat de complete supply chain soepel verloopt. Vanaf het moment dat een project of productieorder binnenkomt, zorg jij ervoor dat materialen tijdig beschikbaar zijn, de planning klopt en alle betrokken afdelingen optimaal samenwerken. Je bent verantwoordelijk voor de volledige keten: van inkoop en voorraadbeheer tot productieplanning en logistiek. Je houdt continu overzicht, signaleert knelpunten voordat ze ontstaan en stuurt bij waar nodig. Dankzij jouw planning en coördinatie verlopen productieprocessen efficiënt en kunnen klanten rekenen op een betrouwbare levering. Daarnaast kijk je verder dan de dagelijkse operatie. Je analyseert processen, werkt aan verbeteringen binnen het ERP-systeem en gebruikt data om de organisatie continu slimmer en efficiënter te laten werken. Je houdt je onder andere bezig met: Verantwoordelijk voor het volledige inkoopproces en leveranciersmanagement; Onderhouden van leveranciersrelaties en onderhandelen over prijs- en leveringsafspraken; Bewaken en optimaliseren van voorraadniveaus; Opstellen, beheren en bewaken van de productieplanning; Afstemmen met projectleiders, productie, magazijn en logistiek; Organiseren van inkomende en uitgaande goederenstromen; Signaleren en oplossen van plannings- en capaciteitsvraagstukken; Analyseren van KPI’s, levertijden en voorraadgegevens; Optimaliseren van ERP-processen en werkmethodes; Initiëren en begeleiden van verbeterprojecten binnen de supply chain. Je werkt in een veelzijdige functie waarin plannen, organiseren, analyseren en samenwerken continu samenkomen. Geen dag is hetzelfde en je hebt direct invloed op de prestaties van de gehele organisatie. WAT BRENG JE MEE? Je bent analytisch sterk, houdt overzicht en vindt het leuk om processen continu te verbeteren. Je schakelt makkelijk tussen verschillende afdelingen en weet mensen met elkaar te verbinden. Daarbij durf je beslissingen te nemen en werk je gestructureerd. Daarnaast breng je mee: HBO werk- en denkniveau, bij voorkeur richting Supply Chain, Logistiek, Technische Bedrijfskunde of vergelijkbaar; Ervaring binnen supply chain, productieplanning, logistiek of inkoop; Ervaring met ERP-systemen; Sterk analytisch vermogen; Goede communicatieve en onderhandelingsvaardigheden; Een proactieve, gestructureerde en oplossingsgerichte werkhouding. WAT BIEDT DE ORGANISATIE JOU? Je komt terecht binnen een groeiende technische organisatie waar kwaliteit, samenwerking en continu verbeteren centraal staan. De lijnen zijn kort, jouw ideeën worden gewaardeerd en je krijgt veel vrijheid om de supply chain verder te professionaliseren. Daarnaast kun je rekenen op: Een salaris tussen €4.000 en €4.800 bruto per maand, afhankelijk van kennis en ervaring; Een sleutelrol met veel verantwoordelijkheid en zelfstandigheid; Direct op contract bij FibreMax; 32 vrije dagen; Veel ruimte om processen te optimaliseren en verbeteringen door te voeren; Werken binnen een ambitieus en groeiend bedrijf; Korte lijnen en een informele werksfeer; Ruimte voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling en groei; Goede primaire en secundaire arbeidsvoorwaarden; Uitzicht op een vast dienstverband. OVER FIBREMAX Je komt te werken bij een innovatieve technische organisatie waar kwaliteit en betrouwbaarheid centraal staan. Door de groei van de organisatie ontstaat ruimte voor een Supply Chain Manager die structuur aanbrengt, processen optimaliseert en ervoor zorgt dat inkoop, logistiek en productie perfect op elkaar aansluiten. Je krijgt veel verantwoordelijkheid en de kans om écht impact te maken op de verdere professionalisering van de organisatie. INTERESSE? Bel, app of mail met Hendrik Link via: 06 – 47 78 08 99✉️ hendrik@intechniek.com Hij vertelt je graag meer over de functie, de organisatie en de mogelijkheden. title: Auxiliar Operacional - Juazeiro do Norte
description: A Locmed Hospitalar é uma empresa reconhecida na locação e venda de equipamentos médico-hospitalares desde 2001. Com um compromisso sólido em oferecer produtos de alta tecnologia e um atendimento personalizado e humanizado, já atendemos mais de 22 mil clientes em mais de 300 municípios e 400 hospitais e clínicas em diversos estados brasileiros. Graças ao nosso cuidado e experiência, a Locmed se tornou uma referência nacional, proporcionando as melhores soluções em ventilação mecânica invasiva e não-invasiva, oxigenoterapia, apneia obstrutiva do sono, home care, terapia respiratória, mobilidade e muito mais. Se você procura um ambiente que valoriza a tecnologia e o cuidado com as pessoas, a Locmed é o lugar ideal para você! Venha se juntar a nós e contribuir para a saúde e o bem-estar de muitos. Estamos ansiosos para conhecê-lo! Requisitos: Noções básicas de manutenção preventiva e corretiva; Ensino Médio Completo; Conhecimento básico em informática; Habilitação A e B; Disponibilidade para viagens. O que esperamos de você: Realizar visitas em hospitais e clinicas para manutenções corretivas e preventivas, higienização e testes operacionais dos equipamentos hospitalares; Auxiliar no carregamento, descarregamento, entrega e recolhimento de equipamentos; Realizar conferência, inspeção e verificação do funcionamento dos equipamentos; Efetuar visitas domiciliares para inspeção dos equipamentos instalados; Auxiliar na montagem, desmontagem, organização e conservação dos equipamentos; Demais atividades pertinentes ao cargo. E se você ainda tem alguma dúvida se a Locmed é um bom lugar para se trabalhar, olha só quais são os benefícios de ser um(a) Locmediano (a): Vale transporte ou auxílio combustível (Não realizamos descontos); Vale refeição ou alimentação (Não realizamos descontos); Seguro de Vida (ICATU); Plano de saúde (Unimed e Amil); Plano odontológico; Convênio SESC; Convênio Farmácia; 20% de desconto nos produtos comercializados pela LOCMED; Incentivos Educacionais; Wellhub (antes Gympass); Programas de Qualidade de Vida (Psicoterapia na empresa). Esperamos que o time da Assistência possa ter você como parte dele em breve!! title: Consultant SAP Digital Manufacturing (all genders)
description: Wir gehören zu den Top-Adressen in der IT-Welt und fokussieren uns auf die Optimierung der Kerngeschäfts­prozesse unserer Kunden. Durch den smarten Einsatz maßgeschneiderter IT-Lösungen unterstützen wir unsere Kunden dabei, das Beste aus ihrem Business und aktuellen Technologien herauszuholen. Unseren Erfolg aber erreichen wir nur durch eins: die Menschen bei adesso! Sie bringen Ideen, fachliches Know how und Technologien zusammen. Gemeinsam programmieren wir (deine) Zukunft bei adesso. DEINE ROLLE Produktionsanalysen: Du führst Produktionsanalysen (auch auf dem Shopfloor) durch, erhebst Anforderungen und gestaltest Digitalisierungs-Use-Cases. Strategische Beratung: Du berätst unsere Kunden zu dem sinnvollen Einsatz von Software in der Produktion und hilfst ihnen dabei die richtige Lösung für ihre Herausforderungen zu finden. Fachkonzepte: Du entwickelst Fachkonzepte für die SAP Digital Manufacturing und setzt diese in Projekten um. Projektleitung: Du leitest Projekte und begeisterst Stakeholder für den Erfolg der digitalen Transformation. Wissenstransfer: Du teilst dein Wissen durch Blogbeiträge, Vorträge und Schulungen und stärkst das interne Know-how. Sales Support: Du unterstützt das Sales-Team bei der Akquise spannender, neuer Projekte. DEIN PROFIL Abschluss: Du hast einen Abschluss (Studium oder Ausbildung) im Bereich (Wirtschafts-) Informatik, (Wirtschafts-) Ingenieurwesen, Produktionstechnik, Maschinenbau, Elektrotechnik oder vergleichbare Kenntnisse. SAP-Erfahrung: Du bringst 5 Jahre Erfahrungen mit der Implementierung von SAP ME / SAP DM mit. Maschinendatenerfassung: Du kennst Standards wie OPC-UA und hast erste Erfahrungen mit SAP PCo oder dem Production Connector. Moderation: Du kannst Workshops leiten, Präsentationen in PowerPoint erstellen und Kalkulationen in Excel durchführen. Projektleitung: Du hast bereits (Teil-)Projekte erfolgreich geführt und Stakeholder überzeugt. KI Kompetenz: Du setzt KI-Tools in deinem Fachgebiet souverän ein, bewertest die Ergebnisse kritisch und unterstützt Kunden und adessi dabei, Potenziale von KI zu nutzen. Sprachkenntnisse: Deine sehr guten Deutsch- und guten Englischkenntnisse ermöglichen dir eine sichere Kommunikation. Unser Versprechen: Du wirst dich bei uns wohlfühlen! Kollegial, familiär und auf Augenhöhe – wir leben Austausch, Teamgeist und einen respektvollen Umgang miteinander. Diversität und verschiedene Blickwinkel werden bei uns genauso geschätzt wie du als Mensch. Das und viel mehr steht für unser ganz besonderes Wir-Gefühl. Für das es sogar ein Wort gibt: adessi. Unsere Kultur und das Zusammenarbeiten sind geprägt von gegenseitiger Wertschätzung, Anerkennung und Unterstützung. Das verbindet uns hierarchieübergreifend – auch im Home-Office. So belegen wir zum wiederholten Mal den 1. Platz beim Wettbewerb „Deutschlands Beste Arbeitgeber in der ITK“!. Unser umfangreiches Trainingsangebot und ein transparentes Laufbahnstufenmodell für alle sorgen dafür, dass deine Entwicklung bei uns nicht stillsteht. Denn gemeinsam zu wachsen und Chancengeber zu sein, das ist programmiert in unserer DNA. title: AI & Automation Consultant SAP (all genders)
description: DEINE ROLLE KI greifbar machen: Als AI & Automation Consultant bildest du die Brücke zwischen Fachbereichen und Technologie: Du identifizierst KI- und Automatisierungs-Use-Cases, erstellst Business Cases und begleitest die Umsetzung von der Idee bis in den produktiven Betrieb. Use-Cases entlang der SAP-Prozesse erkennen: Entlang der SAP-Prozesslandschaft (z. B. Finance, Supply Chain, Procurement, HR) spürst du Potenziale auf, priorisierst Use-Cases und übersetzt sie in konkrete Maßnahmen. Workshops & Discovery-Sessions gestalten: In Formaten zu SAP Business AI, Joule Agents, Document AI und SAP Build Process Automation arbeitest du gemeinsam mit unseren Kund:innen an tragfähigen Lösungen. Joule-Lösungen konzipieren: Du verantwortest die fachliche Konzeption von Joule Skills und Agents und begleitest deren Konfiguration in Joule Studio gemeinsam mit dem Development-Team – inklusive Anbindung von MCP-Servern. Roadmaps und Datenfundament definieren: Du entwickelst Roadmaps und Business Cases für KI-Initiativen und berätst zu SAP Business Data Cloud (inkl. Datasphere) als Basis für datengetriebene Anwendungen. Change aktiv begleiten: Durch Schulungen, Demos und engen Austausch mit Fachanwender:innen treibst du das Change Management voran und stärkst die Akzeptanz von KI-Lösungen. DEIN PROFIL Qualifikation: Ein abgeschlossenes Studium der (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, Data Science, Computer Science oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation bildet deine Basis. Beratungserfahrung: Idealerweise verfügst du über Erfahrung in der SAP-Beratung, IT-Beratung oder einer ähnlichen Rolle mit Schwerpunkt Digitalisierung und Prozessoptimierung. KI-Verständnis: Begriffe wie LLM, GenAI, RAG und MCP kannst du erklären, einordnen und weißt, wann welcher Ansatz sinnvoll ist. Tool-Erfahrung: Du verfügst über erste fundierte praktische Erfahrungen mit SAP; idealerweise hast du bereits mit SAP S/4HANA gearbeitet. Berührungspunkte mit SAP Business AI / Joule, SAP BTP oder Document AI sind ebenfalls von Vorteil. Erfahrungen mit vergleichbaren Plattformen – etwa Microsoft Copilot Studio, Power Automate oder Google Vertex AI – erleichtern dir den Einstieg zusätzlich. Brücke zwischen Welten: Komplexe KI-Themen bereitest du so auf, dass Fachbereiche sie verstehen – du fühlst dich sowohl in der Business- als auch in der IT-Welt zuhause. Kommunikationstalent: Du kommunizierst sicher auf Deutsch und Englisch, intern sowie extern. Unser Versprechen: Du wirst dich bei uns wohlfühlen! Kollegial, familiär und auf Augenhöhe – wir leben Austausch, Teamgeist und einen respektvollen Umgang miteinander. Diversität und verschiedene Blickwinkel werden bei uns genauso geschätzt wie du als Mensch. Das und viel mehr steht für unser ganz besonderes Wir-Gefühl. Für das es sogar ein Wort gibt: adessi. Unsere Kultur und das Zusammenarbeiten sind geprägt von gegenseitiger Wertschätzung, Anerkennung und Unterstützung. Das verbindet uns hierarchieübergreifend – auch im Home-Office. So belegen wir zum wiederholten Mal den 1. Platz beim Wettbewerb „Deutschlands Beste Arbeitgeber in der ITK“!. Unser umfangreiches Trainingsangebot und ein transparentes Laufbahnstufenmodell für alle sorgen dafür, dass deine Entwicklung bei uns nicht stillsteht. Denn gemeinsam zu wachsen und Chancengeber zu sein, das ist programmiert in unserer DNA. title: AI Technical Architect — SAP & Core Business Processes (Remote)
description: Arxada is a global leader in innovative solutions that protect our world. Our groundbreaking technologies, in-depth regulatory know-how, manufacturing and process development help our customers to safeguard nutrition, health and infrastructure efficiently through chemistry and biotechnology that enhance sustainability. We offer a broad portfolio of ingredients and services for multiple end markets that include Human Health & Nutrition, Home & Personal Care, Professional Hygiene, Paints & Coatings and Wood Protection. With customers in more than 100 countries, the company achieved sales of CHF 2 billion in 2024. Headquartered in Basel (Switzerland), Arxada employs 3,200 associates across 24 production sites and 14 R&D centers, all committed to our customers’ success. As an AI Technical Architect — SAP & Core Business Processes you will drive the design, build, and industrialization of AI capabilities across Arxada’s SAP landscape. Acting as a hands-on technical leader, you will bring AI into core business processes—embedding intelligence directly into finance, supply chain, procurement, and manufacturing operations. You will own architecture and delivery of process‑embedded AI solutions, ensuring they are scalable, compliant, and aligned with a clean‑core S/4HANA strategy. Partnering closely with business and technology stakeholders, you will translate complex requirements into production‑ready solutions while mentoring a small team as a player‑coach. This is a unique opportunity to shape the future of AI within a global organization, combining innovation with real operational impact. Key Responsibilities Design, build, and deploy AI use cases across the SAP estate using SAP BTP, AI Core, Joule, and Datasphere Own end‑to‑end architecture for process‑embedded AI across finance, treasury, supply chain, procurement, sales, and warehouse operations Ensure clean‑core principles by developing side‑by‑side extensions on SAP BTP, protecting the S/4HANA roadmap Integrate master data (PLM/MDM) as a trusted foundation for AI use cases and data-driven decision making Connect plant and MES signals into ERP processes, enabling end‑to‑end operational visibility and analytics Embed compliant and auditable AI solutions within regulated environments (e.g. GAMP 5, 21 CFR Part 11, EU Annex 11) Lead and mentor a small team of engineers and functional experts, driving delivery and technical excellence Manage AI lifecycle, MLOps practices, and technical debt to ensure scalable, maintainable, and cost-efficient solutions Qualifications & Experience 8+ years of experience in SAP architecture/engineering with a strong hands‑on delivery track record Deep expertise in SAP technologies including BTP, AI Core, Datasphere, and Business AI / Joule Strong understanding of core business processes across finance, supply chain, procurement, and sales Proven experience with SAP integration and extensibility (Integration Suite/CPI, side‑by‑side architecture) Solid coding and MLOps capabilities, with experience designing AI solutions in regulated environments Experience leading engineering or cross-functional teams as a hands-on “player‑coach” Strong ownership mindset with the ability to drive initiatives end‑to‑end Experience with S/4HANA transformation and migration programs Exposure to GxP or other regulated environments and SAP MDM/PLM Experience integrating MES and ERP systems in manufacturing environments Fluency in English; Spanish is an advantage Arxada has world class offering in two distinct areas: Microbial Control Solutions (MCS) focuses on threats posed by microorganisms to people and planet through five distinct business lines including Professional Hygiene, Home & Personal Care, Wood Protection, Paints & Coatings and Material Protection. Nutrition, Care & Environmental (NCE), meanwhile, provides materials used to manufacture composites for electronics, aerospace and other markets, as well as vitamins and nutritional ingredients, chemicals and performance intermediates, and raw materials for a wide range of high-performance applications. Arxada is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, status as a qualified individual with disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. description: At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence. We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go. Join EY and help to build a better working world. Service line: SAP - Position Title: BTP Developer – Manager. At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence. We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go. Join EY and help to build a better working world. About Global Delivery Services Global Delivery Services refers to EY's worldwide network of service delivery centers. The GDS team plays an important role in EY’s strategy by ensuring effective support to EY’s growth agenda. Our journey started in 2002 with approximately 200 people. Today we stand at 80,000+ professionals in ten locations around the world. We operate in Argentina, China, Hungary, India, Philippines, Poland, Sri Lanka, Mexico, Spain and the United Kingdom. Client service is focused on providing Consulting, Assurance, Tax, Strategy & Transactions, and Knowledge support to our clients around the world. The teams enable account teams worldwide to provide seamless, high-quality, value-added support, helping deliver exceptional client service. Enablement Services provides cost-effective, high-skilled, and innovative services to support EY’s global and local enablement teams. Markets, BMC, AWS, Finance and Accounting, Risk Management, Procurement, People Shared Services, IT Service Delivery and IT Global Infrastructure services, are among the services offered by Enablement Services. Our innovation specialists serve the GDS Client Service and Enablement Services teams, along with Service Lines, Core Business Services and Sectors. The team brings the desired environment, technologies and skilled teams together for facilitation, rapid prototyping and innovative thinking. The competencies offered include analytics, digital, user experience, mobile technology, infrastructure, Microsoft technologies and open innovation. The Opportunity We are seeking an experienced SAP Developer with 7 to 11 years of hands-on expertise in SAP technologies, particularly SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), Core Data Services (CDS), ABAP, and SAPUI5/Fiori. In this role, you will be responsible for building, enhancing, and maintaining SAP applications leveraging these technologies. Your Key Responsibilities Lead decision-making processes related to SAP application development and optimization. Manage and optimize resources effectively to ensure timely and quality delivery of tasks. Oversee team management including personnel allocation, task assignment, and performance evaluation. Ensure the team is equipped with the necessary tools and support to achieve organizational objectives. Drive process improvements and foster a collaborative team environment. Skills And Attributes For Success Proficient in ABAP, Object-Oriented ABAP, and BAdI implementations. Experience with SAP Gateway and HANA Cloud Connector. Strong knowledge of data provisioning and testing OData services. Familiarity with SAP Workflow. Understanding of SAP Basis and Authorization concepts. Ideal Candidate Profile Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (BE/BTech), MCA, or equivalent with 7-11 years of relevant industry experience. Hands-on experience in SAPUI5 and Fiori elements-based app development and front-end extensions. Skilled in developing Core Data Services (CDS). Expertise in developing and consuming RESTful services using SAP BTP. Experience in extending standard Fiori applications. Basic understanding of SAP BTP and its services. Good to have experience in S/4 key user extensions, developer extensions, and Side-by-Side extensions using SAP BTP. Strong analytical skills, problem-solving capability, and attention to detail. Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage stakeholders at all levels. Proven ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team. SAP certifications in relevant technologies (e.g., SAP Certified Development Associate) are preferred but not mandatory. What We Offer You At EY, we’ll develop you with future-focused skills and equip you with world-class experiences. We’ll empower you in a flexible environment, and fuel you and your extraordinary talents in a diverse and inclusive culture of globally connected teams. Learn more. Are you ready to shape your future with confidence? Apply today. To help create an equitable and inclusive experience during the recruitment process, please inform us as soon as possible about any disability-related adjustments or accommodations you may need. Our Commitment : As a commitment, we persistently endeavour to embody our values, fulfil our purpose, and champion inclusiveness. Our dedication is to cultivate EY into an environment where diverse perspectives are celebrated, creating a supportive atmosphere for individuals to authentically be themselves and contribute their utmost. Professional Development : From entry-level employees to senior leaders, we believe in continuous learning. We offer opportunities to build new skills, take on leadership roles, and connect and grow through mentorship. People and Culture : In our dynamic workplace, diversity, equity, and inclusiveness are ingrained in our culture. We're united by a commitment to create an environment where every individual's differences are valued, practices are equitable, fostering a sense of belonging. Our shared values include integrity, respect, teaming, inclusiveness, energy, enthusiasm, courage to lead, and building relationships based on doing the right thing. Benefits : Embark on a transformative career journey with us and indulge in a suite of premium benefits, encompassing exclusive health and wellness packages, enticing rewards, and cutting-edge learning opportunities that empower you to continually grow and excel in your professional and personal development. How to Apply: If you are passionate to join us and are aligned with our commitment to building a better working world, we invite you to apply by completing our user-friendly form with personal and professional information and by providing your consent to data privacy. Successful candidates advance to a competency-based interview. If mutual interest persists, a job offer awaits. “We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to Diversity, Equity & Inclusion”. EY | Building a better working world EY is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets. Enabled by data, AI and advanced technology, EY teams help clients shape the future with confidence and develop answers for the most pressing issues of today and tomorrow. EY teams work across a full spectrum of services in assurance, consulting, tax, strategy and transactions. Fueled by sector insights, a globally connected, multi-disciplinary network and diverse ecosystem partners, EY teams can provide services in more than 150 countries and territories. title: Senior Executive
description: Position: Senior Executive – Account payable Location: Kolkata Industry: FMCG Job Summary: We are looking for a detail-oriented and process-driven Accounts Payable professional with 5–7 years of experience in Indian accounting, MIS reporting, and bank reconciliations. The ideal candidate should have strong knowledge of accounting controls, financial reporting, compliance, and ERP systems, preferably SAP. Key Responsibilities Accounting & Financial Controls Post provisions and other accounting entries for month-end and year-end closure. Ensure accurate recording of transactions and preparation of debit notes. Monitor and follow up on outstanding recoveries related to rent & maintenance, electricity, municipal taxes, IT services, royalty income, foreign travel, and other miscellaneous expenses. Review model agreement files and supporting documents. Maintain accounting records in compliance with company policies and statutory requirements. Bank Reconciliation Prepare periodic Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all operating bank accounts. Investigate and resolve unreconciled transactions in a timely manner. Coordinate with banks and internal stakeholders to resolve discrepancies. Maintain proper documentation and audit trails for reconciliation activities. Follow up with relevant teams for clearance of pending reconciliation items. Financial Reporting Support monthly, quarterly, and annual financial closing activities. Maintain accruals and provisions related to payables. Assist in balance sheet reconciliations in coordination with the General Ledger and Finance teams. Prepare financial reports and schedules required for quarterly and annual audits. MIS Reporting Prepare and publish periodic MIS reports on Accounts Payable performance. Track KPIs such as invoice processing turnaround time (TAT), aging analysis, pending liabilities, and spend analysis. Prepare monthly and year-to-date publicity reports with budget vs. previous year comparisons. Provide analytical insights to support management decision-making. Support internal and statutory audits through accurate reporting and data analysis. Required Skills & Qualifications 5–7 years of relevant experience in Accounts Payable, Indian Accounting, MIS Reporting, and Bank Reconciliation. Bachelor's degree in Commerce, Finance, Accounting, or a related discipline. Strong understanding of Indian accounting principles, financial reporting, and internal controls. Hands-on experience with SAP ERP (preferred). Show more Show less Seniority level Associate Employment type Full-time Job function Accounting/Auditing and Finance Industries Consumer Goods

description: About Setel: The Future of Mobility Introduced in July 2018, Setel is a mobile platform that aims to delight customers by innovating for better, inclusive mobility. Setel serves customers across Malaysia by powering one app as the constant companion to ease motorists’ journey across fueling, parking, EV charging, motor insurance, road tax, auto assistance, general purchases, and more across an ecosystem of PETRONAS petrol stations, retail partners, and online merchants. In this role you will: Execute daily reconciliations related to e-wallet transactions to ensure accuracy and completeness. Perform monthly bank reconciliations to ensure alignment with accounting records. Assist with research, data entry, filing, and maintaining accurate financial records. Support the preparation of regulatory reports (e.g. balance sheet, income statement, and other statutory documents). Work with accounting software. Handle sensitive and confidential financial information with integrity and professionalism. Collaborate with accounting, data, and cross-functional teams to compile and analyze financial data. Take on additional tasks or projects to deepen understanding of e-money operations and finance processes. Assist with any related tasks, projects, and other assigned duties as and when deemed necessary. Ensure adherence to the compliance of company policies, industry regulations and legal requirements. You're a great fit if you have/are: A bachelor’s degree in Finance/Accountancy/Banking/Business Studies/Administration/Management/Economics or equivalent. Able to be join us in September 2026 for 6 months. Proficiency in MS Word, MS Excel. Knowledge in SAP. Conceptual understanding of computer systems and visualization. Analytical skills and have the ability to work under pressure. The ability express ideas verbally (and follow application information with people from other areas). Strong organizational skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines. Ability to handle sensitive information with confidentiality. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. What Makes Working With Us Awesome Our people and culture: You will get to work with awesome and friendly colleagues to whom you can expect to collaborate well to deliver your work. Empowerment is given and you will get a lot of opportunities for peer-learning. Availability of tools and applications: You will be provided with different tools to facilitate your work. Automate your work whenever possible so that you can focus on delivering impact for your role. Development focused: Your learning and growth matters most for us. We are people centric and always ready to help our people to define what they want to make an impact on and craft their learning plan accordingly. Cool Perks/ Benefits Our office elevates our values on the importance of collaboration & engagement. We want our work to be fun and to support this, we have spacious collaboration spaces, a well-stocked kitchen and gaming area. Hybrid work arrangement. Exciting sport and engagement activities to strengthen employee bonding. Personal Data Protection Setel Ventures Sdn Bhd (“Setel”, “we”, “our” “us”) is committed to protecting and respecting your privacy. This Setel privacy statement (“Privacy Statement”) explains what personal data we collect about you, when and why we collect it, how we use it, the conditions under which we may disclose it to others, your rights to your personal data and how we keep it secure. This Privacy Statement covers both our online and offline collection activities, including personal data that we collect through online platforms such as websites, applications, third party social networks or our online and physical events, or through other third parties that we work with. Please read this carefully to understand our views and practices regarding your personal data. title: Consultant junior audit IT (F/H)
description: Quel sera votre rôle dans la #TeamDeloitte ? Postes à pourvoir en septembre ou fin novembre 2026. Postes basés à Paris-La Défense. Tous nos postes sont ouverts au télétravail. Rejoindre Deloitte, c'est dire oui à une expérience qui a du sens, celle où les rencontres et les missions auprès de nos clients vous poussent vous développer chaque jour. C’est évoluer dans un environnement de travail fondé sur la confiance, la transmission et l’intelligence collective, une entreprise labelisée Great Place to Work , attachée au bien-être, et à l’inclusion. Vous intégrez nos équipes en tant que consultant junior audit IT. Vous travaillez en étroite collaboration avec nos clients de grands groupes nationaux et internationaux. Le pôle IT & Analytics fournit des services d’audit et de conseil en gestion des risques informatiques dans des environnements souvent complexes et des thématiques variées (environnement ERP, digitalisation, développement de la virtualisation et du Cloud, environnements Agiles et Dev-Ops, RPA, Intelligence Artificielle, externalisation de la gestion des SI…). Son activité est un métier clé pour Deloitte et elle contribue ainsi directement à la transformation du métier de l’audit et du conseil via des approches et des technologies innovantes, centrées sur la gestion des risques. Vos missions sont?: Participer à l’identification et à l’évaluation des risques informatiques dans le cadre de missions d’audit et de conseil ; Contribuer à la sélection et à la personnalisation d'approches, de méthodes et d'outils de gestion des risques IT ; Garantir le niveau de qualité des livrables et assurer une forte valeur ajoutée à nos clients ; Développer de manière pro-active vos compétences au travers des formations, du mentorat et des interactions avec les clients ; Participer à des projets mêlant des expertises de différentes activités (Data Analytics, IA, Contrôle interne, etc.).En rejoignant Deloitte, vous aurez l’opportunité de développer un set de compétences structurées autour des dimensions suivantes : leadership, métier et spécialité. Grâce aux missions variées auxquelles vous participerez et aux programmes de formations proposés, vous pourrez renforcer progressivement ces compétences, en acquérir de nouvelles et évoluer ainsi au sein de notre firme. Au-delà de vos compétences techniques, vous disposez des qualités suivantes : Capacité à travailler en équipe pluridisciplinaire et en mode projet Qualité rédactionnelle Rigueur, organisation, curiosité intellectuelle Sens de la communication, esprit d’équipe et collectif, adaptabilité Et si c’était vous ? Vous êtes titulaire d’un diplôme Bac +5 (école de commerce, institut d’études politiques ou université). Vous disposez de connaissances et d'une appétence forte pour les ERP (solutions majeures du marché telles que SAP et Oracle), la RPA (Robotic Process Automation), le Cloud et la gestion de projet IT. Une double compétence en gestion et système d'information sera particulièrement appréciée. Votre anglais est courant, à l’écrit comme à l’oral. #ALLIN : Tous les candidats ont leur place chez Deloitte sans aucune distinction : nous croyons en un environnement inclusif. title: Consultant, Functional Transformation SAP
description: Our Deloitte Enterprise Performance team is at the forefront of enterprise technology, working across finance, supply chain, and IT operations to deliver holistic performance improvement and digital transformation. Join our team of strategic advisers and architects, differentiated by our industry depth to collaborate with leading solution providers and leverage your experience in strategy, process design, technology enablement, and operational services to enable heart-of-the-business solutions. Recruiting for this role ends on 08/31/2026. Work you'll do As a Consultant, Functional Transformation on the GPS SAP business transformation team, you will be responsible for supporting SAP-enabled transformation initiatives across the implementation lifecycle. Support SAP implementation and modernization efforts, including SAP S/4HANA programs and ECC-to-S/4HANA transformation activities. Configure SAP functionality to support business process requirements and fit-to-standard design decisions. Document requirements, assumptions, risks, issues, and decisions in collaboration with business, technical, integration, and data teams. Support process design workshops, integrated testing, and deployment activities, including unit testing, system integration testing, user acceptance testing, cutover, and stabilization. Develop, maintain, and govern implementation deliverables, including solution design documents, configuration rationale, test scripts, cutover inputs, deployment readiness materials, and status updates. A successful candidate would possess these skills: Ability to work independently and collaborate as part of a team Effective written and verbal communication skills Meticulous attention to detail and quality of work product Ability to build and sustain professional relationships Ability to lead projects or workstreams Ability to manage and prioritize multiple tasks in a fast-paced and dynamic environment Strong interpersonal skills and professional demeanor Ability to meet deadlines Ability to provide clear guidance to others The team Deloitte's Government & Public Services (GPS) practice - our people, ideas, technology and outcomes - is designed for impact. Serving federal, state, & local government clients as well as public higher education institutions, our team of professionals brings fresh perspective to help clients anticipate disruption, reimagine the possible, and fulfill their mission promise. Our SAP business transformation services drive to improve performance and value delivered by the full suite of SAP solutions. Qualifications Required: Bachelor's degree and ability to obtain and maintain a Secret Clearance 2+ years of experience working in regulated environments with enterprise resource planning controls, audit readiness, and compliance requirements 2+ years of experience producing or governing implementation deliverables, including solution design documentation, configuration rationale, test artifacts, cutover plans, and deployment readiness materials 1+ years of experience delivering SAP implementations, including SAP S/4HANA transformation work 1+ years of experience configuring SAP S/4HANA and/or SAP ECC Ability to travel 25%, on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sectors you serve. Must be legally authorized to work in the United States without the need for employer sponsorship, now or at any time in the future. Preferred: Active Secret Clearance Experience supporting Department of Defense and/or U.S. Federal civilian agency enterprise resource planning modernization programs The wage range for this role takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; licensure and certifications; and other business and organizational needs. The disclosed range estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the position may be filled. At Deloitte, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is $82,600 to $162,800. You may also be eligible to participate in a discretionary annual incentive program, subject to the rules governing the program, whereby an award, if any, depends on various factors, including, without limitation, individual and organizational performance. title: Material Sciences - Graduates - AI Training - Leeds, UK
description: Material Sciences - Graduates - AI Training - UK About Prolific Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are building the biggest pool of quality human data in the world. Over 35,000 AI developers, researchers, and organizations use Prolific to gather data from paid study participants with a wide variety of experiences, knowledge, and skills. The role We're looking for Materials Science Specialists to join our Expert Network to help train and evaluate cutting-edge AI models using real scientific expertise. If you have a background in materials research, engineering, or technical analysis, we'll send you a quick 10- to 15-minute test to assess your skills and suitability for AI tasks. If successful, you'll be invited to join Prolific as a participant, where you'll get paid to train and evaluate powerful AI models. Researchers looking for your skills tend to pay $40+/hr, depending on skills and experience level . You must be prepared to complete paid tasks that require one hour of uninterrupted work , though many are shorter. What you'll bring Educational Background: at minimum, a BSc (Bachelor of Science) in Materials Science, Metallurgy, Chemical Engineering, or a closely related STEM field. Technical Literacy: deep interest or experience in materials research, including the ability to interpret phase diagrams, crystalline structures, and property data. Analytical Rigor: strong attention to detail and the ability to concentrate on complex technical tasks for extended periods. Professional Verification: a valid LinkedIn profile or professional history to verify your credentials during the screening process. A PayPal account to receive payment from our clients What you'll be doing in the role AI Evaluation & Ranking: comparing multiple AI-generated technical explanations or research summaries and ranking them based on scientific accuracy and logic. Strategic Document Audit: reviewing Materials Science scientific papers alongside AI-generated graphical abstracts to identify inaccuracies or misrepresentations. Fact-Checking: verifying that the AI-generated data (formulas, material properties, and experimental results) faithfully represent the source scientific documentation. Model Alignment: providing high-level feedback to help AI models produce "enterprise-ready" corporate and scientific intelligence. Technical Verification: auditing the structural integrity of summaries involving complex chemical compositions and material behaviors. Key Technologies Generative AI (LLMs): training and fine-tuning models specialized in STEM and corporate intelligence. Scientific Visualization: evaluating AI-generated graphical abstracts and data visualizations. Research Databases: navigating technical literature to provide "ground truth" data. Why Prolific is a great platform to join as a Participant Joining our platform as a Prolific participant will give you the chance to influence the AI models of the future using professional legal expertise. Once you pass our assessment, you can join Prolific in just 15 minutes, and start enjoying competitive pay rates, flexible hours, and the ability to work from home. We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and companies with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high-quality, ethically sourced human behavioural data and feedback. This data is the cornerstone of developing more accurate, nuanced, and aligned AI systems. We believe that the next leap in AI capabilities won't come solely from scaling existing models, but from integrating diverse human perspectives and behaviours into AI development. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of AI innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity. Links to more information on Prolific Website Youtube Privacy Statement By submitting your application, you agree that Prolific may collect your personal data for recruiting and global organisation planning. Prolific's Candidate Privacy Notice explains what personal information Prolific may process, where Prolific may process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over Prolific use of your personal personal information. description: Role Description The Site Deployment Lead is responsible for ensuring full deployment readiness at the assigned manufacturing site within the program. Acting as the central coordination point, this role oversees all on-site deployment activities and ensures alignment between local teams, leadership, and the broader program organization. The position is highly execution-focused, requiring strong on-the-ground leadership to drive ERP implementation, coordinate cross-functional activities, and ensure successful adoption of new processes and systems in a manufacturing environment. Responsibilitie sTake full ownership of deployment readiness for the assigned site(s) .Coordinate local execution across all functional workstreams, including Make, Maintain, Quality, Plan, Deliver, Finance, and R&D .Lead and drive key deployment activities such as testing, end-user training, and cutover preparation and execution .Identify, track, and manage risks, issues, and dependencies, ensuring timely mitigation .Act as the primary point of contact for all deployment-related matters at site level .Align and engage stakeholders ranging from operators and key users to site leadership and transformation management .Ensure effective collaboration between departments, even without direct hierarchical authority .Support the implementation of the new ERP system by translating plans into practical execution on the shop floor .Oversee the development, implementation, and stabilization of processes and work instructions .Drive adoption and motivate teams through change, particularly where immediate benefits may not be evident . Requiremen tsMinimum of 7 years’ experience in supply chain transformation, ERP implementation, or large-scale project deliver y.Proven track record with SAP deployments, system cutovers, or business transformation program s.Experience in manufacturing environments is strongly preferre d.Demonstrated ability to lead execution-focused initiatives in complex, multi-stakeholder environment s.Hands-on, action-oriented mindset with a strong focus on delivery and result s.Strong stakeholder management skills across all organizational levels (operators, key users, management, and senior leadership ).Ability to influence and drive alignment without formal authorit y.Excellent coordination, planning, and time management skill s.Strong interpersonal skills: able to challenge, motivate, and engage teams effectivel y.Resilient, pragmatic, and solution-driven approac h.Positive and motivating attitude, with a strong focus on enabling change adoptio n. title: Senior Manager, ERP and Master Data
description: Working with Us Challenging. Meaningful. Life-changing. Those aren’t words that are usually associated with a job. But working at Bristol Myers Squibb is anything but usual. Here, uniquely interesting work happens every day, in every department. From optimizing a production line to the latest breakthroughs in cell therapy, this is work that transforms the lives of patients, and the careers of those who do it. You’ll get the chance to grow and thrive through opportunities uncommon in scale and scope, alongside high-achieving teams. Take your career farther than you thought possible. Bristol Myers Squibb recognizes the importance of balance and flexibility in our work environment. We offer a wide variety of competitive benefits, services and programs that provide our employees with the resources to pursue their goals, both at work and in their personal lives. Read more: careers.bms.com/working-with-us. Position Summary The Senior Manager, ERP and Master Data is responsible for managing day to day activities, program oversite, and continuous improvement associated with design, development, delivery, validation, maintenance, and support of ERP Master Data at Leiden, NL Cell Therapy Facility. Duties/Responsibilities Promotes and enforces master data standard systems, policies, and procedures for the ERP platform in Leiden CTF. Promotes an environment of continuous quality and improvement of processes, standards, and procedures. Collaborate within the Supply Chain department and with manufacturing, quality control, quality assurance, IT, and global master data management to determine business requirements for the design of master data. Translate business requirements into a robust data design, which is compliant, efficient, and effective. Work closely with business owners to establish and maintain service level expectations across all functions and site systems. Continuously integrate with the business to understand current business processes and to provide ERP technical guidance that enables and supports plans and strategies. Coordinate with Global IT and other manufacturing sites on standardization of system lifecycle management in terms of implementation, support, validation, change management, and system data requirements from a local and global perspective. Utilizes a high-level of understanding of pharmaceutical manufacturing processes, procedures, controls, and GMP requirements to utilize ERP master data to best meet business needs. Coordinate the testing of master data with peers and internal customers. Develop and execute cGMP change controls and change requests for master data changes. Provide department representation on site and network projects. Lead the effort to create and maintain data management strategies related to the GMP environment and to ensure that those strategies consistently support company initiatives. Develop and provide technical training to users. Assist in the development and prioritization of testing manufacturing and supply chain related setup and data from the site ERP system and associated boundary systems. Collaborate with site management and IT to develop business requirements for business process improvements within global information systems. Coordinate with IT and other site departments to maintain supporting technologies that are compliant, efficient, effective, and reliable. Performs business impact assessment on changes to ERP system, boundary systems, and business processes. Develop risk-based testing approach by understanding how to translate business requirements into data design to ensure a compliant, efficient, and effective system. Collaborate with IT and other site departments to manage testing execution related to the ERP system (i.e., development testing, integration testing, user acceptance testing, regression testing). Act as site project coordinator as required for new systems, enhancements to current systems, and/or required regression or other testing coordinated at a global level. Provide on-call support to manufacturing and supply chain in case of emergent system issues Other responsibilities may be assigned as dictated by business needs. Reporting Relationship Reports to Associate Director, Supply Planning, Controls and ERP Master Data. This position does not have direct reports. Qualifications Proficient in MS Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Excel Advanced knowledge of cGMP, GXP regulations and industry best practices High degree of flexibility and tolerance for change Ability to take and follow direction, work in both a team environment and independently, with minimal supervision Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written. Must have the ability to work with and influence people at varying levels within the company and varying technical backgrounds. Demonstrated ability to follow SOPs and works within GMP guidelines. Proactive, highly organized, detail oriented and self-directed. Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks. Attention to detail and follow-through Ability to multi-task in a fast-paced environment Preference for a bachelor’s degree and/or equivalent with at least 5 years of directly related experience in cGMP manufacturing facility, Biotechnology, Medical Devices or Pharmaceuticals Company At least 5 years of experience with ERP/MRP Systems (i.e., SAP, Oracle, etc.). If you come across a role that intrigues you but doesn’t perfectly line up with your resume, we encourage you to apply anyway. You could be one step away from work that will transform your life and career. Uniquely Interesting Work, Life-changing Careers With a single vision as inspiring as “Transforming patients’ lives through science™ ”, every BMS employee plays an integral role in work that goes far beyond ordinary. Each of us is empowered to apply our individual talents and unique perspectives in a supportive culture, promoting global participation in clinical trials, while our shared values of passion, innovation, urgency, accountability, inclusion and integrity bring out the highest potential of each of our colleagues. On-site Protocol BMS Has An Occupancy Structure That Determines Where An Employee Is Required To Conduct Their Work. This Structure Includes Site-essential, Site-by-design, Field-based And Remote-by-design Jobs. The Occupancy Type That You Are Assigned Is Determined By The Nature And Responsibilities Of Your Role: Site-essential roles require 100% of shifts onsite at your assigned facility. Site-by-design roles may be eligible for a hybrid work model with at least 50% onsite at your assigned facility. For these roles, onsite presence is considered an essential job function and is critical to collaboration, innovation, productivity, and a positive Company culture. For field-based and remote-by-design roles the ability to physically travel to visit customers, patients or business partners and to attend meetings on behalf of BMS as directed is an essential job function. Supporting People With Disabilities BMS is dedicated to ensuring that people with disabilities can excel through a transparent recruitment process, reasonable workplace accommodations/adjustments and ongoing support in their roles. Applicants can request a reasonable workplace accommodation/adjustment prior to accepting a job offer. If you require reasonable accommodations/adjustments in completing this application, or in any part of the recruitment process, direct your inquiries to adastaffingsupport@bms.com. Visit careers.bms.com/eeo-accessibility to access our complete Equal Employment Opportunity statement. Candidate Rights BMS will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records, pursuant to applicable laws in your area. If you live in or expect to work from Los Angeles County if hired for this position, please visit this page for important additional information: https://careers.bms.com/california-residents/ Data Protection We will never request payments, financial information, or social security numbers during our application or recruitment process. Learn more about protecting yourself at https://careers.bms.com/fraud-protection. Any data processed in connection with role applications will be treated in accordance with applicable data privacy policies and regulations. If you believe that the job posting is missing information required by local law or incorrect in any way, please contact BMS at TAEnablement@bms.com. Please provide the Job Title and Requisition number so we can review. Communications related to your application should not be sent to this email and you will not receive a response. title: Senior HR Controller (m/w/d)
description: Senior HR Controller (m/w/d) ​ Leipzig/Halle ​ unbefristet ​ Vollzeit Willkommen an den Flughäfen Leipzig/Halle und Dresden! Über drei Millionen Passagiere pro Jahr nutzen unsere Flughäfen, um zu zahlreichen Zielen in Europa, Afrika und Asien zu gelangen. Aber wir sind mehr als nur ein Gateway: Wir schlagen jährlich rund 1,4 Millionen Tonnen Fracht um. Der Flughafen Leipzig/Halle ist eines der größten Luftfrachtdrehkreuze in Europa. Mit einem engagierten Team von über 1.100 Mitarbeitenden an beiden Standorten sind wir ein wichtiger Wirtschaftsmotor für die Region. In über 220 ansässigen Unternehmen in Leipzig, Halle und Dresden arbeiten über 17.200 Menschen. Kommen Sie zu uns und werden Teil unserer Flughafenfamilie. Ihre Aufgaben: Verantwortung für das strategische und operative HR Controlling sowie die kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung relevanter Steuerungsinstrumente Erstellung, Analyse und Interpretation von HR-Kennzahlen, Reports und Management-Dashboards Durchführung von Personalkostenplanungen, Forecasts und Abweichungsanalysen Entwicklung fundierter Handlungsempfehlungen für Geschäftsführung, HR-Management und Führungskräfte Sicherstellung einer transparenten und datenbasierten Unternehmenssteuerung im Personalbereich Weiterentwicklung von HR Reporting, People Analytics und Controlling-Prozessen Analyse personalwirtschaftlicher Daten sowie Identifikation von Trends, Risiken und Optimierungspotenzialen Identifikation und Umsetzung von Automatisierungs- und Digitalisierungspotenzialen innerhalb HR-relevanter Prozesse Mitarbeit an strategischen HR-Projekten und der kontinuierlichen Verbesserung von Reporting- und Analyseinstrumenten Ihr Profil: Abgeschlossenes Studium der Betriebswirtschaft, im Personalmanagement, Controlling oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung im HR-Controlling oder im Finance-/Controlling-Umfeld mit HR-Bezug Fundierte Kenntnisse im Personalcontrolling, HR-Analytics, in der Personalkostenplanung sowie im Forecasting Gute Kenntnisse im Arbeits-, Tarif-, Lohnsteuer- und Sozialversicherungsrecht sowie in den Grundlagen der Entgeltabrechnung Erfahrung im Umgang mit Rückstellungen, Abgrenzungen, HGB-Abschlüssen sowie der Analyse von Personalkosten Sehr gute Kenntnisse im Reporting, der Datenanalyse und der Aufbereitung komplexer Datenstrukturen Sicherer Umgang mit MS Excel sowie Erfahrung mit HR-Systemen (Sage, SAP) und BI-Tools (Power BI) Ausgeprägte analytische Fähigkeiten, hohe Zahlenaffinität Hohe Kommunikations-, Beratungs- und Präsentationskompetenz Unser Angebot: Einzigartiges Flughafenfeeling Attraktive Vergütung: Bezahlung nach Tarifvertrag, Urlaubsgeld, Jahressonderzahlung, vermögenswirksame Leistungen sowie eine betriebliche Altersvorsorge 30 Tage Erholungsurlaub Home Office: wir bieten die Möglichkeit des mobilen Arbeitens Eine sehr gute Anbindung mit den öffentlichen Verkehrsmitteln Nachhaltige Mobilität ist uns wichtig: wir fördern das Deutschlandticket sowie ein Jobrad Corporate Benefits, Urban Sports Club u.v.m. Kontakt: Starten Sie durch und senden Sie uns Ihre aussagekräftige Bewerbung mit Ihrem Lebenslauf und Ihren Zeugnissen. Wir freuen uns auf Sie! Mitteldeutsche Flughafen AG , Personalabteilung Leipzig/Halle – Stefanie Worch, Postfach 1, 04029 Leipzig, ✆ Telefon 0341 / 224 1411, ✉ jobs@karriereflieger.de, ℹ www.karriereflieger.de Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Full-time Job function Human Resources Industries Airlines and Aviation Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Leipzig/Halle Airport by 2x See who you know

title: Controller
description: We are a dedicated group of scientists, physicians, and businesspeople working together toward one goal: to discover and develop therapies that can unleash the power of the body’s immune system to treat and potentially cure cancer. We are passionate about the work we do every day at Agenus to deliver on the promise of immuno-oncology. If you’re looking for an inspired environment, passionate colleagues, and a culture that nurtures learning, innovation and team success, we invite you to join us. At Agenus, we are advancing innovative immunotherapies to improve outcomes for patients with difficult-to-treat cancers. We are seeking a hands-on Controller to support our growing public biotechnology organization. Reporting to the Vice President of Global Financial Operations, this role will partner across Finance, HR, Legal, and external advisors to help ensure the integrity of our financial reporting while supporting the continued growth of the organization. This role is required to be onsite in Lexington 5 days a week In The Role You Will Accounting Operations and Close Process Lead the monthly, quarterly, and annual accounting close processes, ensuring timely and accurate preparation of financial statements. Oversee general ledger accounting, journal entries, account reconciliations, consolidations, accruals, and financial statement analysis. Manage accounting for key biotechnology-related areas, including R&D expenses, clinical trial accruals, CRO and CMO costs, manufacturing-related costs, collaboration agreements, licensing arrangements, milestones, royalties, grants, and cost-sharing arrangements. Oversee accounting for equity, stock-based compensation, debt, warrants, leases, restructuring activities, and other complex financial instruments. Ensure appropriate accounting treatment for significant corporate transactions, including financing arrangements, licensing agreements, strategic collaborations, acquisitions, divestitures, restructurings, and other business development transactions. Maintain and improve accounting policies, procedures, close calendars, and documentation standards to support operational efficiency, accuracy, and scalability. SEC Reporting And Public Company Compliance Lead or support the preparation and review of SEC filings, including Forms 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, proxy statements, registration statements, earnings releases, and other investor-facing financial disclosures. Agenus maintains public SEC filings, quarterly reports, annual reports, current reports, proxy filings, and registration statements through its investor relations reporting platform. Ensure financial statements and disclosures comply with U.S. GAAP, SEC rules, Nasdaq requirements, and company policies. Partner with Legal, Investor Relations, FP&A, external counsel, and external auditors on financial disclosures, MD&A, risk factors, non-GAAP measures, subsequent events, and other reporting matters. Prepare and review technical accounting memos supporting significant accounting conclusions, including revenue recognition, collaboration accounting, debt and warrant accounting, equity transactions, leases, impairment analyses, going-concern considerations, restructuring charges, and fair-value measurements. Monitor new accounting standards and SEC developments, assess their impact on Agenus, and lead implementation as needed. Internal Controls and SOX Compliance Lead or support Agenus’ Sarbanes-Oxley compliance program, including control design, documentation, testing, remediation, and ongoing monitoring. Maintain an effective internal control environment over financial reporting. Identify process gaps, control deficiencies, and opportunities to strengthen financial governance. Partner with internal and external auditors to support control testing, audit requirements, and remediation activities. Promote a culture of accountability, accuracy, compliance, and continuous improvement across the finance organization. Audit, Tax, Treasury, and External Advisors Serve as a primary contact for external auditors and manage audit timelines, deliverables, requests, and issue resolution. Coordinate with tax advisors on income tax provision, tax compliance, R&D tax credits, transfer pricing, state and local tax matters, and other tax-related activities. Support treasury activities, including cash management, investment accounting, debt compliance, covenant reporting, and financing transaction support. Partner with valuation specialists and technical accounting advisors on complex areas such as warrants, convertible or structured debt, equity instruments, fair-value measurements, collaboration arrangements, and impairment analyses. Support board and audit committee materials, as needed. Qualifications: About you Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field. CPA required. 10+ years of progressive accounting and finance experience. Public-company accounting and SEC reporting experience required. Prior experience as a Controller, Assistant Controller, Director of Accounting or similar senior accounting role. Strong knowledge of U.S. GAAP, SEC reporting, SOX compliance, and internal controls over financial reporting. Experience working with external auditors and managing quarterly reviews and annual audits. Experience with complex accounting areas such as equity, stock-based compensation, warrants, debt, leases, revenue recognition, collaboration agreements, clinical trial accruals, and restructuring activities. Strong technical accounting skills and ability to prepare and review accounting position papers. Demonstrated ability to lead teams, manage competing priorities, and meet tight deadlines. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to explain complex accounting matters to non-accounting stakeholders. Preferred Qualifications Biotechnology, pharmaceutical, life sciences, or healthcare industry experience strongly preferred. Experience in a clinical-stage or commercial-stage public biotechnology company. Prior experience in public accounting, preferably with a Big Four or national accounting firm. Experience with collaboration, licensing, royalty, milestone, grant, manufacturing, or cost-sharing arrangements. Experience supporting capital markets transactions, debt financings, warrant issuances, strategic collaborations, or business development transactions. Familiarity with ERP systems such as Oracle and SAP Experience with equity administration platforms and SEC reporting tools. Experience supporting audit committee materials and executive-level financial reporting. Learn More About Agenus Explore our innovative pipeline and connect with us on LinkedIn . Why You’ll Love Working at Agenus Benefits We're proud to offer a comprehensive and inclusive benefits program designed to support your well-being, growth, and life outside of work: Medical, Dental & Vision Coverage – Competitive options for both US & UK employees Flexible Spending Accounts – For healthcare and dependent care expenses (US) Flexible Vacation Plan – We believe time away leads to better thinking and innovation. Care.com Memberships – Support for family care (US) Education Assistance Program – Invest in your learning and development (US) 401(k) Match Employee Stock Purchase Plan Employee Assistance Program (EAP) – Resources for mental health, well-being, and life support Reward & Recognition Program – Celebrate contributions that move the mission forward What We Believe Patients First. We’re here to make a difference—for every cancer patient. Bold & United. Big goals require strong teams. Integrity Always. Respect and honesty guide everything we do. Own It. We take responsibility—for results and how we achieve them. Always Learning. Curiosity drives innovation. Bring Your Best. Every day matters. title: Material Sciences - Graduates - AI Training - London, UK
description: Material Sciences - Graduates - AI Training - UK About Prolific Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are building the biggest pool of quality human data in the world. Over 35,000 AI developers, researchers, and organizations use Prolific to gather data from paid study participants with a wide variety of experiences, knowledge, and skills. The role We're looking for Materials Science Specialists to join our Expert Network to help train and evaluate cutting-edge AI models using real scientific expertise. If you have a background in materials research, engineering, or technical analysis, we'll send you a quick 10- to 15-minute test to assess your skills and suitability for AI tasks. If successful, you'll be invited to join Prolific as a participant, where you'll get paid to train and evaluate powerful AI models. Researchers looking for your skills tend to pay $40+/hr, depending on skills and experience level . You must be prepared to complete paid tasks that require one hour of uninterrupted work , though many are shorter. What you'll bring Educational Background: at minimum, a BSc (Bachelor of Science) in Materials Science, Metallurgy, Chemical Engineering, or a closely related STEM field. Technical Literacy: deep interest or experience in materials research, including the ability to interpret phase diagrams, crystalline structures, and property data. Analytical Rigor: strong attention to detail and the ability to concentrate on complex technical tasks for extended periods. Professional Verification: a valid LinkedIn profile or professional history to verify your credentials during the screening process. A PayPal account to receive payment from our clients What you'll be doing in the role AI Evaluation & Ranking: comparing multiple AI-generated technical explanations or research summaries and ranking them based on scientific accuracy and logic. Strategic Document Audit: reviewing Materials Science scientific papers alongside AI-generated graphical abstracts to identify inaccuracies or misrepresentations. Fact-Checking: verifying that the AI-generated data (formulas, material properties, and experimental results) faithfully represent the source scientific documentation. Model Alignment: providing high-level feedback to help AI models produce "enterprise-ready" corporate and scientific intelligence. Technical Verification: auditing the structural integrity of summaries involving complex chemical compositions and material behaviors. Key Technologies Generative AI (LLMs): training and fine-tuning models specialized in STEM and corporate intelligence. Scientific Visualization: evaluating AI-generated graphical abstracts and data visualizations. Research Databases: navigating technical literature to provide "ground truth" data. Why Prolific is a great platform to join as a Participant Joining our platform as a Prolific participant will give you the chance to influence the AI models of the future using professional legal expertise. Once you pass our assessment, you can join Prolific in just 15 minutes, and start enjoying competitive pay rates, flexible hours, and the ability to work from home. We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and companies with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high-quality, ethically sourced human behavioural data and feedback. This data is the cornerstone of developing more accurate, nuanced, and aligned AI systems. We believe that the next leap in AI capabilities won't come solely from scaling existing models, but from integrating diverse human perspectives and behaviours into AI development. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of AI innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity. Links to more information on Prolific Website Youtube Privacy Statement By submitting your application, you agree that Prolific may collect your personal data for recruiting and global organisation planning. Prolific's Candidate Privacy Notice explains what personal information Prolific may process, where Prolific may process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over Prolific use of your personal personal information. title: Ordonnanceur SAP - H/F
description: Grand Large Yachting est un groupe français de plus de 900 collaborateurs, leader mondial sur le marché spécialisé de la construction de voiliers destinés à la grande croisière sous les marques Allures Yachting, Garcia Yachts, Gunboat, ORC, Outremer et RM. Dans le cadre de notre développement à Lorient, nous recrutons notre ordonnancier SAP pour participer à la production de nos catamarans Outremer ainsi que de nos catamarans ORC . Rejoignez une entreprise qui réussit chaque jour le challenge de réunir métiers d'expertise et ambiance conviviale ! Le Poste Sous la responsabilité du Responsable de Production et en lien étroit avec le service Méthodes et les Chefs d’équipe, vos principales missions seront : Assurer la planification et l’ordonnancement des ordres de fabrication dans SAP, en fonction des plannings de production et des priorités de livraison. Suivre et ajuster les ordres de fabrication selon l’avancement réel des ateliers. Vérifier quotidiennement les pointages opérateurs (temps de présence et temps d’activité). Solder les opérations terminées et corriger les écarts éventuels dans SAP. Garantir la fiabilité des données de production et la cohérence entre les temps prévus et les temps réalisés. Profil Recherché Afin de répondre au mieux aux besoins du poste, nos attentes sont les suivantes : Créer et suivre les ordres de fabrication dans SAP. Assurer le suivi de l’avancement des opérations et replanifier si nécessaire. Contrôler les saisies de temps opérateurs et corriger les anomalies. Solder les opérations terminées dans SAP. Éditer et analyser les indicateurs de suivi (écarts de temps, productivité, retards). Transmettre les informations nécessaires aux Chefs d’équipe pour garantir le respect des délais. Participer aux réunions de production et proposer des améliorations pour optimiser l’utilisation de SAP et la fiabilité des données. Assurer une interface fluide avec les services Méthodes, Qualité, Approvisionnement et Logistique. Nous Attendons Donc De Vous Maîtrise de l’outil SAP (module PP, idéalement). Connaissances en gestion de production et en ordonnancement. Bonne maîtrise d’Excel et des outils bureautiques. Capacité à analyser des écarts de temps et proposer des actions correctives. title: Full-Time SAP Architect
description: The role involves serving as a Solution Architect and SAP Expert with deep functional knowledge, primarily focusing on SAP architecture within industries like Industrial Manufacturing and Retail. Key responsibilities include providing strategic advice to clients on SAP RISE discussions, supporting sales initiatives, and delivering SAP-related projects. The candidate will contribute to developing SAP IP, promote SAP AI and Build innovations, and assist in designing solutions for client management and local sales opportunities. Qualifications require a minimum of ten years' SAP experience, especially in architecture, with relevant certifications or hands-on expertise in SAP AI and SAP Build. Strong understanding of Enterprise Architecture, excellent communication skills, and the ability to influence cross-functional teams are essential. Applicants must be authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship. The role emphasizes integrity, professionalism, and personal responsibility in a collaborative environment, offering opportunities for professional growth and development. title: Demand Planner
description: Department: Planning Location: Los Angeles HQ Compensation: $90,000 - $110,000 / year Description SKIMS is a solutions-oriented brand creating the next generation of underwear, loungewear, shapewear, and essentials. We are setting new standards by delivering technically innovative, comfort-driven products with elevated design for everybody. At the intersection of culture, innovation, and commerce, SKIMS is a global brand redefining modern apparel. Our teams are built on creativity, collaboration, and operational excellence, and we are committed to fostering an inclusive, high-performance environment where ambitious, curious, and values-driven individuals can do the best work of their careers. As we continue to scale globally, our HQ teams play a critical role in shaping strategy, building world-class infrastructure, and driving sustainable growth across markets. We are hiring an analytical, commercially minded, and highly collaborative Demand Planner to join our Planning team. This role will own the demand forecast across multiple product categories, connecting data, product strategy, customer behavior, and channel trends into a clear demand signal for the business. The ideal candidate is curious, detail-oriented, and able to turn complex information into actionable insights that drive smarter inventory and planning decisions. Key Responsibilities · Own the demand forecast across multiple product categories, translating statistical forecasts, business trends, and commercial inputs into actionable plans. · Lead weekly forecast reviews, identifying risks, opportunities, and key changes versus plan. · Partner with Merchandising to understand launches, assortment shifts, lifecycle strategies, and category dynamics. · Collaborate with Channel Planning to ensure demand forecasts align with channel-level sales and inventory plans. · Work with Data Science to improve forecast accuracy by incorporating promotional activity, customer behavior, cannibalization risk, and other demand drivers. · Support OTB and financial planning conversations by clearly communicating forecast changes, demand trends, and inventory implications. · Analyze forecast performance, including forecast accuracy, bias, demand variability, inventory health, and trend versus plan. · Present demand insights and recommendations to cross-functional partners with clarity and confidence. · Support new product launches by evaluating historical analogs, launch plans, category trends, and channel expectations. · Evolve demand planning tools, reporting, and processes as the business continues to scale. Skills, Knowledge and Expertise · Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Supply Chain, Merchandising, Retail Management, or a related field. · 3–5 years of experience in demand planning, merchandise planning, inventory planning, forecasting, or a related analytical planning function. · Experience in retail, apparel, consumer products, or another fast-paced consumer business preferred. · Strong understanding of forecasting, inventory management, OTB planning, and retail financial metrics. · Experience working with forecast accuracy, forecast bias, demand trends, and business-driven forecast adjustments. · Advanced Microsoft Excel skills, including pivot tables, lookups, and large data set analysis. · Experience with planning systems such as o9, Blue Yonder, Anaplan, Relex, SAP IBP, Toolio, Oracle, or similar platforms. · Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to translate data into clear business recommendations. · Excellent communication skills with the ability to influence cross-functional partners. · Highly organized and comfortable managing multiple categories, priorities, and deadlines in a fast-paced environment. What We’re Looking For· A commercially minded planner who can connect data, product, and customer demand to drive smarter business decisions. · Someone who brings strong analytical judgment and can translate complex trends into clear, actionable recommendations. · A proactive problem solver who identifies risks early, challenges assumptions, and brings solutions forward. · A collaborative partner who builds trust and alignment across Merchandising, Channel Planning, Finance, Supply Planning, and Data Science. · An agile, detail-oriented individual who thrives in a fast-paced, high-growth environment with evolving priorities. · Someone who is curious, forward-thinking, and excited by the opportunity to help build a more accurate, scalable, and innovative demand planning function. Benefits, Culture and Perks Benefits and Culture Up to 100% Company Paid Healthcare (medical, dental, vision) Kind Body Fertility Benefits 401(k) savings plan with up to 4% match Unlimited PTO Full Access to LinkedIn learning Employee Discounts Perks (HQ Location) Free weekly catered lunch at HQ – M/T/W/Th Dog-Friendly office on a Thursday and Friday Free Swag Giveaways Annual Holiday Party Annual Summer Party Invitations to pop-ups and other company events Complimentary daily office snacks and beverages Show more Show less Seniority level Mid-Senior level Employment type Full-time Job function Management and Manufacturing Industries Retail Apparel and Fashion Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at SKIMS by 2x See who you know
