title: Commercial Finance Analyst
description: Job Title Commercial Finance Analyst Business Unit North America Business - GCC Location: Sun House, Mumbai At Sun Pharma, we commit to helping you “Create your own sunshine” — by fostering an environment where you grow at every step, take charge of your journey and thrive in a supportive community. Are You Ready to Create Your Own Sunshine? As you enter the Sun Pharma world, you’ll find yourself becoming ‘Better every day’ through continuous progress. Exhibit self-drive as you ‘Take charge’ and lead with confidence. Additionally, demonstrate a collaborative spirit, knowing that we ‘Thrive together’ and support each other’s journeys.” Job Summary The Commercial Finance Manager will play a critical role in driving data-driven decision-making and supporting commercial strategies through robust financial analysis and reporting. This position requires strong financial acumen, advanced Excel skills, presentation skills, and the ability to interpret complex data sets to provide actionable insights. Working hours will be between 2PM-11PM, remote option available. Role Objectives Enhance data-driven decision-making across commercial operations. Improve pricing agility to support competitive positioning. Strengthen customer and product governance through accurate reporting and analysis. Core support to US front end, at the commercial ops team. Key Responsibilities Sales & Pricing Analysis: Consolidate and analyze sales trends, pricing performance, and margin impacts. Provide actionable insights to support pricing strategies and commercial decisions. Customer Governance & Reporting: Monitor customer fill rates and service-level compliance. Prepare governance reports to ensure adherence to contractual obligations. Product Performance Reporting: Develop product one-pagers summarizing key financial and operational metrics. Support product profitability analysis and lifecycle management. Order & Contract Analytics: Analyze customer order patterns to identify efficiency opportunities. Maintain and track contractual and bid files, ensuring compliance and timely updates. Commercial Support: Assist in bid preparation and pricing proposals through financial modeling. Partner with sales and operations teams to optimize commercial performance. Maintaining the win/loss tracker. Reporting weekly sales performance along with major variance and coordinating with sales team to capture reasoning for under/over performance. Assisting in the preparation and updating of budget Working very closely with cross functional teams Other ad hoc support Forecasting: Working closely with marketing to prepare and update the customer contractual file. Assist marketing with forecasting and implementing a tool to track accuracy. Automation: Play a key role in automation of: Sale trend, including pricing and contract formulary Governance around sales performance Tracking daily orders (pre-/post invoicing) Bid management file Product one pagers Live sales performance dashboard Monthly IMS Dashboard Order release, excluding EOM products Chargeback dashboard with internal & external media to ensure the proper execution of strategies Job Requirements Educational Qualification CA/MBA in Finance, Accounting, Business, or related fields. Experience Above 3 years Strong financial analysis and modelling skills. Advanced Excel proficiency (pivot tables, complex formulas, data visualization). Familiarity with SAP or similar ERP systems (preferred). Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. Ability to work in a fast-paced, data-driven environment Your Success Matters to Us At Sun Pharma, your success and well-being are our top priorities! We provide robust benefits and opportunities to foster personal and professional growth. Join us at Sun Pharma, where every day is an opportunity to grow, collaborate, and make a lasting impact. Let’s create a brighter future together! Disclaimer: The preceding job description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employee within this classification. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and qualifications required of employees as assigned to this job. Nothing herein shall preclude the employer from changing these duties from time to time and assigning comparable duties or other duties commensurate with the experience and background of incumbent(s). description: Job Requirements Job Title: Executive/Sr. Executive - Supply Chain Data & Distribution Operations Analyst Job Type: Permanent, Full-time Function: Logistics Business: Godrej Consumer Products Ltd. Location: Mumbai, Maharashtra, India About Godrej Industries Group (GIG) At the Godrej Industries Group, we are privileged to serve over 1.1 billion consumers globally through our businesses with market leadership positions in the consumer products, real estate, agriculture, financial services and chemicals industries. www.godrejindustries.com About Godrej Consumer Products Limited (GCPL) Godrej Consumer Products is a leading emerging markets company. As part of the over 125-year young Godrej Group, we are fortunate to have a proud legacy built on the strong values of trust, integrity and respect for others. At the same time, we are growing fast and have exciting, ambitious aspirations. www.godrejcp.com About The Role As Executive/Sr. Executive, you will be responsible for mastering data management for GT distribution and discontinued SKUs, driving continuous improvement on the O9 planning platform, and building analytical capabilities to support Primary Logistics and SLOB/liquidation activities. As the distribution network evolves and system complexity grows, your position will also be pivotal in anchoring data integrity, operational visibility, and decision-support across the function. Key responsibilities GT distribution & master data management Own and manage master data for GT distribution channels, including discontinued SKU lifecycle management Ensure data accuracy, completeness, and governance across all relevant systems Transition data management activities into a structured, sustainable process Coordinate with commercial and supply chain teams to reflect real-time changes in the distribution master data O9 platform – Data management, logic & testing Manage data inputs, configurations, and business logic within the O9 planning platform Conduct rigorous testing of O9 system outputs and validate planning assumptions against ground realities Drive continuous improvement of data quality on O9 and assess downstream impact on planning outputs Partner with the O9 implementation and business teams to identify and resolve data discrepancies Primary logistics – Data analytics & automation Consolidate and own analytics related to Primary Logistics Build automated reporting and tracking mechanisms Provide actionable insights to support logistics planning, cost optimisation, and service levels SLOB analysis & liquidation support Lead analysis on Slow-Moving and Obsolete (SLOB) inventory to identify risks and liquidation opportunities Develop and maintain dashboards to track SLOB metrics, ageing inventory, and liquidation progress Work closely with commercial and supply planning teams to recommend and execute liquidation strategies Dashboard development & reporting Build and maintain operational dashboards across key supply chain metrics (logistics, SLOB, O9 outputs, distribution) Standardise reporting templates and ensure timely, accurate reporting to senior stakeholders Proactively identify gaps in data visibility and propose solutions to address them Regional warehouse (RWH) network revalidation Support the revalidation of Regional Warehouse locations in light of changes in the distribution network Analyse network data to assess efficiency, coverage, and optimisation opportunities for RWH positioning Prepare data-backed recommendations to inform strategic decisions on warehouse footprint Who are we looking for? Education Graduate / Postgraduate in Supply Chain Management, Operations, Business Analytics, or a related discipline Experience 3–6 years of relevant experience in supply chain operations, data management, or logistics analytics Hands-on experience with SAP (mandatory) for master data and transactional data management Working knowledge of O9 Solutions or similar supply chain planning platforms is strongly preferred Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, macros, data modelling) Experience with SLOB management, inventory analytics, or liquidation processes is a strong advantage Skills Skilled in data visualisation and dashboards, logistics and distribution analytics, and master data governance Strong communication skills — ability to present complex data insights to non-technical stakeholders clearly Effective stakeholder management — comfortable working across cross-functional teams including commercial, logistics, and planning Structured problem-solving with a continuous improvement mindset High ownership and accountability — ability to transition informal processes into formalised workflows Attention to detail with the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously Work Experience 4-6 years Benefits What’s in it for you? title: Senior Executive - S&OP
description: Job Requirements About Godrej Industries Group (GIG) At the Godrej Industries Group, we are privileged to serve over 1.1 billion consumers globally through our businesses with market leadership positions in the consumer products, real estate, agriculture, financial services and chemicals industries. https://www.godrejindustries.com/ About Godrej Agrovet Limited (GAVL) Godrej Agrovet is a food and agri conglomerate, dedicated to improving the productivity of Indian farmers by innovating products and services that sustainably increase crop and livestock yields. www.godrejagrovet.com Role Purpose The role is responsible for driving Supply Chain Excellence and strengthening S&OP (Sales & Operations Planning) governance across Godrej Agrovet businesses. The incumbent will collaborate with Business Unit (BU) Supply Chain, Manufacturing, Procurement, Sales, Finance, and Planning teams to improve planning maturity, operational visibility, process standardization, and overall supply chain performance. The role will act as a central coordination function to S&OP deployment & improvement, KPI governance, and continuous improvement initiatives across business units. Key Responsibilities S&OP Governance & Planning Drive standardized S&OP / IBP processes across business units. Collaborate with BU planning teams to improve forecast accuracy, supply planning discipline, and inventory visibility. Facilitate periodic reviews on demand, supply, inventory, and service level performance. Support business teams in resolving planning and operational challenges through structured problem-solving. Supply Chain Excellence Drive cross-functional supply chain excellence initiatives across planning, inventory, warehousing, logistics, and network operations. Identify opportunities to improve service levels, optimize inventory, and enhance operational efficiency. Support digitization, automation, and analytics initiatives within supply chain functions. Standardize processes, KPIs, dashboards, and reporting frameworks across businesses. Analytics & MIS Develop and manage supply chain dashboards and performance review mechanisms. Analyze supply chain KPIs and generate actionable insights for leadership reviews. Monitor key metrics including Forecast Accuracy, OTIF, Fill Rate, Inventory Days, Slow-moving Inventory, and Production Plan Adherence. Ensure data governance and reporting accuracy across ERP and planning systems. Process Improvement & Transformation Support supply chain transformation and capability-building initiatives. Participate in the implementation and enhancement of planning systems and analytics platforms. Drive process harmonization and operational governance across locations and business units Educational Qualification MBA / PGDM in Supply Chain, Operations, Agribusiness, or related field preferred. Bachelor’s degree in engineering, Operations, or related discipline. Skills & Competencies Strong understanding of S&OP / IBP and supply chain processes. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Advanced Excel and reporting capabilities. Exposure to SAP ERP and planning tools, Power BI preferred. title: Project managment -Financial Control
description: OVERVIEW KPMG entities in India are professional services firm(s). Member firms are affiliated with KPMG International Limited. KPMG was established in India in August 1993. Our professionals leverage the global network of firms, and are conversant with local laws, regulations, markets and competition. KPMG has offices across India in Ahmedabad, Bengaluru, Chandigarh, Chennai, Gurugram, Jaipur, Hyderabad, Jaipur, Kochi, Kolkata, Mumbai, Noida, Pune, Vadodara and Vijayawada .KPMG entities in India offer services to and international clients in India across sectors. We strive to provide rapid, performance-based, industry-focused and technology-enabled services, which reflect a shared knowledge of global and local industries and our experience of the business environment . Why Choose KPMG India? Dynamic Work Environm ent: Be a part of an innovative team that values creativity and fresh ideas.👨 💼Growth Opportunit ies: We provide excellent training, mentorship, and career advancement .📈Attractive Benef its: Competitive salaries, health insurance, and more! Job Summary We are seeking a detail-oriented and results-driven Project Manager – Financial Control to oversee financial governance, budgeting, forecasting, and cost control across projects. The role ensures financial discipline, transparency, and compliance while supporting project delivery through effective financial planning and monitoring. Key Responsibilities Project Financial Planning & Budgeting Develop and manage detailed project budgets, financial models, and forecasts Partner with project teams to align budgets with project scope and timelines Monitor budget utilization and identify potential financial risks early Financial Monitoring & Control Track project expenditures and ensure adherence to approved budgets Conduct variance analysis (budget vs actuals) and recommend corrective actions Implement cost control measures to improve project profitability Reporting & Analysis Prepare periodic financial reports, dashboards, and KPIs for stakeholders Provide insights on project financial performance and risks Support senior management with strategic financial decision-making Governance & Compliance Ensure compliance with company financial policies, accounting standards, and audit requirements Maintain proper documentation for financial transactions and approvals Support internal and external audits related to project finances Stakeholder Management Collaborate with finance, procurement, and project teams to ensure financial alignment Communicate financial status, risks, and opportunities to stakeholders clearly Act as a key financial advisor to project managers and business leaders Process Improvement Identify opportunities to improve financial processes, tools, and reporting mechanisms Drive automation and standardization in financial controls Support implementation of ERP or financial systems enhancements Key Skills & Competencies Strong understanding of project accounting, budgeting, and forecasting Advanced financial analysis and cost control skills Proficiency in MS Excel and financial systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, ERP tools) Strong problem-solving and analytical thinking Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills Attention to detail and high level of accuracy Qualifications & Experience Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, or related field CA / CPA / CFA / MBA (Finance) preferred 5–10 years of experience in financial control, project finance, or FP&A Experience working in project-based environments (e.g., IT, consulting, infrastructure, or manufacturing) Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Budget adherence and cost variance Forecast accuracy Project profitability / margin improvement Timeliness and accuracy of financial reporting Compliance audit results Preferred Attributes Experience in cross-functional project environments Knowledge of risk management frameworks Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously The opportunity is now! If you are interested in being part of a dynamic team, serving clients and reaching your full potential – KPMG Advisory Services is for you! Able to translate business requirements into scalable SAP solutions. Fluent German and English. In return for: 70,000-100,000 UER base plus equity and many other benefits. You will have hybrid & flexible working set up and also have the chance to shape ERP strategy from implementation through ongoing operations. Key skills: SAP / HANA4 / Public / Cloud / ERP / SAP Operations / Governance / Continuous Improvement / Stakeholders / Defense / Police / Robotics / AI / Platform / Aerospace Disclaimer: Attis Global Ltd is an equal opportunities employer. title: SAP MM Analyst (m/f/x)
description: Your Role As SAP MM Analyst, you will play a dual role in our Tech organisation. You will ensure the stability and reliability of our financial core while simultaneously acting as a functional lead in high-impact projects. You will be the bridge between finance and our broader tech ecosystem, ensuring that our End-to-End processes remain scalable and standardised. Your Mission Act as the first point of contact for MM operations (Purchasing, Inventory, Vendor Master Data, Invoice Verification), identifying root causes across work-streams and applying structured analysis to resolve them independently. Partner with business teams, including Fleet and Operations, in order to own and evolve FINN's Procure-to-Pay process end-to-end, from purchase requisition through goods receipt to invoice posting, ensuring tight integration with FI-AP. Continuously monitor and improve procurement workflows, ensuring they align with SAP Global Standards and Clean Core principles. Support and enable others within the team by documenting best practices for lean development and system usage. Drive the implementation of technical solutions within larger business projects, overseeing initial design, integration, and deployment — including the strategic re-implementation of standard P2P at FINN. Support the design of modern integrations using SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to connect S/4HANA with procurement platforms and internal tools. Challenge the status quo. Proactively look for configuration, release strategies, or automation solutions that save time and enhance the requester and approver experience in SAP. Your Profile 3+ years of hands-on experience in SAP MM. Solid understanding of the MM-FI integration (automatic account determination, GR/IR, tax handling, asset accounting) and MM-SD touchpoints. Proven experience with interface design in SAP Integration Suites (CPI, BTP). You are obsessed with SAP Standards and shy away from custom solutions in order to keep the system maintainable. You actively advocate for returning to standard. You have experience in full-cycle implementations or major roll-outs (ideally including a P2P or procurement rollout) and feel comfortable moving between deep technical tasks and high-level project management. You are proactive, decisive, and grow stronger by solving complex problems in a fast-paced environment. Excellent communication and collaboration skills to work effectively with procurement, finance, and cross-functional tech teams. Highly analytical and data-driven, coupled with customer obsession and eagerness to work in a fast paced and impact-driven environment. Why FINN? Remote Work: You have the flexibility to work either in our Munich office or remotely ±3 hours of the Munich time zone (CET/ CEST) within the EU. Shared Success (VSOP Equity Program): We win together. Through our Virtual Stock Option Program, you benefit directly from the long-term growth and success of FINN. Fuel your Growth (3.000€ Development Budget): Standing still is not an option. With your annual development budget for further training and seminars, you decide how you want to grow personally and professionally. Family First (Childcare Subsidy & Flexibility): We support you and your family. Benefit from a monthly childcare subsidy, exclusive access to daycare spots in Munich, and maximum flexibility to balance work and family life stress-free. Company Pension Scheme (bAV): We think about your future. FINN subsidizes your company pension plan with a strong employer contribution of 66%. Experience our Product (25% Car Subscription Discount): Become a FINN driver yourself! You will receive a generous employee discount or can alternatively take advantage of our attractive bike leasing scheme. Health & Mindset (Sports & Wellbeing): Your energy is our drive. Benefit from a subsidized EGYM Wellpass membership for fitness and sports, as well as professional mental health support. Munich Office Life: Barista-style coffee, matcha, and a luscious fruit basket await you at our modern headquarters in Munich. On top of that, there is a daily lunch subsidy for those in the office. Team Spirit & Corporate Events: We are more than just colleagues. Whether it's summer events by the lake or simply a relaxed after-work drink, we celebrate our milestones together at regular team events. €68,000 - €81,000 a year Additionally, you will benefit from virtual company shares (VSOP - Virtual Stock Option Plan). Your individual placement within this range will be determined during the application process and is based on your experience, qualifications, and specific expertise. The stated range reflects our current planning at the time of publication; we reserve the right to make future adjustments in line with market developments. About Us FINN is Germany’s leading car subscription platform and one of the country's few unicorns in the mobility sector. With over 400 colleagues from more than 50 nations, we are building the future of mobility and making access to your own car more seamless than ever before. What truly makes us proud, however, isn’t just the numbers, but what lies behind them: our strong team drive, the will to challenge the status quo, and the ambition to make a real difference. With us, you have the opportunity to take on direct responsibility in one of the fastest-growing scale-ups in Germany and actively shape our success story. We´re just getting started! More information: www.finn.com Equal Opportunities for Everyone FINN is an equal opportunity employer. We embrace and celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We are open to all groups of people without regard to age, color, national origin, race, religion, gender, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, marital status, or any other legally protected characteristics. Ready for the future of mobility? Apply now! Come on board and become part of our Hyper-Growth Story! Your first point of contact is Anna - we keep things moving fast and guide you every step of the way. Everything you need to know about our hiring process and company culture is in our Careers @ FINN booklet . We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. title: Consultant - Supply Chain Management
description: Who You'll Work With Driving lasting impact and building long-term capabilities with our clients is not easy work. You are the kind of person who thrives in a high performance/high reward culture - doing hard things, picking yourself up when you stumble, and having the resilience to try another way forward. In return for your drive, determination, and curiosity, we'll provide the resources, mentorship, and opportunities you need to become a stronger leader faster than you ever thought possible. Your colleagues—at all levels—will invest deeply in your development, just as much as they invest in delivering exceptional results for clients. Every day, you'll receive apprenticeship, coaching, and exposure that will accelerate your growth in ways you won’t find anywhere else. When you join us, you will have: Continuous learning: Our learning and apprenticeship culture, backed by structured programs, is all about helping you grow while creating an environment where feedback is clear, actionable, and focused on your development. The real magic happens when you take the input from others to heart and embrace the fast-paced learning experience, owning your journey. A voice that matters: From day one, we value your ideas and contributions. You’ll make a tangible impact by offering innovative ideas and practical solutions, all while upholding our unwavering commitment to ethics and integrity. We not only encourage diverse perspectives, but they are critical in driving us toward the best possible outcomes. Global community: With colleagues across 65+ countries and over 100 different nationalities, our firm’s diversity fuels creativity and helps us come up with the best solutions for our clients. Plus, you’ll have the opportunity to learn from exceptional colleagues with diverse backgrounds and experiences. World-class benefits: On top of a competitive salary (based on your location, experience, and skills), we provide a comprehensive benefits package to enable holistic well-being for you and your family. Your Impact As a consultant in our two-year Operations Excellence Program (OEP), you will work on client engagements in the same way as any other McKinsey consultant. You will play an active role in problem solving for clients including identifying issues, forming hypotheses, designing, and conducting analyses, and synthesizing conclusions into recommendations. You will gain leading edge operations and consultancy skills, primarily through on-the-job coaching, which will be complemented by the OEP’s structured capability-building program. We view the OEP position as the start of a rewarding, challenging and highly flexible career with McKinsey and the Operations practice. During your first few years with the firm, you will serve as a consultant on multiple client engagements and work with a number of colleagues and clients in a range of industry and operations service lines. You will acquire the necessary knowledge and expertise to help companies achieve excellence in their operations. Along with other members of the OEP community, you will dedicate many days to functional training and knowledge development under the leadership of our operations experts. You will follow a curriculum based on operations excellence themes and will receive advanced training in one of six specific areas of operations: manufacturing, product development, purchasing, service operations, supply chain management, or capital excellence. Upon completion of the program, successful consultants will join our global group of leading experts in operational excellence. We already have OEP graduates working at all levels of the firm from associate to partner. You will be based in one of our European offices as part of our Operations Practice. Our Operations Practice assists our clients in solving complex operational challenges. Blending strategic thinking with hands-on practicality, our teams of consultants and experts work to develop and implement operational strategies that solve our clients’ most critical problems. McKinsey’s Supply Chain Management service line is part of our Operations Practice and helps leading companies in all industries develop and implement future-oriented logistics strategies. We do this by making significant improvements in customer service while reducing invested capital and cost by transforming and optimizing their supply chains from end to end. Please submit your application in English, including your CV and transcripts (high school, university, etc.), with all relevant grades. #Operations2026 #EuropeEP Your Qualifications and Skills Master’s degree and exceptional academics required Initial experience in supply chain management (e.g., design and implementation of logistics strategy, network design and optimization, transportation, warehouse design and optimization, supply chain planning and management processes, supply chain modeling and optimization) Proven record of leadership in a work setting and/or through extracurricular activities Mobility and enthusiasm for taking on European assignments during the work week Exceptional analytical and quantitative problem-solving skills Ability to communicate complex ideas effectively, both verbally and in writing, in English and local office language Show more Show less Seniority level Associate Employment type Full-time Job function Consulting Industries Business Consulting and Services Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at McKinsey & Company by 2x See who you know

title: Service Delivery Manager (SDM) – SAP Application Management Services (AMS)
description: Role Summary We are looking for an experienced Service Delivery Manager to lead SAP AMS engagements for strategic customers. The role will ensure reliable service delivery across SAP, S/4HANA and adjacent enterprise systems, while managing stakeholders, resolving escalations, driving service improvement, and identifying opportunities for account growth. Key Responsibilities Own end-to-end SAP AMS service delivery across incidents, requests, problems, changes, releases, and continuous improvement. Ensure delivery against agreed SLAs, KPIs, governance standards, contractual commitments, and client expectations. Act as the primary delivery interface for client stakeholders, account teams, solution teams, and onsite/offshore delivery teams. Lead service governance forums and provide clear reporting on service performance, risks, issues, and improvement actions. Manage escalations proactively through timely communication, root cause analysis, and preventive action planning. Drive service stability, automation, productivity improvement, and value creation across the AMS landscape. Support deal shaping, transition planning, renewals, service expansion, and identification of new business opportunities. Required Experience Strong experience in SAP AMS / managed services delivery, preferably in global or multi-country environments. Good understanding of SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA landscapes across key business processes such as Finance, Procurement, Sales, Manufacturing, Supply Chain, Logistics, and Master Data. Exposure to adjacent systems such as SAP BTP, Ariba, SuccessFactors, Concur, EWM, TM, IBP, SAC, BW/4HANA, middleware, cloud platforms, and reporting solutions. Working knowledge of ITIL processes, including incident, problem, change, release, request fulfilment, service level management, and continual service improvement. Proven experience in stakeholder management, service governance, escalation handling, team leadership, and commercial delivery management. Key Skills and Competencies Excellent stakeholder management and executive communication skills. Strong escalation management with a structured, calm, and outcome-focused approach. Commercial awareness and ability to identify service expansion, automation, and transformation-led growth opportunities. Strong governance discipline across actions, risks, issues, dependencies, service metrics, and improvement plans. Ability to lead diverse onsite/offshore teams and build a responsive, customer-focused service culture. Desirable Qualifications and Certifications ITIL certification preferred. PMP, PRINCE2, Agile, Scrum, or equivalent delivery certification desirable. SAP S/4HANA exposure or certification preferred. Experience with ServiceNow, Jira, Azure DevOps, Solution Manager, or SAP Cloud ALM desirable. Show more Show less

title: SAP Junior Consultant* Production Planning
description: PERFECT PLACE TO GROW! concircle verbindet Technologie, Prozessverständnis und Umsetzungsstärke – und zählt damit zu den führenden Digitalisierungspartnern der produzierenden Industrie. Als Österreichs Nr. 1 für „IT Strategy & Implementation“ begleiten wir mit über 100 Kolleg*innen namhafte Industrieunternehmen bei ihrer digitalen Transformation. DEINE AUFGABEN BEI CONCIRCLE Gemeinsam mit deinem Team arbeitest du an innovativen Lösungen, sammelst wertvolle Praxiserfahrung und übernimmst Schritt für Schritt mehr Verantwortung in folgenden Bereichen: Analyse, Design und Optimierung von innovativen Produktionsprozessen. Konzeption und Entwicklung maßgeschneiderter Lösungen in S/4 HANA-Projekten. Unterstützung im laufenden Betrieb und bei Anwender*innenschulungen. DAS IST UNS WICHTIG Abgeschlossenes technisches oder wirtschaftliches Studium mit Schwerpunkt im Bereich Logistik oder Produktion. Einblicke in Produktionsprozesse z.B. durch Praktika oder Werkstudierendentätigkeiten. Interesse an IT-gestützten Lösungen sowie eine ausgeprägte Affinität zur Digitalisierung. Verhandlungssichere Deutschkenntnisse (min. C1), sehr gute Englischkenntnisse (mind. B2) . VON UNS KANNST DU ERWARTEN Regelmäßige Teamevents fördern den Teamgeist und stärken den Zusammenhalt. Attraktives Gehaltspaket mit Fixgehalt, variablen Anteil und diversen Benefits. Internationale Projekte in In- und Ausland mit bis zu 50% Reisetätigkeit. Coaching & Mentoring: Individuelle Begleitung durch erfahrene Kolleg*innen von Anfang an. Ein erfahrenes Team, das Zusammenarbeit und gegenseitige Unterstützung aktiv lebt. Schulungen und Zertifizierungen: Die Möglichkeit, deine Karriere gezielt voranzutreiben. Wir suchen nach Persönlichkeiten, die gemeinsam mit uns Großes erreichen wollen. Hierfür bieten wir dir sehr gerne ein attraktives Gehaltspaket, das ein Fixum und einen variablen Teil sowie zusätzliche Benefits beinhaltet. Die Details dazu besprechen wir in einem persönlichen Gespräch. Wir freuen uns auf deine Bewerbung: application.99-DE@concircle.com . Weitere Infos: www.concircle.com/karriere. *Wir setzen auf Vielfalt, lehnen Diskriminierung ab und denken nicht in Kategorien wie etwa Geschlecht, ethnische Herkunft, Religion, Behinderung, Alter oder sexuelle Identität. title: Materials Specialist
description: About the Role: Ensure timely availability, staging, and field distribution of materials. Key Responsibilities: Provide first point of call for all material related issues. Interface with Company materials and warehouse teams. Coordinate tagging, kitting, staging, and material readiness for work fronts. Coordinate material internal transportation, storage and preservation. Track critical materials and shortages. Support expediting of missing or damaged materials. Maintain material status reporting for execution teams. Ensure surplus materials are well preserved and timely reconciled with Company stores and obtain relevant Company authorities sign-off on reconciliation sheets. Tracks outstanding materials POs (both Company and Contractors sourced) using Company SAP system or any other relevant Company or Contractor system. Provide technical support in terms of material availability alternatives. Qualifications & Experience Degree in a relevant engineering discipline (B.Sc./M.Sc./B.Eng.) (preferably in Mechanical) and membership of internationally accepted applicable professional Institute. Minimum 10 years of experience in oil & gas projects and Turnarounds / Shutdowns in end-to-end material management. description: About Job: Responsible for planning, work preparation, sequencing, and progress tracking to support efficient turnaround execution. Provide accurate durations and resource requirements for overall Turnaround activities by ensuring resource levelling. Prepare L4 Schedule in Primavera P6 and ensure activities are logically sequenced and aligned with Company integrated plan. Prepare look-ahead plans. Track physical progress against plan, highlight concerns and update schedules. Support re-sequencing and recovery planning. Interface with Company planning and scheduling teams. title: Consultant SAP Expert - Bordeaux
description: Description De L'entreprise Sopra Steria, acteur majeur de la Tech en Europe, avec 51 000 collaborateurs dans près de 30 pays, est reconnu pour ses activités de conseil, de services et solutions numériques. Il aide ses clients à mener leur transformation digitale et à obtenir des bénéfices concrets et durables. Le Groupe apporte une réponse globale aux enjeux de compétitivité des grandes entreprises et organisations, en combinant une connaissance approfondie des secteurs d’activité et des technologies à une approche collaborative. Sopra Steria place l’humain au cœur de son action et s’engage auprès de ses clients à tirer le meilleur parti du numérique pour construire un avenir positif. En 2025, le Groupe a réalisé un chiffre d’affaires de 5,6 milliards d’euros. Description Du Poste The world is how we shape it Votre futur environnement de travail : Notre ADN et notre positionnement chez nos clients Aéronautique sont axés autour de la migration et de l’intégration de la solution SAP S/4HANA. Nous sommes reconnus comme un acteur majeur des grands programmes de transformation SAP, aussi bien sur des projets nationaux qu’internationaux. Pour soutenir cette dynamique, Sopra Steria poursuit le développement de ses SAP Expertise Units , véritables pôles d’excellence rassemblant des consultants seniors et experts reconnus. Ces équipes jouent un rôle clé dans le delivery des programmes SAP, la structuration des offres et la montée en compétence des équipes. Deux valeurs fondent notre approche SAP : l’expertise et la transmission du savoir . Concevons ensemble le monde SAP S/4HANA de demain. Votre rôle et vos missions : En tant que Consultant·e SAP Senior / Expert·e , vous intervenez comme référent fonctionnel et/ou solution sur des projets complexes à forte valeur ajoutée. À ce titre, vous : Apportez votre expertise lors du recueil et de l’analyse des besoins métiers complexes, Pilotez et sécurisez les conceptions fonctionnelles et les choix de solutions, en vous appuyant sur les best practices SAP, Accompagnez la montée en compétence de consultants juniors sur les phases de build, paramétrage, tests et déploiement, Intervenez en amont des projets sur les phases de cadrage, d’avant-vente et de définition des trajectoires S/4HANA, Contribuez activement à la capitalisation, à la structuration des offres Contribuer aux projets internes de construction de démonstrateurs et PoCs. Contribuer à la communauté SAP de l'agence L’environnement technique couvre un périmètre étendu : SAP S/4HANA ou ECC sur les modules SD/MM, FI/CO, PS, CS, PP, WM, PM,, et autres composants de l’écosystème SAP. Qualifications Votre profil : Diplômé·e d’une école d’Ingénieurs, d’un Master 2 Universitaire ou équivalent, Vous disposez d’une expérience significative (minimum 5 à 8 ans) en tant que consultant SAP, Vous êtes intervenu·e sur plusieurs projets d’implémentation, de migration S/4HANA ou de maintenance complexe, Vous possédez une forte capacité d’analyse et de prise de recul, vous permettant d’adresser des enjeux métiers et SI structurants, Reconnu·e pour votre expertise, vous avez un réel goût pour le partage de connaissances et le rôle de référent, La maîtrise de l’anglais, à l’oral comme à l’écrit, est indispensable dans un contexte international et multiculturel. Informations supplémentaires Informations supplémentaires Un accord télétravail pour télétravailler jusqu'à 2 jours par semaine selon vos missions. Un package avantages intéressants : des titres restaurants, accès aux subventions des activités sociales & culturelles. Des centaines de formations pour développer vos compétences et évoluer au sein du Groupe Des plateformes de formations en autonomie pour vous accompagner votre développement personnel La possibilité de s'engager auprès de notre fondation ou de notre partenaire « Vendredi ». De très nombreuses opportunités en CDI peuvent vous attendre à l’issue du stage. Employeur inclusif et engagé, notre société œuvre chaque jour pour lutter contre toute forme de discrimination et favoriser un environnement de travail respectueux. C’est pourquoi, attachés à la mixité et à la diversité, nous encourageons toutes les candidatures et tous les profils. https://www.soprasteria.fr/nous-connaitre/nos-engagements Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Full-time Job function Information Technology Industries Software Development, IT Services and IT Consulting, and Business Consulting and Services Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Sopra Steria by 2x See who you know

description: Description De L'entreprise Sopra Steria, acteur majeur de la Tech en Europe, avec 51 000 collaborateurs dans près de 30 pays, est reconnu pour ses activités de conseil, de services et solutions numériques. Il aide ses clients à mener leur transformation digitale et à obtenir des bénéfices concrets et durables. Le Groupe apporte une réponse globale aux enjeux de compétitivité des grandes entreprises et organisations, en combinant une connaissance approfondie des secteurs d’activité et des technologies à une approche collaborative. Sopra Steria place l’humain au cœur de son action et s’engage auprès de ses clients à tirer le meilleur parti du numérique pour construire un avenir positif. En 2025, le Groupe a réalisé un chiffre d’affaires de 5,6 milliards d’euros. Description Du Poste The world is how we shape it Votre futur environnement de travail : Notre ADN et positionnement chez nos clients Aéronautique est axé autour de la migration et de l'intégration de la solution SAP S/4HANA. Nous sommes reconnus chez nos clients comme acteur majeur S/4 HANA qui se traduit par la participation à de grands programmes de transformation. Pour répondre à cette ambition, nous avons créé et continuons de développer le SAP Expertise Unit de Bordeaux. Nous participons ainsi depuis Bordeaux au delivery des programmes SAP nationaux et locaux. Après avoir intégré une dizaine de consultants seniors en ce début d'année, la vision long terme de Sopra Steria insuffle un plan de recrutement ambitieux et pluri annuel des compétences SAP. Deux valeurs principales dans le SAP Expertise Unit : l'expertise et la transmission du savoir. Concevons ensemble le monde Aéronautique SAP S/4HANA de demain ! Votre rôle et vos missions: Rejoignez une équipe de consultants seniors qui accompagnera votre montée en compétence en tant que Consultant SAP. Vous intervenez ainsi sur la chaîne de valeur de projets : Vous prenez en charge le recueil des besoins auprès des utilisateurs, Vous pilotez des évolutions fonctionnelles sur votre périmètre, Vous menez à bien les évolutions, Vous animez des workshops et sessions de travail avec les clients, Vous rédigez et présentez des recommandations, appuyées par les best practices SAP, Vous contribuez activement à toutes les étapes projet : conception, paramétrage, tests, formation, support, L'environnement technique couvre un périmètre vaste : S/4HANA ou ECC sur les modules SD/MM, FI/CO/PS, CS, PP, WM, PM, BI et bien d'autres encore. #BORDEAUX Qualifications Votre profil : Diplômé(e) d'une école d'Ingénieurs ou d'un Master 2 Universitaire ou équivalent, vous avez une première expérience avérée du métier de consultant SAP. Vous avez participé à un ou plusieurs projets d'implémentation ou de maintenance SAP. Vous avez acquis une certaine hauteur de vue, vous permettant de comprendre les enjeux d'un projet et d'être force de proposition. Rigoureux, à l'écoute de votre client, vous avez envie de rejoindre une entreprise leader et innovante sur S4/HANA La maîtrise de l'anglais à l'oral et à l'écrit est attendue pour agir avec nos clients et équipes offshore. Informations supplémentaires Les avantages à nous rejoindre : Un accord télétravail pour télétravailler jusqu’à 2 jours par semaine selon vos missions. Un package avantages intéressant : une mutuelle, un CSE, des titres restaurants, un accord d’intéressement, des primes vacances et cooptation. Des opportunités de carrières multiples : plus de 30 familles de métiers, autant de passerelles à imaginer ensemble. Plusieurs centaines de formations accessibles en toute autonomie depuis l’app mobile avec Sopra Steria Academy. La possibilité de s'engager auprès de notre fondation ou de notre partenaire « Vendredi ». L'opportunité de rejoindre des collectifs (formations, conférences, veille, et bien plus encore…). Employeur inclusif et engagé, notre société œuvre chaque jour pour lutter contre toute forme de discrimination et favoriser un environnement de travail respectueux. C’est pourquoi, attachés à la mixité et à la diversité, nous encourageons toutes les candidatures et tous les profils. https://www.soprasteria.fr/nous-connaitre/nos-engagements Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Full-time Job function Information Technology Industries Software Development, IT Services and IT Consulting, and Business Consulting and Services Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Sopra Steria by 2x See who you know

We are growing and are looking for people to join our team. You'll be part of an entrepreneurial, high-growth environment of 300.000 employees. Our ERP services, covering strategy, implementation and innovation, help clients deliver true value and achieve their transformation agenda. The Role Lead complex SAP business transformation programs from inception to completion Ensure successful delivery of programs within budget, on time, and to quality standards Manage and motivate geographically distributed teams, including multi-vendor and complex client organizations Develop and implement well-structured program delivery frameworks Collaborate with IT, business, and change management teams to ensure program success Maintain strong working relationships with stakeholders at all levels Requirements Proven project and program delivery track record across multiple full lifecycle SAP programs, including multi-country roll-outs of significant size, scope and complexity for ECC and S/4 HANA Experience in global delivery and working with offshore resources Ability to lead teams in complex environments that include multi-vendors, complex client organization structures and geographically distributed teams Experience of working with global teams and a track record of leading teams of consultant based locally and remotely In depth knowledge of how to set up programs for success including IT, Business and Change components Ability to define well-structured program delivery frameworks and apply these practically. Outstanding communication skills (verbal and written) and presentation skills, with the ability to influence C-Level stakeholders within client organizations Ability to lead teams to prepare large proposals and program plans, facilitate leverage differentiators (e.g. specific consulting frameworks, etc.) Ability to articulate value based propositions, own and review program financials, and lead proposal presentations in order to create a compelling propositions for clients Substantial proven experience working in client facing consulting roles Have experience in gathering, validating, synthesizing, documenting, and communicating data and information for a range of audiences Project-related mobility/willingness to travel Benefits About Infosys Consulting Be part of a globally renowned management consulting firm on the front-line of industry disruption and at the cutting edge of technology. title: Senior Product Manager
description: Location: Remote, Netherlands Employment Type: Full-Time Benefits Offered: Vision, Medical, Life, Dental, 401K Gross annual base salary: EUR 75,000-97,500 Additional variable compensation and benefits may apply. Total compensation is based on experience, skills, and location using objective, job-related criteria. Summary OneStream is seeking Senior Product Managers to lead strategic initiatives, manage complex product areas, and deliver exceptional customer experiences. You will leverage deep business acumen and market insights to define and evolve mid-term visions, influence business cases, and anticipate market trends. You will own product domains, ensure roadmap alignment with business objectives, guide cross-functional teams, and represent the voice of the customer at an executive level. Primary Duties And Responsibilities Understand the Office of the CFO, roles and responsibilities, and build business cases to influence direction. Define and evolve mid-term product visions that align with the long-term vision of the company; anticipate market/competitor trends. Demonstrate in-depth knowledge of product area and related personas; able to demo and be the go-to person for questions. Understand entire product and related solutions at a high level; in-depth knowledge of related areas and personas. Partner on platform and product technical designs; guide tech debt trade-offs and manage roadmap impact from technical decisions. Manage stakeholders beyond QA/Dev/Scrum (PreSales, SCs, Consultants, Support); negotiate alignment and drive consensus across teams. Lead segment/market discovery related to product domain; anticipate emerging needs and lead 1:1 customer calls. Communicate vision across organization; executive-ready storytelling; present at All Hands, Splash, Webinars, Enablement Sessions. Collaborate cross-functionally; set standards for interaction and best practices; closely collaborate with Commercialization. Mentor BAs and other PMs; set best practices; influence cross-functional leadership; aid QA and DEV education cross-functionally. Actively seek feedback from multiple sources; use information to improve decisions; proactively take on new projects and explore innovation opportunities; foresee and mitigate roadmap impacts from strategic shifts. Reframe problems and make strategic trade-offs at product level; maximize customer value and satisfaction; assess priorities across unrelated stakeholders; deliver creative solutions. Collaborate with GPM, influence and execute on multi-release roadmap; drive cross-team alignment; adjust plans based on priority changes; negotiate priorities. See not just the short term but how features fit into long-term vision; understand dependencies and issues that could arise later; own product area/domain. Run multiple project deliveries; unblock teams; improve processes; partner on product launch strategy; involved in longer-term roadmap execution; mitigate and manage delivery risks; ensure delivery fits with larger product. Required Education And Experience Bachelor's degree or equivalent in Accounting, Finance, Business, Information Technology or similar. 7-12 years of experience in Corporate Performance Management (CPM), Product Management, solutions delivery, business analysis, training, and documentation management. Experience with CPM applications such as OneStream, Oracle Hyperion, SAP, or other CPM solutions. Hands-on technical experience with CPM domains: financial modeling, data integration, consolidation, reporting, budgeting, forecasting, and planning. Experience working in an Agile environment. Knowledge of object-oriented languages such as Java and C++. Preferred Education And Experience Experience building enterprise software solutions. Experience using Jira, Confluence, Office 365, and Microsoft Visio. Familiarity with flow chart software (Visio, Draw.io, Miro). Product Manager Certification (PMC) a plus; CPA or MBA a plus. Big 4 experience a plus. Strong understanding of accounting terms and systems. Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities Deep business acumen and market insight; understands Office of the CFO and market direction. In-depth product and domain knowledge; able to demo and answer questions for a variety of personas. Technical fluency; partners on platform and product architecture, guides tech debt trade-offs. Stakeholder management; manages broad stakeholder groups, negotiates alignment, drives consensus. Customer-centric; leads market discovery, anticipates needs, leads customer calls. Communication and presentation; executive-ready storytelling, presents at major events. Collaboration and facilitation; sets standards, collaborates cross-functionally, leads commercialization efforts. Mentorship; mentors BAs and PMs, sets best practices, influences cross-functional leadership. Adaptability; seeks feedback, proactively improves, mitigates impacts from strategic shifts. Strategic thinking and product vision; reframes problems, makes strategic trade-offs, maximizes customer value. Requirements and roadmap management; influences and executes multi-release roadmap, negotiates priorities. Product craft; owns product area/domain, understands long-term dependencies. Execution and delivery; runs multiple projects, unblocks teams, manages risks, ensures fit with larger product. Supervisory Responsibilities This position does not have any supervisory responsibilities. Travel Travel Requirement: Regular travel is not expected for this position. Work Authorization and Security Clearance Employee must be legally authorized to work in the country of the job position as a precondition of employment. Physical, Visual, and Mental Demands While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to hear and speak effectively, reach with hands and arms, make repetitive movement of hands and fingers such as typing and/or writing. The position is mostly sedentary, involving sitting and/or standing, but the employee may occasionally lift and/or move up to 10 to 25 pounds. The employee must be able to understand, remember, and apply oral and/or written instructions or other information, apply common sense in performing job, and can organize and prioritize responsibilities. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. title: Logistics & Supply Chain Management Expert- Remote
description: Job Title: Logistics & Supply Chain Management Expert Job Type: Contractor Location: Remote Job Summary Logistics & Supply Chain Management Specialists to contribute expertise to a customer's project focused on optimizing supply chain processes and documentation. In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input. No prior experience in AI is required — your domain knowledge is what matters. This engagement prioritizes professionals with advanced knowledge of global logistics, an analytical mindset, and fluency in English. Scope of Work Review, analyze, and provide input on logistics documents such as packing lists, bills of lading, and inventory registers for authenticity, accuracy, and industry compliance. Develop and annotate sample documentation to highlight best practices in logistics and supply chain management. Evaluate and suggest improvements in current supply chain workflows based on real-world experience. Deliver detailed feedback on logistics-related scenarios, focusing on document structures and process optimization. Collaborate with project teams to clarify requirements and explain complex logistics concepts through clear communication. Participate in quality assurance processes by validating the relevance and correctness of supply chain data sets. Contribute to the creation of training materials and resources reflecting industry standards and nuanced supply chain challenges. Preferred Qualifications 3+ years of hands-on experience in logistics and supply chain management, particularly with packing lists, bills of lading, and inventory registers. Comprehensive understanding of logistics workflows, documentation, and global supply chain operations. Ability to communicate complex technical concepts with clarity in English. Meticulous attention to detail when reviewing and creating supply chain documentation. Proven analytical skills and experience identifying process improvements in a logistics context. Master’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Logistics, or a related field is a plus. Comfort working independently in a remote, collaborative environment and delivering timely, high-quality contributions. title: EMEA Pricing & Tenders Director / Senior Director m/f/d
description: At Zimmer Biomet, we believe in pushing the boundaries of innovation and driving our mission forward. As a global medical technology leader for nearly 100 years, a patient’s mobility is enhanced by a Zimmer Biomet product or technology every 8 seconds. As a Zimmer Biomet team member, you will share in our commitment to providing mobility and renewed life to people around the world. To support our talent team, we focus on development opportunities, robust employee resource groups (ERGs), a flexible working environment, location specific competitive total rewards, wellness incentives and a culture of recognition and performance awards. We are committed to creating an environment where every team member feels included, respected, empowered and recognised. What You Can Expect This role is critical in ZB EMEA’s new operating model consisting of 3 BU’s (RDTS, SET & EM). The EMEA Pricing Sr Director designs and delivers the EMEA pricing strategy, pricing governance, and leads the price setting, price getting and price netting processes across all business segments. The Pricing Director leads a team of cluster pricing managers and their pricing & operations teams to ensure price increase programs are implemented successfully margin erosion is minimized and value capture is optimized. The pricing team uses technology (Vendavo, Power BI, SAP) to improve price performance and maximize revenue growth and profitability within the region. The Pricing Director will work closely with the segment leaders in defining the pricing strategy as well as with the cluster and country commercial leads and with Finance to implement it. How You'll Create Impact Set up and drive execution of EMEA pricing policy, governance framework and process. Support BU marketing teams in developing EMEA pricing strategies, guidelines such as floor prices & margins per brand or SKU for new launches and key established brands Deploy price reduction approval matrices (PRAMs) across all countries in EMEA Identify sources of Margin leakage and pockets of opportunity to increase margins at all levels Participate or delegate in DAAC approval process and review distributor list prices The Pricing Director chairs the EMEA pricing committee and leads the process to prepare, recommend, document and track decisions of this committee. Lead preparation, decision making, implementation & tracking of EMEA pricing committee Set up a standardized EMEA pricing approval process for large and cross-border deals Develop a process to manage cross-border pricing negotiations impacting multiple countries and BU’s The Pricing Director drives the execution of insights derived from the Analytics CoE. Design, build and deploy pricing technology including CPQ and analytics throughout EMEA Review the process of adapting list prices periodically across countries and segments Publishes a monthly EMEA ‘price report” to identify pricing trends, opportunities and challenges Pricing Director ensures execution of price increase and margin expansion initiatives through direct reporting line of cluster Pricing Managers. Works with country pricing/tendering/commercial teams to build EMEA-wide analytics, processes, tools and capabilities Increase prices in line with corporate guidelines, cost inflation or other pressures to improve margins Leads Pricing cluster Managers in conducting price sensitivity analyses across the ZB portfolio and actioning on price improvement opportunities Work closely with Market Access to identify opportunities to improve pricing and reimbursement environment What Makes You Stand Out Ability to lead large multi-country, multi-cultural teams Experience in implementing price management & price analytics technology across several countries Expert knowledge of pricing models and techniques in medical devices in Europe Expert knowledge of buying and tendering processes in medical devices in Europe Strong analytical and quantitative modelling skills Strong communication skills Experience with change management and actioning change in a diverse organisation Strong project management and cross-country collaboration skills Strong written and verbal command of English + additional European language preferred Strong work ethic, going beyond what is asked for and applying own critical thinking Team player, high degree of flexibility and responsiveness to changing inputs Can do mind-set, self-driven, never satisfied - continuous improvement mind-set Your Background Bachelor or higher. Business administration, finance or marketing preferred. Minimum of 5 years of leading pricing teams in EMEA in medical devices. Minimum of 10 years of hands-on pricing and tendering experience in EMEA in medical devices Travel Expectations Up to 30% EOE/M/F/Vet/Disability Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Full-time Job function Other Industries Medical Equipment Manufacturing Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Zimmer Biomet by 2x See who you know

title: Assistant(e) de Direction
description: Position Souhaitez-vous rejoindre la Maison des rêves ? Dans cette Maison, nous connaissons notre raison d’être qui est de rendre le monde plus beau et plus heureux, à l’instar du vœu le plus cher de Christian Dior. En nous rejoignant, vous consoliderez notre vision : Celle de devenir la Maison la plus désirable au monde, une référence absolue du luxe à la française, un symbole de joie et d’espérance. Cette Vision Est Portée Par Nos Six Valeurs Que Vous Incarnerez Au Quotidien En Tant Que Créateurs De Rêves - Des Valeurs Qui Nous Engagent Et Nous Définissent Créer avec Passion Savoir faire Ensemble avec Joie Exceller pour un Luxe Ultime Conquérir avec Audace Faire Triompher L’Inclusivité S’Engager et Agir pour Réenchanter le monde. Design your dreams with Dior. Votre Impact Au sein de la Direction de l'un de nos départements, vous assistez le top management et vous contribuez à la dynamique globale de l’équipe par la prise en charge de certaines missions. Position RESPONSABILITES DU POSTE : Vos Missions au jour le jour Assister La Direction Gestion de l’agenda, des notes de frais, Gestion de l’organisation de ses déplacements, Analyser et organiser les informations transmises et préparer les documents qui lui sont nécessaires pour faciliter sa prise de décision (compilation information, analyses excel), Prise en charge de l’organisation des réunions et séminaires, Rédiger les comptes rendus et mettre en forme les présentations pour des réunions ad-hoc, Gestion des droits réseaux, des ressources partagées de l’équipe, Compilation et pilotage du budget global de l’équipe en lien direct avec le contrôle de gestion (création et commandes fournisseurs, devis et facturation), Suivre les projets opérationnels. Assurer la transmission et la coordination d’informations au sein de l’équipe Être l’interface des autres services internes partenaires de l’équipe. Animer l’équipe Organisation de tous événements de l’équipe (team building / moments conviviaux / visites des marchés). Les responsabilités décrites dans cette fiche de poste sont susceptibles d'évoluer en fonction des besoins de l'entreprise et des projets en cours. Profile Vous êtes issu(e) d’une formation supérieure universitaire et avez idéalement 10 ans d'expérience dans un poste mêlant suivi administratif et coordination de projets opérationnels. Vous avez très envie de travailler auprès d’une direction exigeante et formatrice, d’intégrer une équipe dynamique en pleine transformation et de participer à son animation au quotidien. Votre maîtrisez de ces six compétences transverses clés qui reflètent nos valeurs : Capacité de gestion des parties prenantes Communication et compétences interpersonnelles . Intégrité Souci du Détail Rigueur Professionnelle Résolution de Problèmes Compétences Techniques Maîtrise des outils informatiques : pack office, messagerie, SAP. Anglais courant nécessaire. Additional information L'inclusion n'est pas seulement un principe noble, mais le fondement même de notre excellence. Dans un monde où nos clients, nos talents et nos idées émanent d'horizons de plus en plus diversifiés, notre diversité constitue notre force. Chez Parfums Christian Dior, cela se traduit par une Maison où chacun se sent respecté, accueilli et valorisé, quelles que soient ses singularités. Nous en sommes convaincus : une Maison qui reflète cette diversité est une Maison durable et dynamique, solidement ancrée dans le rêve et la réalité. L'inclusion ne se décrète pas ; elle se vit au quotidien. Et grâce à nos collaborateurs, nous progressons concrètement : chaque jour, nous nous engageons à ouvrir nos portes à des talents issus de milieux variés. (Si vous nécessitez des aménagements spécifiques durant le processus de recrutement, n'hésitez pas à le mentionner dans votre candidature.) Additional information for internal candidate Êtes-vous un employé d’LVMH ? Merci de candidater sur : WORK @LVMH – Voices Recruitment steps Soumettez votre candidature via notre site web. Un recruteur examinera votre dossier. Si votre profil correspond aux exigences définies par notre équipe de recrutement, vous serez contacté(e) pour un premier entretien avec les Ressources Humaines. Par la suite, vous passerez des entretiens en présentiel avec le Directeur. Le processus décrit ci-dessus est susceptible d'évoluer en fonction des besoins de l'entreprise. title: IS Supply Chain - SAP MM specialist CDI H/F
description: IS Supply Chain - SAP MM Specialist CDI H/F QUI SOMMES-NOUS ? Reconnue comme la première entreprise de cosmétiques fondée au Japon, et classée au 8ème rang mondial des groupes de beauté, Shiseido incarne l’excellence absolue dans le domaine des soins, du maquillage et des parfums. La réputation de l’entreprise, fondée en 1872, s’appuie sur plus de 150 ans de recherche et d’innovation au service de la Beauté. Les marques du Groupe sont aujourd’hui distribuées dans plus de 120 pays et régions. Shiseido emploie environ 33 000 collaborateurs de près de 100 nationalités et a généré un chiffre d’affaires de 6,7 milliards d’euros en 2024. Guidés par la mission de l’entreprise « Beauty Innovations for a Better World », les collaborateurs de Shiseido sont animés par des valeurs qui illustrent son héritage unique : « People First », « Diversity », « Art & Science », « Japanese », « Uncompromising Quality » et « Omotenashi ». Shiseido EMEA, basé à Neuilly-sur-Seine, couvre les activités mondiales des marques de parfum Issey Miyake, Narciso Rodriguez, Zadig&Voltaire, Max Mara, Shiseido Fragrances et Serge Lutens ; de la marque de soin Gallinée ; ainsi que les activités et la distribution régionale de plusieurs marques du Groupe : Clé de Peau Beauté, Docteur Dennis Gross, Drunk Elephant, NARS, Shiseido Ginza Tokyo et Tory Burch. La région compte également un centre d’innovation européen (EIC) dédié à la recherche et au développement ainsi que 2 sites de production basés dans le Loiret. RAISON D’ETRE Le SAP MM Specialist est responsable de la mise en œuvre et de la maintenance des solutions applicatives relatives au domaine Supply Chain, notamment sur le périmètre Purchase‑to‑Pay et Logistique. À ce titre, il garantit la fiabilité, la cohérence et l’évolutivité du paysage applicatif. Il assure également la gestion des rôles et autorisations au sein des solutions informatiques, en accompagnant les utilisateurs dans leurs demandes d’accès à SAP. Il collabore avec les parties prenantes internes et externes, métiers et IT, en charge de l’implémentation et du maintien en condition opérationnelle des solutions fonctionnelles du domaine Supply Chain et veille à l’alignement et à la cohérence globale des solutions au sein du système d’information. ORGANISATION Reporte au Responsable des Applications Supply Chain Collabore avec les ressources métiers et IT de la Supply Chain et des domaines connexes Localisation : Neuilly-sur-Seine Type de contrat : CDI MISSIONS PRINCIPALES La liste des tâches n’est pas exhaustive, elle peut être amenée à évoluer. Description Du Poste Le SAP MM Specialist devra intervenir à la fois sur le maintien en condition opérationnelle et sur les évolutions de la solution (demandes d’évolution, projet) tout en étant le garant de la gestion des autorisations du système. Missions principales Assurer la maintenance corrective et évolutive sur les applications de son périmètre et leur intégration : Assurer la disponibilité et la performance des solutions Supply Chain, notamment SAP (S/4 et ECC) Purchase to Pay & Logistique Assurer le support fonctionnel auprès des utilisateurs Gérer les incidents Gérer les demandes d’évolution : être force de proposition dans le cadre de l’évolution des applications, analyser les évolutions et les prendre en charge, organisation des ateliers, rédactions des spécifications fonctionnelles, paramétrage des outils locaux, réalisation des tests, suivi de la validation métier et de la mise en production Traiter et suivre les demandes d’accès, les rôles et autorisations (le paramétrage des rôles / autorisations étant assuré par une autre équipe dédiée) Superviser et exécuter les traitements batch / jobs Maintenir la documentation technico-fonctionnelle à jour Réaliser les tests de non-régression lors des montées de version Mener les Projets Recueillir et analyser les besoins métiers Proposer des solutions adaptées Participer au paramétrage et à la configuration SAP Contribuer aux phases de conception, build, recette et déploiement Respecter les processus de Release Management Collaborer avec les Key Users métier, les partenaires internes (ex. équipes TMA SAP S/4), les partenaires externes (ex. TMA SAP ECC, éditeurs, intégrateurs…) et les membres de la DSI Profil Candidat Formation Bac +5 (Systèmes d’information, ERP, Supply Chain) Expérience confirmée de 3 ans minimum sur SAP MM (achats, logistique, master data), avec connaissances SAP GRC-Autorisations (optionnel) Bonne compréhension des processus Supply Chain Expérience en projet d’implémentation ERP / SAP Capacité à évoluer dans un environnement international – Maîtrise de l’anglais Bonnes compétences en communication et capacité à vulgariser des sujets techniques Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Full-time Job function Management and Manufacturing Industries Personal Care Product Manufacturing Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Shiseido by 2x See who you know

title: Consultant(e)/Manager AMOA SAP MM /SD/ PP – Industrie(F/H)
description: Description Square Management est un cabinet de conseil spécialisé en stratégie et organisation, fondé en 2008. Nous accompagnons nos clients dans l’anticipation, la conception et la réalisation de leurs projets de transformation, en France, en Belgique et au Luxembourg. Notre expertise s’organise autour de deux offres complémentaires : Une offre de conseil en stratégie sous la marque Circle Strategy Une offre de conseil en organisation et management, Square Management Pour répondre aux défis de nos clients, nous avons structuré notre offre autour de 10 domaines d’excellence qui représentent les expertises phares du cabinet : AI & Data, Digital & Marketing, Innovation, Organisation & Efficiency, People & Change, Payments, Regulatory & Compliance, Risk & Finance, Supply Chain et Sustainability. Square Management est un acteur unique, qui, fort d’un réseau de 800 collaborateurs, développe des méthodes spécifiques pour chacun de ses domaines d’excellence, aidé des 25 chercheurs du Square Research Center. Notre engagement est au cœur de notre identité : labellisés B Corp et médaillés de platine EcoVadis, nous figurons parmi le top 1% des entreprises évaluées. Nous sommes engagés via la Fondation Square, qui soutient des associations œuvrant dans les secteurs de l’entrepreneuriat, de l’enfance et de la santé. Missions Rattaché(e) à l’équipe Industrie & Services, vous accompagnez nos clients grands comptes et ETI des secteurs de l’Industrie, notamment Luxe, Agroalimentaire, Pharmacie et Cosmétique, dans leurs projets de transformation et de déploiement SAP MM / SD / PP. En tant que Consultant(e) AMOA SAP MM / SD / PP, vous intervenez en interface entre les équipes métiers Supply Chain, Logistique, Production et les équipes techniques sur l’ensemble des phases projet. Cadrage & analyse Recueil et analyse des besoins fonctionnels auprès des directions Supply Chain, Logistique, Production et Achats Rédaction des expressions de besoins, cahiers des charges fonctionnels et spécifications détaillées Analyse des flux métiers : approvisionnement, gestion des stocks, production, ventes et distribution Réalisation de diagnostics des processus existants et identification des axes d’optimisation opérationnelle Conception & déploiement SAP MM / SD / PP Conception des solutions SAP MM / SD / PP : paramétrage fonctionnel, maquettage, recette Savoir challenger les paramétrages fonctionnels proposés par l’intégrateur. Accompagnement des équipes techniques dans la traduction des besoins métiers en solutions SAP Réalisation et coordination des phases de tests : tests unitaires, tests d’intégration, UAT Gestion des flux logistiques et de production : achats, stocks, ordonnancement, planification et distribution Participation aux projets de migration et reprise de données Contribution aux projets S/4HANA Supply Chain et optimisation des processus industriels Conduite du changement & formation Rédaction des supports de formation et animation des sessions utilisateurs Accompagnement au démarrage (go-live) Contribution à la conduite du changement auprès des équipes métiers Support aux key users durant les phases critiques de déploiement Pilotage & développement Suivi de l’avancement des chantiers et reporting auprès des directions métiers et IT Coordination des parties prenantes : MOE, intégrateurs, directions Supply Chain et Production Participation aux réponses aux appels d’offres et au développement commercial du cabinet Encadrement et montée en compétences de consultants juniors Contribution aux initiatives internes et aux programmes R&D du cabinet Profile Vous êtes diplômé(e) d’un Bac+5 (école d’ingénieurs, école de commerce, université) avec une spécialisation en Supply Chain, Logistique, Systèmes d’Information ou équivalent. Vous justifiez d’au moins 3 ans d’expérience acquise en cabinet de conseil ou en ESN sur des missions AMOA SAP MM / SD / PP dans un environnement industriel. Compétences attendues Maîtrise des processus logistiques et industriels couverts par SAP MM / SD / PP Bonne connaissance des flux achats, approvisionnements, gestion des stocks, production et distribution Expérience sur au moins un projet de déploiement ou de transformation SAP de bout en bout, du cadrage au go-live Maîtrise des phases de recette, conduite du changement et accompagnement métier Solides capacités de rédaction fonctionnelle : spécifications, cahiers de recette, supports utilisateurs Aisance dans un rôle d’interface entre les métiers Supply Chain / Production et les équipes techniques Connaissance des enjeux industriels : traçabilité, gestion des flux, optimisation des stocks, planification de production, service client Une connaissance de SAP S/4HANA est un plus Nous rejoindre Développement & Expertise Le Square Institute : Développez vos compétences grâce à notre programme de formation personnalisé et certifiant. Le Square Research Center : La possibilité de solliciter nos experts sur vos problématiques complexes et de collaborer sur des missions innovantes. Management de proximité : Un suivi de carrière avec votre Business Manager et un accompagnement avec notre système Référent / Référé. L'accès à nos Domaines d’Excellence : Une communauté pour enrichir votre expertise et participer pleinement à la vie interne du cabinet. Votre package et vos avantages Rémunération : Système de variable attractif valorisant votre engagement, prime de cooptation, participation (PEE/PERECO, compte épargne temps - CET). Quotidien : Télétravail selon mission, titres-restaurant, locaux modernes situés au cœur de Neuilly-sur-Seine. Abonnements sportifs : Wellpass. Avantages CSE : Bien-être, art et culture. L’esprit Square Management : Une ambiance stimulante et conviviale, portée par une vie d’équipe riche (soirées DOMEX, événements sportifs, Summer Party). Nos postes sont ouverts aux personnes bénéficiant d’une reconnaissance de travailleur handicapé. Square Management encourage la diversité et l’égalité sur le lieu de travail. Tous les candidats qualifiés H/F sont pris en considération pour un emploi sur un pied d’égalité. NOTRE PROCESSUS DE RECRUTEMENT Entretien 1 : découverte avec un(e) Talent Recruiter (30 min) Entretien 2 : avec un(e) Manager (45 min) Entretien 3 : avec un(e) Consultant(e) expérimenté(e) (45 min) Échange final : avec un(e) Partner et proposition d’embauche Apply Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Full-time Job function Consulting, Information Technology, and Sales Industries Business Consulting and Services Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Square Management by 2x See who you know
