title: Job Posting Title Global Tech Process Expert – SAP SD/MM & Intercompany
description: LEAP is one of the major transformation programs currently at Pernod Ricard. It is focused on designing and building our future-proof ERP system, enabling the harmonization of all transactional processes across the Group. As part of this large-scale transformation program, the Global Tech Process Expert – SAP SD/MM & Intercompany will play a critical role in ensuring that Order-to-Cash (SD), Procure-to-Pay (MM), Intercompany processes and API-based integrations are accurately defined, designed, developed, and implemented within the SAP S/4HANA Private Edition landscape. This role requires deep expertise in SAP SD and MM modules, strong knowledge of intercompany flows (including Advanced Intercompany), and solid experience in API-based integrations, from both business and technology perspectives. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Process Design Act as a subject matter expert for SAP SD/MM processes, intercompany flows and API-based integrations. Collaborate closely with Supply Chain, Finance, logistics and IT stakeholders to understand, challenge and translate functional requirements into robust and scalable SAP solutions. Contribute to the design of end-to-end intercompany processes, including Order-to-Cash, Procure-to-Pay (standard and Advanced Intercompany). Ensure consistency and integration across modules (SD, MM, FI/CO, TM, WMS, PP-PI, QM). Define integration patterns using APIs (synchronous/asynchronous) to connect SAP with external applications through a centralized middleware. Contribute to documenting user stories and detailed functional specifications including processes, data models, interfaces and API contracts (payloads, endpoints, error handling). Secure convergence (standardization & harmonization) while fostering automation, scalability and data reliability. Stay up to date with SAP best practices, S/4HANA innovations and API-first integration strategies. Build & Test Contribute to the documentation of use cases, test scenarios, test scripts and data migration strategy. Configure and customize SAP S/4HANA SD/MM functionalities including sales, billing, procurement and intercompany flows. Design and validate SAP standard APIs (OData, SOAP) and custom APIs via SAP Integration Suite / middleware. Work closely with development teams to implement and secure RICEFW objects and API integrations. Ensure proper end-to-end integration testing via APIs across SD, MM, FI and logistics systems. Support and lead testing phases (unit testing, integration testing, UAT). Ensure data integrity, consistency and performance of API integrations. Support the definition and execution of cut-over strategy. Training & Roll-Out Contribute to training material documentation and deliver sessions to key users. Support roll-out strategy and deployment across entities, ensuring integration readiness. Post Go-Live / Hypercare Support post go-live activities from both functional and technical perspectives. Monitor API integrations, interfaces and message flows in production. Troubleshoot and resolve system or integration issues. Support business teams to stabilize processes and ensure adoption. Project Management Report on activities within the Program Management framework. Anticipate risks, identify deviations and propose mitigation actions. Ensure delivery aligns with timeline, quality and budget expectations. Promote CORE model solutions and SAP best practices. Key Interactions Strong interactions with Supply Chain, Finance, IT teams, integration partners and external platforms (3PL, carriers). Facilitate workshops, design sessions and decision-making forums. Drive alignment on intercompany and integration design. Ensure strong documentation discipline and transparent communication. YOUR PROFILE You have strong expertise in SAP SD and MM within SAP S/4HANA Private Edition. You bring proven experience with intercompany processes, including Advanced Intercompany. You have solid experience with API-based integrations, including OData, REST, SOAP, and SAP Integration Suite / CPI. You have a good understanding of SAP BTP integration services and middleware architectures. You are familiar with authentication mechanisms (OAuth, certificates), as well as integration monitoring and error handling. You can design scalable, resilient integration architectures that support complex business processes. You combine strong analytical capabilities with excellent stakeholder management and communication skills. Nice to have You have knowledge of SAP TM and eWM. You have experience within the Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) industry. You have exposure to the beverage industry. You are familiar with excise products and related regulatory requirements. You have contributed to global transformation or SAP rollout programs. WAIT THERE IS MORE We offer you an outstanding and collaborative workplace that embodies our sharing & conviviality culture, the possibility to work remotely (up to 2 days a week), a very complete mutual insurance, an attractive compensation including profit‑sharing, continuous learning opportunities, employee events, and more. Pernod Ricard is committed to offering equal opportunities to all talents. Our recruitment methods focus on skills and competencies. description: Description de l'entreprise Talan est un groupe international de conseil et d’expertise technologique en forte croissance, présent sur plusieurs continents. Nous accompagnons les entreprises dans leurs transformations stratégiques et digitales en combinant expertise métier, intégration de solutions et innovation technologique. Nous travaillons avec les plus grands éditeurs du marché : SAP, Oracle, Microsoft, Salesforce, ServiceNow. Notre ambition : aider les entreprises à moderniser leurs systèmes, intégrer l’IA et créer de la valeur durable. #Talan Description du poste Votre mission : Piloter les Projets d'Intégration SAP et Optimiser les Processus Métier Logistique Dans un environnement de projets dynamique, vous piloterez l'intégralité du cycle de vie des projets d'intégration SAP . De l'analyse approfondie des besoins clients à la mise en œuvre de solutions fonctionnelles concrètes, vous serez un acteur clé de la transformation digitale des processus métier de nos partenaires. Votre expertise et votre vision seront essentielles pour améliorer leur efficacité opérationnelle . Vous aurez l'opportunité d' implémenter des solutions SAP innovantes comme le S/4 Hana , Public Cloud ou encore IBP , et d'assurer un excellent niveau d'engagement et de qualité même après le déploiement (post Go-Live). Qualifications Nous recherchons un(e) passionné(e) de SAP avec au moins 4 ans d’expérience en tant que Consultant SAP Logistique, spécialisé(e) dans les modules WM, MM et/ou PP, etc. Vous avez une capacité à comprendre et à analyser les besoins métiers, d’excellentes compétences en communication et de gestion de projet, un esprit d’équipe, de la rigueur, et une maîtrise de l’anglais, avec des déplacements ponctuels à l’étranger. Vous souhaitez rejoindre une société spécialisée sur SAP, reconnue pour son expertise , capable de répondre à toutes les solutions SAP . Une entreprise ou l'innovation et l'humain sont au cœur de nos préoccupations. Vous cherchez à participer à des projets innovants . L’accompagnement personnalisé est important pour vous. Vous l'avez compris, nous cherchons de belles personnalités, des personnes motivées et dynamiques, capable de relever les défis que nous confient nos clients. #TalanFrance Informations supplémentaires Quels avantages offrons nous ? Télétravail jusqu’à 3 jours selon les missions, prime d’équipement de 100 € Offre de formation complète sur site et à distance. title: Finance- Accounts Payable Manager
description: The following description is not intended as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties and skills required of personnel so classified. Management retains the discretion of adding or changing the duties of the position at any time. The following description is not intended as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties and skills required of personnel so classified. Management retains the discretion of adding or changing the duties of the position at any time. Summary We are seeking an experienced, pragmatic, and analytical Accounts Payable Manager to lead our corporate AP function. The ideal candidate will seamlessly balance hands-on operational excellence with strategic leadership. You will first ensure smooth daily P2P operations and accurate month-end closings, while empowering and mentoring the AP team. Leveraging your strong analytical mindset, you will perform regular financial reviews to identify cost inefficiencies and, ultimately, architect professionally, drive systemic efficiency, timeliness, and absolute accuracy. Key Responsibilities Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. Core AP Operations &P2P Process Management. Oversee the daily operations of the Accounts Payable team, ensuring timely and accurate processing of vendor invoices, employee expense reports, and cash disbursements. Enforce strict 3-way match protocols (PO, Goods Receipt, and Invoice) across all legal entities, findting and tracking any variances. Manage corporate treasury payments and bank disbursement batches, optimizing Days Payable Outstanding (DPO) while maintaining healthy vendor relationships and safeguarding internal control (SoD) frameworks. Lead the AP month-end and year-end closing process, including accrued expenses estimates, accounts payable aging analysis, and intercompany reconciliations. 2.Team Leadership & Talent Enablement. Lead, mentor, and develop the Accounts Payable team, establishing clear performance metrics (KPIs) aligned with process accuracy and cycle times. Empower team members by translating complex compliance requirements into practical, actionable training and standard operating procedures (SOPs). Design and execute a reliable talent succession plan, identifying high-potential team members and guiding them toward career advancement within the finance organization. AP Analytics & Reporting Develop and present comprehensive monthly/quarterly AP review reports for leadership, highlighting departmental spending trends, process cycle times, and operational. Analyze key operational metrics (e.g., invoice processing costs, payment accuracy rates) and monitor T&E compliance and recurring vendor disbursement patterns to detect and eliminate cost inefficiencies. Workflow Architecture & Foolproof Design Diagnose & Optimize: Identify systemic process vulnerabilities from historical data and review reports, and continuously optimize the end-to-end P2P and expense reimbursement workflows. Implement Foolproof Controls: Embed proactive "foolproof" logical checks within the ERP and automated AP systems to automatically intercept errors before they occur (e.g., system blocks for duplicate invoice numbers, redundant supplier bank detail modifications, or overlapping vehicle rental periods). System Automation: Leverage advanced technologies (OCR, automated workflows) to achieving maximum efficiency and absolute accuracy. QUALIFICATOINS Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. CPA, CMA, or professional certifications are highly preferred. Minimum 5-8 years of corporate accounting experience, with at least 3 years in a supervisory or leadership role within a US-affiliated or multinational company. Exceptional logical reasoning, capable of translating raw ledgers into executive-ready review reports and diagnosing subtle tracking anomalies. Strong process engineering mindset with a track record of implementing automated, systemic controls that eliminate manual inefficiencies and safeguard financial accuracy. Excellent coaching, communication, and SOP authoring abilities. Deep understanding of US GAAP, SOX/internal control frameworks, and Segregation of Duties (SoD). Proficiency in enterprise ERP (SAP, Oracle, NetSuite) and AP tools (Concur, Coupa). KEY COMPETENCIES： Ability to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies within high-volume transaction data; translates raw financial ledgers into actionable management insights. A self-driven urge to optimize processes and eliminate manual bottlenecks; passionate about leveraging technology to build error-proof (Poka-Yoke) workflows. Strong cross-functional coordination and communication skills; capable of holding departments accountable to T&E policies and compliance guidelines firmly but diplomatically. Adaptable to fast-paced corporate environments, with the ability to manage competing priorities during critical periods. ROLE PROGRESSION & SUCCESS MEASURES: Days Payable Outstanding (DPO): Optimized according to company cash flow strategy. Invoice Processing Cycle Time (DPO): Average time taken from invoice receipt to system booking. T&E Policy Compliance Rate: Accuracy of employee expense claims and reduction in repetitive/anomalous claims. Audit Deficiencies: Zero material weaknesses or significant deficiencies identified in the P2P cycle during internal/external audits. Our management team is dedicated to this policy with respect to recruitment, hiring, placement, promotion, transfer, training, compensation, benefits, employee activities and general treatment during employment. title: SAP S/4HANA System Specialist
description: Brief Description SAP S/4HANA System Specialist About The Role As part of the Americas team, SANY is seeking a driven, forward-thinking, and highly motivated SAP S/4HANA System Specialist , to lead and support our enterprise-wide ERP transformation initiative as we transition from SAP ECC 6.0 to SAP S/4HANA. This highly visible role will serve as the primary ERP subject matter expert and strategic partner for SANY America, working closely with leadership, business stakeholders, China headquarters, and external implementation partners. With deep investment and backing from SANY Group, the Americas business continues to grow rapidly, and this role is critical to delivering competitive, customer-focused products that support long-term market expansion. The SAP S/4HANA System Specialist will play a critical role in planning, executing, and supporting SANY America's SAP S/4HANA transformation program. This individual will participate throughout the entire project lifecycle, from business requirements gathering and solution design through testing, deployment, and post-go-live support. Following implementation, the successful candidate will own ongoing ERP operations, continuous improvement initiatives, and system optimization efforts to ensure the platform effectively supports business growth and operational excellence. With current revenues over $500 million, SANY America continues to make strong progress and significant investments in the Americas market; the organization is now pursuing an aggressive growth strategy and is looking to move quickly beyond this initial platform and increase market share. Key Responsibilities ERP Transformation Leadership Support and drive the migration from SAP ECC 6.0 to SAP S/4HANA. Participate in ERP roadmap planning and implementation strategy development. Assist in evaluating and selecting SAP implementation partners and consultants. Collaborate on business process design, solution architecture, and system configuration. Coordinate System Integration Testing (SIT) and User Acceptance Testing (UAT). Support data migration planning, execution, and validation. Assist with cutover planning and go-live readiness activities. Provide post-implementation hyper care support. Manage project risks, issues, timelines, and action plans. Deliver project status updates to executive leadership and stakeholders. Business Process Optimization Work with the sales team to develop channel strategy for asphalt pavers identifying dealer gaps, target geographies, and priority customer segments. Support the launch of new paver models in North America, coordinating across product, service, and marketing teams. Facilitate workshops with business leaders and end users. Gather, document, and analyze business requirements. Identify opportunities to improve and standardize business processes. Evaluate SAP best practices and recommend scalable solutions. Minimize unnecessary customization while maximizing business value. Support key business functions including: Finance and Accounting Sales and Distribution Procurement and Inventory Management Manufacturing Operations Supply Chain Management System Administration & Continuous Improvement Serve as the primary SAP system owner following go-live. Provide ongoing user support and issue resolution. Maintain SAP configurations and change management processes. Manage user access, security roles, and authorization controls. Coordinate upgrades, enhancements, patches, and system maintenance. Monitor system performance, integrations, and background jobs. Develop user training materials and support documentation. Maintain SOPs, knowledge bases, and operational documentation. Lead ERP enhancement initiatives and continuous improvement projects. Support internal audits, compliance requirements, and governance activities. Required Qualifications 10+ years of SAP implementation and support experience. 5+ years of SAP S/4HANA experience. Experience participating in at least two full-cycle SAP S/4HANA implementations. Experience serving as a: Core Project Team Member Module Lead Solution Lead Solution Architect Strong understanding of manufacturing ERP environments. Preferred Qualifications SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA migration projects. Brownfield migration experience. Global ERP rollouts. Multi-country implementation experience. Global template deployment experience. FUNCTIONAL EXPERTISE Candidates should possess strong expertise in at least two core SAP functional areas. Preferred Functional Combinations FI/CO (Finance & Controlling) + SD (Sales & Distribution) FI/CO (Finance & Controlling) + MM (Materials Management) Additional Experience In The Following Areas Is Highly Desirable PP (Production Planning) QM (Quality Management) EWM (Extended Warehouse Management) Service Management INDUSTRY BACKGROUND Required Manufacturing industry experience Preferred Construction Equipment Heavy Machinery Industrial Equipment Automotive Manufacturing Agricultural Equipment Experience with organizations such as Caterpillar, Komatsu, Volvo Construction Equipment, John Deere, CNH Industrial, Liebherr, Hitachi Construction Machinery, or Terex is highly valued. BUSINESS PROCESS KNOWLEDGE Experience Supporting End-to-end Business Processes Including Order-to-Cash (O2C) Procure-to-Pay (P2P) Record-to-Report (R2R) Plan-to-Produce Service-to-Cash Warranty Management EDUCATION Bachelor's degree required in: Information Systems Computer Science Software Engineering Finance Supply Chain Management Business Administration Related field Master's degree preferred. Certifications Preferred SAP S/4HANA Certification SAP Solution Architect Certification SAP Activate Certification PMP Certification Language Requirements Fluent English required. Mandarin Chinese language skills strongly preferred. Travel Requirements Occasional domestic travel within the United States. International travel to China as required by project needs. Work Environment & Physical Requirements On site-Peachtree City, GA Compensation & Benefits Compensation will be competitive and commensurate with experience. It will include a base salary and an annual performance bonus. Medical, dental, and vision Paid Time Off 401(k) savings plans Health Savings Account (HSA) Flexible Spending Accounts (FSAs) Company paid STD and LTD benefits Life Insurance Employee Assistance Program (EAP) EQUAL OPPORTUNITY STATEMENT SANY America is an Equal Opportunity Employer and considers all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, or any other protected status under applicable federal, state, or local law. DRUG AND ALCOHOL FREE WORKPLACE NOTICE SANY America is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a drug and alcohol-free workplace consistent with applicable federal, state, and local laws. Applicants may be required to complete pre-employment screenings, including drug and/or alcohol testing, as a condition of employment where permitted by applicable law. Testing requirements, employment practices, and workplace policies may vary based on work location, position responsibilities, business necessity, workplace safety considerations, customer requirements, contractual obligations, and applicable regulatory standards. SANY America complies with applicable federal, state, and local laws relating to marijuana/cannabis use, including applicable medical and recreational marijuana laws where permitted. However, applicants and employees are prohibited from being impaired or under the influence of alcohol, illegal drugs, or other prohibited substances while working, operating Company vehicles or equipment, performing safety sensitive duties, conducting Company business, or while on Company property, consistent with applicable law. Certain positions may be designated as safety sensitive or subject to additional regulatory, customer, contractual, operational, or workplace safety requirements and may require participation in pre employment, random, reasonable suspicion, post-accident, return to duty, follow up, or other drug and/or alcohol testing programs in accordance with applicable federal, state, and local laws. title: Business Analyst
description: Payrate: $42.75 - $42.75/hr. Summary: The main function of a business operations specialist is to maintain vendor relationships, acting as a business analyst for non-IT projects or filling organizational roles above an administrative level, but below a management level. Other responsibilities may include maintaining all project management processes, including project goals, deadlines, metrics, and budget. The business operations specialist usually reports to a project manager or director. Responsibilities: Partner with internal distribution partners to coordinate shipments and escalations. Perform data analytics on the inventory flow of materials from production through third-party warehouse vendors. Work with the Financial Strategy team to manage all inventory at the third-party vendor warehouse and perform device inventory tracking for all retail display locations using Warehouse Management System. Support and streamline forward and reverse logistics. Complete adhoc audits of all samples in consolidator’s inventory. Drive Retail Supply Chain’s innovation efforts, managing those projects from initiation to close. Prepare weekly executive reports detailing the status of open projects/initiatives. Leverage PPT & Excel proficiency to translate detailed information into brief and clear executive updates in e-mail and dashboard reporting. Utilize dashboards & tableau to provide decision makers on demand data. Establish and maintain communication services across business units or from the project team to the organization. Maintain the storage and retrieval of all project communications data and business metrics. Review contracts, cost proposals, and contract supplements. Establish and document business processes. Set up project and work breakdown structures. Track project budgets and expenditures, monitor transaction controls and costs against budgets. Qualifications: Bachelor's Degree from an accredited university in business management, economics, finance, accounting or a relevant field. 3 years of experience in Business Operations, Financial Operations, or Supply Chain Management. Demonstrated experience in navigating cross-functional teams. Mobile industry or consumer electronics retail experience strongly preferred. Advanced to expert proficiency in MS Excel, PowerPoint, & Tableau. Skills: Strong analytical skills and ability to communicate key takeaways through impactful story-telling. Objectively able to look at available information and make recommendations that fit the business needs on multiple scales of scope. Positive attitude and ability to adjust as changes occur. Demonstrated leadership and ability to lead cross-functional partners without direct authority. Knowledge of SAP inventory and logistics system preferred. Ability to apply accounting and mathematical principles to work as needed. Ability to analyze business trends and project future revenues and expenses. Pay Transparency: The typical base pay for this role across the U.S. is: $42.75 - $42.75/hr. Non-exempt positions are eligible for overtime at a rate of 1.5 times the base hourly rate for all hours worked in excess of 40 in a work week, or as required by state or local law. Final offer amounts, within the base pay set forth above, are determined by factors including your relevant skills, education and experience. Full-time employees are eligible to select from different benefits packages. Packages may include medical, dental, and vision benefits, health savings accounts with qualified medical plan enrollment, 10 paid days off, 3 days paid bereavement leave, 401(k) plan participation with employer match, life and disability insurance, commuter benefits, dependent care flexible spending account, accident insurance, critical illness insurance, hospital indemnity insurance, accommodations and reimbursement for work travel, and discretionary performance or recognition bonus. Sick leave and mobile phone reimbursement provided based on state or local law. title: Financial Analyst
description: Payrate: $50.00 - $50.00/hr. Summary: The main functions of a financial analyst are to gather and analyze financial information. They typically conduct quantitative analyses of information affecting investment programs of public or private institutions. A typical financial analyst is responsible for analyzing and communicating financial information for clients. Responsibilities Develop and maintain excellent working relationships with all assigned levels within and outside the company. Gather and analyze large sets of data from multiple sources, preparing both quantitative and qualitative analysis of information. Manage vendor invoices, ensuring their accuracy and timely payment processing. Reconcile accounts and maintain accurate financial records to foster strong business partnerships with Sales finance, Channel Marketing, Procurement, and AP teams. Submit budget requests (Plan Proposals) for Marketing programs and ensure adherence to budget policies, procedures, and regulations. Manage the payment schedule, ensuring timely payments to vendors, and maintain communication regarding payment status. Verify purchase orders against invoices to ensure goods and services are received and paid for correctly. Assist in month-end closing activities, including accruals and reconciliations. Ensure compliance with company policies, procedures, and relevant regulations. Review POP (proof of performance), BOL, Contracts, Term Sheets (MDF and SD) for payment. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Business, Accounting, or related field and a minimum of 3-5 years of progressive, post-baccalaureate experience in the job offered or occupation. Experience in managing A/R, A/P, and financial closing processes. Demonstrated ability to identify areas of process improvement and manage improvement projects until completion. Experience in business process review and analysis, including financial and operational analysis and audits. Skills Strong attention to detail and organizational skills. In-depth knowledge and expertise with SAP or other financial systems. Demonstrated competency in both oral and written communication modes for both internal and external personnel at various levels, especially in the sales and marketing, and financial areas. Ability to work independently and in a team environment to achieve personal and team goals and complete assignments within established time frames. Demonstrated excellence in time management skills and follow-up to ensure meeting on-time deliverables. Ability to make decisions and solve problems on time-sensitive matters. Pay Transparency: The typical base pay for this role across the U.S. is: $50.00 - $50.00 /hour . Non-exempt positions are eligible for overtime at a rate of 1.5 times the base hourly rate for all hours worked in excess of 40 in a work week, or as required by state or local law. Final offer amounts, within the base pay set forth above, are determined by factors including your relevant skills, education and experience. Full-time employees are eligible to select from different benefits packages. title: Purchasing Analyst
description: At Polaris Inc., we have fun doing what we love by driving change and innovation. We empower employees to take on challenging assignments and roles with an elevated level of responsibility in our agile working environment. Our people make us who we are, and we create incredible products and experiences that empower us to THINK OUTSIDE. Position Overview As a Purchasing Analyst, you will manage supplier activity for the Parts, Garments & Accessories business to support on-time product delivery to Polaris distribution facilities. You will coordinate closely with demand planning analysts to ensure appropriate purchase quantities are reviewed, ordered, and adjusted to support inventory and service level objectives. In this role, you will help manage purchase orders, supplier performance, backorders, and purchase order or invoicing discrepancies. This is a compelling opportunity to contribute directly to inventory performance and supplier execution within a large, cross-functional supply chain environment. Key Responsibilities Manage supplier relationships to support consistent performance and on-time delivery to Polaris distribution facilities. Review system-recommended purchase quantities and collaborate with demand planning analysts to improve purchasing accuracy. Approve purchase orders, monitor delivery status, and expedite orders as needed to support fill rate goals. Resolve backorder issues in alignment with service level and customer support objectives. Execute supplier cost and inventory reduction initiatives. Negotiate cost, lead time, minimum order quantities, and economic order quantities to support business objectives. Analyze and resolve supplier invoicing, purchase order, and related discrepancy issues. Proactively identify and communicate urgent supplier, delivery, or purchasing issues to leadership. Desired Competencies Communication: You clearly convey information, ideas, and expectations to diverse audiences, adjusting your message to ensure understanding and alignment. Collaboration: You work effectively with others across functions and levels, contributing to shared goals while respecting different perspectives. Planning & Organization: You manage time, resources, and priorities effectively to meet deadlines and deliver consistent results. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree. Minimum of 1 year of relevant experience managing the purchase order lifecycle. Strong aptitude for solving problems and improving processes. Strong ability to negotiate and resolve conflict. Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and communication skills. Ability to work effectively with minimal supervision. Ability to work in a hybrid environment. Ability to travel up to 10%. This position is not eligible for sponsorship. The starting pay range for Minnesota is $64,550 to $84,750 per year. Individual salaries and positioning within the range are determined through a wide variety of factors including but not limited to education, experience, knowledge, skills, and geography. While individual pay could fall anywhere in the range based on these factors, it is not common to start at the high end or top of the range. #HYBRID To qualify for this position, former employees must be eligible for rehire, and current employees must be in good standing. We are an ambitious, resourceful, and driven workforce, which empowers us to THINK OUTSIDE. Apply today! At Polaris we put our employees first, by offering a holistic approach to their health and financial wellbeing. Polaris is proud to offer competitive compensation, including a market-leading profit-sharing plan that is fundamental to our pay-for-performance culture. At Polaris, employees are owners of the company through company contributions to our Employee Stock Ownership Plan and discounted employee stock purchases plan. Employees receive a generous matching contribution to 401(k), financial wellness education and consultation to plan for their financial future. In addition to competitive pay, Polaris provides a comprehensive suite of benefits, including health, dental, and vision insurance, wellness programs, paid time off, gym & personal training reimbursement, life insurance and disability offerings. Through the Polaris Foundation and our Polaris Gives paid volunteer time off, we support employees who actively volunteer their time, efforts, and passions to improve the health and wellbeing of the communities in which they live, play and work. Employees at Polaris drive our success and are rewarded for their commitment. About Polaris As the global leader in powersports, Polaris Inc. (NYSE: PII) pioneers product breakthroughs and enriching experiences and services that have invited people to discover the joy of being outdoors since our founding in 1954. Polaris' high-quality product line-up includes the Polaris RANGER®, RZR® and Polaris GENERAL™ side-by-side off-road vehicles; Sportsman® all-terrain off-road vehicles; military and commercial off-road vehicles; snowmobiles; Slingshot® moto-roadsters; Aixam quadricycles; Goupil electric vehicles; and pontoon and deck boats, including industry-leading Bennington pontoons. Polaris enhances the riding experience with a robust portfolio of parts, garments, and accessories. Proudly headquartered in Minnesota, Polaris serves more than 100 countries across the globe. www.polaris.com EEO Statement Polaris Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer and will make all employment-related decisions without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, marital status, familial status, status with regard to public assistance, membership or activity in a local commission, protected veteran status, or any other status protected by applicable law. Applicants with a disability that are in need of an accommodation to complete the application process, or otherwise need assistance or an accommodation in the recruiting process, should contact Human Resources at 800-765-2747 or Talent.Acquisition@Polaris.com . To read more about employment discrimination protection under U.S. federal law, see: Know Your Rights: Workplace Discrimination is Illegal (eeoc.gov) . title: Supply Chain Analyst
description: Job Details Description About Olympic Steel: Since 1954, Olympic Steel has been focused on safe, profitable, and sustainable growth through the direct sale of a wide range of processed metal products and several metal-intensive branded products. Guided by our Core Values, we put safety first – always, and we believe in cultivating an inclusive workplace where employee belonging and empowerment are foundational to everything we do. Olympic Steel offers employees the best of both worlds: the culture and community of a closely connected team with the resources, opportunity, and stability of a large, growing organization. Job Description The Supply Chain Analyst will support the Shaw Stainless and Alloy division by analyzing inventory, purchasing activity, and supply chain data to align supply with customer demand and business objectives. This person will develop reporting methods and analytical tools to support purchasing decisions, inventory optimization, and operational performance. The Supply Chain Analyst will support the Shaw Stainless and Alloy division by developing relationships with the vendors and collaborate with internal sales team to resolve any customer or supplier issues, Develops diffrent reporting methods and structures,conducts research on market conditionsand helps advise divisions on purchasing and any upcoming supply challenges, Analyze data to identify areas of supply chain operations to improve efficiency, works on business projects and support new and existing business to enhance supply chain operations. Qualifications Bachelor’s Degree in Supply Chain, Operations, Business or other related field Previous steel experience strongly preferred 1+ year of Supply chain experience in a manufacturing setting, including ERP systems Applicants must be authorized to work in the United States without the need for visa sponsorship. Why Work For Olympic Steel Olympic Steel offers comprehensive benefits, including: Medical, dental, and vision benefits Paid holidays and vacation 401(k) with employer match Tuition reimbursement & Employee Discount Programs Referral program We offer more than a competitive total compensation package. We provide our employees the ability to build a meaningful life-long career. When you work at Olympic Steel, you can make a significant impact – at our company, in the local community and in the world. For a comprehensive view of our benefits visit Steelperks.com. title: Director, Supply Chain
description: Position Summary We are seeking an experienced Director of Supply Chain to lead end‑to‑end supply chain strategy and execution for a prescription drug, preparing for its first commercial launch. This role will be responsible for building and managing all supply chain capabilities required to transition from clinical to commercial supply, including demand planning, S&OP, direct oversight and execution of drug product manufacturing, packaging, and labeling operations, external manufacturing and distribution readiness, CMO/CPO/3PL management, product release planning, and launch inventory management. In this small biotech organization, the Director serves as the single‑threaded owner of finished drug product supply, from manufacturing through delivery to the market. The role requires both strategic leadership and hands‑on execution, working cross‑functionally with Technical, Quality, Regulatory, Finance, Commercial, and external partners to ensure launch readiness and ongoing commercial supply continuity. Key Responsibilities Launch Supply Chain Strategy & Launch Readiness Develop and execute the end-to-end commercial supply chain strategy and launch readiness plan (manufacturing, packaging/labeling, distribution, logistics) to ensure uninterrupted supply. Lead demand forecasting, supply planning, inventory strategy (risk buffers), and scenario modeling to support decisions. Define and track supply chain KPIs and dashboards to drive performance and proactively manage risk. Drug Product Manufacturing Management Own end-to-end drug product manufacturing for commercial supply (planning, campaign readiness, execution, and continuity). Serve as primary interface to drug product CMOs; align schedules, materials, yields, and release timing; partner with Technical and Quality on deviations and lot disposition. Manage tradeoffs across supply risk, inventory, cost, and timelines in a resource-constrained environment. Packaging, Labeling & Commercial Product Presentation Lead commercial packaging and labeling operations end-to-end (CPOs, artwork readiness, serialization readiness, packaging execution). Manage label lifecycle (version control, change control, label inventory) to meet regulatory and distribution requirements and minimize obsolescence. Third‑Party Logistics & Commercial Distribution Lead 3PL operations (warehousing, order fulfillment, distribution, returns, reconciliation) and ensure network readiness via SOPs, SLAs, and KPI monitoring. Oversee GDP compliance and import/export activities to enable compliant, reliable distribution. Planning, S&OP & Inventory Management Build and run the S&OP process, integrating inputs from Commercial, Finance, Quality, and Operations to align demand, supply, and inventory. Develop demand forecasts; manage finished goods, WIP, and packaging inventory to support launch and ongoing supply while minimizing write-offs and obsolescence. Commercial Supply Governance & Change Management Own commercial change control; assess supply, inventory, and distribution impacts of CMC, labeling, site, and process changes. Partner with Technical, Quality, and Regulatory to implement post-approval changes and lifecycle plans (alternate sites, process improvements, scale changes) while minimizing supply disruption. Product Release, Compliance and Regulatory Interface Own commercial lot release planning; align testing, documentation, disposition, and distribution release. Lead cross-functional release readiness reviews; partner with Quality and Regulatory to support GMP/GDP compliance, serialization, and distribution requirements. Cost of Goods & Systems Develop and maintain commercial COGS models with Finance/Technical; drive supply-led cost improvements while maintaining compliance and reliability. Lead supply risk assessments and mitigation (safety stock, alternate sourcing); implement and improve supply chain systems (ERP, forecasting, inventory) and supporting SOPs. Qualifications Required 10+ years of experience in pharmaceutical or biotech supply chain, including at least 5 years in a leadership role. Direct, hands‑on experience supporting the commercial launch of a prescription drug, including operational ownership of drug product manufacturing, packaging/labeling, and distribution. Strong understanding of GMP/GDP requirements and FDA regulatory expectations. Bachelor’s degree in supply chain, engineering, life Sciences, operations, or equivalent professional certification. Experience managing CMOs, CPOs, and 3PLs in a regulated environment. Preferred Experience in small‑to‑mid‑size biotech companies transitioning from clinical to commercial stage. ERP or supply chain system implementation experience (e.g., NetSuite, SAP, Oracle). Serialization and track‑and‑trace experience. Key Competencies Strategic thinker with strong operational execution capability. Hands‑on leader comfortable wearing multiple hats. Skilled in risk management, scenario planning, and cross‑functional alignment. Clear, effective communicator with strong stakeholder‑management skills. Ability to build structure and discipline in an evolving organization. description: Title: Quality Analyst - WFH Experience: Fresher, 2+ years of experience Salary: 10 LPA As a Quality Analyst fresher, you'll be responsible for ensuring product quality by conducting tests, identifying defects, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to resolve issues, ultimately contributing to the delivery of high-quality products. Responsibilities Testing and Quality Assurance: Conduct thorough quality tests on products and services, including manual and automated testing. Identify defects and areas for improvement in products and processes. Develop and document test plans, test cases, and test scripts. Analyze test results and identify trends or patterns. Collaboration and Communication: Collaborate with cross-functional teams (development, product management, etc.) to resolve quality issues. Document and report defects for corrective action. Communicate quality findings and recommendations to stakeholders. Documentation and Reporting: Maintain accurate and up-to-date documentation of test procedures, results, and findings. Prepare reports on quality metrics and issues. Process Improvement: Propose solutions to enhance overall quality and identify areas for improvement. Contribute to the development and implementation of quality standards and procedures. Learning and Development Stay up-to-date with the latest testing methodologies and tools. Seek opportunities for continuous learning and development in the field of quality assurance. Technical Skills & Qualifications Basic understanding of quality assurance principles and methodologies. Familiarity with testing tools and techniques. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Good communication and interpersonal skills. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Attention to detail and a commitment to quality. Basic understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC). A bachelor's degree in a related field (e.g., Computer Science, Engineering) or equivalent experience. title: Asst. Finance Manager
description: Business Title : Assistant Finance Manager Location : Baddi (Himachal Pradesh) ABOUT UNILEVER : Be part of the world’s most successful, purpose-led business. Work with brands that are well-loved around the world, that strive to improve the lives of our consumers and the communities around us every day. Every day, nine out of ten Indian households use our products to feel good, look good and get more out of life – giving us a unique opportunity to build a brighter future. We promote innovation, big and small, to make our business win and grow; and we believe in business as a force for good. Our brilliant business leaders and colleagues provide mentorship and inspiration, so you can be at your best. At HUL, we believe that every individual irrespective of their race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, nationality, caste, disability or marital status can bring their purpose to life. So, apply to us, to unleash your curiosity, challenge ideas and disrupt processes; use your energy to make the world a better place. As you work to make a real impact on the business and the world, we will work to help you become a better you! About HUL Finance Unilever is the place where you can bring your purpose to life through the work you do, creating a better business and a better world. You will live with brands that are loved and improve the lives of our consumers and the communities around us. We have a strong conviction that sustainable business growth is only possible when you bring together the best talent and give them the opportunity to fully harness their potential. The Finance team at HUL is a mix of extremely motivated, highly talented and curious would be leaders of tomorrow. We are the CFO factory to the world and pride ourselves in the fact that many of the past, present and future CFOs of the most reputed Indian companies trace back their roots to HUL and the fertile grooming grounds of it finance function. As technology is changing and shaping our lives and everything else around us like never before, Finance team is uniquely positioned to make the most of this opportunity and lead the organization transition into the brave new digital world. At HUL Finance we are pioneers who aim to set the pace and benchmarks for others to follow. So if you are looking to work with an organization which has a deeply embedded value culture, believes in business being a multi stakeholder model and has a Purpose which transcends profits then HUL and Finance @ HUL just might be the place for you. Job Description We are looking to hire an Assistant Finance Manager for our factory who will be responsible for managing the financial operations of a manufacturing site. The responsibilities will include: Cost Control & Budgeting: Drive factory cost management through detailed analysis of production costs, variance tracking, and adherence to annual budgets. Finance Business Partner , responsible for forecasting , tracking, and reporting an annual spend of approximately Rs 190 Cr across 15 sites (both 3P and 2P). The role ensures adherence to compliance and controls, supports statutory audit requirements, manages cost actualization and payments, and partners with the business to deliver targeted savings. Compliance & Controls: Maintain strong internal controls at the factory in all areas like- inventory, assets, gate movements, segregation of duties, etc and ensure audit readiness. Performance Management: Partner with operations to identify efficiency opportunities, track KPIs, and deliver savings initiatives without compromising quality or safety. Technology Enablement: Leverage tools like SAP, Power BI, and advanced analytics to improve process hygiene and reporting accuracy. Stakeholder Engagement: Collaborate with supply chain, procurement, and central finance teams to align factory performance with business objectives and work closely to identify optimisation opportunities. Key Skills & Requirement In HUL Finance, we believe in building careers and recruit not just for a role but for a career in the organisation. We move people every 2 – 3 years and aim at giving our employees holistic experiences across the verticals of finance. We are looking for a CA/ MBA with 2 – 5 years of experience with strong problem solving and analytical abilities and excellent inter-personal and communication skills. Our commitment to Equality, Diversity & Inclusion Unilever embraces diversity and encourages applicants from all walks of life! This means giving full and fair consideration to all applicants and continuing development of all employees regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, race, religion or belief, sex, sexual orientation, marriage and civil partnership, and pregnancy and maternity. Note: "All official offers from Unilever are issued only via our Applicant Tracking System (ATS). Offers from individuals or unofficial sources may be fraudulent—please verify before proceeding." description: Description De L'entreprise Fondé en 2025 avec déjà plus de 100 ans d'expérience, RYDGE Conseil c’est la nouvelle référence du conseil aux entrepreneurs ! Chaque jour, nos 4 500 Rydgers, répartis dans 200 bureaux partout en France, accompagnent plus de 100 000 clients - dirigeants de TPE, PME et ETI- pour transformer leurs ambitions en réalité grâce à une approche 360° au travers de 4 grands métiers - conseil, expertise comptable, gestion sociale et avocats – soutenus par nos fonctions transverses. Notre mission ? Entreprendre ensemble en confiance. Entreprendre aux côtés de nos clients entrepreneurs. Ensemble, parce que nous sommes une équipe, un collectif à toutes épreuves. En confiance, parce que notre relation est authentique et que nous maitrisons l’art et la science du conseil. Rejoindre RYDGE Conseil, c’est intégrer un collectif soudé qui valorise l’autonomie et l’initiative. Ici, chacune de vos décisions fait réellement la différence, vous avez l’opportunité de tracer votre propre voie et de contribuer directement au succès des entrepreneurs qui façonnent l’économie de demain. ALORS, PRENEZ DE L’AVANCE, VENEZ ÉCRIRE L’AVENIR AVEC NOUS : VENEZ RYDGER ! Description Du Poste En collaboration avec l'équipe Expertise Comptable, vos missions, en tant qu'Analyste comptable expérimenté F/H consistent à : Etablir la révision des comptes grâce à nos outils de pilotage dynamiques Préparer les états financiers et le projet de comptes annuels en vue de leur restitution au client Sécuriser les procédures vis-à-vis de l’administration fiscale et fiabiliser les données déclarées pour le compte de chaque client Formuler des recommandations (gestion, comptabilité, finances, fiscalité) Détecter et prévenir les besoins et enjeux spécifiques de vos clients grâce à vos analyses de données Accompagner les assistants et analystes débutants de l'équipe Qualifications Vous êtes titulaire d’une formation supérieure Bac+4/5 et bénéficiez d’une expérience minimum de 3 ans en comptabilité, finances ou gestion Vous recherchez un environnement de travail 100% digital et apprenant Vous êtes à l’aise avec l’élaboration et l’analyse des tableaux de bord Vous avez une grande capacité d’analyse qui vous permettra de préparer les rendez-vous clients Vous êtes doté d’un bon relationnel et vos qualités sont l’intégrité, la vivacité d’esprit, la rigueur et l’autonomie. Informations supplémentaires Tous nos postes sont ouverts aux personnes en situation de handicap. Un cadre flexible : télétravail possible. N'attendez plus, rejoignez un environnement de travail de qualité où le sens du collectif est clé. title: Regional SAP Maintenance Super Key User
description: Job Description Are You Ready to Make It Happen at Mondelēz International? Join our Mission to Lead the Future of Snacking. Make It with Pride. The role will provide technical leadership for the development, standardization and optimization of SAP Plant Maintenance across multiple food manufacturing sites. This role serves as the functional owner of SAP PM within Maintenance and Asset Reliability. The Regional SAP Maintenance Super Key User is responsible for designing how SAP is structured and used to support work management, preventative maintenance, reliability processes and MRO integration. How You Will Contribute You will: Design and configure SAP PM structures including equipment hierarchies, task lists, maintenance plans and workflows. Serve as the regional functional owner of the SAP PM system working on continuously improving SAP functionality aligned with business needs. Leverage CMMS to improve spare parts management system to support planned and unplanned maintenance. Identify and implement SAP system improvements, workflow enhancements, automations opportunities and process simplifications. Act as the regional SAP subject matter expert by developing and delivering training programs for planners, supervisors and core team members. Create SAP work instructions, drive adoptions of standardized SAP processes across all sites. Enable accurate reporting for PM compliance, Backlog, breakdown metrics by supporting the development of dashboards and validate data accuracy and consistency across all bakeries. Maintain master data standards, ensuring accurate systems to govern work order closure and failure coding to eliminate duplicate and obsolete parts. Partner with IT for upgrades and fixes to drive SAP enhancements and improvements. Align SAP PM and SAP MM to improve material planning and reduce emergency purchases. What You Will Bring A desire to drive your future and accelerate your career and the following experience and knowledge: Technical expertise in the maintenance area within FMCG industry and a deep knowledge in SAP MM integration knowledge. Significant experience in SAP PM and work management systems knowledge. Advanced SAP with working Excel and Power BI reporting. Excellent communication (verbal & written), coaching, and leadership skills in a team-based environment. Demonstrated abilities in Analytics, problem solving and team building with a systems thinking and improved data quality mindset. Experience in interfacing with the broader Supply Chain functions and a high level of capability around managing relationships and outcomes. More About This Role What extra capabilities you will bring: Dealing with diversities in a multicultural environment. Combine seeming opposites like being compassionately tough, setting strong but flexible standards. Conflict Management Leadership success within a regulated labor environment. This role requires the ability to work and communicate effectively at all levels within the organization. Education / Certifications: Bachelor’s degree in engineering or related technical field required. Job specific requirements: 5-10+ years’ experience within Maintenance with leadership role implementing SAP PM. Proven SAP Plant maintenance experience. Experience working within a plant maintenance organization within FMGC industry. Demonstrated ability to function effectively in fluid, fast-paced situations, with the personal leadership to influence and positively motivate self and peers. Must be able to effectively communicate both verbally and in writing with individuals at all levels of the organization. Familiarization with OSHA and FDA regulations. High standards for discipline and execution with a structured process-oriented mindset. Verbal and written English language. Travel requirements: Travel to the various manufacturing sites across the US as needed. Salary and Benefits: The base salary range for this position is $109,000 to $149,875; the exact salary depends on several factors such as experience, skills, education and location. In addition to base salary, this position is eligible for participation in a highly competitive bonus program with possibility for overachievement based on performance and company results. In addition, Mondelez International offers the following benefits: health insurance, wellness and family support programs, life and disability insurance, retirement savings plans, paid leave programs, education related programs, paid holidays and vacation time. Some of these benefits have eligibility requirements. Many of these benefits are subsidized or fully paid for by the company. No Relocation support available Business Unit Summary The United States is the largest market in the Mondelēz International family with a significant employee and manufacturing footprint. Here, we produce our well-loved household favorites to provide our consumers with the right snack, at the right moment, made the right way. We have corporate offices, sales, manufacturing and distribution locations throughout the U.S. to ensure our iconic brands—including Oreo and Chips Ahoy! cookies, Ritz, Wheat Thins and Triscuit crackers, and Swedish Fish and Sour Patch Kids confectionery products —are close at hand for our consumers across the country. title: Production and Supply Chain Manager (Global Apparel)
description: Position Overview As the Production and Supply Chain Manager at Teton Outfitters, you will lead the demand and supply planning, global sourcing, production, compliance, and quality functions that support hard- and soft-line products manufactured across international markets. You will establish a structured sales and operations planning (S&OP) process, strengthen supplier performance, and use data to align inventory and production with business demand. You will lead a small team that includes on-site, remote, and international employees and contractors, and partner with Product, Development, Finance, Logistics, Distribution, and Sales. This role offers a compelling opportunity to build effective supply chain processes, improve product availability, and shape the structure and capabilities of a developing team. Key Responsibilities Develop and lead the S&OP process, aligning demand forecasts, supply plans, production capacity, inventory strategies, and business priorities. Create and execute global sourcing and production strategies that improve supplier performance, product availability, cost competitiveness, lead times, and production capacity. Lead and develop a team of on-site, remote, and international employees and contractors across supply planning, sourcing, production, quality, and compliance. Analyze supply, demand, inventory, spend, pricing, and operational data using NetSuite, business intelligence tools, and performance dashboards to guide decisions and establish appropriate safety stock levels to deliver service improvements. Establish supplier performance metrics and scorecards, hold vendors accountable for service and quality expectations, and address delivery, capacity, and production risks. Negotiate supplier pricing, lead times, delivery terms, contracts, and service expectations while maintaining product quality and supply continuity. Oversee the development, implementation, and maintenance of quality management processes, documentation, training, reporting, and vendor social compliance programs. Evaluate product specifications, material tolerances, quality data, and customer feedback to identify trends, resolve issues, and improve finished-product quality. Partner cross-functionally with Product, Finance, Logistics, Distribution, and Sales to improve planning, product development, process flows, and operational performance. Lead continuous improvement initiatives using Lean methods, root cause analysis, Kaizen events, process documentation, and key performance indicators. Actively participate and prepare the organization for an SAP ERP transition in a few years. Desired Competencies Leadership: You inspire, guide, and empower others to achieve their best by setting clear direction, fostering trust, and creating an environment where people feel supported, motivated, and accountable for delivering results. Data & Analytical Literacy: You interpret data, metrics, or reporting to inform decisions, identify trends, and measure outcomes relevant to your role. Planning & Organization: You manage time, resources, and priorities effectively to meet deadlines and deliver consistent results. Decision Making: You are able to make informed and calculated decisions using qualitative and quantitative insights, but at times operating with some ambiguity Qualifications Bachelor’s degree required. Minimum of 10 years of supply chain experience in the apparel or consumer products industry, including exposure to overseas contract manufacturing. Minimum of 5 years of experience building, leading, and developing teams. Demonstrated experience leading the SIOP process to balance demand forecasts, inventory investments, and supply capabilities to support service, revenue, and profitability targets. Experience managing global sourcing, overseas production, supplier relationships, and contract manufacturing operations. Strong understanding of quality assurance and quality control principles and their application within a supplier or manufacturing environment. Experience analyzing supply chain and inventory data using enterprise systems and business intelligence tools; NetSuite and/or SAP experience is beneficial. Demonstrated ability to negotiate supplier costs, lead times, terms, contracts, and performance expectations. Ability to travel occasionally, including internationally. Ability to work full-time in Rigby, Idaho (hybrid work structure; Tues, Weds & Thurs in-office), with flexibility to address time-sensitive escalations involving international operations. Relocation assistance available to non-local candidates. This position is not eligible for sponsorship. #HYBRID To qualify for this position, former employees must be eligible for rehire, and current employees must be in good standing. We are an ambitious, resourceful, and driven workforce, which empowers us to THINK OUTSIDE. Apply today! About Teton Outfitters Teton Outfitters outfits consumers around the world with its diverse portfolio of iconic Powersports brands, including KLIM and 509. Founded in 1998, Teton Outfitters is one of the world’s fastest growing apparel, helmets, goggles and accessories companies within the Powersports channel. Operations span numerous geographies, product categories and distribution locations. Teton Outfitters is committed to delivering innovative products to that excite our customers and create a unforgettable experience. EEO Statement Teton Outfitters is an Equal Opportunity Employer and will make all employment-related decisions without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, marital status, familial status, status with regard to public assistance, membership or activity in a local commission, protected veteran status, or any other status protected by applicable law. location: Rio de Janeiro, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
description: Com 125 anos, a Gerdau é a maior empresa brasileira produtora de aço e uma das principais fornecedoras de aços longos e especiais do mundo. No Brasil, também produz aços planos e minério de ferro. Com o propósito de empoderar pessoas que constroem o futuro, é referência de internacionalização no setor industrial do país, está presente em vários países nas Américas e conta com 30 mil colaboradores. Sua atuação incluirá desde cadastro de clientes, implantação de pedidos, gestão de atendimento da carteira e interação e engajamento das demais áreas para viabilizar atendimento aos pedidos. Além do suporte a força de vendas e atendimento direto aos clientes. Tem papel essencial na satisfação dos nossos clientes, contribuindo na relação, melhora na assertividade na data de entrega e atendimento às necessidades dos clientes. Liberações e tratativas financeiras junto ao time do financeiro e cliente; Qualificações: Conhecimento nas rotinas de gestão da carteira (vendas internas); Atendimento e suporte ao time de vendas e clientes; Desejável conhecimento no sistema SAP (ambiente GE4); Excelente habilidade na comunicação verbal e escrita; Excel intermediário; Formação superior completa ou cursando último ano. O que desejamos: Bom relacionamento interpessoal, empatia e habilidade de comunicação; Gostar da área comercial e facilidade em se comunicar com clientes e força de vendas; Capacidade de mobilização de pessoas e engajamento; Habilidade de se relacionar com todos os públicos; Capacidade analítica e estratégica; Habilidade de questionar e provocar; Comprometido(a) com prazos e entregas; Comprometido(a) com sigilo das informações; Facilidade em trabalhar em equipe; Sentimento e atitude de dono; Espírito colaborativo e ágil. Temos como princípio a promoção de um ambiente diverso e inclusivo. title: CONSULTOR SAP - FICO S/4HANA
location: Rio de Janeiro, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
description: Grupo Granado abre as portas para você! Estamos em busca de um Consultor SAP - FICO S/4 HANA para fazer parte da nossa equipe. Se você é uma pessoa organizada, dinâmica e comprometida, esta pode ser a oportunidade perfeita para você. Como Consultor SAP - FICO S/4 HANA, você será responsável por na sustentação dos módulos FI e CO do sistema SAP S/4HANA. Responsabilidades e atribuições Responsável na sustentação dos módulos FI, CO e FSCM (Gestão de Crédito e Administração de Caixa) do sistema SAP S/4HANA, com projetos completos de acordo com as metodologias oficiais de implementações, sustentações e demais atividades. Criar e gerenciar plano de testes integrados dos cenários de FI (GL,AA, AP, AR), CO, PS e FSCM; Conduzir workshops para definição da estrutura organizacional do módulo, conceituando seus principais elementos, vantagens e desvantagens, conseguindo orientar o cliente na melhor estrutura com base nas melhores práticas do mercado; Configuração de FI (GL, AA, AP, AR), CO e FSCM em SAP versões 6.0 e acima; Requisitos e qualificações Requisitos: Graduação completa; Sólida experiência em implementação e sustentação dos módulos SAP FI, CO e FSCM em ambiente SAP S/4HANA; Vivência no segmento de varejo; Experiência em configurações dos módulos FI (GL, AA, AP e AR), CO e FSCM, além de integrações com outros módulos SAP; Conhecimento em SAP ECC e S/4HANA, Localização Brasileira e processos fiscais e bancários; Experiência com integrações via SAP CPI e APIs nativas, especificações funcionais (WRICEF), testes integrados e Migration Cockpit; Vivência com metodologias Ágil e/ou Híbrida e condução de workshops; Conhecimento em PS (Project System) será considerado um diferencial. Desejável: Certificação SAP S4/HANA FI; Conhecimento de Microsoft Office (Excel, Powerpoint, Word e Project). Conhecimento Agile e mecanismos de Scrum e gestão de histórias de usuários. Informações adicionais Um ambiente integrado, leve e acolhedor, onde a colaboração, o respeito e a parceria fazem parte da nossa rotina. Horário flexível, promovendo mais autonomia para você organizar sua rotina e ganhar qualidade de vida. Vale-Refeição, para você aproveitar suas pausas com tranquilidade e bons momentos. Plano de saúde, plano odontológico e seguro de vida, porque cuidar de você e de quem você ama é prioridade. TotalPass, incentivando o movimento, a energia e o equilíbrio entre corpo e mente. Descontos exclusivos nas nossas marcas: Granado, Phebo e Care Natural Beauty — porque quem faz parte também merece aproveitar. Um ambiente que estimula a criatividade, a inovação e o aprendizado contínuo, com apoio ao seu desenvolvimento por meio do Programa de Educação Continuada, que inclui subsídios para cursos de aprimoramento, graduação, pós-graduação e idiomas. Parcerias educacionais com instituições como Estácio e Open English, facilitando ainda mais sua jornada de desenvolvimento. Programa de Participação nos Resultados, valorizando e reconhecendo quem cresce junto e contribui com nossa história. Kit Bebê Granado, um carinho especial para colaboradores que se tornam papais ou mamães, celebrando a chegada do seu bebê com produtos desenvolvidos com o cuidado e a tradição que fazem parte da nossa história. Happy Day | Folga de Aniversário, o seu dia merece uma comemoração especial! Aproveite uma folga no dia do seu aniversário e celebre do seu jeito. Escritório no Edifício Vista Guanabara, com uma vista inspiradora que traz mais leveza e bem-estar à rotina de trabalho. title: Corporate Manager
description: Position Summary We are seeking an experienced Corporate IT Manager in Information Technology, including leadership roles in IT Infrastructure, Information Security, Digital Transformation, and IT Governance . The ideal candidate will lead enterprise-wide technology initiatives, ensure secure and reliable IT operations, and align technology strategy with business objectives. Key Responsibilities IT Strategy & Leadership Develop and execute the organization's IT strategy in alignment with business goals. Lead the IT department and manage cross-functional technology teams. Define IT roadmaps, budgets, and technology investment plans. Advise executive leadership on emerging technologies and digital innovation. Establish IT governance frameworks, policies, and standards. IT Infrastructure Management Oversee enterprise IT infrastructure, including servers, storage, virtualization, cloud platforms, networks, and data centers. Ensure high availability, scalability, and performance of IT systems. Lead infrastructure modernization and cloud migration initiatives. Manage disaster recovery, backup, and business continuity plans. Optimize infrastructure costs while maintaining service quality. Information Security & Cybersecurity Develop and implement enterprise information security strategies. Ensure compliance with cybersecurity frameworks and industry standards. Lead vulnerability assessments, penetration testing, and security audits. Manage Security Operations, Identity & Access Management (IAM), and endpoint security. Respond to cybersecurity incidents and implement corrective actions. Promote security awareness across the organization. Digital Transformation Drive digital transformation initiatives across business functions. Evaluate and implement emerging technologies to improve operational efficiency. Lead automation, AI, cloud, and process optimization projects. Collaborate with business stakeholders to identify technology opportunities. Measure and report the business value of digital initiatives. IT Operations & Service Management Ensure stable IT operations and high service availability. Implement and maintain ITIL-based IT Service Management processes. Monitor IT KPIs and Service Level Agreements (SLAs). Improve incident, problem, change, and asset management processes. Manage vendor performance and outsourced IT services. Enterprise Applications Oversee implementation and support of ERP, CRM, HRMS, and other enterprise applications. Ensure application integration and data integrity. Manage software licensing and lifecycle. Governance, Risk & Compliance Ensure compliance with ISO 27001, NCA ECC, NIST, CIS Controls, and relevant regulatory requirements. Develop IT policies, standards, and procedures. Conduct IT risk assessments and mitigation planning. Support internal and external audits. Project & Portfolio Management Lead large-scale IT projects from planning through implementation. Manage project budgets, timelines, and resources. Coordinate with vendors, consultants, and business stakeholders. Apply Agile, Waterfall, or hybrid project management methodologies. Vendor & Financial Management Manage strategic relationships with technology vendors. Negotiate contracts and service agreements. Prepare and control IT operating and capital budgets. Evaluate technology investments and ROI. People Leadership Lead, mentor, and develop IT managers, engineers, analysts, and support teams. Conduct performance reviews and succession planning. Build a high-performing and collaborative IT organization. Required Qualifications Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Information Systems, Computer Engineering, or a related field. Minimum 10 years of IT experience , including 5+ years in a managerial or leadership role . Required Technical Skills Enterprise IT Infrastructure Cloud Computing (Microsoft Azure, AWS, or Google Cloud) Network Architecture Virtualization (VMware, Hyper-V) Microsoft Windows Server & Linux Administration Active Directory & Azure AD Microsoft 365 Administration Storage & Backup Solutions Disaster Recovery & Business Continuity Cybersecurity Operations Firewalls (Palo Alto, Fortinet, Cisco) SIEM Solutions Endpoint Security Identity & Access Management Zero Trust Architecture IT Governance Digital Transformation Enterprise Architecture ERP Systems (SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics) Project Management Vendor Management Budget Management Preferred Certifications CISSP ITIL Foundation or ITIL Managing Professional PMP or PRINCE2 Microsoft Azure Solutions Architect AWS Solutions Architect CCNP or CCIE CISM ISO 27001 Lead Implementer or Lead Auditor TOGAF (preferred) Show more Show less

title: IT Applications Lead
description: About the Role Riyadh Cement Company (RCC) is looking for a hands-on Non-SAP Business Applications Lead to take full ownership of the delivery, support, and management of our non-SAP business applications. These are the business-critical systems that support our core operations — including customer-facing platforms, logistics and weighbridge systems, governance and compliance solutions, service management tools, and other operational applications that run the business outside the SAP environment. Working under the IT Applications Manager, you will own these applications end-to-end — ensuring they are reliable, well-supported, integrated with SAP, and continuously improved to meet business needs. What You'll Do Own the applications — take full operational responsibility for all non-SAP business applications (availability, performance, support, and enhancements) Support the business — act as the primary point of contact for business users, resolving issues and translating needs into improvements Deliver projects — lead the delivery of new applications and enhancements from requirements to coordinate Strong experience in application support and lifecycle management (incident, problem, change, enhancement) Solid understanding of application integration (APIs, web services, middleware) Experience coordinating with software vendors and implementation partners Strong business engagement skills — able to work directly with business users and translate needs into solutions Familiarity with ITIL / ITSM practices Highly Preferred Experience integrating applications with SAP S/4HANA (functional level) SQL / reporting knowledge for data troubleshooting and insights Experience in industrial, manufacturing, or Tadawul-listed environments Exposure to customer portals, logistics/weighbridge systems, or GRC/compliance applications Mindset Strong ownership and accountability — you treat the applications as your own Organized, disciplined, and delivery-focused Comfortable working with autonomy under a manager's governance Continuous improvement mindset Certifications (a Plus) title: Supply Management Planner
description: Payrate: $23.65 - $23.65/hr. Summary: We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Service Parts Supply Management Planner to join our team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for ensuring the on-time delivery of service parts to our customers by working closely with suppliers, buyers, and forecast analysts. This role involves using various management tools and platforms to manage parts supply and performance metrics. Responsibilities Utilize SPERT, ZMDelinq, and Lights (SAP): Ensure the timely delivery of parts by managing these web-based applications and platforms. Collaborate with Stakeholders: Work closely with suppliers, buyers, and forecast analysts for their commodity of parts. Performance Metrics: Track and manage metrics including FILL rate, On-Time Delivery, FLASH/Backorder Cycle Time, and Past Due orders. Qualifications Education: Bachelor's degree in a business-related field or equivalent experience. Experience: Minimum of 3 years of planner experience or equivalent. Open to recent graduates. Skills Proficiency in MS Office, especially Excel. Easily trainable with excellent verbal and written communication skills. SAP experience is a plus. Pay Transparency: The typical base pay for this role across the U.S. is: $23.65 - $23.65 /hour . Non-exempt positions are eligible for overtime at a rate of 1.5 times the base hourly rate for all hours worked in excess of 40 in a work week, or as required by state or local law. Final offer amounts, within the base pay set forth above, are determined by factors including your relevant skills, education and experience. Full-time employees are eligible to select from different benefits packages. Packages may include medical, dental, and vision benefits, health savings accounts with qualified medical plan enrollment, 10 paid days off, 3 days paid bereavement leave, 401(k) plan participation with employer match, life and disability insurance, commuter benefits, dependent care flexible spending account, accident insurance, critical illness insurance, hospital indemnity insurance, accommodations and reimbursement for work travel, and discretionary performance or recognition bonus. Sick leave and mobile phone reimbursement provided based on state or local law. Consent to Communication and Use of AI Technology: By submitting your application for this position and providing your email address(es) and/or phone number(s), you consent to receive text (SMS), email, and/or voice communication whether automated (including auto telephone dialing systems or automatic text messaging systems), pre-recorded, AI-assisted, or individually initiated from Aditi Consulting, our agents, representatives, or affiliates at the phone number and/or email address you have provided. These communications may include information about potential opportunities and information. Message and data rates may apply. Message frequency may vary. You represent and warrant that the email address(es) and/or telephone number(s) you provided to us belong to you and that you are permitted to receive calls, text (SMS) messages, and/or emails at these contacts.
