title: Demand Planner
description: Client / Company Our client is looking for a Response Planner within Integrated Planning. This is a category-focused, customer-neutral role that owns the service and business waste agenda and monitors the related KPIs. You will work closely with internal stakeholders and the Genpact organisation to drive continuous process improvements. What will you do? As a Response Planner, you are responsible for short-term demand/supply reconciliation (up to approx. 6 weeks). You identify and mitigate short-term service risks, set priorities, and ensure the organisation is involved in time when disruptions may occur. Key responsibilities include: Aligning and deciding on escalation, allocation and customer priorities together with Sales and Customer Service; Driving service performance towards agreed targets and clearly communicating service risks to the business; Supporting short-term demand/supply reconciliation and defining demand-driven mitigation plans (e.g. via AATP Business Rules); Coordinating local actions regarding residuals and blocked stock, including managing/using the POM tool; Performing root cause analyses on business waste and strengthening preventive actions; Driving operations planning process improvements together with Genpact; Reviewing promo uplifts (X-6 and X+6) for availability together with the Market Supply Planner. What you will get A key role in short-term service control within Integrated Planning; Close collaboration with stakeholders (incl. Sales, Customer Service, Supply Planning/Genpact); Working with planning and allocation tools such as AATP, SAP/S/4HANA, RTVA/Fenix, Kinaxis Supply (view), POM Tool, VF. (Additional employment conditions such as salary, contract type and working hours have not been provided yet—share these and I will add them in the same style.) What you bring Strong planning mastery and analytical capability; Experience in stakeholder management and performance management; Understanding of market, channel and customer dynamics; Deep process knowledge and strong communication skills; Stress resilience and the ability to switch quickly during short-term service issues; Experience with and/or affinity for (some of) the following systems is a plus: AATP, SAP/S/4HANA, Kinaxis, POM tooling. KPIs (what you will be measured on) Excess Stock; Dispatch Rate % (DR) and Customer Service Level (SAMBC); Adoption of AATP Business Rules; Reporting line / Level; Reporting to: Integrated Planning Manager BU; Work level: WL1. About Grafton & How to Apply Grafton is your reliable partner for the next step in your career. With market knowledge and a transparent approach, we guide you toward opportunities that truly fit you. Apply directly or send your CV to Kimberley Kramer at kimberley.kramer@grafton.com. Questions? Feel free to reach out. title: SAP Developer
description: Jouw energie in de plus Als SAP Developer bouw je mee aan één geïntegreerd systeem waarmee collega’s onze hoogspanningsstations beter kunnen plannen en beheersen en zo de energietransitie versnellen. Waarom Werken Bij Stedin Jou Energie Oplevert Werken aan complexe SAP‑vraagstukken die direct impact hebben op het energienet. Hybride werken met veel ruimte om thuis te werken. Flexibele werktijden die je werk‑privébalans versterken. Werk om trots op te zijn Als SAP Developer zorg je voor de IT-oplossingen voor de collega’s die hoogspanningsstations bouwen. Je ontwikkelt en onderhoudt een geïntegreerd systeem op basis van SAP-PS, SAP-PPM en SAP Logistics met koppelingen naar Relatics (SEIS) en Oracle Primavera Cloud. Samen met procesregisseurs en business consultants worden de bouwprocessen zo optimaal mogelijk ondersteund, zodat projecten in de uitbouw van de hoogspanningsnetten beter te plannen en te beheersen zijn. Daarmee lever je direct een bijdrage aan de energietransitie. Een Dag Als SAP Developer Met een kop koffie of thee van de ‘gebarista’ start de dag met de daily stand-up, waar prioriteiten en mogelijke obstakels worden doorgenomen. Daarna ga je aan de slag met je ontwikkelwerk. Aan het begin van de sprint bespreek je met het DevOps-team en de Product Owner de doelen voor de komende twee weken en help je de waarde van het product te maximaliseren, met afwisselend planning, refinement en uitvoerend werk. In de loop van de ochtend voer je kennismakingsgesprekken om de businesscontext van de applicatie goed te begrijpen en heb je korte online meetings om integraties af met andere DevOps-teams. Je werkt samen aan user stories, zoals een ontwerp voor implementatie van congestieoplossingen. Lunchen doe je in het bedrijfsrestaurant op de 18e verdieping met uitzicht over Rotterdam of tijdens een wandeling door de stad. ’s Middags staat een refinement voor de volgende sprint op de agenda en wordt wekelijks verdiepend gekeken naar user stories voor het komende kwartaal. Aan het einde van de dag stem je met de Epic Owner de volgende stappen in de productontwikkeling af, pak je waar mogelijk enkele beheerverzoeken op in het ticketsysteem en loop je zo naar station Blaak om snel weer thuis te zijn. Hier laad jij van op Alle financiële arbeidsvoorwaarden op een rij, weet je meteen waar je aan toe bent: Een bruto maandsalaris tussen € 4.548,05 en maximaal € 7.267,09 (bij 40 uur en afhankelijk van jouw kennis en ervaring), 24,5 vakantiedagen en pensioenopbouw bij ABP waarbij wij 70% van de premie betalen. Bovenop je salaris ontvang je een persoonlijk budget van 24% (inclusief 8% vakantiegeld). Jij kiest wat je met het budget doet: bijvoorbeeld extra salaris of (tot max. 35,5) verlofdagen. In onze rekentool zie je hoe het werkt. Jaarlijks € 700,- (bruto) om te besteden aan je eigen energie, bijvoorbeeld voor je sportabonnement of je nieuwe sportkleding. Daar Komt Dit Nog Bovenop Moderne thuiswerkfaciliteiten en een thuiswerkvergoeding van € 2,40 per dag. Bij Stedin werken we 50% thuis en 50% op kantoor. Reizen naar kantoor doe je met de ov-vrijregeling. Dat is een ov-kaart die je ook in je vrije tijd kan gebruiken. Of met de flexibele reisregeling, waarbij je zelf kiest, combineert en varieert hoe je naar je werk reist en een extra vergoeding krijgt als je met de fiets komt. Een laptop en telefoon, of € 30,- netto per maand als je je eigen telefoon gebruikt. Vol energie vooruit met team Zakelijk Complex Je sluit aan bij de Agile Release Train (ART) ‘Zakelijk Complex’, midden in de energietransitie. Het team bestaat uit ongeveer tien collega’s, onder wie business analisten en applicatie engineers. Samen werken jullie 2 dagen per week op kantoor, dit kunnen de locaties Delft en Rotterdam (Blaak en/of Coolse Poort) zijn, dat stemmen jullie onderling af. Met deze kwaliteiten groei jij hier verder Als SAP developer ben je enthousiaste teamspeler die graag met verschillende afdelingen samenwerkt aan het verbeteren van bedrijfsprocessen. Met sterke communicatieve vaardigheden schakel je makkelijk tussen stakeholders en collega’s. Je bent nieuwsgierig naar nieuwe technologieën en oplossingen, altijd met een klantgerichte en analytische blik om complexe SAP-vraagstukken op te lossen. Verder Heb Je Hbo werk- en denkniveau en minimaal 3 jaar ervaring als SAP Developer. Minimaal 3 jaar aantoonbare ervaring met ABAP, SAP S/4HANA en Fiori/UI5 en brede kennis van het SAP BTP Platform en integraties zoals SAP PI/PO, API’s en interfaces. Je hebt ervaring om te werken in een Agile-SAFe-omgevingen en je beschikt over uitstekende kennis van de Nederlandse taal (niveau C1/C2). Zet de stap naar jouw volgende uitdaging We kijken ernaar uit je te ontmoeten. Dit Is Nog Goed Om Te Weten Je mag een motivatiebrief meesturen, dat hoeft niet. Je werkt in principe 40 uur per week. Maar wil je 32 of 36 uur werken, dan is dat ook een optie. title: Data scientist
description: Note: Deadline for applying is 23.59 the day before the Job Posting End Date. UNILEVER IS HIRING Data Scientist /Data Foundation / CD XOPS Location: Air Office Full-time Local conditions apply The role will be closed once the best match is found even before the posting end date About Unilever Unilever is one of the world’s leading suppliers of Food, Home, and Personal Care products, operating in over 190 countries and reaching more than 2 billion consumers every day. Our portfolio includes iconic brands such as Dove, Knorr, Domestos, Hellmann’s, Persil, Cif, Tresemmé, Rexona, and Axe. Guided by our purpose—to make sustainable living commonplace—we aim to grow our business while addressing the challenges of climate change and human development, enabling people everywhere to live well within the limits of the planet. About Global Data & Technology (GDT) Data Foundation within GDT exists to make Unilever data-intelligent, empowering critical business decisions through data, advanced analytics, and AI. Our vision is a future-fit Unilever—an organisation where data underpins every aspect of decision-making: accelerating innovation, strengthening brands, driving excellence in customer execution, enhancing consumer experiences through personalisation, and transforming internal operations for efficiency and scale. We bring together specialists across the business and offer a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of data science, business impact, and cutting-edge technology, with a direct link to Unilever’s growth and purpose. About The Role As a Data Scientist, you will play a key role in delivering advanced analytical and AI solutions that support commercial and operational decision-making across markets. This is a hands-on, mid-senior individual contributor role, requiring strong technical depth, the ability to operate autonomously, and close collaboration with product, engineering, and business partners. Key Responsibilities Partner closely with product, business, and analytics stakeholders to deeply understand problem statements and translate them into actionable, data-driven solutions. Design, build, and deploy predictive and prescriptive models using classical statistical methods and machine learning. Lead fast-paced, agile experimentation, iterating quickly to demonstrate value and inform product development and releases. Apply design thinking principles to develop solutions that are scalable, usable, and aligned with business needs. Act as a technical expert in advanced modelling techniques, supporting solution design and peer reviews. Develop and evolve capabilities in Generative AI and Agentic AI, contributing to next-generation analytics solutions. Industrialise and scale solutions on cloud-based platforms, ensuring robustness, performance, and maintainability. Areas of Focus Build end-to-end data science solutions addressing complex business problems through diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive analytics. Unlock new insights by integrating internal and external data sources (e.g. retailer data, econometric models, panel data). Innovate new methodologies driven by local market needs, contributing to the global D&A product pipeline. Operate primarily as a strong individual contributor, working across multiple markets and teams in close partnership with business interpreters. Champion continuous improvement through: Simplified and standardised ways of working Improved development and delivery practices About You Experience & Expertise Degree qualified in a relevant technical discipline (Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Econometrics, Operations Research, or similar). Strong track record of solving complex business problems using quantitative and statistical approaches. 3 yrs or more experience in Deep expertise in several of the following with solid theoretical grounding: Recommendation engines (Required) Churn modelling (Required) Regression and econometric modelling (Preferred) Time Series Forecasting (Preferred) Experience developing and industrialising machine learning and advanced analytics solutions. Advanced proficiency in Python and Spark, with experience working in Databricks and Azure environments. Proven ability to work with large, complex, high-dimensional datasets, both structured and unstructured (e.g. SAP, POS data). Exposure to LLMs and agent-based systems is a plus. Ways of Working Excellent communication, active listening, and presentation skills, able to engage audiences at different levels of the organisation. Highly inquisitive and analytical mindset, with a passion for problem solving, simplification, and continuous improvement. Well-organised, with strong time management skills and the ability to prioritise effectively in a fast-paced, global environment. Proactive self-starter who thrives with autonomy while collaborating effectively within cross-functional and virtual teams. Passion for delivering excellence; and developing a culture of “easy to do business with”, where stakeholder’s needs are anticipated High ethical standards in data handling and decision-making. WHAT IS IN IT FOR YOU? Unilever is the place where you can be yourself and bring your purpose to life with the work that you do – creating a better business and a better world. We offer an exciting & dynamic work environment where you can make things happen. Furthermore, we offer some great reward and benefits! Attractive total remuneration package: excellent company pension, bonus, share scheme. Flexible cross-disciplinary career opportunities and a wealth of training opportunities & wellbeing resources whenever and wherever. Plenty of company-paid holidays to further ensure your work-life balance is maintained. We encourage an inclusive culture, which comes to life with interchangeable public holidays, paid paternity leave of 6 weeks and our transgender policy. Under the Hybrid Working principles, you will be expected to spend a minimum of 40%-60% in the office or at customers, suppliers or partners to connect and collaborate. For the time you work from home, we will ensure you are well equipped. When you are at the office, you can enjoy our tasty canteen with prepped food and own products. Informal culture and being the first one trying our new products. My Fitness Plan (reduction on your Fitness Subscription). Homework allowance Company laptop and mobile phone Green Mobility Policy. EXCITED? Join Unilever and our team! To apply, you must do so online. Please do not forget to upload your CV and a motivation letter. Your application will be reviewed against our requirements, and we will be in touch shortly after the closing date to provide you with an update on the status of your application. Looking forward to meeting you! Please note this is a Direct Search led by Unilever. Applications from agencies will not be accepted, nor will fees be paid for unsolicited CVs. Equal Opportunity Employer: Unilever is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regards to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, national origin, disability, age, parenthood, pregnancy or any other basis protected by applicable law, and will not be discriminated against. By highlighting the gender diversity at the workplace, Unilever encourages women equally men to apply. If you look for a job after a long career break or after any type of leave, do not hesitate to apply. Please save a copy of this vacancy for personal use as it will disappear from the website after closing of the role. title: Internship 26' - Procurement Strategy and Operations
description: Are you ready to kick-start your career at one of the world’s most iconic companies? At Unilever, we’re not just about creating products – we’re about building dreams, opportunities, and innovation. With over 400 brands like Dove, Knorr, and Lipton, we’re committed to making sustainable living commonplace. This is more than just an internship; it’s a chance to learn, grow, and leave your mark at one of the largest FMCG companies on the planet. What makes this internship exciting? As an intern in the Procurement Department, your ideas will drive impact, and your creativity will take center stage. The team will provide you with opportunities to develop a robust understanding of procurement strategies and practices within a multinational context. You'll learn about supply chain processes, deliver KPIs, and gain project management experience. Enhance your analytical skills, proficiency in digital tools, and understanding of sustainable sourcing, while cultivating communication, problem-solving, and teamwork skills. This isn’t just about learning – it’s about leading! Here’s what your journey will look like: Participate in procurement and supply chain projects to improve efficiency and sustainability. Support the development and execution of procurement strategies and initiatives. Manage and analyse data to support decision-making and optimize procurement processes. Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to ensure effective communication and project execution. You’re The Perfect Fit If You’re available for 5 to 6 months starting August-September 2026 (minimum 32 hours/week). You are enrolled in a Bachelor (3rd or 4th year) or Master program, preferably in a related field. You are fluent in English. You are keen to learn about supply chain principles, including inventory management, logistics, and procurement. You are ready to lead projects and contribute to supply chain goals. You like working on diverse projects, including data analysis and stakeholder management. You demonstrate structured and analytical thinking. You are self-driven and tech-savvy. Why you’ll love working with us? We know internships are all about growth and exploration – and we’ve got you covered. A €625 gross/month allowance. Travel reimbursement (if you don’t have a student travel card). Fun and engaging intern programs – onboarding, events, and activities with fellow interns. Access to extensive training programs for personal and professional growth. A flexible working environment tailored to your success. A behind-the-scenes experience at one of the world’s largest and most innovative FMCG companies. Application Process Click "Apply now" and upload your CV and motivation letter. Don't forget to complete the questionnaire we send you. It's your chance to shine! Our team will carefully review your application. We receive a lot of applications, so please bear with us as we give each one the attention it deserves. If we think you might be a great fit, we'll schedule a friendly phone call to get to know you better and hear about your motivations. We'll present your profile to the manager. If they’re impressed, you'll be invited for an interview to discuss the opportunity further. After the interview, the manager will provide feedback on your performance. If you are selected, you'll receive an offer letter with all the exciting details about your internship. Once you accept the offer, we'll guide you through the onboarding process, including any necessary paperwork, training sessions, and introductions to your awesome team. You'll kick off your internship, get integrated into the team, and start working on your cool projects and responsibilities. Unilever is more than a workplace – it’s a space to bring your passions to life while creating impact. Are you ready to be part of something extraordinary? Let’s make it happen together! title: Global Billing Administrator - Finance
description: Job Description Global Billing Administrator Location: Netherlands - Belgium - Remote UK Do you take pride in keeping a billing cycle clean from end to end? Are you someone who spots a discrepancy before it becomes a problem, and knows how to navigate a complex, multi-stakeholder environment without dropping the ball? If billing, invoicing and revenue operations are where you do your best work, this one's worth a look. At Cerba Research, we are committed to finding the best talent to join our team. We have partnered with Vector Talent to manage the recruitment process for this role. If you're interested in applying, please direct all applications and questions to Vector Talent. The Role Cerba Research is looking for a Global Billing Administrator to own the billing and invoicing lifecycle for clinical trial projects. You'll be responsible for ensuring client invoices are accurate, timely and compliant, working across internal teams to keep the revenue cycle moving and resolving issues before they escalate. This is a role for someone who comes from a billing or accounts receivable background and understands what it takes to keep a high-volume, multi-entity invoicing function running cleanly. SAP experience is essential, you'll be working within an established project billing structure and expected to hit the ground running. About Cerba Research With over 40 years of clinical research expertise, Cerba Research has built a unique offering spanning clinical trial management, specialty lab and biomarker services, and medical devices and diagnostics. Supporting clients from research and development through to commercialisation, Cerba Research combines the deep therapeutic understanding of a specialist with the capacity and global reach of a central lab. Their global accredited laboratory network covers the US, Europe, Asia-Pacific, Australia and Africa, underpinned by a mission to transform clinical research and help patients access new treatments faster. Why Join? Real Ownership - You're not supporting someone else's billing process. You own it. If something's wrong, you're the one who catches it and fixes it. Meaningful Complexity - Multi-currency, multi-entity, multi-stakeholder billing across a global clinical research business. It keeps you sharp. Cross-functional Reach - You'll work alongside Project Management, Finance, Contracts and Proposals, and client-facing teams. It's a role with visibility. Established Infrastructure - A structured SAP environment, clear processes, and a team that knows what good looks like. How You'll Add Value Owning the end-to-end billing cycle for clinical trial projects, from invoice preparation through to cash collection Ensuring accuracy and timeliness across all client invoices, working to agreed milestones and study requirements Acting as the first point of contact for billing queries and disputes, resolving issues quickly and professionally Maintaining clean billing data across SAP and aligned systems, flagging discrepancies before they become problems Supporting month-end close activities related to billing and revenue recognition Producing regular reporting on billing performance, aged debt and cash flow for key stakeholders Your Responsibilities Prepare, issue and track client invoices in SAP in line with project milestones and contractual terms Manage billing master data and resolve invoice discrepancies, credit notes and disputes in a timely manner Monitor accounts receivable and follow up on outstanding balances, escalating where necessary Liaise with Project Managers, Contracts and Proposals, and the Finance team to ensure billing accuracy and completeness Maintain data integrity across SAP and connected systems, ensuring alignment between billing records and project budgets Support month-end close activities including accruals, reconciliations and revenue reporting Prepare dashboards and billing performance summaries for internal stakeholders Support audit requirements and maintain compliance with internal controls Job Requirements About You You've built your career in billing, invoicing or accounts receivable and you know the revenue cycle inside out. You don't need to be chased to stay on top of your workload, and you're comfortable working across multiple projects and stakeholders simultaneously. A background in a project-driven or clinical research environment is a strong plus, but what matters most is that you're precise, proactive and reliable. 3-5 years of experience in a billing, accounts receivable or Order to Cash role, ideally within a complex, multi-entity environment Hands-on SAP experience is essential, experience within a billing or accounts receivable context preferred Strong understanding of invoicing processes, revenue recognition principles and cash collection Excellent attention to detail and organisational skills, able to manage high volumes accurately and to deadline Confident communicator with a proactive approach to query resolution and stakeholder management Experience in a project-driven or clinical research environment is a strong plus but not essential Travel Minimal travel expected for this role. title: SAP Business Analyst
description: Looking for an experienced Business Analyst to support an enterprise SAP S/4HANA transformation focused on Product, Quality, and New Product Introduction (NPI). This role requires strong business analysis skills, stakeholder collaboration, and experience supporting end-to-end SAP implementation activities. Key Responsibilities: Support Product, Quality, and NPI workstreams throughout the SAP ERP implementation lifecycle. Gather, analyze, document, and validate business requirements from Business Process Owners (BPOs), SMEs, and key stakeholders. Create and maintain As-Is/To-Be process documentation, business process flows, and functional requirements. Collaborate with SAP functional teams to ensure business requirements are accurately translated into solution designs. Conduct gap analysis, support solution validation, and identify process improvement opportunities. Coordinate and support User Acceptance Testing (UAT), including test planning, test script development, execution, defect tracking, and reporting using Jira/Xray. Required Skills: Experience as a Business Analyst, Functional Analyst, or Supply Chain Analyst supporting ERP or SAP implementations. SAP S/4HANA implementation experience Business requirements gathering & process mapping Product Master Data, BOM, NPI, PLM, or SKU Lifecycle Manufacturing, Product Quality, or Supply Chain processes UAT, defect management, and data validation Stakeholder management and cross-functional collaboration Experience with Jira/Xray, Agile, CPQ/CTO is a plus Show more Show less

title: Continuous Improvement Intern - From September 2026
description: Gate Gourmet is a leading global provider of a full range of catering services for primarily the airline industry. The focus is on culinary and operational excellence – and consistently providing the highest quality products and services tailored to strengthen the brand of our customers. Working with our customers and business partners, we create innovative offerings that delight the senses and make for a memorable dining experience, in the air and on the ground. We are looking for a motivated and hands-on Continuous Improvement (CI) Intern with an interest in Lean Manufacturing and operational excellence. In this role, you will support improvement initiatives focused on Safety, Quality, Delivery, and Cost while working closely with experienced CI specialists and Lean Black Belt experts. This internship offers valuable hands-on experience in process optimization, Lean methodologies, and problem solving within a dynamic and fast-paced industrial environment. Main Responsibilities Students will also have the opportunity to combine this internship with a thesis assignment linked to real business challenges and operational improvement projects. Possible focus areas include. Master Production Planning Outbound and internal logistics Cost reduction business cases Defect reduction initiatives Improving production processes and workflows By working on these topics, students will gain valuable hands-on experience while contributing directly to operational performance and continuous improvement initiatives within the organization. This combination of practical involvement and analytical research provides an excellent opportunity to apply academic knowledge in a dynamic industrial environment. Profile Second, third or final-year HBO student in Production Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Operations, Logistics, or a related technical field Strong analytical skills with a problem-solving mindset and attention to detail Genuine interest in Lean Manufacturing, Operational Excellence, and Continuous Improvement methodologies Proactive, hands-on, and eager to learn within a fast-paced operational environment Strong communication and teamwork skills, with the ability to collaborate across different departments Able to work independently, take initiative, and contribute to improvement projects from analysis through implementation What We Offer A monthly internship allowance ranging from €549 to €749 Hands-on experience in Continuous Improvement, Lean Manufacturing, and Operational Excellence within a dynamic operational environment Coaching and guidance from experienced Lean and CI professionals, including exposure to best practices and improvement methodologies The opportunity to work on projects that create real business impact and contribute directly to operational performance improvements A dynamic, international, and multicultural working environment with close collaboration across different teams and departments Valuable practical experience that supports both personal and professional development The possibility to travel within Europe as part of operational or improvement-related projects Requirements The possibility to apply for a Certificate of Conduct (VOG) and a Certificate of No Objection (VGB) (mandatory requirement) Willingness to undergo screening by the Royal Netherlands Marechaussee (for this, it is important that you have lived consecutive in the Netherlands for the past eight years) Show more Show less Seniority level Internship Employment type Internship Job function Supply Chain Industries Airlines and Aviation Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at gategourmet by 2x See who you know

description: SAP Prozessberater (m/w/d) SAP kann vieles sein: reine Systembetreuung oder echte Prozessgestaltung. Bei dieser Position liegt der Fokus klar auf Letzterem. Gesucht wird ein SAP Business Partner, der Prozesse End-to-End versteht und als zentrale Schnittstelle zwischen Fachbereichen, IT und externen Partnern agiert. Statt Tickets abzuarbeiten, geht es hier darum, Abläufe aktiv weiterzuentwickeln, Lösungen zu konzipieren und Projekte mitzugestalten. Ein international aufgestelltes Technologieunternehmen aus dem industriellen Produktions- und Automatisierungsumfeld mit langjähriger Marktpräsenz und hoher Innovationskraft. Hinter der mittelständischen Struktur steht die Stabilität eines globalen Konzerns, wodurch langfristige Perspektiven und moderne Technologien miteinander kombiniert werden. Besonders spannend: Die S/4HANA-Transformation läuft bereits und der geplante Go-Live ist für die kommenden Jahre vorgesehen. Dadurch bietet die Position viel Gestaltungsspielraum innerhalb einer modernen SAP-Transformation. Ihre Aufgaben • Analyse und Optimierung bestehender Geschäftsprozesse im Produktions- und Logistikumfeld • Schnittstellenfunktion zwischen Fachbereichen, interner IT und externen Partnern • Entwicklung und Konzeption neuer SAP-Lösungen mit Fokus auf End-to-End-Prozesse • Mitarbeit sowie eigenständige Leitung von SAP- und Digitalisierungsprojekten • Unterstützung im 1st- und 2nd-Level-Support • Durchführung von Workshops und Schulungen für Key User und Fachbereiche • Mitarbeit im Rahmen der laufenden S/4HANA-Transformation Das bringen Sie mit • Erfahrung im SAP-Umfeld, idealerweise in Produktions- oder Logistikprozessen • Kenntnisse in SAP PP, MM oder angrenzenden Modulen • Verständnis für Prozessanalysen und konzeptionelles Arbeiten • Erfahrung in der Zusammenarbeit mit Fachbereichen • Gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse • Strukturierte und lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise Das erwartet Sie • Internationales Unternehmensumfeld mit langfristiger Stabilität • Viel Gestaltungsspielraum innerhalb eines laufenden S/4HANA-Programms • Moderne SAP- und IT-Landschaft • Flexible Arbeitszeiten und attraktive Rahmenbedingungen • Individuelle Weiterbildungs- und Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten • Flache Hierarchien und kurze Entscheidungswege • Gesundheitsangebote, betriebliche Zusatzleistungen und modernes Arbeitsumfeld Show more Show less Seniority level Associate Employment type Full-time Job function Information Technology and Analyst Industries Technology, Information and Internet Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at OP Technology by 2x See who you know

title: Logistiker Bahnbetrieb (m/w/div.)
company: 舒驰容器（上海）有限公司
description: Diese Spannenden Aufgaben Erwarten Sie Be- und Entladen von Eisenbahnwaggons und LKWs mittels Portalkran Durchführen der Ladungssicherung und Wareneingangskontrolle Entpacken und Einlagern von Stahlcoils Vorbereiten und Verladen von Spaltbandcoils für den innerbetrieblichen Verkehr Unterstützen des Teamleiters bei Wartungs- und Instandhaltungsarbeiten Entladen von Leerpaletten und Beladen von Platinenpaletten mittels Stapler Entsorgen von Abfällen der Coilverpackung Das Sollten Sie Mitbringen Erfahrung im Umgang mit Stahlcoils und Stahlerzeugnissen Berufserfahrung im gewerblich-technischen Bereich Kran- und Staplerschein von Vorteil Technisches Grundverständnis und Bereitschaft zum Erwerb erforderlicher Qualifikationen Bereitschaft zur 2-Schichtarbeit und zum Arbeiten im Freien sowie Samstagsarbeit Freude am selbstständigen und verantwortungsbewussten Arbeiten Ein hohes Maß an Verantwortungsbewusstsein, Zuverlässigkeit und Durchsetzungsvermögen Was macht SCHÜTZ überhaupt? Seit 1958 treiben uns neue Ideen und deren konsequente Umsetzung an. Heute sind wir führend in der Stahl- und Kunststoffverarbeitung und entwickeln unsere Produkte und Dienstleistungen sowie unsere Prozesse und Verfahren kontinuierlich weiter. Mit den vier Geschäftsbereichen PACKAGING SYSTEMS, ENERGY SYSTEMS, INDUSTRIAL SERVICES und COMPOSITES nimmt SCHÜTZ Spitzenpositionen in den jeweiligen Märkten ein. Dabei steht SCHÜTZ für umfassendes Know-how und modernste Technologie. Mit drei Standorten im Westerwald sind wir in der Region zwischen Frankfurt, Koblenz und Köln fest verwurzelt. Unser Stammsitz in Selters ist als Kompetenzzentrum und größtes Werk zugleich Motor und Zentrale für die Entwicklung der weltweiten SCHÜTZ Gruppe. Hier werden innovative Produktlösungen entwickelt, modernste Produktionsanlagen im eigenen Maschinen- und Anlagenbau konstruiert und Schlüsselkomponenten für unsere globale Verpackungsproduktion gefertigt. Aktuell verfügt SCHÜTZ weltweit über 60 Produktions- und Servicestandorte mit mehr als 7.000 Mitarbeitern in 24 Ländern und einem Gesamtumsatz von über 2,3 Milliarden Euro im Jahr 2024. Bei aller Begeisterung für Technologie: Es sind unsere Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter, die den nachhaltigen Erfolg und die Leistungsfähigkeit unseres Unternehmens ausmachen! Sie haben noch Fragen? Unsere Ansprechpartnerin Frau Mikaela Polonio, Telefon 02626 77-436, freut sich auf Ihren Anruf. title: SAP Business Process Owner
description: Job Title: SAP Business Process Owner Location: Simpsonville, SC Reports to: Director – Planning & Fulfillment Employment Type: Full-time Industry: Manufacturing | Mechanical Power Transmission Components | Engineering Services Job Function: Design | Configuration | Implementation | Maintenance Job Summary: Provide support for the Dodge business as an SAP Business Process Owner. This position will touch all aspects of SAP associated with Dodge Operations, including Purchasing/Supply Chain, Manufacturing, Quality, Planning & Fulfillment and Warehousing & Logistics. This BPO will maintain current SAP procedures, as well as work to continually improve and streamline activities for the Dodge Operations Team. Additional responsibilities are to troubleshoot problems, implement standards and requirements, and support audit and compliance reviews. Responsibilities would include all domestic and international Dodge locations. Key Responsibilities SAP Operations problem resolution. This position is called upon to resolve challenges faced by SAP users within the Dodge Business. Support any integration with necessary software, including VIP, Sievo, Compliant Pro, Power BI and Shop Floor Display. Administer/implement SAP Table changes where necessary. This includes production support, testing, and user training. Investigate ABAP programs when necessary to determine if there are issues or if improvements can be made. Work with IT Application Developers and SAP Programmers to estimate the effort required to develop and support applicable solutions and services. Assist in the installation, management, maintenance, and optimization of internal and cross-functional processes related to SAP systems and services. Serve as an expert in SAP Configuration by implementing any changes needed for functionality or improvement. Ensure the integrity and confidentiality of information residing in the SAP Systems. Support auditors with the development of internal control procedures. Monitor and enforce user access policies to ensure compliance to internal control procedures and segregation of duties policies. Provide general non-security related SAP Administration technical support as needed. Work with multiple internal and external teams. Support future migration to SAP’s S/4 HANA platform. Required Qualifications Ability to travel when required, up to 20%. Good business judgment & strong organizational skills. Ability to plan, coordinate, and set priorities. Ability to participate in after-hours when necessary. Experience in SAP system administration of large enterprise systems. Must have a work authorization in the US. Fluency in spoken and written English. Physical Requirements Extended periods of sitting at a workstation. Speaking in person and over the telephone or Teams online and the ability to hear, comprehend, and document detailed information from others. Ability to type, reach and grasp. Intermittent standing and walking within the office environment. Ability to communicate verbally and in writing for extended periods over the phone and in person. Occasional lifting or carrying objects weighing less than 10 lbs. Essential Job Functions Conduct thorough analysis of current operational processes to identify areas for improvement. Lead process redesign initiatives to drive efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and quality enhancements. Establish and maintain key process performance metrics and KPIs. Support Dodge Industrial operations teams in identifying and optimizing processes within ERP systems. Collaborate with other Business Process Owners on large-scale, cross-department initiatives. Provide strategic recommendations to leadership regarding process innovation and system enhancements. Ensure alignment of operational processes with corporate objectives and industry best practices. Why Join Us? Work alongside a collaborative team. Be part of an industry leader with a strong brand reputation and an innovation-driven culture RBC Bearings is an Equal Opportunity Employer, including disability and protected veteran status. title: SAP Application Engineer
description: Summary Apple is where individual imaginations gather together, committing to the values that lead to great work. Every new product we build, service we create, or Apple Store experience we deliver is the result of us making each other’s ideas stronger. That happens because every one of us shares a belief that we can make something wonderful and share it with the world, changing lives for the better. It’s the diversity of our people and their thinking that inspires the innovation that runs through everything we do. When we bring everybody in, we can do the best work of our lives. Here, you’ll do more than join something — you’ll add something. SAP Development focuses on designing, building, and maintaining software solutions that enhance and optimize critical business processes. As part of this team, the SAP Application Engineer contributes to the delivery of scalable, secure, and high-performance solutions by applying advanced technologies and engineering best practices. The role involves collaborating with cross-functional, multi-regional teams to address complex business challenges and supports strategic organizational goals by enabling reliable, future-ready enterprise capabilities. Description The SAP Application Engineer plays a pivotal role in designing and delivering high-quality enterprise solutions by participating in architectural discussions, developing scalable technical frameworks, and aligning solutions with business and technology strategies. The role requires a self-motivated, detail-oriented approach; strong analytical and problem-solving skills; adaptability in a fast-paced environment; and the ability to proactively learn and apply emerging technologies to enhance solution delivery. The ideal candidate takes ownership of driving timely, efficient, and high-quality solutions while partnering effectively with global, cross-functional teams and stakeholders. Responsibilities Contribute to architecture review sessions and support the design of scalable, secure, and high-performance SAP solutions. Develop reusable, flexible technical frameworks that support long-term enterprise needs. Deliver robust SAP solutions within tight timelines while maintaining quality and compliance standards. Drive issue resolution across teams through strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Proactively explore, evaluate, and adopt new technologies and development frameworks. Acquire new skills quickly and apply them to enhance solution design and implementation. Manage multiple concurrent tasks and initiatives, adapting effectively to evolving priorities. Collaborate and communicate clearly with distributed, multi-regional teams and stakeholders. Minimum Qualifications In-depth ABAP development experience in RICEFW, OData service, REST/SOAP-based API design and integration. In-depth experience working with S/4 HANA, CDS views, AMDP, and RAP. In-depth experience in performance tuning of HANA models. MS/BS in Computer Science or equivalent experience. Preferred Qualifications Excellent problem-solving skills with a developer-at-heart attitude. Knowledge of software engineering practices and standard methodologies for the full software development lifecycle, including coding standards, code reviews, source control management, build processes, testing, and deployment. Knowledge of SAP technologies/frameworks such as SAPUI5, Fiori, and CAP is a plus. Knowledge of the SAP Business Technology Platform and Services is a plus. By welcoming as many perspectives as possible, we help you build a career where you feel like you belong. If you're motivated by the idea of making a real impact, and joining a team where we pride ourselves in ensuring Apple business keeps running, this might be the dream job! Apple’s Information Systems & Technology (IS&T) organization manages key business and technical infrastructure at Apple that influences how online orders are placed, overall customer experience with order, AppleCare service offering, Financial book closure and much more. We are looking for a SAP enterprise specialist to be part of the global production support team with primary focus on APAC region. You must also have understanding of the underlying Order-to-Revenue data models. You will work closely across regions with business and technical teams in multiple locations and time zones. title: Quality Expert (Remote)
description: Foods you love. Brands you trust. And a career that empowers you to grow. At Nestlé USA, we’re all working towards the same goal – to delight and deliver for our consumers. With a rich portfolio of beloved brands, including DiGiorno, Toll House, and Coffee mate, in 97% of U.S. households, we have a unique opportunity – and responsibility – to be there for every moment in our consumers’ lives. Joining Nestlé means becoming part of an inclusive workplace that inspires innovation, encourages strategic thinking and creativity, and celebrates your achievements. No matter where you work within the organization, you are empowered to challenge the status quo, embrace risk-taking, and pioneer new ideas. Our supportive and collaborative environment encourages bold ambitions and continuous learning so that everyone can grow and thrive. This position is not eligible for Visa Sponsorship. Position Summary Reporting ultimately to the Director of Quality Contract Manufacturing, supporting Nestlé USA & Nestlé Canada, & Nestle Professional through the Senior Manager. This position will be responsible for providing leadership, mentoring, and support for all Quality aspects within our contract manufacturing operations with technical expertise and support to factories assigned and other functions. Job Duties/Responsibilities Build technical skills and capability in Contract manufacturing factories to assure autonomy. Responsible for compliance to Nestlé policies, and federal, state, and local regulations as required. Responsible for product quality and food safety of contract manufacturing sites. Effective use of TPM, NCE, and Quality tools to understand trends and to develop plans that drive continuous improvement and achieve business objectives. Coach and educate functional teams on quality processes and provide upfront support to project teams to support rapid onboarding and project launches. Collaborate with contact manufacturing managers to lead improvements at the locations to eliminate defects, complaints and drive improvement of site. Support customer complaint resolution with factories. Assist in product recalls or withdrawals as necessary. Request support as needed from other functions to achieve business objectives. Track and Analyze Coman Site Audit Requirements, FOB, and CC activities and prepare Monthly Completion Status Reports and ad hoc reports; Determine, highlight and escalate issues / risks as needed. Travel to contract manufacturing site locations for onsite support of projects, and continuous improvement activities; perform on site assessments as required to determine vendor approvals. Responsible for uploading and assuring accuracy of sites information in QIR, VAM, & SMI, NCITES, including but not limited to upload of audit, annual risk assessment, and other SAP systems as required. Ensure accurate contract manufacturing site risk and hazards in VAM through collaboration with Contract Manufacturing Quality Managers and Contract Manufacturing managers. Trip reports to be issued for every visit to the site. Review and maintain current Third-Party Audit Reports for Food Safety & Quality Management Systems based on hazards controlled by the contract manufacturing sites. Travel as needed to support collaboration with stakeholders. Other duties related to quality function; office weeks, team meetings, operational reviews, factory support, projects, assigned to manage opportunities for improvement (OFI) with key impact stakeholder drive vendor performance and business impact. Other duties as assigned by manager. Experience And Education Requirements Technical degree in a food-related field. Food Safety Management System; Food Safety Team Leader or Qualified Internal Auditor), FSCC 22000 Food Safety Management Lead Auditor (or GFSI equivalent), Food Process/Product Experience. Qualified PCQI (Preventative Controls Qualified Inspector). Nestlé Quality Leadership training or factory experience. Minimum 7 years food-related quality assurance experience and in-depth understanding and example of improvement in: Food Safety & Quality Management System processes and procedures; implementation and auditing. Hazard Analysis, Validation & Verification of PRP’s and Control Measures and HACCP plans. Beverage manufacturing (ESL, aseptic, pasteurization, canning/bottling), Foods manufacturing (dry/wet mix, RTE, nRTE, frozen, pizza & snacking), Coffee manufacturing (whole bean, R&G, concentrates), thermal process technical expertise preferred. Excellent Collaboration. Quality System performance Assessment and Improvement. Goal Alignment and Focused Problem Solving (GSTD, DMAIC, Q-Pillar). Strong verbal / written communications skills. Improvement and relentlessly evolve mindset. Proficient in the use of MS Excel and Word, PowerPoint, SAP, Trace gains, SharePoint. Ability to work individually and be able to work effectively in a team environment. Project management skills preferred. Be agile to quickly learn new food processing technologies and resolve critical food safety aspects alone or through consultation with internal / external authorities as appropriate. Have strong knowledge and understanding of food safety / HACCP, GMP’s, Allergen Controls, Sanitary design/controls, pest control, basic food regulations, specifications, and basic food technology. Thrive working in a dynamic environment with minimal direct supervision and have strong leadership, social, and self-initiative skills. Extensive Travel – up to 75%. The approximate pay range for this position is $124,000-155,000 base salary. Please note that the pay range provided is a good faith estimate for the position at the time of posting. Final compensation may vary based on factors including but not limited to knowledge, skills, and abilities as well as geographic location. Nestle offers performance-based incentives and a competitive total rewards package, which includes a 401k with company match, healthcare coverage and a broad range of other benefits. Incentives and/or benefits packages may vary depending on the position. Learn more at:About Us | Nestlé Careers (nestlejobs.com) It is our business imperative to remain a very inclusive workplace. In addition, with our commitment to an inclusive work environment, we recognize the exceptional engagement and innovation displayed by individuals with disabilities. title: Regional Head of Finance
location: Son en Breugel, North Brabant, Netherlands
description: Regional Head of Finance Location: Son en Breugel, The Netherlands (Hybrid) Lead the Finance Transformation of an International Organisation Robert Half is partnering with OQEMA to recruit a Regional Head of Finance. This is an exceptional opportunity for an experienced finance leader to play a key role in building OQEMA's new regional finance organisation and leading its finance transformation across 13 legal entities in eight countries. As Regional Head of Finance, you will play a leading role in building and leading a centralised finance organisation serving 13 legal entities across eight countries. Reporting directly to the Regional CFO, you will drive finance transformation, develop high-performing international teams and help shape the future of finance across the region. If you enjoy building organisations, leading change and partnering with senior business leaders in an international environment, this role offers a unique opportunity to make a lasting impact. About OQEMA OQEMA is one of Europe's leading chemical distribution companies, with more than 100 years of experience and operations in more than 20 countries. Headquartered in Germany, the Group supplies industrial and specialty chemicals to customers across a broad range of industries. As part of its long-term growth strategy, OQEMA is investing significantly in digitalisation, finance transformation and process optimisation. A key strategic initiative is the creation of a regional finance hub in the Netherlands, bringing together finance activities from multiple European entities into one high-performing organisation supported by SAP S/4HANA. Joining OQEMA means becoming part of an ambitious international organisation where finance plays a strategic role in supporting business performance and future growth. The Role The Regional Head of Finance is a senior finance leader responsible for the financial stewardship, governance, controllership and performance of 13 legal entities across eight countries . Reporting directly to the Regional CFO , you will lead Finance Managers, Team Leaders and the central finance function, ensuring each entity operates in accordance with Group policies, financial control standards and strategic objectives. You will provide expert guidance, constructive challenge and support to the regional finance teams while partnering closely with senior leadership to deliver finance transformation programmes, growth initiatives and Group strategic priorities. As the regional finance organisation continues to evolve, you will play a key role in building a scalable, high-performing finance function that acts as a true business partner to the organisation. Key Responsibilities Lead, develop and inspire the regional finance organisation. Manage Finance Managers, Team Leaders and transactional finance teams. Provide expert guidance on accounting, tax, treasury, reporting and compliance matters. Maintain oversight of financial reporting, governance, statutory compliance and internal controls. Ensure effective management of cash flow, working capital, foreign exchange exposure and banking relationships. Partner closely with the Regional CFO and local Managing Directors to support business growth and profitability. Drive finance transformation and support the implementation of Group finance strategies. Foster a culture of continuous improvement, standardisation, digitalisation and operational excellence. Oversee statutory audits and maintain effective relationships with auditors and external stakeholders. Develop finance capability through coaching, mentoring and succession planning. Candidate Profile You are an experienced finance leader who enjoys building organisations, driving transformation and leading international teams through change. You will ideally bring: A recognised professional accounting qualification (ACA, ACCA, CIMA, RA, CPA or equivalent). At least 10 years of progressive finance leadership experience. Experience as a Head of Finance, Finance Director, Group Financial Controller or Regional Financial Controller. Proven experience leading finance transformation, organisational change or shared service environments. Strong leadership experience managing international finance teams. Experience operating successfully within multinational and matrix organisations. A proactive, solution-oriented mindset with the ability to work independently and take ownership. Strong financial governance, reporting and compliance expertise. Experience with SAP S/4HANA would be considered a strong advantage. Experience with international VAT and cross-border finance operations is advantageous. Fluent English is essential. Why Join OQEMA? This is considerably more than a traditional Head of Finance position. It offers the opportunity to: Build and shape a regional finance organisation. Lead a major international finance transformation. Work directly with the Regional CFO and senior executive leadership. Influence the future finance operating model across eight countries. Drive organisational change within an international business. Develop high-performing finance teams. Contribute to digitalisation, automation and operational excellence. Make a lasting impact within a growing international organisation. Interested? If you would like to learn more about this opportunity, please feel free to contact me for a confidential conversation. Justin Dorst Director | Senior & Executive Placements Robert Half | The Netherlands Show more Show less Seniority level Director Employment type Full-time Job function Accounting/Auditing and Finance Industries Chemical Manufacturing and Wholesale Chemical and Allied Products Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at OQEMA Group by 2x See who you know

title: SAP Transformation Lead
description: Job Description Salary Range £62,000 - £90,000 Leonardo UK operates a grade-based salary framework with broad bands. The salary range shown reflects the approved grade band for this role, or a narrower hiring range published within that band, and is benchmarked against the external market. Exceptions above the standard range are managed through governance controls to protect internal equity. Your Impact Join Leonardo UK as an SAP Transformation Lead and play a pivotal role in reshaping the digital backbone of a leading defence organisation. This is a rare opportunity to lead a complex, enterprise-wide SAP transformation—driving innovation across engineering, manufacturing, and operations while ensuring the highest standards of security, performance, and compliance in a mission-critical environment. You will take ownership of a major SAP upgrade programme, guiding the organisation from a legacy landscape to a future-ready platform aligned with long-term strategic goals. Working at the intersection of technology and business, you will define the transformation roadmap, lead solution design and integration across SAP, PLM, and MES systems, and enable a seamless digital thread that enhances visibility, efficiency, and decision-making across the enterprise. In this role you will Own and drive the end-to-end upgrade of the legacy SAP system (including all functions). Ownership of end-end business processes which impact on SAP (BD –Planning - Engineering- Manufacturing – Customer Support – Finance). Define and manage the upgrade strategy, including scope, deployment approach, risk mitigation, and resource planning. Lead technical assessment, solution design, data migration strategy, and cutover planning. Ensure all processes, custom code, and integrations are mapped, rationalised, or redesigned to meet future business needs. Develop and maintain a multi-year SAP technology roadmap aligned with business objectives and defence-industry compliance requirements, which is to include the system management of secret (or higher) parts or products in an operational environment. Identify opportunities for standardisation, process harmonisation, automation, and adoption of new SAP capabilities. Define governance frameworks for enhancement requests, prioritisation, and change control. Serve as the primary SAP interface with PLM and MES programme teams. Design and own the integration strategy between SAP, PLM, and MES to ensure seamless engineering, manufacturing, and supply-chain workflows. Ensure data alignment, Bill of Materials (BOM) synchronisation, configuration management, and traceability across systems (including secret parts or products). Champion end-to-end digital thread capability. Collaborate with Future Factory, Engineering, Supply Chain, Operations, IT Security, and Programme Leadership to define requirements and ensure solution alignment. Manage external partners and system integrators, ensuring delivery quality and adherence to standards. Communicate progress, relevant KPI’s, risks, and decisions to senior leader. Ensure all SAP solutions comply with defence-sector regulations, cybersecurity standards, export controls, and audit requirements. Establish high-quality documentation, system standards, and configuration controls. What You'll Bring Proven experience leading major SAP upgrade or S/4HANA transformation programmes. Expert knowledge of core SAP modules (e.g., PP, MM, QM, SD, FI/CO) and integration technologies (BAPIs, IDocs, APIs). Strong background in SAP integration with PLM and MES environments. Experience working in highly regulated or defence/aerospace industries. Exceptional stakeholder management, communication, and leadership skills. Strong understanding of cybersecurity, configuration control, and compliance frameworks. Experience with SAP EWM, IBP, or SAP Cloud products. Knowledge of digital thread, model-based engineering, and Industry 4.0 principles. Project management certification (PMP, PRINCE2, Agile). Experience with classified system environments. Security Clearance This role is subject to pre-employment screening in line with the UK Government’s Baseline Personnel Security Standard (BPSS). An additional range of Personnel Security Controls referred to as National Security Vetting (NSV) may apply, this could include meeting the eligibility requirements for The Security Check (SC) or Developed Vetting (DV). For more information and guidance please visit https //careers.uk.leonardo.com/gb/en/security-and-vetting Why join us At Leonardo, our people are at the heart of everything we do. We offer a comprehensive, company-funded benefits package that supports your wellbeing, career development, and work–life balance. Whether you're looking to grow professionally, care for your health, or plan for the future, we’re here to help you thrive. Time to Recharge Enjoy generous leave with the opportunity to accrue up to 12 additional flexi-days each year. Secure your Future Benefit from our award-winning pension scheme with up to 15% employer contribution. Your Wellbeing Matters Free access to mental health support, financial advice, and employee-led networks championing inclusion and diversity (Enable, Pride, Equalise, Armed Forces, Carers, Wellbeing and Ethnicity). Rewarding Performance All employees at management level and below are eligible for our bonus scheme. Never Stop Learning Free access to 4,000+ online courses via Coursera and LinkedIn Learning. Refer a friend Receive a financial reward through our referral programme. Tailored Perks Spend up to £500 annually on flexible benefits including private healthcare, dental, family cover, tech & lifestyle discounts, gym memberships and more. Flexible working Flexible hours with hybrid working options. For part time opportunities, please talk to us about what might be possible for this role. For a full list of our company benefits please visit our website. Leonardo is a global leader in Aerospace, Defence, and Security. Headquartered in Italy, we employ over 53,000 people worldwide including 8,500 across 9 sites in the UK. Our employees are not just part of a team—they are key contributors to shaping innovation, advancing technology, and enhancing global safety. At Leonardo we are committed to building an inclusive, accessible, and welcoming workplace. We believe that a diverse workforce sparks creativity, drives innovation, and leads to better outcomes for our people and our customers. If you have any accessibility requirements to support you during the recruitment process, just let us know. Primary Location GB - Edinburgh Additional Locations GB - Basildon, GB - Luton - Cap. title: SAP Consultant – Manufacturing (PP, QM) (Remote in Spain)
description: About The Role We are looking for an experienced SAP Consultant specializing in Production Planning (PP) and Quality Management (QM) to join our team. You will lead end-to-end SAP S/4HANA implementations in the manufacturing domain, ensuring seamless integration and business process optimization. Key Responsibilities Manage full life cycle implementations of SAP S/4HANA in Production Planning & Manufacturing Execution / Quality Management. Evaluate business requirements and identify relevant SAP capabilities and best practices. Plan, prepare, and execute Fit-Gap / Fit-to-Standard workshops. Design solutions and drive adoption for identified gaps and process improvements. Ensure successful system implementation in collaboration with nearshore/offshore teams. Act as a trusted advisor for clients and take on project management responsibilities. Requirements 8–10 years of experience in SAP PP and QM. 2–5 full project lifecycles in SAP PP/QM implementations. Strong knowledge of Production Planning, Shop-Floor Manufacturing Execution, and/or Quality Management processes, with the ability to translate business needs into system specifications. Hands-on experience and/or certification in SAP S/4HANA. Preferably experienced in international template rollout projects. Solid business skills including stakeholder management, problem-solving, and resilience. Degree in Economics, Information Technology, or equivalent. Proven ability to gather, validate, synthesize, and communicate data for diverse audiences. Excellent interpersonal skills and C2 proficiency in English (and local language if applicable). Willingness to travel for project-related activities. What We Offer: Permanent contract and remote work from anywhere in Spain. Participation in international projects with leading clients. Career development and continuous training in SAP technologies. Collaborative and multicultural work environment. Consideramos de vital importancia que nuestra organización se fundamente en nuestro mejor activo y marca de valor añadido que es nuestro equipo humano. title: Specialist, QA SAP
description: Join a Leader in Pharmaceutical Manufacturing — And Build What’s Next As Spokane’s Largest Manufacturing Company , Jubilant HollisterStier LLC is a well-established force in the pharmaceutical and biopharmaceutical industries, providing a complete range of manufacturing services that support lifechanging medicines around the world. We are nationally recognized for our expertise in producing sterile injectable vials and lyophilized products, and our Allergy business is a global leader in allergenic extracts that support treatments for allergies and asthma. Jubilant HollisterStier is proudly part of the global, Jubilant Pharma family. established force in the pharmaceutical and biopharmaceutical industries, providing a complete range of manufacturing services that support lifechanging medicines around the world. For the Builders, Innovators, and Doers — This Is Your Place If you are someone who thrives on solving complex problems, improving systems, and building real solutions , you’ll feel at home here. At Jubilant HollisterStier, your ideas matter — and your impact is felt globally. Why builders choose us: Immediate impact: Your work directly supports essential medicines and allergenic therapies used worldwide. Benefits start on day one: Medical, Dental, Vision, FSA/HSA, Life, AD&D, and Disability benefits begin immediately because your well-being shouldn’t have a waiting period. A career you can grow: We provide opportunities for growth, promotion, and annual raises, with midpoint compensation reserved for high performance and longevity. A culture of improvement: We value people who thrive on continuous improvement, innovation, and hands on contribution. A stable industry leader: Backed by Jubilant Pharma, we pair long term stability with forward looking innovation. If you want to build something meaningful with a team that values your drive, skills, and ideas — you belong here. Job Description: The QA SAP Specialist provides support/oversight for the quality related SAP modules. These modules include Material Numbers, Inspection Plans (Specifications), Sampling (Draws and Plans), Dynamic Modification Rules (DMR - Skip lot testing), Notifications (ZD, ZO, ZN, etc), BOM (Bill of Materials) and Recipes. Ensure Material numbers and Inspection plans are configured correctly before releasing for use Create/Assign Sampling draws and plans to materials as required Ensure the correct DMR's are assigned to materials that qualify for skip lot testing Oversee ZO notifications for specifications, material master’s and general SAP requests Recognize/partner/oversee process improvement projects and/or SAP changes/upgrades Review and approve new BOM's and Recipes Assist in training of users in the SAP Specifications system Qualifications: BS or BA required OR 7 years of experience in lieu of a BS/BA degree required. Minimum of 2 years required, 5 preferred, of pharmaceutical regulated industry. In-depth experience with SAP can be used in lieu of pharmaceutical experience required. FDA Regulated Industry experience required. In-depth proficiency of SAP T-codes and functionality preferred. High computer aptitude Proficiency in TrackWise and Master Control (SOP and SPEC revisions) required. Microsoft Word and Excel required. Excellent interpersonal skills Interaction with departments and inspectors/auditors from regulatory agencies and customer companies required. Other Physical requirements: Prolonged sitting and standing in front of a computer. Location: Spokane, WA — On Site, Full- -Time Shift: Monday-Friday Compensation & Benefits That Start on Day One — Because Your Well Being Matters Hiring Wage: $31.00 – $41.94, with opportunities for growth, promotion, and annual raises. At Jubilant HollisterStier, we don’t believe in waiting periods. From the moment you join us, you and your family have access to a full suite of benefits designed to support your health, stability, and long-term success. Starting on your first day, you receive: Medical, Dental & Vision coverage Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) & Health Savings Accounts (HSA) Life & AD&D Insurance Short-Term & Long-Term Disability Insurance Employee Assistance Program Unlock Your Potential If you’re seeking a dynamic and rewarding career, we welcome your application today. Apply online: Careers - Jubilant HollisterStier Jubilant HollisterStier is an EEO/AA Employer . All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or protected veteran status. title: SAP BASIS - Manager
location: St John’s, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada
description: At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence. We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go. Join EY and help to build a better working world. The opportunity EY Technology Consulting is a fast-moving and high-growth area within EY. We are currently looking for a Manager – SAP BASIS to join our Enterprise Applications team. This role offers variety, responsibility, and the opportunity to realize your leadership potential. We are looking for an individual who combines experience leading SAP projects with an aptitude for driving sales pursuits and for helping grow our business. Your Key Responsibilities Provide recommendations and guidance for the tuning and optimization of the SAP landscape to our clients Manage BASIS initiatives across multiple clients and help manage their deliverables on schedule and accurately Install, Configure, Administer and Support SAP S/4 HANA, ERP6.00 EHP Systems, Process Integration / Process Orchestration (PI/PO), BW / BW4HANA, BPC, NetWeaver Fiori/Gateway (Embedded and Distributed), Solution Manager, Web Dispatcher, Business Objects Platform (Business Intelligence and Data Services), and Lumira Platform, etc. Architect, Install, Configure, Administer and Monitor HANA, MaxDB, Sybase ASE, SQLAnywhere, and MS SQL Databases Perform and Support SAP System and SAP Client Refreshes customers Administer, manage and support SAP clients, transports, users, roles, gateway Day-to-Day system monitoring of a 3 tier SAP landscape with multiple integration points for ABAP, Java, and other SAP systems Perform Solution Manager Basic, Managed System Configuration, EWA, Technical Monitoring, CTS+, BPMon, Solution Desk, ChaRM, etc. Plan and Implement ABAP-based Support pack stack, Support Pack Upgrades, Language Installs, etc. Document Standards, Systems Architecture, runbooks, and monitoring Infrastructure Perform Knowledge Transfer sessions for various types of audiences Actively participate in the knowledge transfer and sharing process with other consultants Provide guidance and support to SAP Basis / Technology Architecture resources Skills And Attributes For Success Effectively manage diverse skills and backgrounds and foster an innovative and inclusive team-oriented work environment. Strong written and verbal communication, presentation, client service and technical writing skills, coupled with a strong interest in further developing and integrating enterprise domain knowledge with technology skills. Demonstrate characteristics of a forward thinker and self-starter that thrives on new challenges and adapts quickly to learning new knowledge. Ability to lead a team of consulting and client professionals on multiple projects. Being a leader in our EY SAP practice mentoring our team members through career coaching A strong work ethic. To qualify for the role you must have A degree in computer science, accounting, finance or a related field (MBA or Business Undergraduate degree preferred). SAP certification/s highly preferred. Experience working with RISE with SAP and GROW with SAP. A bachelor's degree and approximately 7+ years of related work experience; or a master's degree and approximately 6-8 years of related work experience Experience in SAP System installations, System Upgrades, OS/DB Migrations, Add on deployments, System Rename, Dual stack split, Kernel upgrade, Host agent installation/upgrade, Client copy procedures etc. Experienced in SAP System installations, System Upgrades, OS/DB Migrations, Add on deployments, System Rename, Dual stack split, Kernel upgrade, Host agent installation/upgrade, Client copy procedures. Experience with SAP Fiori is preferred 2+ years of Project lead experience that includes at least 1 full S/4 HANA project life-cycle in a lead role preferred Wide Experience in SAP HANA Database Administration, SAP S4HANA/NW/ERP versions starting from NW 7.0 and ECC 4.6. Ability to drive the client to make difficult decisions during transformation programs, navigate complex business problems, and translate to SAP functional requirements. Ideally, you’ll also have Prior consulting experience and a demonstrated history of driving revenue within existing clients and in developing new prospects at the "C" level of Fortune 500 companies. What We Look For We’re interested in passionate leaders with a strong vision and a desire to stay on top of trends in the IT Advisory space. If you have a strong passion for helping businesses achieve their IT strategy, this role is for you. What We Offer The EY benefits package is designed to support your physical, emotional, financial, and social wellbeing. Our extensive benefits include comprehensive medical, dental, and prescription drug coverage, as well as mental health benefits, a robust Employee Assistance Program and group savings plans to promote your overall wellbeing. We offer generous time off, including personal days, vacation days, and additional firm-wide holidays, along with the option to purchase extra vacation days. Employees can take advantage of EY's exclusive learning programs tailored just for them. We also provide internal opportunities for career development and advancement, enabling you to grow within the firm. Get involved in meaningful volunteering through EY Ripples and make a positive impact in the community. Learn about our commitment to DE&I at https://www.ey.com/en_ca/about-us/corporate-responsibility/equity Are you ready to shape your future with confidence? Apply today. To help create the best experience during the recruitment process, please describe any accommodations you may need. EY reports salary ranges in accordance with applicable provincial pay transparency legislation. Individual salaries within the anticipated salary ranges noted below are determined through a wide variety of factors including but not limited to internal equity, education, relevant experience, knowledge, and applicable skill sets. Managers for Vancouver $110,000 to $160,000 Managers for Toronto: $110,000 to $160,000 Managers for Victoria: $99,000 to $149,000 Diversity and Inclusion at EY Diversity and inclusiveness are at the heart of who we are and how we work. We’re committed to fostering an environment where differences are valued, policies and practices are equitable, and our people feel a sense of belonging. We embrace diversity and are committed to combating systemic racism, advancing gender equity and women in leadership, advocating for the 2SLGBTQIA+ community, promoting our neuroinclusion and accessibility initiatives, and are dedicated to amplifying the voices of Indigenous peoples (First Nations, Inuit, and Métis) nationally as we strive towards reconciliation. Our diverse experiences, abilities, backgrounds, and perspectives make our people unique and help guide us. Because when people feel free to be their authentic selves at work, they bring their best and are empowered to build a better working world. EY | Building a better working world EY is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets. Enabled by data, AI and advanced technology, EY teams help clients shape the future with confidence and develop answers for the most pressing issues of today and tomorrow. EY teams work across a full spectrum of services in assurance, consulting, tax, strategy and transactions. Fueled by sector insights, a globally connected, multi-disciplinary network and diverse ecosystem partners, EY teams can provide services in more than 150 countries and territories.
