title: Solution Architect (SAP S/4 HANA)
description: Solution Architect (SAP S/4 HANA) – Tampa, FL At PMI U.S., we are building a modern nicotine business—focused on helping make a future without cigarettes a reality in America. As the U.S. businesses of Philip Morris International, we are investing in new products, science, and capabilities to provide the approximately 25 million legal age adults who still smoke with better alternatives. Our approach is rooted in innovation, responsible marketing, and a growing U.S. footprint that spans manufacturing, technology, and commercial operations across the country. That creates real opportunity. You’ll have the space to take ownership, develop new ideas, and contribute to work that is shaping our business and the category. We’re looking for people who are curious, collaborative, and motivated by progress—because the scale of what we’re building creates room to grow in different directions. About The Role The Solution Architect is responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining logistics solutions within the SAP and extended platform landscape. The role focuses on identifying and analyzing business needs, translating business requirements into application requirements, and providing technical advice and consultation on complex logistics applications. The IT Solution Expert drives continuous improvement of business processes through new technology or alternative uses of existing technology. Your ‘day To Day’ Lead the design of end-to-end Order-to-Cash (O2C) solutions across B2C business processes, ensuring scalability, reliability, and alignment with business objectives. Develop and maintain solution outlines, architecture designs, interface specifications, and technical roadmaps for O2C capabilities and related commerce platforms. Translate business requirements into solution architectures leveraging SAP S/4HANA, SAP CAR, and surrounding commerce ecosystems. Collaborate closely with Domain Architects, Enterprise Architects, Product Teams, and Engineering Teams to ensure alignment with enterprise architecture standards, target architectures, and strategic initiatives. Collaborate closely with Domain Architects, Enterprise Architects, Product Teams, and Engineering Teams to ensure alignment with enterprise architecture standards, target architectures, and strategic initiatives. Design and govern integrations across SAP and non-SAP platforms, including SAP Hybris, Avalara, Microsoft Dynamics, payment providers, 3PL partners, CRM, and customer experience platforms. Drive architecture decisions for key O2C capabilities including order management, pricing, taxation, fulfillment, inventory visibility, customer service, returns, refunds, reconciliation, and financial integration. Define architecture patterns and integration approaches using APIs, event-driven architecture, SAP BTP, Integration Suite, middleware, and cloud-native services. Identify architectural risks, technical debt, performance bottlenecks, and security concerns, recommending mitigation plans and continuous improvements. Provide technical leadership during project delivery, ensuring solutions are implemented according to approved designs and industry best practices. Stay current with emerging technologies, industry trends, and retail commerce innovations, recommending Key Skills Strong expertise in SAP S/4HANA Order-to-Cash processes, including Sales & Distribution, Billing, Pricing, Customer Master, Credit Management, and Financial Integration. Deep knowledge of SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR) and its role within omnichannel retail and consumer commerce ecosystems. Proven experience designing solutions for B2C, D2C, omnichannel commerce, and consumer order fulfillment processes. Strong understanding of U.S. business processes and regulatory requirements, including sales tax, consumer sales, retail operations, and commercial fulfillment. Experience integrating SAP with modern commerce and customer platforms, particularly Shopify, Microsoft Dynamics 365, CRM platforms, payment gateways, and logistics providers. Expertise with integration technologies including REST APIs, OData, SAP BTP, SAP Integration Suite, Event-Driven Architecture, Middleware, and EDI. Strong understanding of cloud-based architectures, microservices, security principles, and enterprise integration patterns. 8+ years of experience in SAP solution architecture, enterprise application architecture, or digital commerce solution design. 5+ years of experience architecting Order-to-Cash and customer-facing commerce solutions in B2C, D2C, retail, or consumer products environments. Demonstrated experience leading large-scale SAP transformation, digital commerce, or omnichannel initiatives. Experience working in complex, highly integrated enterprise environments with multiple platforms and vendors. Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Engineering, Business Information Systems, or related field. Preferred Qualifications Certifications: SAP S/4HANA Solution Architecture SAP CAR SAP Integration Suite / SAP BTP TOGAF, SAFe Architect, or equivalent architecture certification Annual Base Salary Range: $120,000 - $150,000 What We Offer We offer a competitive base salary, annual bonus (applicable based on level of position), great medical, dental and vision coverage, 401k with a generous company match, incredible wellness benefits, commuter benefits, pet insurance, generous PTO, and much more! We have implemented Smart Work, a hybrid model (3 days in office) of working that promotes flexibility in the workplace. Seize the freedom to define your future and ours. We’ll empower you to take risks, experiment and explore. Be part of an inclusive, diverse culture where everyone’s contribution is respected; Collaborate with some of the world’s best people and feel like you belong. Pursue your ambitions and develop your skills with a global business – our staggering size and scale provides endless opportunities to progress. Take pride in delivering our promise to society: To improve the lives of millions of smokers. PMI is an Equal Opportunity Employer. PMI is headquartered in Stamford, Conn., and its U.S. affiliates have more than 3,000 employees. PMI has been an entirely separate company from Altria and Philip Morris USA since 2008. PMI’s affiliates first entered the U.S. market following the company’s acquisition of Swedish Match in late 2022. Philip Morris International and its U.S. affiliates are working to deliver a smoke-free future. Since 2008, PMI has invested $12.5 billion globally to develop, scientifically substantiate and commercialize innovative smoke-free products for adults who would otherwise continue to smoke with the goal of transitioning legal-age consumers who smoke to better alternatives. In 2022, PMI acquired Swedish Match – a leader in oral nicotine delivery – creating a global smoke-free champion led by the IQOS and ZYN brands. The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has authorized versions of PMI’s IQOS electronically heated tobacco devices and Swedish Match’s General snus as Modified Risk Tobacco Products and renewal applications for these products are presently pending before the FDA. For more information, please visit www.pmi.com/us and www.pmiscience.com. #PMIUS 29547 Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Full-time Job function Engineering and Information Technology Industries Manufacturing Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at Philip Morris International U.S. by 2x See who you know

title: SAP BI Engineer – BW4HANA (SAP BI HANA Technology Specialist)
description: For our long-standing business partner AIRBUS Defence and Space, we are looking for you as a new employee of Tintschl Technik GmbH. The position holder (f/m/d) works as SAP BI HANA Technology Specialist . Your Tasks Act as Digital focal point and BI Specialist for all reporting requirements, ensuring service quality and end user satisfaction Be responsible for BI developments and collaborate closely with internal Airbus colleagues, including nearshore and offshore teams Provide end-to-end solutions focused on business outcomes and user experience Leverage technical trends and innovative technologies and contribute to BI strategy and roadmap definition Deliver business value through continuous innovation on an international scale Adapt rapidly in an evolving environment through multifunctional and international teams Deliver projects on time, on quality and on cost using agile or waterfall methodologies Analyze business requirements and translate them into digital solutions Develop BI solutions according to development best practices Ensure seamless integration of new tools and changes within the existing application landscape Continuously simplify and consolidate the application landscape Provide specialist advice for scoping, development, deployment, implementation, change management, maintenance and evolution of BI information systems Coordinate communication with other digital areas Lead change management activities after deployment of new tools Your Profile Master’s degree or Diploma in Computer Science, Engineering or a comparable qualification At least 5 years of experience in IT / information systems Strong technical knowledge in BW and deep experience in BW4HANA developments (mandatory) Technical knowledge in BO reporting tools such as Lumira and Analysis for Office (mandatory) BO administrative skills are required ABAP knowledge is highly recommended Additional BI skills in UI5, Fiori, CDS and ODATA are desirable Fluent English (negotiation level) What we can offer you Permanent employment contract Above-tariff remuneration with attractive bonuses Continuous personal support through a fixed contact person Increasing holiday and Christmas bonus within the first 4 years Bonus payment "Employees recruit employees" up to 1,000 € Company pension plan Possibility of home office in agreement with our clients Flexible working hours through a personal flexitime bank account Individual further training opportunities Option of permanent employment at our client’s company Interested? We look forward to receiving your application! About Tintschl The Tintschl Group, headquartered in Erlangen, has stood for premium service competence in the fields of human resources, engineering and IT since 1997. Our employees are specialized in the implementation of technically and organizationally demanding projects for our nationwide customers. Equal opportunities are an essential part of our corporate culture and the key to a diverse and inclusive working environment. We promote diversity, inclusion, work-life balance and equal opportunities for all people. Find the perfect job with us and apply. We look forward to welcoming you. title: SAP Administrator
description: Description The SAP Administrator is a people and consumer focused leader who is supportive of an inclusive environment. With direction from the Planning & Logistics Manager, the SAP Administrator develops and improves system usage to optimise data integrity and decision making. You’ll join the Site Management Team, partnering with key collaborators within a fast-paced environment. You will generate and sense-check production control and performance information to drive and facilitate improvement projects using basic LEAN methodology. title: ERP COE SAP Basis Expert M/F
description: Job Description: Airbus Commercial Aircraft is looking for a ERP COE SAP Basis Expert (f/m) to join our Digital CoE for ERP Team based in Toulouse, France. Joining the ERP COE Platforms means becoming a key player in a strategic partnership dedicated to strengthening platform operations and accelerating our global business transformation. As a transversal Product Service Line (PSL) within the Digital Enterprise (DE) Center of Excellence for ERP our mission is to provide robust operational foundations while driving innovation across the organization. Your working environment : Global capital of aeronautics and European capital for space research, Toulouse is a dynamic city in the southwest of France served by an international airport. Ideally located between the Mediterranean sea and the Atlantic ocean and close to the Pyrenees mountains, it offers plenty of options for outdoor activities ! How we care for you : Financial rewards : Attractive salary, agreements on success and profit sharing schemes, employee savings plan abounded by Airbus and employee stock purchase plan on a voluntary basis. Work / Life Balance : Extra days-off for special occasions, holiday transfer option, a Staff council offering many social, cultural and sport activities and other services. Wellbeing / Health : Complementary health insurance coverage (disability, invalidity, death). Depending on the site: health services center, concierge services, gym, carpooling application. Individual development : Great upskilling opportunities and development prospects with unlimited access to +10.000 e-learning courses to develop your employability, certifications, expert career path, accelerated development programmes, national and international mobility. At Airbus, we support you to work, connect and collaborate more easily and flexibly. Wherever possible, we foster flexible working arrangements to stimulate innovative thinking. Your challenges : SAP System Administration : Install, configure, upgrade, and maintain various SAP systems (e.g., ECC, S/4HANA, BW, PI/PO, SolMan, Fiori) across different environments (Development, Quality, Production). Performance Monitoring & Tuning : Proactively monitor SAP system performance, identify bottlenecks, and implement optimization strategies to ensure high availability and responsiveness. Security & Compliance : Manage user authorizations, profiles, and roles within SAP systems. Implement and enforce security policies and ensure compliance with internal and external audit requirements. Patching & Upgrades : Plan and execute SAP Basis patching, kernel upgrades, and EHP installations, ensuring minimal downtime and seamless integration. Backup & Recovery : Implement and manage robust backup and recovery strategies for all SAP systems to ensure data integrity and business continuity. Troubleshooting & Support : Provide expert-level troubleshooting and resolution for complex SAP Basis issues, working closely with functional teams, developers, and external vendors. Transport Management :Administer and optimize the SAP Transport Management System (TMS) to ensure efficient and controlled movement of changes across landscapes. System Landscape Design : Contribute to the design and evolution of the SAP system landscape, including sizing, architecture, and integration with other systems. Automation & Scripting : Develop and implement automation scripts and tools to streamline routine Basis tasks and improve operational efficiency. Documentation & Knowledge Sharing : Create and maintain comprehensive technical documentation for SAP Basis procedures, configurations, and incident resolutions. Share knowledge and mentor junior team members Your boarding pass : Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology or a related field. Master's degree is a plus. Minimum of 7-10 years of hands-on experience as an SAP Basis Administrator or Consultant in a large-scale, complex enterprise environment preferably within the aerospace or manufacturing industry. Technical Expertise: Profound knowledge of SAP NetWeaver architecture and components. Extensive experience with SAP ECC, S/4HANA, BW, PI/PO, Solution Manager and Fiori. Strong expertise in database administration for SAP systems (e.g., HANA, Oracle, DB2, SQL Server). Proficiency in operating systems relevant to SAP (e.g., Linux, Windows Server, AIX). Experience with SAP cloud solutions (e.g., SCP, RISE with SAP) is highly desirable. Familiarity with SAP security concepts, user management, and GRC. Solid understanding of networking concepts relevant to SAP landscapes. Experience with scripting languages (e.g., Shell, Python, PowerShell) for automation. Certifications : Relevant SAP Basis certifications (e.g., SAP Certified Technology Associate) are highly preferred. Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills. Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to collaborate effectively with diverse teams. Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a fast-paced environment. Strong sense of ownership and accountability. Fluent in English (both written and spoken); French is a plus. This position requires a security clearance or will require being eligible for clearance by the recognized authorities. Not a 100% match? No worries ! Airbus supports your personal growth with customized development solutions. Take your career to a new level and apply online now ! title: Junior SAP Support Specialist
description: We are looking for a Junior SAP Support Specialist to strengthen our team. You will have the opportunity to support our clients and enhance your SAP Customer Experience skills. If you are ready to contribute and grow within our dynamic environment, we invite you to apply. Kindly note that this role supports remote work, but only from within Ukraine. Responsibilities Provide 2nd Level Support, handling incident and service tickets in accordance with SLA requirements Perform technical monitoring of a solution, troubleshoot and resolve issues related to the solution Collaborate with IT team and end users to analyze, reproduce, and resolve issues Use Jira and Confluence for ticket tracking, project coordination, and documentation Provide feedback and recommendations for improvements to the custom solution Document support processes, resolutions, and client interactions accurately Requirements Motivated individual who is eager to learn Understanding of business processes within a company environment Upper-Intermediate level of English, both spoken and written (B2+) Residence in Ukraine (remote work is available only for candidates based in Ukraine) Practical experience in identifying and resolving technical issues with the solution Nice to have Background in customer support or previous experience as a support agent Familiarity with Jira and Confluence Experience as a SAP user We offer With us you can: Work on a flexible schedule remotely or from any of our comfortable offices or coworking spaces in Ukraine Receive the necessary equipment to perform your work tasks Change projects and technology stacks within EPAM Gain experience in various business domains (Insurance, E-commerce, Healthcare, Finance, Travelling, Media, Artificial Intelligence, and more) Relocation opportunities may be available for eligible candidates, depending on the role and openings at other EPAM locations Participate in volunteer, charity programs and communities (both technical and interest-based) We focus on your professional growth: You can plan your individual career path together with your manager Receive regular feedback from colleagues Improve your English for free with certified teachers (Speaking Clubs, client interview preparation courses, etc.) Get the opportunity to undergo free training and certification in AWS, GCP, or Azure Clouds Use the internal E-learn training program (18,200+ specialized training and mentoring programs) Access corporate accounts on LinkedIn Learning, Get Abstract and other partner resources Study at EPAM Solution Architecture School with the instructors who are practicing architects Develop as a leader, join Delivery Management, Resource Management, Leadership Essentials school and more Participate in internal communities (500+ meetups, technical discussions, brainstorming sessions, online events and conferences annually) What we offer: Vacation and sick leave (including a sick leave without a medical certificate) A wide range of Voluntary Medical Insurance programs providing both medical treatment and various preventive options (including sports activities) Medical insurance for family members at corporate rates Company support during significant life events (childbirth or adoption, marriage, etc.) Support for psychological comfort: discounts on services from mental health specialists or coaches, thematic training E-kids program - a free programming language training program for EPAMers' children Kindly be advised that the set of benefits, including learning, certification, and other opportunities, may vary depending on the role you apply for. Our recruiter will be able to share more details about the specific opportunity during your general interview. EPAM strives to provide its global team of over 62,350 professionals in more than 55 countries with opportunities for professional growth from day one of collaboration. Our colleagues are the source of EPAM's success, so we value cooperation, strive to always understand our clients' business and aim for the highest quality standards. No matter where you are, you will join a dedicated, diverse community that will help you realize your potential to the fullest.

title: SAP Delivery Manager
description: We're working on a confidential SAP Delivery Manager opportunity with a major international organisation going through a large-scale ERP transformation. This is not a “keep the lights on” SAP role. It is a proper delivery leadership position, owning SAP rollout activity across multiple business units, geographies, and operating environments. You’ll be responsible for making sure major SAP programmes are delivered properly: on time, within budget, with strong governance, clear stakeholder alignment, and real business adoption. The role will suit someone who has been close to large SAP ECC / S/4HANA rollouts, ideally in a manufacturing, engineering, industrial, or complex global business environment. You’ll be working closely with senior business and IT leadership, managing external delivery partners / system integrators, driving programme governance, and helping the business move towards a standardised SAP template rather than allowing every region or entity to drift into its own version of the process. What makes this interesting: This is a high-impact role inside a global SAP transformation where the decisions you make will directly affect how multiple parts of the business operate. You won’t just be tracking project plans. You’ll be shaping delivery, challenging partners, managing risk, driving adoption, and making sure the programme delivers value after go-live. The role will involve: Owning end-to-end SAP implementation and rollout delivery across multiple entities Managing delivery quality, governance, risk, budget, and programme outcomes Building and executing integrated delivery plans across scope, timelines, resources, and budget Working with senior stakeholders across business and IT Managing external SAP partners / SIs and holding them accountable for delivery Driving standardisation through adoption of a common SAP template Supporting business change, user adoption, and post-go-live value realisation Running governance forums, steering meetings, escalation routes, and decision-making processes The background I’m looking for: Strong SAP programme / delivery management experience Experience with large-scale SAP ECC and/or S/4HANA rollouts Background delivering across multiple countries, entities, sites, or business units Strong SI / vendor management experience Confident managing senior business stakeholders Comfortable owning governance, risk, cost, quality, and delivery outcomes Manufacturing, engineering, industrial, supply chain, or complex global business experience would be a strong advantage Willingness to travel when required This is likely to be contract to hire , so it would suit someone open to proving themselves in the role and potentially converting into a longer-term leadership position. The end client is confidential at this stage, but it is a serious global business with a major SAP roadmap ahead of them. If this sounds like the kind of programme you should be leading, send me a message and I can share more detail. title: Vice President- ERP Transformation - US
description: Alysian is a high-performing, boutique advisory firm specializing in delivering tailored strategic and technological solutions for leading private equity firms and their portfolio companies. With decades of combined cross-sector expertise, Alysian excels in bridging commercial vision to reality with technology execution — enabling rapid value creation at enterprise scale. We operate with a uniquely agile delivery model, combining deep domain expertise in digital strategy, transformation, and technology innovation. Our entrepreneurial culture and speed-to-impact approach have earned us repeated impact recognition from leading PE firms. At Alysian, we build long-term partnerships grounded in trust, performance, and a relentless drive for client success. Core values Data Driven – Strategic advisory based on a depth of relevant data. Speed & Precision – Agile delivery with tangible results. Impact-Driven – Measurable outcomes aligned to strategy. Expertise & Trusted Advisor – Deep domain knowledge with integrity. Entrepreneurial – Proactive problem-solving and ownership mentality. Alysian is expanding its US footprint and is looking for a VP, ERP Transformation to lead end-to-end ERP implementations and broader enterprise technology transformations for Private Equity clients and their portfolio companies. You will work closely with Alysian Partners, PE deal teams, and portfolio CIOs/IT leaders to define ERP strategy, lead selection and implementation programs, and ensure value realisation post-close. This role is ideal for individuals who have led large-scale ERP transformations (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics 365) and can operate confidently in carve-out, integration, and post-merger environments. Key Responsibilities Depending on experience, the role will cover at least two of the following areas: ERP & Enterprise Systems Transformation Lead end-to-end ERP strategy, selection, and implementation across Finance, Supply Chain, Commercial, and HR. Align ERP design and functionality with business strategy and operating model evolution. Act as a senior advisor across core platforms (ERP, CRM, and enterprise systems). Technology Due Diligence & IT Strategy (value creation lens) Support diligence by assessing maturity, scalability, and fit of legacy enterprise systems. Identify value creation levers such as systems consolidation, modernization, and cloud migration. Translate diligence insights into investment-grade recommendations and executable roadmaps. Operating Model & Architecture Design Design future-state IT operating models and application architecture to support growth and integration. Partner with business and tech stakeholders to ensure technology choices align to strategic goals. Implementation & Program Leadership Drive ERP and enterprise technology delivery from pre-deal planning through post-deal execution. Run governance/PMO cadences, manage dependencies, track budgets, and own risk management. Oversee deployment readiness, cutover, hypercare, and benefits realisation. Vendor & Partner Oversight Lead SI/vendor selection, commercial inputs, and performance management. Challenge vendors constructively, ensuring delivery quality and accountability. Change Management & Stakeholder Communication Develop stakeholder engagement plans to drive adoption and value realisation. Communicate progress, risks, and decisions through board-ready materials (decision papers, steering packs, executive updates). Candidate Profile 10–15 years of experience across consulting, transformation advisory, systems integration, or restructuring. Trained in a high-quality professional services environment. Hands-on exposure to large-scale ERP and core systems programs (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Oracle, Microsoft D365, NetSuite, IFS, Infor, Workday, Salesforce, ServiceNow). Track record leading programs in the €10m–€100m+ range. Proven ability to challenge system integrators and vendors constructively and credibly. Sharp executive communication: PowerPoint storylining, decision papers, and board-ready outputs. Comfort moving between delivery detail and executive summary without losing altitude. Evidence of progression and meaningful tenure in strong firms. Benefits Why Join Alysian Consulting? Our vision is to become the leading independent boutique tech advisory firm, transforming value creation in private equity through technology and data. We partner with top-tier PE firms (mega-funds in Europe & the US) to drive sustainable growth for portfolio companies and communities. At Alysian, you’ll work at the intersection of technology, strategy, and investment—where every project has a measurable impact on business performance. We partner with top-tier private equity firms, giving you access to high-profile transformations, boardroom conversations, and some of the most exciting portfolio companies in the market. We offer: Frontline PE exposure, from day one Client-facing work with US/EU Mega and Large Cap deal teams and PortCo CXOs; you lead modules that move EBITDA, not just make slides. Outcome ownership in small strike teams Leadership access, clear mandates, and the autonomy to design and ship cost-out / value-creation plays in weeks. Remote-first with limited travel Work from anywhere in US/Canada with sustainable US/EU overlap; optional office use; travel about 2–3 weeks/year. Builder’s seat in a scaling CoE Join the founding wave—shape operating models, codify playbooks, and help build the platform the firm scales on. Meritocracy with real velocity Progress based on outcomes; accelerated responsibility and pod leadership for top performers. title: SAP Architect
description: About the Company We are seeking an experienced ILM Technical Architect to lead the design and execution of SAP Information Lifecycle Management (ILM) architecture for the SAP BRIM Archiving Project. The role requires deep expertise in SAP ILM, data archiving strategy, and SAP BRIM/S/4HANA landscapes, with strong ownership of architecture decisions, compliance alignment, and stakeholder engagement in an onsite (US remote) model. About the Role The role requires deep expertise in SAP ILM, data archiving strategy, and SAP BRIM/S/4HANA landscapes, with strong ownership of architecture decisions, compliance alignment, and stakeholder engagement in an onsite (US remote) model. Responsibilities Design and own the SAP ILM architecture for BRIM data archiving and retention Define archiving strategy, data objects, retention rules, and lifecycle policies aligned with business and compliance requirements Lead ILM configuration and technical design, including object selection, rule assignment, and data flow design Work closely with SAP BRIM, S/4HANA, functional, technical, and BASIS teams to ensure seamless implementation Support data readiness, mock runs, validation, and production execution of archiving activities Ensure adherence to data privacy, legal hold, retention, and audit requirements Identify and mitigate technical risks related to data volume, performance, and system stability Provide technical leadership and guidance to development and support teams Prepare and maintain architecture documents, technical designs, and runbooks Support cutover, go‑live, and post‑archiving validation activities Qualifications Strong hands‑on experience as an SAP ILM Technical Architect Proven expertise in SAP ILM architecture design, data archiving, and retention management Solid understanding of SAP BRIM and SAP S/4HANA data models Experience working on large‑scale archiving or system lifecycle management projects Strong knowledge of compliance, audit, data privacy, and legal retention requirements Excellent stakeholder communication and technical leadership skills Required Skills Experience with SAP ILM Store, Retention Management, and Legal Hold Exposure to SAP BRIM modules (FICA, CI, Convergent Mediation) Experience working in regulated or audit‑driven enterprise environments Ability to work effectively in onsite–offshore delivery models Preferred Skills Typically 10–12+ years of overall SAP experience with deep ILM specialization Pay range and compensation package Not specified. title: Financial Analyst
description: About Huzzle At Huzzle, we connect exceptional talent with top opportunities at leading companies across the UK, US, Canada, Europe, and Australia. Our clients include fast-growing startups, digital agencies, SaaS companies, and tech-enabled businesses across industries such as MarTech, FinTech, EdTech, and eCommerce. Unlike traditional outsourcing agencies, we place you directly with innovative companies where you become a valued member of the team. Job Summary We are looking for a detail-oriented and analytical Financial Analyst to support business growth through data-driven financial insights. This role is ideal for candidates with strong financial modeling, reporting, budgeting, and forecasting experience who thrive in a fast-paced remote work environment. The ideal candidate is highly organised, proficient with financial tools and spreadsheets, and capable of translating complex financial data into actionable recommendations for leadership teams. Key Responsibilities Analyze financial data and prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual reports. Develop financial models, forecasts, and budgeting plans to support business objectives. Monitor company performance by tracking KPIs, revenue trends, and operational metrics. Conduct variance analysis and identify financial risks and opportunities. Assist with strategic planning, pricing analysis, and profitability assessments. Prepare presentations and reports for leadership and stakeholders. Support cash flow forecasting and expense management initiatives. Collaborate with cross-functional teams including operations, sales, and executive leadership. Ensure accuracy and integrity of financial data across systems and reports. Recommend process improvements to enhance financial efficiency and reporting accuracy. Requirements Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Business, or a related field. 2+ years of experience in financial analysis, FP&A, accounting, or corporate finance roles. Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets. Experience with financial modeling, forecasting, and budgeting. Familiarity with accounting software and ERP systems such as NetSuite, Xero, QuickBooks, or SAP is preferred. Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills. Strong attention to detail and organizational abilities. Ability to communicate financial insights clearly to non-financial stakeholders. Comfortable working independently in a remote environment. Experience in SaaS, FinTech, eCommerce, or startup environments is a plus Benefits 💰 Competitive salary based on experience and qualifications. 🌎 Fully remote role with flexible work environment. 📈 Long-term career growth opportunities with international companies. 🚀 Work with fast-growing startups and innovative global businesses. 🎯 Exposure to strategic financial planning and executive decision-making. 🤝 Collaborative and supportive remote team culture. title: SAP Functional Architect Associate Director
description: This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a SAP Functional Architect Associate Director based in United States. This role offers the opportunity to lead complex SAP transformation initiatives and help organizations solve critical business challenges through innovative technology solutions. You will serve as a trusted advisor to enterprise clients, shaping SAP S/4HANA strategies across finance and operations environments. The position combines technical architecture expertise, client engagement, business development, and team leadership responsibilities. You will work with senior stakeholders to design scalable solutions, deliver impactful consulting engagements, and identify opportunities for future growth. The role provides a collaborative environment where experienced professionals mentor teams, influence strategy, and drive digital transformation. You will contribute to thought leadership while helping clients maximize the value of modern SAP platforms and services. Accountabilities As a SAP Functional Architect Associate Director, you will lead SAP consulting engagements by combining deep functional expertise, solution architecture capabilities, and strong client relationship management. You will guide teams, support business development activities, and ensure successful delivery of strategic SAP initiatives. Lead the design and implementation of SAP S/4HANA Public and Private Cloud solutions, providing architectural guidance and functional expertise. Develop SAP solution strategies and architectures that address complex business requirements across finance and operations. Support client pursuits by designing solution approaches, creating proposals, and identifying opportunities to leverage SAP capabilities. Advise senior client stakeholders on SAP S/4HANA functionality, benefits, implementation considerations, and transformation strategies. Create industry-focused demonstrations, presentations, and solution materials to showcase SAP capabilities. Lead client workshops, discovery sessions, information gathering activities, and executive presentations. Provide expertise across SAP Finance and Supply Chain modules, supporting successful adoption and delivery. Collaborate with alliance partners and internal teams to establish licensing strategies and identify growth opportunities. Build and maintain strong relationships with executives, clients, and professional networks to support business development. Manage and mentor consulting teams, including coaching, performance feedback, professional development, and engagement oversight. Promote a culture of collaboration, knowledge sharing, innovation, and continuous improvement across teams. Contribute to thought leadership initiatives and help expand SAP consulting capabilities. Requirements The ideal candidate brings extensive SAP consulting experience, strong leadership capabilities, and the ability to translate complex business needs into practical technology solutions. This role requires a combination of technical knowledge, strategic thinking, and client-facing expertise. Bachelor’s degree in MIS, CIS, computer science, business, or a related discipline. 9+ years of experience working in professional services, consulting, or enterprise technology environments. Demonstrated experience leading SAP S/4HANA implementations and transformation programs. Strong knowledge of SAP solution design, architecture principles, and enterprise application strategies. Experience supporting SAP S/4HANA Finance and Operations solutioning activities. Ability to advise executives and business leaders on SAP strategy, capabilities, and implementation approaches. Experience developing SAP demos, presentations, proposals, and client-facing materials. Strong understanding of SAP licensing models and collaboration with technology partners. Proven experience managing and developing teams, including mentoring, coaching, performance management, and workload coordination. Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to explain complex concepts to both technical and executive audiences. Ability to identify business development opportunities and contribute to client growth strategies. Proficiency with Microsoft Office applications, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint; experience with Visio or similar tools is a plus. SAP professional certification, such as S/4HANA Sales Authorization, Customer Engagement Executive, Presales Consultant, or LOB Solution Consultant, is strongly preferred. Benefits Competitive base salary range of approximately $163,000 - $278,000 annually, depending on experience, skills, and location. Annual bonus opportunity with a target of 16%. Estimated total cash compensation range of approximately $189,080 - $322,480. Hybrid work model combining remote flexibility with meaningful in-person collaboration based on project and client needs. Medical, dental, and vision insurance coverage. Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) and Health Savings Accounts (HSA). Life, accident, and short/long-term disability insurance. Adoption and fertility assistance programs. Paid parental leave of up to 10 weeks. 401(k) savings plan with employer match contributions. Flexible Choice Time Off program for vacation, personal needs, and sick time. Up to 20 days of paid time off for new hires, plus paid holidays. Opportunities for professional growth, leadership development, and career advancement. Exposure to diverse industries, complex technology initiatives, and high-impact consulting projects. title: Accounting Paid Consultant - Disbursement Controls & Expense Processing
description: We are seeking experienced AP payments and disbursement professionals (or T&E / corporate-card program leads) to provide expert insight as part of a project for our AI Marketplace. No preparation required — just your real-world experience. This is a one-time, paid, project-based interview engaged on a contract basis — not a full-time role. Location: U.S.-based experts only Engagement : One-time, project-based interview Work Type: Remote Estimated rate: $100/hour (prorated for the 30-minute task) What You’ll Do You’ll complete a ~ 30-minute recorded AI-led video interview where you explain how you actually do your work in practice. This interview will focus on: Payment selection, payment-file creation, approval, and reconciliation workflows ACH, wire, check, card, bank-portal, positive pay, and returned-payment handling Off-cycle payments, duplicate-payment prevention, and disbursement fraud controls Expense-report review, corporate-card reconciliation, policy violations, and employee experience tradeoffs You will be asked to explain workflows specifically within: payment runs, payment approval, bank-file controls, failed-payment handling, expense review, and corporate-card reconciliation workflows. You should only apply if you can confidently walk through these areas step-by-step based on your own experience and respond to AI-led interview questions with depth, clarity, and real-world examples. What We’re Looking For 6+ years in AP payments, treasury operations support, disbursements, T&E processing, or corporate-card administration Direct, hands-on experience in payment runs, payment approval, bank-file controls, failed-payment handling, expense review, and corporate-card reconciliation workflows Experience making or influencing decisions related to payment timing, fraud controls, urgent payment exceptions, expense policy enforcement, and card-program risk Familiarity with tools such as: SAP, Oracle, NetSuite, Coupa Pay, Concur, Expensify, Amex @ Work, bank portals, Kyriba, Excel Strong ability to clearly explain workflows, decisions, and reasoning Experience owning or directly executing these workflows in a real production environment Experience working in a cross-functional finance, strategy, treasury, investor-relations, or business-partnering environment About the Interview The interview is designed to elicit step-by-step explanations of real workflows and decision-making processes, with an emphasis on detailed, experience-based responses rather than high-level summaries. This is a project-based engagement. Selected participants will be paid for completing a recorded AI-led video interview; hourly rates are estimates based upon anticipated time of completion. Participants will control their own schedule, provide their own tools to complete the interview, and may participate in other opportunities as they choose. Once you complete the interview, we’ll send your video recording and transcript, along with AI-generated insights, to the internal research team and AI lab partner. This data will be used to generate a structured list of tasks associated with your profession and for AI model improvement. title: Procurement Specialist
description: Procurement Specialist — Part-Time, Remote (Odoo or Similar ERP) Location: Remote (United States) Schedule: Part-time, 20–30 hours per week Compensation: $35–$50 per hour, commensurate with experience Benefits: Health plan, 401(k), supplemental insurance, and parental leave Reports to: Operations Manager/Director About the Company We are a multi-brand consumer products company operating three distinct brands under a single holding company. We sell across direct-to-consumer (D2C), Amazon, and wholesale channels, sourcing both finished goods and raw materials — domestically and internationally (primarily China). Fulfillment is handled through a network of third-party logistics (3PL) partners across the United States and Canada. (Company name is withheld at this stage and shared with candidates during the interview process.) About the Role We're hiring a hands-on Procurement Specialist to own purchasing across all three of our brands. You'll translate sales and production demand into buying decisions, then manage every purchase order from first quote to warehouse receipt — working entirely in Odoo . This is a remote, part-time position with a high degree of autonomy, and it includes a full benefits package. For the right person, there's a clear path to grow this role into a full-time position over time. It's an ideal fit for an organized buyer who's comfortable juggling multiple brands, suppliers, and logistics partners at once. Key Responsibilities Analyze inventory needs across three brands, aligning purchasing with sales demand (D2C, Amazon, wholesale) and production schedules Plan production runs for subcontracted manufacturing and purchase the raw materials required to support them Coordinate with suppliers and obtain quotes; compare pricing, lead times, minimum order quantities, and terms Create, issue, and manage purchase orders end-to-end in Odoo — from quote through receipt Track open POs and inbound shipments through to receiving; confirm accurate receipt and resolve quantity, cost, or damage discrepancies Coordinate with the logistics team and 3PL partners (three in the U.S., one in Vancouver, Canada) on inbound shipments, including imports arriving primarily from China Monitor stock levels, reorder points, and replenishment to prevent both stockouts and overstock Maintain accurate item, vendor, cost, and lead-time data in Odoo across all three entities Build and maintain supplier relationships; negotiate price, terms, and lead times Partner with sales, operations, and finance to forecast demand and flag supply risks early Required Qualifications 1+ year of hands-on ERP experience — Odoo strongly preferred. Comparable ERP experience (e.g., NetSuite, SAP, Microsoft Dynamics, Fishbowl) will also be considered (please name the ERP(s) you've used in your application) 1–3 years of procurement, purchasing, or supply-chain experience Demonstrated experience creating and managing purchase orders from quote to receipt Solid understanding of inventory management — reorder points, lead times, and matching supply to demand Strong spreadsheet skills (Excel or Google Sheets) Clear written and verbal communication; confident working directly with suppliers Highly organized and self-directed, able to manage purchasing for multiple entities independently in a remote environment Authorized to work in the United States Bachelor's degree preferred, not required Preferred (Nice-to-Have) Direct, hands-on experience specifically with Odoo (Purchase, Inventory, and Manufacturing modules) Experience importing from China and familiarity with international freight, Incoterms, and customs/duties Experience coordinating with 3PL or warehouse partners Experience in a multi-entity or multi-brand operation Familiarity with D2C, Amazon (Seller/Vendor Central), and wholesale fulfillment Exposure to production planning and subcontract manufacturing APICS/CPIM (or similar) supply-chain certification Schedule, Location & Compensation Part-time: 20–30 hours per week, with potential to grow into a full-time role for the right candidate Fully remote within the United States $35–$50 per hour, commensurate with experience Benefits: health plan, 401(k), supplemental insurance, and parental leave How to Apply Submit your resume along with a brief note describing your hands-on Odoo experience and the types of products or supply chains you've purchased for. We are an equal-opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or veteran status. title: Customer Operations - Talent Community
description: At PointClickCare our mission is simple: to help providers deliver exceptional care. And that starts with our people. As a leading health tech company that’s founder-led and privately held, we empower our employees to push boundaries, innovate, and shape the future of healthcare. With the largest long-term and post-acute care dataset and a Marketplace of 400+ integrated partners, our platform serves over 30,000 provider organizations, making a real difference in millions of lives. We also reinvest a significant percentage of our revenue back into research and development, ensuring our employees have the resources to innovate and make a lasting impact. Recognized by Forbes as a top private cloud company and honored as one of Canada’s Most Admired Corporate Cultures, we offer flexibility, growth opportunities, and meaningful work. At PointClickCare, we empower our people to be the architects of a smarter healthcare future; one that is human-first and accelerated by AI to create meaningful and lasting change. Employees harness AI as a catalyst for creativity, productivity, and thoughtful decision-making. By integrating AI tools into our daily workflows, collaboration is enhanced, outcomes are improved, and every team member has the proficiency to maximize their impact. It all starts with our hiring practices where we uncover AI expertise that complements our mission, and we continue to invest in training and development to nurture innovation throughout the employee journey. Join us in redefining healthcare — so it doesn’t just survive, it thrives. To learn more about PointClickCare, check out Life at PointClickCare and connect with us on Glassdoor and LinkedIn. Travel to Office expectations** For Remote Roles : If this role is remote, there will be in-office events that will require travel to and from the Mississauga and/or Salt Lake City office. These will include, but not limited to, onboarding, team events, semi-annual and annual team meetings. For Hybrid Roles : If this role is Hybrid, there will be an expectation to reside within commutable distance to the office/location specified in the job listing. This will include, but not limited to, weekly/bi-weekly/monthly events in the office with your specific team. This is a requirement for this role. title: Sr. Consultant - SAP Production Planning
description: Overview We are seeking a highly skilled Senior Consultant - SAP Production Planning to lead and deliver manufacturing-focused SAP solutions across ECC and S/4HANA environments. This role requires deep functional expertise, strong cross-module integration capabilities, and the ability to guide clients through complex transformation initiatives, including S/4HANA migrations and advanced manufacturing optimization. The ideal candidate will combine hands-on configuration experience with strategic advisory skills, driving end-to-end solution design while mentoring junior team members and engaging with executive stakeholders. Key Responsibilities Solution Design & Delivery Lead end-to-end SAP PP solution design and execution for manufacturing clients Conduct fit-to-standard workshops and define optimal business processes Configure and implement: Master data structures (BOMs, routings, work centers, production versions) Demand management and MRP strategies (MTS, MTO, CTO) Capacity planning and production scheduling S/4HANA & Transformation Support ECC to S/4HANA migrations (brownfield and greenfield approaches) Lead transition from classic PP to embedded PP-DS Design and implement MRP Live, predictive MRP, and DDMRP concepts Enable adoption of Fiori shop floor apps and Manufacturing Cockpit Integration & Cross-Functional Alignment Drive integration across SAP modules, including: PP-MM (procurement-driven production, subcontracting, external processing) PP-SD (ATP, sales order-driven production, scheduling) PP-QM (quality inspection, batch release, in-process controls) PP-FI/CO (product costing, WIP, variance analysis, settlement) PP-EWM/WM (material staging, warehouse integration) PP-PM (resource and equipment availability) Manufacturing Innovation Implement solutions leveraging: Embedded PP-DS and advanced scheduling tools SAP Digital Manufacturing Cloud (DMC) and MES integration patterns Discrete, process, and repetitive manufacturing scenarios (REM, PI sheets, backflushing) Variant configuration (LO-VC and S/4 AVC) Program Leadership & Advisory Lead RICEF identification, prioritization, and estimation for PP workstreams Define data migration strategies for PP master data and production orders Develop cutover and parallel-run strategies for manufacturing operations Provide client-facing advisory, including executive presentations and steering committees Contribute to SOW development and effort estimation Team Leadership Mentor junior consultants and provide technical oversight Drive best practices and ensure quality delivery across project teams Requirements SAP Production Planning Expertise Strong hands-on experience with: Classic MRP (ECC) and production planning processes Master data design: BOMs, routings, work centers, resources Demand management and supply strategies (MTS, MTO, CTO) Capacity planning and detailed scheduling S/4HANA & Advanced Planning Experience with: Embedded PP-DS S/4HANA manufacturing capabilities and migration strategies Transition from ECC PP to S/4HANA solutions Integration Experience Proven track record integrating PP with MM, SD, and other logistics modules Preferred Qualifications Experience with: MRP Live (S/4HANA) Variant Configuration (LO-VC) and Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC) All manufacturing order types: Discrete manufacturing Process manufacturing (PI sheets, batch management) Repetitive manufacturing (REM) Exposure to: Demand-Driven MRP (DDMRP) and predictive planning models SAP Digital Manufacturing Cloud (DMC) and MES integrations Manufacturing Fiori apps and shop floor digitalization Nice-to-Have Skills Experience in additional SAP modules: WM/EWM, SD, FI/CO, or broader SCM Deep expertise in Variant Configuration, including: LO-VC to AVC migration strategy and execution CPQ (Configure-Price-Quote) tools Familiarity with: SAP BTP / BAIP and modern development frameworks (MTA, CAP, RAP) Integration architecture across SAP and non-SAP systems Key Competencies Strong analytical and problem-solving skills Excellent stakeholder management and communication abilities Ability to translate business requirements into scalable SAP solutions Leadership mindset with a focus on mentoring and collaboration Education & Experience Bachelor's degree in Supply Chain, Engineering, Information Systems, or related field 7+ years of SAP PP experience, including full title: Regional Head of Sales – North America
description: The Company Voith Hydro is a global leader in hydropower technology, delivering innovative solutions and lifecycle services to utilities and power producers worldwide. As part of the Voith Group, the company plays a key role in advancing sustainable energy generation and modernizing critical hydroelectric infrastructure. To support its growth ambitions in North America, Voith Hydro is seeking a commercially driven Head of Sales to lead business development and market expansion across the United States, Canada, and Mexico. The Opportunity This is a highly visible leadership role with responsibility for driving profitable growth across the North American hydropower market. Reporting to the VP Sales & Tendering and working closely with Global Sales, the Regional Head of Sales is responsible for leading all regional sales activities and driving the go-to-market strategy to achieve sustainable and profitable growth. The role focuses on expanding Voith’s customer portfolio, developing new market opportunities, and building a high-performing, customer-centric sales organization. Initially an individual contributor, the successful candidate will help shape the commercial structure and build a team as the business continues to grow. Key Responsibilities Develop and execute the North American sales strategy aligned with global business objectives. Drive new business development across the United States, Canada, and Mexico. Build and manage a healthy pipeline of project and service opportunities. Lead strategic customer engagement and contract negotiations. Expand relationships with utilities, EPCs, OEMs, government entities, and other key stakeholders. Collaborate closely with Tendering, Engineering, Project Management, Service Hubs, and Global Key Account Management. Ensure accurate forecasting, pipeline management, and CRM discipline. Deliver profitable order intake and sustainable revenue growth. Build and develop a high-performing sales organization over time. Candidate Profile We are looking for a technically credible and commercially accomplished sales leader with experience in the hydropower sector. Required Qualifications Bachelor's degree in Engineering or a related technical discipline; advanced degree preferred. 10+ years of sales, business development, or commercial leadership experience within an international project-based industrial environment. Experience in hydropower, ideally within an OEM, EPC, utility, or related infrastructure organization. Proven success managing large and complex sales opportunities, often valued between $20M and $400M. Demonstrated expertise in pipeline management, forecasting, proposal development, and contract negotiations. Experience working within matrix organizations and collaborating with engineering and project delivery teams. Knowledge of North American utility and government contracting environments. Experience with Salesforce or similar CRM platforms and SAP is a plus. Personal Profile The ideal candidate combines technical understanding with strong commercial instincts and an entrepreneurial mindset. Key characteristics include: Strategic and growth-oriented Hands-on and execution-focused Strong relationship builder Credible with both technical and executive stakeholders Comfortable operating in a complex matrix environment Collaborative, resilient, and results-driven Location & Travel Preferred location(s): York, PA or Montreal, Canada.Hybrid or Remote possible. Travel approximately 25–40% throughout North America Why Join Voith Hydro? This is a unique opportunity to shape the commercial future of a globally recognized hydropower leader. The Head of Sales will have a direct impact on market growth, customer engagement, and the development of a new commercial organization across North America. For an entrepreneurial sales leader with hydro expertise, this role offers significant visibility, autonomy, and long-term growth potential. title: Payroll Manager
description: Offering trendy fashion accessories at great prices – that's our vision. It's a vision we've been implementing successfully since 1990. With our strong brands SIX, I AM, TOSH and several white and private labels, we're represented through over 24.200 sales areas in 21 countries worldwide. This makes beeline one of the largest international suppliers of fashion jewelry and accessories. Over 3.200 dedicated employees ensure that we continue to open up new sales areas and markets, and that our fashionable product lines, are continually expanded. With passion, responsibility and a high level of teamwork, we are growing…fast, and looking for enthusiastic and experienced people to help us on this remarkable journey! Payroll Manager This is a Remote position based out of the US. Mission To oversee the payroll execution and systems for beeline, North America, coordinating with international partners where required. Scope The Payroll Manager will report to the Director of HR Operations and supervises 1-2 team members, overseeing Payroll and supporting a shared HRIS resource. Role Expectations Achieve seamless transfer of payroll data by delivering timely and accurate systems interfaces with internal and external partners. Ensure compliance with federal/local legislation and internal Company policies. Serve as a partner to field leaders, ensuring user-friendly processes, guideline and documentation. Serve as a partner to Finance and Controlling by understanding their business and functional objectives and translating that to HRIS strategies that will support those objectives. Support local and global HR systems enhancements. Ensure team has clear objectives aligned with business outcomes, receives consistent feedback and appropriate consequences based on their performance. Drive cross-functional development for team, focused on skill development and employee engagement. Immediate Critical Outcomes Basic understanding of current systems, client groups, and business objectives (First 30 days) Establish relationships with team members and understand their current assignments, annual objectives and development goals (First 30 days) Establish and implement feedback mechanisms to gauge progress and identify areas of improvements. (First 90 days) Critical Experiences Bachelor’s degree Minimum 5 years of experience in HR administration and payroll department Minimum 2 years of management experience Solid knowledge of SAP Beeline Guiding Principles and Competency Behavior Expectations Focus & Impact Entrepreneurial Thinking: Initiates innovation through out-of-the-box thinking Drive for Results: Inspires ambitious performance to achieve goals Accountability: Holds self and others accountable, “says what needs to be said” Teamwork & Collaboration Collaborative Teamwork: Ensures self/team/function contribute to achievement of shared objectives Effective Communication: Shares initiatives in a manner that facilitates buy-in and empowers team Cross-Cultural Sensitivity: Solicits inclusion of others, making sure others points of view are included Growth & Improvement Managing Change: Leads self and coaches team and/or function towards future state Willingness to Develop: Challenges self, team and/or function to seek opportunities for improvement Self-Reflection: Demonstrates awareness of strengths and opportunities; understands impact to team Responsibility & Sustainability Integrity: Actively contributes to organizational governance and compliance standards Resilience: Reacts to adversity without emotion and demonstrates emotional intelligence Managing Resources: Ensures self, team and/or function meet all cost and productivity targets To learn more about the beeline company and our brands, please visit: Follow Us On LinkedIn Follow us on X: @ Beeline is an Equal Opportunity Employer, including the consideration of applicants with a criminal history. title: SAP - Applicationmanager
description: SAP Application Manager Location: Utrecht, Netherlands Employment type: Full-time Industry: Pharmaceutical Production Language: Dutch highly preferred Salary: Up to €6,339 gross per month (depending on experience) About the role We are looking for an experienced SAP Application Manager to join a leading organization within the pharmaceutical production industry. In this role, you will be responsible for the management, optimization, and continuity of critical business applications within a highly regulated manufacturing environment. You will operate at the intersection of IT, production, quality, and business operations. Your focus will be ensuring that key systems such as SAP FICO, MES, and LIMS are reliable, compliant, and continuously improved to support essential pharmaceutical production processes. Due to the regulated nature of the environment, experience with GxP, validated systems, and pharmaceutical manufacturing processes is highly valued. Key Responsibilities Manage and optimize SAP applications within a pharmaceutical production environment. Provide functional application management for systems including: SAP FICO (Finance & Controlling) MES (Manufacturing Execution System) LIMS (Laboratory Information Management System) Analyze and resolve incidents, service requests, and user issues. Support key users and business stakeholders across Finance, Quality, Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and IT. Coordinate with external vendors, SAP consultants, and internal IT teams. Manage testing, documentation, and implementation of system changes. Support releases, upgrades, and continuous improvement initiatives. Ensure compliance with applicable quality and validation requirements. Contribute to improving business processes and application performance. Your Profile Bachelor’s degree or equivalent working experience in IT, Computer Science, Business Information Systems, or a related field. Proven experience as an Application Manager, Functional Application Manager, SAP Consultant, or similar role. Strong experience with SAP FICO is highly preferred. Experience with MES and/or LIMS systems in a manufacturing environment is a strong advantage. Experience within the pharmaceutical, life sciences, medical, or other regulated industries is preferred. Knowledge of validated systems and GxP requirements is a plus. Familiarity with ITIL processes, incident management, and change management. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Excellent communication skills and the ability to work effectively with both technical and business stakeholders. Dutch language skills are highly preferred; English is required. What We Offer A challenging role within a high-quality pharmaceutical production environment. A salary of up to €6,339 gross per month , depending on experience. The opportunity to work with critical enterprise applications supporting pharmaceutical manufacturing. A professional environment focused on quality, innovation, and continuous improvement. Excellent secondary employment benefits and development opportunities. Are you an experienced SAP Application Manager looking for a role where technology directly supports critical pharmaceutical production processes? Apply today and take the next step in your career within the life sciences industry. title: SAP Developer
description: Bouw de beste oplossingen met jouw expertise! Werk met de nieuwste technologieën en maak impact Ben jij een ervaren SAP Developer met een passie voor softwareontwikkeling? Heb je ervaring met UI5, Fiori, BTP, Build, RAP, CAP en ABAP in de cloud, en wil je jouw technische expertise inzetten om klanten écht verder te helpen? Bij SUPERP krijg je de ruimte om jouw ambities waar te maken, de nieuwste ontwikkelingen bij te houden, en oplossingen te bouwen waar je trots op kunt zijn. Over SUPERP SUPERP is een IT-consultancy partner die sinds 1999 het digitale hart van toonaangevende organisaties versterkt. Met meer dan 500 consultants bouwen we aan betrouwbare, toekomstbestendige Digital Core-systemen. We combineren de slagkracht van een grote speler met de betrokkenheid van een specialist. Onze expertise in SAP, Mendix, Salesforce, Microsoft en gebieden als development, AI, security, analytics, quality assurance zorgt voor slimme, flexibele IT-oplossingen. Bij SUPERP werken echte vakidioten met passie voor hun werk. Mensen die complexe IT-vraagstukken door en door snappen én oplossen. Nuchter, betrokken en recht door zee. We denken mee, zeggen waar het op staat en doen wat werkt. Jouw rol als SAP Developer Als SAP Developer bij SUPERP werk je aan uitdagende projecten voor topklanten in Nederland. Je vertaalt complexe wensen van gebruikers naar slimme, gebruiksvriendelijke oplossingen. Vanuit de clean core strategie principes ontwikkelt je business- en webapplicaties, optimaliseer je processen en zorg je ervoor dat jouw code niet alleen werkt, maar ook toekomstbestendig is. Wat je doet: Je ontwikkelt en implementeert oplossingen met UI5, Fiori, BTP, Build, RAP, CAP en ABAP in de cloud. Je werkt nauw samen met klanten en collega’s om hun behoeften te vertalen naar technische oplossingen. Je test, debugt en documenteert jouw code om ervoor te zorgen dat applicaties soepel draaien. Je houdt relevante ontwikkelingen binnen SAP en softwareontwikkeling bij om altijd voorop te blijven lopen. Je bouwt oplossingen die niet alleen technisch sterk zijn, maar ook écht impact maken voor onze klanten. Wat neem je mee? We zoeken SAP Developers die: Ervaring hebben met SAP-technologieën zoals UI5, Fiori, BTP, Build, RAP, CAP en ABAP in de cloud. Een sterke affiniteit hebben met softwareontwikkeling en alles wat daarbij komt kijken. Technisch gedreven zijn en altijd op zoek naar de beste oplossing. Ambitie hebben om klanten zo goed mogelijk te helpen en waarde toe te voegen. Proactief zijn en verantwoordelijkheid nemen voor hun werk. Up-to-date blijven met relevante ontwikkelingen binnen hun vakgebied. HBO- of WO-werk- en denkniveau hebben in informatica of een vergelijkbaar vakgebied. Sterke communicatieve vaardigheden bezitten en goed kunnen samenwerken in een team. Wat bieden wij jou? Bij SUPERP krijg je de vrijheid om te werken zoals jij dat goed acht. Wij geloven in vertrouwen, ruimte en ontwikkeling. Wat je van ons kunt verwachten: Een serieus mooi salaris, afhankelijk van kennis en ervaring. Een maandelijkse winstdelingsregeling op basis van jouw persoonlijke performance, die kan oplopen tot meerdere maandsalarissen per jaar. Flexibele mobiliteit: kies tussen een leaseauto of mobiliteitsvergoeding. 25 vakantiedagen per jaar. Diverse onkostenvergoedingen, waaronder een telefoonvergoeding van netto €45,-. Toegang tot de nieuwste technologieën en trainingen om jezelf te blijven ontwikkelen. Een contract voor onbepaalde tijd. Proces: Maak kennis met SUPERP In twee gesprekken maak je kennis met je toekomstige teamleider, een sales collega en een teamgenoot. Zo krijg je een goed beeld van de organisatie en de mensen waarmee je gaat werken. Heb je specifieke wensen over wie je wilt spreken? Laat het me weten, dan maken we dat mogelijk! Klaar voor de volgende stap? Ben je enthousiast? Mooi! Solliciteren kan heel eenvoudig via Linkedin,mail, app en telefoon! Als je eerst nog wat meer wilt weten over de functie of over werken bij SUPERP, neem contact met me op. Ik neem graag uitgebreid de tijd om je vragen te beantwoorden of je meer te vertellen over ons sollicitatieproces. Contactgegevens: Thijs Cornet t.cornet@superp - 06 58 86 54 18 Ik kijk uit naar je reactie! title: Expert SAP BC (F/H)
description: Lieu : Valence, France Construisons ensemble un avenir de confiance Thales est un leader mondial des hautes technologies spécialisé dans trois secteurs d’activité : Défense & Sécurité, Aéronautique & Spatial, et Cyber & Digital. Il développe des produits et solutions qui contribuent à un monde plus sûr, plus respectueux de l’environnement et plus inclusif. Le Groupe investit près de 4 milliards d’euros par an en Recherche & Développement, notamment dans des domaines clés de l’innovation tels que l’IA, la cybersécurité, le quantique, les technologies du cloud et la 6G. Thales compte près de 81 000 collaborateurs dans 68 pays. Nos engagements, vos avantages Une réussite portée par notre excellence technologique, votre expérience et notre ambition partagée Un package de rémunération attractif Un développement des compétences en continu : parcours de formation, académies et communautés internes Un environnement inclusif, bienveillant et respectant l’équilibre des collaborateurs Un engagement sociétal et environnemental reconnu Votre quotidien En nous rejoignant à Valence, vous intégrez le centre de gravité mondial de nos activités avioniques hélicoptères. Il abrite également nos activités de services numériques. Au sein de la Squad ERP de 20 personnes, vous intervenez principalement dans le cadre d’un projet autour des applications SAP. En tant qu'expert(e) SAP, vous aurez pour missions de : Maintenir et optimiser les applications SAP existantes et futures Piloter le maintien en condition opérationnelles et en condition de sécurité des systèmes SAP Assister et conseiller les équipes métiers et clientes autour des aspects techniques de SAP Effectuer les architectures SAP et les déployer suivant les recommandations de l’éditeur Automatiser les processus de déploiement, de configuration et de gestion de l’application SAP Prendre part aux migrations vers des solutions CLOUD Votre profil Vous êtes diplômé(e) d'un Master ou d'une école d'Ingénieur et justifiez d'une expérience significative sur un poste similaire. Vous avez de fortes connaissances de SAP Basics Components. Vous possédez des compétences en base de données HANA. Vous êtes familier(ère) des systèmes Linux et Windows. Vous connaissez les outils DEVOPS (Ansible-DEVOPS). Vous êtes autonome, innovant(e) et orienté(e) solutions ? Alors ce poste est fait pour vous ! Thales, entreprise Handi-Engagée, reconnait tous les talents. La diversité est notre meilleur atout. Postulez et rejoignez nous ! Le poste pouvant nécessiter d'accéder à des informations relevant du secret de la défense nationale, la personne retenue fera l'objet d'une procédure d’habilitation, conformément aux dispositions des articles R.2311-1 et suivants du Code de la défense et de l’IGI 1300 SGDSN/PSE du 09 août 2021. title: Warehouse Manager
description: Warehouse Manager, Data Centre Business Unit Veenendaal, Netherlands | Permanent | €85,000 + travel expenses I'm partnered with a global networking and connectivity technology manufacturer whose data centre business is growing rapidly. Their Veenendaal distribution centre has reached the point where the data centre product line needs its own dedicated Warehouse Manager, a newly created role with clear ownership of a high-growth business unit. What you'll own: Full warehouse and distribution operations for the data centre segment: receiving, storage, picking, packing, shipping and inventory control Leading, coaching and developing warehouse supervisors and associates Inventory accuracy through cycle counts, audits and reconciliation Capacity planning and growth initiatives as data centre volumes scale KPIs across on-time shipment, productivity, safety, cost per shipment and order accuracy Coordination with supply chain, manufacturing, procurement and customer service, plus external carriers What they're looking for: 5+ years in warehouse, distribution or logistics management Hands-on experience with WMS and ERP systems (SAP ideal) Knowledge of Lean, Six Sigma or continuous improvement methodologies Strong leadership and communication skills Experience in telecoms, electronics manufacturing or high-volume distribution is a plus, as is multi-shift management This is a genuine growth story: the site is scaling fast and this role exists because the business unit has outgrown a single-manager structure. You'll have real visibility and the chance to shape how the data centre operation runs.
