title: SAP Solution Architect
description: Wat ga je doen Vanaf 1 juni jongstleden is de SAP-afdeling een center of excellence geworden! Dit betekent dat jij als professional tot je recht kan komen in een Nederlands familiebedrijf waar een open cultuur heerst! Om te kunnen blijven voldoen aan de wensen van onze klanten zijn we op zoek naar een ervaren SAP Solution Architect. Als SAP Solution architect ben je verantwoordelijk voor het ontwerpen, coördineren en bewaken van de end-to-end SAP-oplossingsarchitectuur bij onze verschillende klanten. Je weet hun wensen te vertalen naar een schaalbare en toekomstbestendige SAP-omgeving. Onze klanten zijn (grote) retail-, productie- en logistieke bedrijven zoals: Jumbo, Vion, Swiss Sense en Nunner Logistics. Ook beheren wij het SAP-landschap, waar jij in samenwerking met je team richting geeft aan de ontwikkeling. Je komt te werken in een hecht team meer dan 40 andere SAP-consultants, ieder met zijn of haar eigen expertisegebied en niveau. Tijdens de verschillende projecten vervul je een belangrijke rol als sparringpartner, adviseur, trainer en coördinator en ben je in staat op verschillende niveaus te communiceren. In het kort ga je het volgende doen: Ontwerpen van end-to-end SAP-oplossingen (S/4HANA, RISE with SAP, SAP BTP, integraties); Ontwerpen van integraties tussen SAP en niet-SAP systemen (bijv. Salesforce, ServiceNow of logistieke platforms); Vertalen van bedrijfsprocessen naar SAP-functionaliteit; Begeleiden van implementatieprojecten en begeleiden van DevOps-teams; Verder helpen bouwen aan onze PLAN-afdeling volgens het IT4IT-principe; Opstellen van SAP-roadmap die aansluit bij de IT-strategie; Evalueren en implementeren van nieuwe (SAP)-technologie zoals Signavio, LeanIX of Cloud ALM; Opstellen en naleven van architectuurprincipes en standaarden. Wie wij zoeken : Jij hebt een “can do”-mentaliteit: je neemt initiatief, denkt in oplossingen en weet zaken voor elkaar te krijgen. Wij zetten met het werven van de rol van SAP Solution Architect een stap vooruit in onze dienstverlening. Jij weet hoe je deze rol binnen de organisatie succesvol vormgeeft. Je bent niet alleen sterk in het snel oplossen van problemen, maar je hebt ook het vermogen om anderen te begeleiden en te versterken. Je toont eigenaarschap, werkt zelfstandig en neemt verantwoordelijkheid. Daarmee vervul je een voorbeeldrol en ben je een bron van inspiratie voor je collega’s. Verder neem je mee: Meerdere jaren ervaring met SAP-implementaties in S/4HANA, SAP BTP, ABAP, Fiori en integratieplatformen; Inzicht in bedrijfsprocessen zoals Order-to-Cash, Procure-to-Pay, Plan-to-Produce, Record-to-Report; Ervaring met architectuurmethodieken zoals TOGAF, ArchiMate, BPMN of UML; Kennis en ervaring op het gebied van integratie tussen SAP en non-sap systemen, met voorkeur SAP Integration suite; Sterke communicatieve vaardigheden; Goede beheersing van de Nederlandse en Engelse taal; Voldoe je niet aan alle eisen? Dan nodigen we je nog steeds uit om te solliciteren! Waarom Simac? Simac is een warme organisatie die nauw samenwerkt vanuit diverse locaties in Nederland en België en waarin een positieve, klantgerichte sfeer heerst. We zijn een ambitieus en innovatief bedrijf met oog én zorg voor de samenleving, onze klanten en onze medewerkers. We willen duurzame relaties aangaan met mensen die bij ons passen, die onze normen en waarden delen. Bij Simac gaan we technologische uitdagingen aan in teamverband, wat zorgt voor de beste oplossing voor onze klanten. Dit is de Simac manier van werken. Uiteraard krijg je van ons het nodige terug: Een aantrekkelijk salaris tussen de € 5.485,- en € 10.353,- passend bij de kennis en ervaring die je met je meebrengt; Een telefoon en laptop van de zaak; Een leaseauto van de zaak óf mobiliteitsvergoeding; Een variabele bonus van max 1 maandsalaris; 26 vakantiedagen, met de mogelijkheid om dagen bij te kopen; 8% vakantiegeld; Een bijdrage van 8% aan je pensioen van door Simac, wat je daarnaast afdraagt bepaal je zelf; De mogelijkheid tot deelname aan ons vitaliteitsprogramma ‘Simac Fit & Vitaal’ waarmee je gebruik kunt maken van o.a. een nieuwe gym op ons kantoor in Veldhoven en verschillende programma’s voor mentaal en fysiek welzijn; De mogeijkheid om een goede thuiswerkplek in te richten; De mogelijkheid tot deelname aan onze collectieve zorgverzekering; De mogelijkheid om hybride en flexibel te werken; Een internet- en thuiswerkvergoeding; Een prettige werkomgeving bij een warm familiebedrijf waar mens en technologie centraal staan; Werken in een schitterend nieuw en modern kantoor in Veldhoven dat is voorzien van de nieuwste technologische snufjes; Extra verlof bij langdurig dienstverband; Een werkgever die investeert in jouw kennis en opleiding. Een assessment maakt onderdeel uit van de selectieprocedure. Daarnaast zal er bij indiensttreding een VOG worden gevraagd. title: Business Analyst SAP (H/F)
description: Leader de l’Ingénierie et des IT Services, présent dans plus de 30 pays, le groupe ALTEN accompagne la stratégie de développement de ses clients dans les domaines de l’innovation, de la R&D et des systèmes d'information technologiques. Plus qu’un poste, découvrez l’aventure qui vous attend au sein d’une entreprise engagée. Description du poste En tant que Business Analyst SAP, vous serez responsable de l'analyse, de la conception et de la mise en œuvre de solutions SAP pour améliorer nos processus métiers. Vous travaillerez en étroite collaboration avec les différentes équipes opérationnelles pour identifier les besoins, proposer des solutions adaptées et assurer leur déploiement efficace. Vos principales responsabilités seront : Analyser les processus métiers existants et identifier les opportunités d'amélioration via des solutions SAP Collaborer avec les parties prenantes pour recueillir et documenter les exigences fonctionnelles Concevoir, configurer et tester des solutions SAP répondant aux besoins identifiés Assurer la formation et le support aux utilisateurs finaux Participer à la gestion de projets liés à l'implémentation ou à la mise à jour de modules SAP Veiller à la conformité des solutions avec les standards et les meilleures pratiques de nos clients dans le secteur de l'aéronautique Qualifications Diplôme universitaire en informatique, en gestion ou dans un domaine connexe. Expérience significative en tant qu'analyste business ou consultant SAP, idéalement dans le secteur aéronautique ou industriel. Maîtrise des modules SAP pertinents pour les processus industriels et de gestion. Excellentes compétences en communication et capacité à travailler en équipe. Esprit analytique et orienté vers la résolution de problèmes. Maîtrise de l'anglais et du français, tant à l'oral qu'à l'écrit. Informations supplémentaires Vous hésitez encore et vous demandez pourquoi vous et pas un autre ? Rejoindre ALTEN, c'est la promesse de participer, en France comme à l'International, aux projets technologiques les plus innovants. Sachez que chez ALTEN nous vous offrons : L’opportunité de travailler pour une entreprise soucieuse d’une démarche RSE ; Un emploi sécurisant en CDI auprès du leader dans les métiers de l’ingénierie et R&D ; Une écoute active et des solutions personnalisées grâce à nos chargés de recrutements et business managers ; Une gestion de carrière et des formations pour monter en compétences ; Une forte cohésion d’équipe avec des Team Buildings réguliers. Prêt à relever des défis technologiques en rejoignant un collectif motivant ? Plus qu’un poste, découvrez l’aventure qui vous attend au sein d’une entreprise engagée. Vous êtes ici chez vous ! title: SAP System Specialist (m/w/d)
description: Das sind Ihre Aufgaben: Technische SAP-Koordination: Du übernimmst die interne technische Betreuung und Koordination unseres SAP S/4HANA Systems und stellst Stabilität, Wartung sowie die kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung sicher. Steuerung von SAP Basis Aktivitäten: Du koordinierst und steuerst SAP Basis Themen wie Updates, Systemänderungen und Zertifikate in enger Zusammenarbeit mit internen sowie externen Partnern. Technischer Ansprechpartner: Du agierst als zentrale Ansprechperson für SAP-nahe Fragestellungen innerhalb der IT sowie gegenüber den Fachbereichen. Anforderungs- und Problemmanagement: Du analysierst, priorisierst und verfolgst technische Anforderungen und Problemstellungen im SAP-Umfeld strukturiert nach. SAP Entwicklung: Du bringst deine ABAP-Erfahrung aktiv ein und arbeitest an der Umsetzung sowie Weiterentwicklung unserer SAP Anwendungen mit. Das bringen Sie mit: Erfahrung in der Betreuung, Einführung oder Weiterentwicklung eines SAP- oder vergleichbaren ERP-Systems (z. B. ECC, S/4HANA, BW) Fundiertes technisches Verständnis von SAP-Systemlandschaften sowie Basis-nahen Prozessen Ausgeprägte Kommunikations- und Koordinationsfähigkeiten im Umgang mit internen Teams und externen IT-Partnern Strukturierte, eigenverantwortliche und lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise mit hohem Verantwortungsbewusstsein (Ownership) Idealerweise Erfahrung im ABAP-Umfeld zur technischen Bewertung und Unterstützung von Entwicklungen Standort: Wangen i.A. title: Staff Systems Configurator, SAP Functional Analyst
description: Job Description General Motors is undergoing a major transformation, both in how we operate and in how we will influence the future of transportation. Our Software Services team is on the forefront of this work, emboldening our culture by seeking out determined, innovative individuals who will join our team to help move us forward and achieve our mission. The General Motors Global IT Organization is seeking to identify highly motivated and qualified individuals for potential future opportunities as a Staff Systems Configurator – SAP Functional Analyst (Level 8 individual contributor) to design, configure, and support SAP solutions that enable our end-to-end value chain across: Source-to-Deliver / Source-to-Pay (Supply Chain & Direct Material Sourcing and Procurement for Automotive) Make-to-Ship (Manufacturing & Plant Operations) Order-to-Delivery (Order Management & Distribution) This requisition is intentionally broad across SAP specialties, including but not limited to SD, FI-CO, MM, PP, WM, EWM, Ariba Direct Material Sourcing and Procurement for Automotive, and S/4HANA. You do not need experience in all of the areas listed; we are seeking strong SAP talent across one or more of these modules and encourage candidates to apply even if their expertise is focused in specific domains. This role is scoped as a Level 8 expert individual contributor in the Systems Configurator job family, leading complex, multi-team SAP initiatives and setting functional direction in partnership with Senior (Level 7) Systems Configurators. Location: Warren, MI or Austin, TX The Role As an SAP Functional Analyst, you will collaborate with cross-functional teams in Product Lifecycle Management, Global Purchasing & Supply Chain (GPSC), Manufacturing, Logistics, and Order Management to gather requirements and to design, configure, and support SAP solutions that meet business needs across the Source-to-Deliver, Make-to-Ship, and Order-to-Delivery spaces. You will implement scalable SAP processes, ensure solutions align with industry best practices and GM’s IT Solution Delivery Methodology, optimize business operations, support and extend SAP modules, drive process improvements through automation and standardization, and help design and build new SAP-based landscapes that transform how work is done today. At the staff level, you will also own the functional roadmap for your domains, lead design decisions that span multiple product teams, and act as the primary advisor to leaders on SAP capability and process strategy. You will: Translate business needs into robust, integrated SAP configurations and functional designs. Optimize business operations in areas such as Order Management, Pricing, Billing, Supply Chain, Logistics, Direct Material Sourcing & Procurement, Purchasing, Production Planning & Execution, Warehousing, and Master Data. Support GM’s journey to S/4HANA and SaaS-first solutions, helping rationalize and modernize the SAP and surrounding application landscape. Define and champion SAP configuration standards, patterns, and guardrails for Senior (Level 7) and mid-level Systems Configurators across your functional domains. Provide final functional sign-off for high-risk or high-impact changes, balancing stability, scalability, and speed of delivery. What You’ll Do Lead SAP configuration and solution design for core business processes such as Order to Cash, Procure to Pay, Production Planning & Execution, and related workflows. This includes ownership of complex, cross-functional initiatives that span multiple SAP products, regions, or business units. Oversee financial postings, cost allocations, and supplier collaboration as they relate to your SAP functional domains. Support sourcing, procurement, supplier management, production planning, logistics, inventory control, warehouse management, and goods movement processes. Collaborate with business stakeholders, analysts, and technical teams to gather requirements and deliver SAP solutions that are scalable, secure, and aligned with GM standards. Influence prioritization and trade-offs across teams to ensure solutions advance key GM objectives, not just local optimizations. Participate in testing, validation, and deployment of SAP enhancements and new functionalities; ensure strong end-to-end process validation. Drive the overall test strategy for your domains and ensure appropriate use of automation and regression testing. Support integration of SAP with external systems, partnering with integration and infrastructure teams. Help evaluate and rationalize the existing manufacturing, supply chain, and order management application landscape, determining which solutions are integrated with, absorbed into, or retired in favor of SAP S/4HANA and related SAP SaaS solutions. Participate in SAP upgrades, migrations, and digital transformation initiatives, including S/4HANA and cloud/SaaS adoption. Serve as a key decision-maker on fit-to-standard vs. extension, aligned with GM’s clean core strategy. Apply standard SAP functionality to meet business needs, minimizing custom development while still addressing critical gaps via RICEFW objects. Contribute to application management and production support, including issue triage, root-cause analysis, and continuous improvement in your functional areas. Act as the top functional escalation point for complex incidents and systemic issues. Create and maintain process documentation, configuration guides, and training materials to support adoption and reuse. Mentor junior team members and promote best practices in SAP solution delivery, testing, and operations. Systematically coach Senior Systems Configurators and other team members, building depth and succession in critical SAP domains. Adhere to organizational IT standards, security protocols, and industry best practices, including relevant regulatory and internal control requirements. Proactively identify control gaps and drive remediation in partnership with Risk, Compliance, and Audit teams. Your Skills & Abilities (Required Qualifications) Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, Business Computing, Information Systems, Supply Chain) or equivalent experience. Extensive hands-on SAP experience, including configuration of one or more relevant modules, for example: SD Order Management, Pricing, Billing, Order-to-Cash. FI-CO integration with Supply Chain, Manufacturing, or Order Management. MM, WM, EWM procurement, inventory, warehouse, and logistics execution. PP / Manufacturing production planning and execution, plan-to-produce. DMe/DMC SAP Digital Manufacturing or other digital manufacturing solutions, and integration with plant-floor and logistics systems. Ariba Direct Material Sourcing and Procurement for Automotive. S/4HANA functional configuration and extensibility. Proven track record leading end-to-end SAP solution delivery for complex programs (e.g., multi-release roadmaps, large deployments, S/4 migrations, or significant new capability introductions). Strong understanding of SAP principles and extensibility approaches, including how to design upgrade-safe, cloud-compliant solutions. Solid business process knowledge in areas such as Order Management, Pricing, Billing, Supply Chain, Logistics, Direct Material Sourcing & Procurement, Purchasing, Production Planning, Warehousing, and Master Data. Experience supporting integration with other SAP modules and external systems, including familiarity with common interface technologies (e.g., IDocs, APIs, middleware). Familiarity with software testing, automation, and quality assurance practices; ability to define and execute test plans and support automated/regression testing. Ability to debug and analyze SAP systems, preferably with some ABAP knowledge to support issue analysis and collaboration with development teams. Demonstrated analytical and problem-solving skills, with a structured approach to diagnosing issues and proposing options. Strong commitment to continuous improvement, teamwork, and integrity, including willingness to learn new SAP and cloud capabilities. Ability to set direction for a functional area, influence architecture and product roadmaps, and make sound trade-off decisions. What Will Give You a Competitive Edge (Preferred Qualifications) SAP certification in one or more relevant modules (e.g., SD, MM, PP, WM/EWM, FI-CO, Ariba, S/4HANA). Experience with SAP Automotive Suite or similar industry-specific solutions supporting OEMs, suppliers, or complex manufacturing. Familiarity with the SAP Integrated Tool Chain – including Signavio (process modeling), SAP Cloud ALM (CALM), and Tricentis (Tosca) – as well as SAP Fiori and SAP BTP, especially for cloud-based SAP extensions and integrations. Exposure to SAP AI / analytics capabilities and apply analytics/AI in manufacturing, supply chain, or order management use cases. Experience with EDI transaction sets (ANSI X12, EDIFACT) and related communication protocols, and/or other integration platforms (e.g., SAP PI/PO, SAP Integration Suite, Sterling, Connect Direct). Background working with manufacturers, suppliers, or service providers, ideally in automotive or complex discrete manufacturing environments. Prior consulting experience or working with global stakeholder groups, including experience in regulated environments (e.g., SOX, internal controls, legal/tax requirements). Demonstrated ability to represent your functional area in senior-level forums (e.g., steering committees, enterprise design councils) and to influence outcomes across organizations without direct authority. Hybrid This role is categorized as hybrid. The successful candidate is expected to report to Warren, MI or Austin, TX, three times per week, at minimum, or as dictated by business needs. GM does not provide immigration-related sponsorship for this role. Do not apply for this role if you will need GM immigration sponsorship now or in the future. This includes direct company sponsorship, entry of GM as the immigration employer of record on a government form, and any work authorization requiring a written submission or other immigration support from the company (e.g., H1-B, OPT, STEM OPT, CPT, TN, J-1, etc). This role is categorized as hybrid. This means the selected candidate is expected to report to a specific location at least 3 times a week {or other frequency dictated by their manager}. This job may be eligible for relocation benefits. About GM Our vision is a world with Zero Crashes, Zero Emissions and Zero Congestion and we embrace the responsibility to lead the change that will make our world better, safer and more equitable for all. Why Join Us We believe we all must make a choice every day – individually and collectively – to drive meaningful change through our words, our deeds and our culture. Every day, we want every employee to feel they belong to one General Motors team. Benefits Overview From day one, we're looking out for your well-being–at work and at home–so you can focus on realizing your ambitions. Learn how GM supports a rewarding career that rewards you personally by visiting Total Rewards resources . Non-Discrimination and Equal Employment Opportunities (U.S.) General Motors is committed to being a workplace that is not only free of unlawful discrimination, but one that genuinely fosters inclusion and belonging. We strongly believe that providing an inclusive workplace creates an environment in which our employees can thrive and develop better products for our customers. All employment decisions are made on a non-discriminatory basis without regard to sex, race, color, national origin, citizenship status, religion, age, disability, pregnancy or maternity status, sexual orientation, gender identity, status as a veteran or protected veteran, or any other similarly protected status in accordance with federal, state and local laws. We encourage interested candidates to review the key responsibilities and qualifications for each role and apply for any positions that match their skills and capabilities. Applicants in the recruitment process may be required, where applicable, to successfully complete a role-related assessment(s) and/or a pre-employment screening prior to beginning employment. To learn more, visit How we Hire . Accommodations General Motors offers opportunities to all job seekers including individuals with disabilities. If you need a reasonable accommodation to assist with your job search or application for employment, email us or call us at 1-800-865-7580. In your email, please include a description of the specific accommodation you are requesting as well as the job title and requisition number of the position for which you are applying. title: Accounting Operations- 6 Month Contract (100% Remote US)
description: About The Role Hopper is hiring an Accounting Operations (Contractor) to own our monthly partner billing cycle end-to-end and provide broader finance operations support across a 6-month engagement, with potential to extend. This is a hands-on, execution-focused role sitting at the intersection of partner relationships, financial operations, and data integrity. If you're someone who thrives on process ownership and running a tight billing cycle without being managed step-by-step, this role is built for you. What Would Your Day-to-day Look Like Run the full monthly partner billing cycle — gathering partner-specific inputs, generating reports using existing tooling, and coordinating internal review and approval through to close Own partner-facing communication, including reviewing and sending billing emails, and responding to partner inquiries directly Triage and investigate data discrepancies, resolving what you can independently and escalating pipeline issues with clear documentation Create, issue, and track partner invoices in NetSuite, match POs to invoices, and confirm remittances are received and applied Complete vendor and customer setup for new partner onboarding, including collecting required documentation, tax forms, and banking details Support month-end close through account reconciliations and journal entry preparation Maintain process documentation and assist with external audit requests as needed Take on ad hoc accounting and finance operations projects as they come up An ideal candidate has 3+ years in billing, accounts receivable, or finance operations, with a track record of running recurring cycles independently Hands-on NetSuite AR and billing experience Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage a deadline-driven monthly cycle without day-to-day oversight Clear, professional written communication — you'll be a direct point of contact for external partners A methodical reconciliation mindset, comfortable chasing down discrepancies across multiple data sources SQL or BigQuery familiarity to investigate data questions before escalating (nice to have) Experience at a high-growth technology, marketplace, or travel company (nice to have) Familiarity with revenue share, commission, or variable partner billing structures (nice to have) Perks And Benefits Of Working With Us Well-funded and proven startup with large ambitions, competitive salary and the upsides of pre-IPO equity packages. Unlimited PTO. Entrepreneurial culture where pushing limits and taking risks is everyday business. Open communication with management and company leadership. Small, dynamic teams = massive impact. Contractors will submit invoices and be paid on a monthly basis through Coupa. Please be aware that because this is an independent contractor engagement, the position is not eligible for standard HTS employee perks, healthcare benefits, or equity. More About Hopper At Hopper, we are on a mission to become the leading travel platform globally – powering Hopper’s mobile app, website and our B2B business, HTS (Hopper Technology Solutions). By leveraging massive amounts of data and advanced machine learning algorithms, Hopper combines its world-class travel agency offering with proprietary fintech products to bring transparency, flexibility and savings to travelers globally. We have developed several unique fintech solutions that address everything from pricing volatility to trip disruptions – helping people travel better and save more on their trips. The Hopper platform serves hundreds of millions of travelers globally and continues to capture market share around the world. The Hopper app has been downloaded over 120 million times and has become largely popular among younger travelers – with 70% of its users being Gen Z and millennials. While everyone knows us as the Gen Z and Millennial travel app, Hopper has evolved to become much more than that. In recent years, we’ve grown into a travel fintech provider, commerce platform, and global travel agency that powers some of the world’s largest brands. Through HTS, our B2B division, the company supercharges its partners’ direct channels by integrating our fintech products on their sites or powering end-to-end travel portals. Today, our partners include leading travel brands like Capital One, Nubank, Air Canada, and many more. Here are just a few stats that demonstrate the company’s recent growth: Billions of dollars worth of travel and travel fintech are sold through Hopper and HTS’ channels every year. Our fintech products – including Cancel for Any Reason and Flight Disruption Assistance – have exceptionally strong CSAT because the terms are always clear, and customers receive instant, no-questions-asked resolutions. Almost 30% of our app customers purchase at least one fintech product when making a booking; and consumers are 1.6x more likely to repurchase if they add fintech to their booking vs if they booked just travel. Given the success of its fintech products, Hopper launched a B2B initiative, HTS (Hopper Technology Solutions), which represents more than 75% of the business. Through HTS, any travel provider (airlines, hotels, banks, travel agencies, etc.) can integrate and seamlessly distribute Hopper’s fintech or travel inventory on their direct channels. As its first HTS partnership, the company partnered with Capital One to co-develop Capital One Travel, a new travel portal designed specifically for cardholders. Other HTS partners include Air Canada, Uber, CommBank, Nubank, Flair Airlines and many more. Hopper has been named the #1 most innovative company in travel by Fast Company. We have raised over $750 million USD of private capital and is backed by some of the largest institutional investors and banks in the world. HTS is primed to continue its growth as the leading travel ecommerce provider in a $1 trillion online shopping category. The Hopper app and website will also continue to be the preferred travel provider for Gen Z and Millennials. Come take off with us! At Hopper, we leverage AI-driven tools to assist our team in efficiently reviewing applications. These tools help us assess qualifications fairly and objectively. By submitting your application, you acknowledge the use of these automated screening technologies. title: Senior SAP Developer
description: Senior SAP Developer. Description Under general direction, instructs, directs, and checks the work of other project engineers. Responsible for developing strategies for technical IT infrastructures and the completion of assigned engineering projects within budgetary and scheduling guidelines. Leads a group of engineers, analysts, and/or technicians assigned for the duration of a project or may function as ongoing lead within a group of engineers associated with one or more technical areas within the telecom function (such as, but not limited to, network design, engineering, implementation, or operations/user support). Does not have formal supervisory responsibilities, although may provide input to performance appraisals. Proficient In The Following Qualifications Perform functional analysis, requirements, definition and SAP module configuration and testing. Ability to develop functional requirements specifications, data mappings, function mapping, setting of configuration tables and setting of transaction/control tables. Ability to coordinate projects across different organizations understanding of the business processes and flows to enable the analysis of business requirements and the development of solutions for the business functions Strong knowledge in SAP HCM HR modules including OM/PA, eCareer, EHS ect. HR Development experience RICEF, InfoType, Logical Databases, OM/PA, etc. ABAP HR Process and Forms SAP Fiori Knowledge In SAP PO S/4 HANA HR Security Fiori Architect Design Architecture Experience - LaunchPad Design, design control-based solutions Familiarity with Agile/Scrum processes and management tools such as VersionOne. Knowledge of and experience in an IT Service management System (preferably ServiceNow) Additional Skills/Experience A minimum of ten (10) years' experience in software development, database administration, and/or related field. A degree from an accredited College/University in Software Engineering, Computer Science, or related discipline is preferred. Able to perform all functional duties independently on high-visibility programs, or mission critical aspects of a given program. Able to oversee the efforts of direct reporting resources and/or be responsible for the efforts of all staff assigned to a specific job. Experience training/mentoring less experienced personnel. Extensive knowledge of and experience with designing, implementing, operating and administering relational DBMSs. Development/support experience with more than one DBMS product (e.g. Oracle, SQL Server). Extensive knowledge of/experience with DBMS Job Control Language (JCL), access methods, performance tuning, device allocation, validation checks, organization, security, documentation and statistical methods. Without assistance, capable of recommending the most appropriate DBMS product after evaluating a set of system requirements. Extensive experience producing database-related documentation such as administration policies, procedures, standards and guidelines. Excellent communication skills. Additional Provisions Pass both a client mandated clearance process to include drug screening, criminal history check and credit check. All candidates must be a US Citizen or have permanent residence status (Green Card). Candidate must have lived in the US for the past 5 years. Cannot have more than 6 months travel outside the United States within the last five years. Military Service excluded. (Exception does not include military family members.) title: Implementierungsberater - Ultimo
description: About Ultimo Ultimo is a leading provider of Enterprise Asset Management (EAM) software for organisations where physical assets are mission-critical to operations. Our platform supports maintenance, inspections, compliance, safety, and asset lifecycle management across complex environments — from utilities and energy providers to logistics networks and industrial businesses. In these industries, uptime, reliability, and operational control are not optional — they are fundamental. Ultimo is part of IFS , combining the stability and global scale of a leading enterprise software group with the agility and entrepreneurial culture of a focused, product-led organisation. Today, Ultimo operates with strong foundations: a proven product, long-standing customer relationships, and a clear ambition to continue raising the bar in how asset-intensive industries operate. Stellenbeschreibung Warum diese Rolle und warum jetzt? Unternehmen stehen zunehmend unter Druck, die Effizienz ihrer Anlagen zu steigern und Kosten zu reduzieren. Ultimo wächst stark, um diese Nachfrage zu bedienen. Dafür suchen wir erfahrene Consultants (m/w/d), die Kunden bei der Einführung unserer EAM-Plattform begleiten und einen echten Mehrwert generieren. Diese Rolle ist klar auf Projektumsetzung und Kundenerfolg ausgerichtet – mit direktem Einfluss auf Prozesse und Ergebnisse beim Kunden. Ihre Aufgaben Analyse & Beratung Durchführung von Workshops zur Aufnahme und Optimierung von Asset- und Instandhaltungsprozessen Beratung zu Best Practices im EAM-/CMMS-Umfeld Umsetzung Übersetzung von Geschäftsanforderungen in funktionierende Systemlösungen Konfiguration von Workflows, Datenstrukturen, Schnittstellen und Reports in Ultimo Projektsteuerung Verantwortung für Implementierungsprojekte von der Analyse bis zur Produktivsetzung Steuerung mehrerer Projekte parallel unter Einhaltung von Zeit, Qualität und Budget Enablement Schulung von Anwendern und Key Usern Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Produkt-, Support- und Consulting-Teams Weiterentwicklung von Methoden und Best Practices Warum Ultimo? Arbeit an praxisnahen Digitalisierungs- und Transformationsprojekten Direkter Einfluss auf den Erfolg unserer Kunden Wachsendes, internationales SaaS-Unternehmen Flexible Arbeitszeiten und Möglichkeit zum mobilen Arbeiten Strukturierte Einarbeitung und gezielte Weiterentwicklung (z. B. Trainings, Zertifizierungen) Klare Entwicklungs- und interne Weiterbildungsperspektiven Attraktives Gehaltspaket Arbeiten in einem kollaborativen, internationalen Teamumfeld Qualifikationen Ihr Profil Mindestens 3 Jahre Erfahrung in der Implementierung von EAM-/CMMS-Lösungen (z. B. Ultimo, SAP PM, IBM Maximo, IFS) Erfahrung in einer projektorientierten oder beratenden Rolle Fachkenntnisse in Instandhaltung, Asset Management, Fleet Management oder Reliability Sie setzen auf Innovation und KI-gestützte Optimierung und sind neugierig darauf, wie neue Technologien im Laufe der Zeit die Leistungserbringung, die Entscheidungsfindung und den Kundennutzen verbessern können Fähigkeit, Geschäftsanforderungen in technische Lösungen zu übersetzen Sicheres Auftreten im Umgang mit Kunden und Stakeholdern Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse (mind. C1) und Englischkenntnisse (mind. B2) Selbstständige Arbeitsweise und hohe Kundenorientierung Reisebereitschaft innerhalb Europas (ca. 20–40 %) Bereit für den nächsten Schritt? Sie möchten Projekte mit sichtbarem Impact umsetzen und Kunden nachhaltig voranbringen? Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre Bewerbung. title: Lead – ERP
description: Marmon Technologies India Private Limited As a part of the global industrial organization Marmon Holdings—which is backed by Berkshire Hathaway—you’ll be doing things that matter, leading at every level, and winning a better way. We’re committed to making a positive impact on the world, providing you with diverse learning and working opportunities, and fostering a culture where everyone’s empowered to be their best. The SAP Lead – Supply Chain Planning provides business process analysis, solution design, and application support across end-to-end planning capabilities in SAP S/4HANA and SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP). This role partners directly with Supply Chain business stakeholders to document requirements, identify process and system gaps, propose options (including proof-of-concept where appropriate), and implement sustainable solutions. Responsibilities include supporting and enhancing planning processes in S/4HANA (including MRP, demand/independent requirements, consumption strategies, and related master data/process integration) and the full IBP solution footprint (e.g., demand, supply, inventory, response & supply, and integrated planning), leveraging modern SAP user experiences and tools such as SAP Fiori and SAP Joule where applicable. Core Skills & Expertise SAP IBP modules: Demand, Supply, Inventory, S&OP IP configuration: planning areas, key figures, heuristics, optimizers Integration: CI-DS, RTI, CIF, CPI-DS for IBP ↔ S/4HANA data flows S/4HANA: MRP, master data, process synchronization Analytics: SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), IBP Excel Add-In, KPI modeling (MAPE, DoS, OTIF) APO to IBP migration experience (preferred) SAP Fiori for MRP & planning execution (MRP Cockpit apps): Monitor/Manage Material Coverage (F0247A/F2101A/F0251A), Monitor Internal/External Requirements (F0263/F0246), Schedule MRP Runs (F1339), Display MRP Issues & Key Figures (F1425/F1426) Fiori launchpad & embedded analytics for planners: KPI cards, analytical lists, variants/filters, and navigation from exceptions to resolution apps (e.g., reschedule/cancel proposals within Manage Material Coverage) Industry experience in regulated environments (preferred) Duties/Responsibilities SAP Lead – Supply Chain Planning (SAP S/4HANA & IBP) Serve as functional lead/SME for supply chain planning across SAP S/4HANA and SAP IBP, owning day-to-day support, continuous improvement, and the planning roadmap. Partner with Supply Chain Planning, Purchasing, Manufacturing, and Distribution stakeholders to elicit/document requirements, facilitate workshops, and translate needs into functional designs and system solutions. Design, configure, test, and deploy planning solutions across: S/4HANA planning (MRP, demand/independent requirements, consumption strategies, planning parameters, and planning master data / process integration). SAP IBP footprint (demand, supply, inventory, response & supply, integrated planning), including planning areas, master data integration, and planning operators. Perform gap analysis against standard SAP functionality; propose solution options and recommend scalable, best-practice-aligned approaches (including proof-of-concept where appropriate). Lead end-to-end delivery of enhancements/projects: functional specifications, configuration, unit/system testing, defect triage, cutover planning, post-go-live support, and knowledge transfer. Support integrations and interfaces for planning (IBP ↔ S/4HANA and related flows), partnering with technical teams on data mapping, monitoring, error handling, and performance/stability improvements. Promote modern SAP user experiences and tools (SAP Fiori apps, embedded analytics, SAP Joule where applicable) to improve usability, adoption, and decision-making. Provide incident and request support (break/fix): troubleshooting, root-cause analysis, prevention actions; document solutions and maintain support runbooks. Develop and maintain functional documentation (process flows, configuration rationale, training materials); support user training, UAT coordination, and change management. Work effectively with global stakeholders across US and EU time zones as part of a global SAP Center of Excellence; act as long-term functional owner accountable for solution health, roadmap evolution, and planning capability maturity. Qualifications SAP Lead – Supply Chain Planning (SAP S/4HANA & IBP) BS/BA in Information Systems, Supply Chain, Business, Computer Science, or related field (or equivalent experience). 2.7+ years of progressive SAP experience, including senior-level functional ownership for supply chain planning processes and solutions. Strong SAP S/4HANA planning experience (MRP, demand/independent requirements, consumption strategies, planning parameters, and planning-relevant master data/process integration). Hands-on SAP IBP experience across demand/supply/inventory/response & supply/integrated planning, including planning areas, master data & integration concepts, and planning operators. Implementation delivery experience (2+ full lifecycle programs preferred) spanning design, build, test, cutover, and hypercare for S/4HANA and/or IBP. Deep IBP configuration skills (planning areas, key figures, attributes, time profiles, forecast models, heuristics, optimizer-based planning) plus analytics enablement using SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and the IBP Excel Add-In. Proven consulting/BA toolkit: requirements elicitation, process mapping, gap analysis, functional specifications, and leading workshops; able to propose solution options that balance standard SAP capabilities with business needs. Strong communication and analytical/troubleshooting skills with a continuous improvement mindset; experience with modern SAP UX/enablement tools (SAP Fiori, embedded analytics; exposure to SAP Joule or similar AI-assisted capabilities preferred). Core Skills SAP IBP (Demand, Supply, Inventory, S&OP) IBP configuration (planning areas, key figures, heuristics, optimizers) Integration (CI-DS, RTI, CIF, CPI-DS) S/4HANA (MRP, master data) SAP Fiori planning apps (MRP Cockpit): Monitor/Manage Material Coverage, Monitor Internal/External Requirements, Schedule MRP Runs, Display MRP Issues/Key Figures Analytics (SAC, IBP Excel Add-In, KPI modeling) Methodology (SAP Activate, Agile) Responsibilities Lead IBP design/configuration Own IBP ↔ S/4HANA integration/data flows Enable analytics/KPI reporting Deliver via SAP Activate/Agile OE roadmap ownership Must-Have Qualifications 7-11 years SAP IBP/S/4 planning Integration tool expertise SAC/Excel Add-In analytics Strong stakeholder skills Preferred APO migration SAP certification Regulated industry experience Fit Ideal for candidates from Big-4, SAP partners, or global delivery centers with hands-on IBP configuration and integration experience. Not a pure AMS support role—requires solution ownership and roadmap maturity. Following receipt of a conditional offer of employment, candidates will be required to complete additional job-related screening processes as permitted or required by applicable law. title: Consultant, Functional Transformation SAP
description: Our Deloitte Enterprise Performance team is at the forefront of enterprise technology, working across finance, supply chain, and IT operations to deliver holistic performance improvement and digital transformation. Join our team of strategic advisers and architects, differentiated by our industry depth to collaborate with leading solution providers and leverage your experience in strategy, process design, technology enablement, and operational services to enable heart-of-the-business solutions. Recruiting for this role ends on 08/31/2026. Work you'll do As a Consultant, Functional Transformation on the GPS SAP business transformation team, you will be responsible for supporting SAP-enabled transformation initiatives across the implementation lifecycle. Support SAP implementation and modernization efforts, including SAP S/4HANA programs and ECC-to-S/4HANA transformation activities. Configure SAP functionality to support business process requirements and fit-to-standard design decisions. Document requirements, assumptions, risks, issues, and decisions in collaboration with business, technical, integration, and data teams. Support process design workshops, integrated testing, and deployment activities, including unit testing, system integration testing, user acceptance testing, cutover, and stabilization. Develop, maintain, and govern implementation deliverables, including solution design documents, configuration rationale, test scripts, cutover inputs, deployment readiness materials, and status updates. A successful candidate would possess these skills: Ability to work independently and collaborate as part of a team Effective written and verbal communication skills Meticulous attention to detail and quality of work product Ability to build and sustain professional relationships Ability to lead projects or workstreams Ability to manage and prioritize multiple tasks in a fast-paced and dynamic environment Strong interpersonal skills and professional demeanor Ability to meet deadlines Ability to provide clear guidance to others The team Deloitte's Government & Public Services (GPS) practice - our people, ideas, technology and outcomes - is designed for impact. Serving federal, state, & local government clients as well as public higher education institutions, our team of professionals brings fresh perspective to help clients anticipate disruption, reimagine the possible, and fulfill their mission promise. Our SAP business transformation services drive to improve performance and value delivered by the full suite of SAP solutions. Qualifications Required: Bachelor's degree and ability to obtain and maintain a Secret Clearance 2+ years of experience working in regulated environments with enterprise resource planning controls, audit readiness, and compliance requirements 2+ years of experience producing or governing implementation deliverables, including solution design documentation, configuration rationale, test artifacts, cutover plans, and deployment readiness materials 1+ years of experience delivering SAP implementations, including SAP S/4HANA transformation work 1+ years of experience configuring SAP S/4HANA and/or SAP ECC Ability to travel 25%, on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sectors you serve. Must be legally authorized to work in the United States without the need for employer sponsorship, now or at any time in the future. Preferred: Active Secret Clearance Experience supporting Department of Defense and/or U.S. Federal civilian agency enterprise resource planning modernization programs The wage range for this role takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; licensure and certifications; and other business and organizational needs. The disclosed range estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the position may be filled. At Deloitte, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is $82,600 to $162,800. You may also be eligible to participate in a discretionary annual incentive program, subject to the rules governing the program, whereby an award, if any, depends on various factors, including, without limitation, individual and organizational performance. title: Teamlead SAP Tech / Integration (m/w/d)
description: Gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft in der Bauindustrie! Übernehmen Sie als Teamlead SAP Tech / Integration (m/w/d) eine zentrale Rolle in unserem globalen SAP-Team und verantworten Sie die technische Stabilität, Integration und Weiterentwicklung unserer SAP-Landschaft. Ihr Profil – individuell und ausgeprägt Sie verfügen über ein abgeschlossenes Studium der Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation. Sehr fundierte Erfahrung in der SAP Basis Administration bringen Sie ebenso mit wie ein sicheres Gespür für stabile und leistungsfähige Systemlandschaften. Auch im Umfeld von SAP BTP konnten Sie bereits praktische Erfahrung sammeln. Ihr breites technisches Verständnis von SAP-Architekturen und Integrationskonzepten ermöglicht es Ihnen, Zusammenhänge schnell zu erfassen und tragfähige Lösungen zu entwickeln. In der disziplinarischen Führung von Mitarbeitenden verfügen Sie über mehrjährige Erfahrung. Eine analytische, strukturierte und verantwortungsbewusste Arbeitsweise ist für Sie selbstverständlich. Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse runden Ihr Profil ab. Ihre Aufgaben – abwechslungsreich und wichtig Sie führen das SAP Tech-/Integrations-Team disziplinarisch und entwickeln es gezielt weiter. Für die SAP Basis Administration übernehmen Sie die fachliche Verantwortung und gewährleisten einen stabilen, leistungsfähigen und sicheren Betrieb der Systemlandschaft. Die SAP BTP gestalten Sie technisch und organisatorisch mit und verantworten Architektur, Governance, Subaccounts sowie relevante Services. Unsere Integrations- und Schnittstellenarchitekturen steuern Sie aktiv und entwickeln diese im Sinne einer zukunftsfähigen SAP-Landschaft kontinuierlich weiter. In enger Abstimmung mit Security, Infrastruktur und Architektur schaffen Sie belastbare technische Lösungen. Ebenso übernehmen Sie die technische Leitung von SAP-Projekten und stehen Fachbereichen sowie Projektteams als kompetente Ansprechperson beratend zur Seite. Im Tagesgeschäft verantworten Sie außerdem das Incident-, Problem- und Eskalationsmanagement im SAP-Umfeld. Die Zusammenarbeit mit externen technischen Dienstleistern steuern Sie zielgerichtet und qualitätsbewusst. Willkommen in der Welt von ARDEX! „Unsere Leidenschaft ist die Entwicklung verantwortungsvoller Lösungen für die Bauindustrie!“ ARDEX ist einer der Weltmarktführer bei hochwertigen bauchemischen Spezialbaustoffen. Als Gesellschaft in Familiensitz verfolgt das Unternehmen seit 75 Jahren einen nachhaltigen Wachstumskurs. Weltweit beschäftigt die ARDEX-Gruppe ca. 4.000 Mitarbeitende, am Hauptsitz in Witten sind es ca. 500 Beschäftigte. ARDEX ist heute in mehr als 50 Ländern auf allen Kontinenten präsent, im Kernmarkt Europa nahezu flächendeckend und erwirtschaftet mit fast zwanzig großen Marken weltweit einen Gesamtumsatz von mehr als 1.110 Millionen Euro. Was uns besonders macht: Bei ARDEX erwartet Sie eine familiäre Arbeitsatmosphäre, in der gegenseitige Unterstützung großgeschrieben wird. Wir legen Wert auf ein respektvolles Miteinander, kurze Wege und ein starkes Teamgefühl – denn gemeinsam erreichen wir mehr. Das bieten wir Ihnen Flexible Arbeitszeiten Homeoffice-Möglichkeiten Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten 30 Urlaubstage Betriebliche Altersvorsorge Betriebsarzt Kantine Jobrad Teamevents Urlaubs- und Weihnachtsgeld Mitarbeiterrabatte Tarifvertrag Ihr Einstieg bei ARDEX Sie möchten das Handwerk mit bauchemischen Spezialbaustoffen versorgen und mit Innovationen in der Bauchemie überzeugen? Sie blühen bei Teamarbeit so richtig auf? Sie brennen für Ihren Beruf und wagen sich aus der eigenen Komfortzone? Sie möchten Ihre und unsere Zukunft aktiv mitgestalten? Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre Bewerbung! So läuft Ihr Bewerbungsprozess ab Bewerbung: Sie schicken uns Ihre Unterlagen – wir melden uns schnellstmöglich zurück. title: SAP Configuration Specialist
description: Drive Accurate Product Configuration & Global Compliance – Join PRC‑Saltillo as an SAP Configuration Specialist! At PRC‑Saltillo, accurate product configuration and data integrity are essential to delivering compliant, high‑quality products worldwide. We’re seeking an SAP Configuration Specialist to manage SAP Material Master data and SAP Variant Configuration (LO‑VC) models that support global manufacturing, regulatory compliance, and scalable product offerings. In this role, you’ll collaborate with cross‑functional teams to support new product introductions, engineering changes, and continuous SAP improvements. If you’re detail‑oriented, analytical, and enjoy translating complex business requirements into effective SAP configurations, this is an opportunity to make a meaningful impact in a mission‑driven organization. Go to our website to apply https://prentkeromich.bamboohr.com/careers Why You’ll Love Working Here Mission Driven Impact : Support systems that enable compliant, high-quality products for customers worldwide. Cross Functional Collaboration : Partner with Engineering, Supply Chain, Regulatory, Manufacturing, and IT teams. Hands On SAP Ownership : Play a key role in SAP Material Master and Variant Configuration strategy and execution. Continuous Improvement Focus : Contribute to ongoing SAP optimization and process improvements. Duties and Responsibilities Maintain and govern SAP Material Master data across multiple plants and company codes. Design, configure, and support SAP Variant Configuration (LO VC) models, including characteristics, classes, and object dependencies. Collaborate with cross functional stakeholders to gather requirements and translate them into effective SAP configurations. Ensure data integrity and system consistency through validation, auditing, and data cleansing processes. Configure products within SAP based on international sales locations to ensure regulatory and market compliance. Support new product introductions and engineering changes by updating Material Master data and VC models. Education and Experience Bachelor’s degree in information systems, Engineering, Supply Chain, or a related field required. Minimum of three years of hands-on experience with SAP Material Master and Variant Configuration (LO VC), or equivalent ERP system experience required. Strong understanding of SAP modules such as SD, MM, PP, and PLM or comparable ERP modules required. At least three years of experience working with BOMs, routings, classification systems, and object dependencies required. Required Skills/Abilities Strong understanding of product structures, BOMs, and configuration dependencies. Ability to translate business and technical requirements into configuration logic and rules. Ability to analyze product structures, routings, and cost data to identify costing variations. Excellent problem-solving skills with strong attention to detail. Strong verbal, written, and interpersonal communication skills. Ability to work collaboratively across teams while managing complex data driven work independently. title: Expert SAP H/F
description: Description du poste Famille / Sous-famille d'emploi ISB - Business Application Devpt Type de contrat Contrat à durée indéterminée Intitulé du poste Expert SAP H/F Contexte du poste: Rattaché au Responsable ERP & Programmes ERP, l'Expert SAP Production, Qualité & Maintenance est le/la référent(e) fonctionnel SAP pour son domaine. Il/Elle est responsable de la cohérence fonctionnelle, de la performance opérationnelle et de l'évolution des solutions SAP supportant les activités industrielles du Groupe. Il/Elle intervient comme interlocuteur privilégié entre les métiers et les équipes informatiques sur l'ensemble des processus liés à la production, à la qualité et à la maintenance. Le poste s'inscrit dans un environnement industriel de chimie fine, spécialités chimiques et activités pharmaceutiques soumis à des exigences réglementaires strictes, notamment les Bonnes Pratiques de Fabrication (GMP/BPF), les exigences d'intégrité des données et les référentiels qualité applicables aux sites du Groupe. Il/Elle est garant: de la continuité des processus métiers couverts par SAP sur son périmètre, d’accompagner les évolutions fonctionnelles attendues par les métiers, de contribuer à la standardisation des pratiques Groupe de préparer les transformations futures du système d'information sur son périmètre. de s’assurer que les solutions déployées répondent aux besoins opérationnels tout en restant cohérentes avec les standards et la feuille de route de l'entreprise. Principales missions: Assurer la performance opérationnelle et le support des solutions SAP Garantir la disponibilité et la fiabilité des processus SAP de son domaine. Superviser le traitement des incidents, demandes de services et évolutions. Prioriser les actions en fonction des impacts métier et des risques opérationnels. Animer les instances de suivi avec les parties prenantes concernées. Assurer la coordination des partenaires externes intervenant sur le périmètre. Évaluer l'impact des incidents et évolutions sur la conformité GMP et les processus validés. Porter l’expertise fonctionnelle du domaine Maintenir une connaissance approfondie des processus de production, qualité et maintenance. Conseiller les métiers dans l'utilisation et l'évolution des solutions SAP. Identifier et promouvoir les bonnes pratiques au niveau Groupe. Participer à la définition, à l'amélioration et à la standardisation des processus métiers. Maîtriser les exigences réglementaires applicables aux processus de fabrication, de gestion de la qualité et de maintenance en environnement GMP. Projets et transformation Recueillir et qualifier les besoins d'évolution des métiers. Piloter ou contribuer aux projets liés à son domaine fonctionnel. Participer aux programmes de transformation du paysage ERP. Assurer la cohérence entre SAP et les autres applications industrielles ou qualité. Contribuer à la préparation des futures évolutions technologiques et organisationnelles. Participer aux activités de validation des systèmes informatisés (CSV/CSA) et aux analyses d'impact réglementaires des évolutions. S'assurer que les projets respectent les exigences GMP, Data Integrity et les standards qualité du Groupe. Gouvernance et qualité Garantir la qualité de la documentation fonctionnelle de son périmètre. Participer aux audits, inspections et revues périodiques des systèmes. Veiller au respect des exigences réglementaires et qualité applicables. Contribuer aux analyses de risques et aux activités de validation lorsque nécessaire. Maintenir la documentation nécessaire à la conformité des systèmes informatisés (spécifications, analyses de risques, matrices de traçabilité, protocoles et rapports de validation). Assurer le respect des processus de Change Control et de gestion des déviations applicables aux systèmes GMP. Données et performance Participer à l'amélioration continue de la qualité des données métiers. Contribuer à la mise à disposition des indicateurs de performance nécessaires au pilotage des activités. Accompagner les initiatives de digitalisation, d'automatisation et de valorisation de la donnée. Veiller à l'intégrité, à la fiabilité et à la traçabilité des données critiques GMP. Les atouts pour réussir: Bac +5 ou équivalent en informatique, ingénierie, supply chain ou industrie Expérience confirmée dans les environnements SAP industriels. Expérience des processus de production, qualité ou maintenance. Participation à des projets de déploiement ou d'évolution ERP. Expérience significative dans un environnement chimique, pharmaceutique, industriel soumis aux GMP/BPF. Expérience des activités de validation des systèmes informatisés (CSV/CSA) et des audits qualité fortement appréciée. Maîtrise de l'anglais professionnel permettant d'interagir avec des équipes internationales, des partenaires externes et des fournisseurs ERP. Localisation du poste Localisation du poste France, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Ecully (69) Show more Show less Seniority level Not Applicable Employment type Full-time Job function Information Technology Industries Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Referrals increase your chances of interviewing at SEQENS by 2x See who you know

title: SAP Solution Architect
description: Maak impact met technologie die ertoe doet. Bij Brabant Water werk je aan digitale oplossingen die bijdragen aan betrouwbare drinkwatervoorziening voor 2,7 miljoen mensen. Als Solution Architect geef jij richting aan ons SAP-landschap en leid je de technische koers binnen een hybride team van professionals. Jij verbindt strategie met uitvoering en IT met business, in een omgeving waar innovatie, maatschappelijke relevantie en samenwerking centraal staan. Dit ga je doen Als Solution Architect ben jij de technische spil binnen ons SAP team. Je ontwerpt en bewaakt schaalbare, toekomstbestendige oplossingen binnen het SAP-ecosysteem, met een sterke focus op Asset management en Supply chain management. De functie combineert strategisch inzicht met hands-on leiderschap en vormt de brug tussen business, architectuur en development. Nauwe samenwerking tussen IT en business is daarmee cruciaal voor succes. Jij werkt in je functie nauw samen met collega’s van IT en externe partners. Tot je werkzaamheden behoort onder meer; Uitdragen van technische visie en richting binnen het SAP DevOps-team; Opstellen en bewaken van solution designs en architectuurprincipes; Samenwerken met Product Owners, business analisten, developers, bedrijfsarchitecten en externe partners; Reviewen van technische ontwerpen en maatwerk; Optimalisatie van CI/CD-processen; Kennisoverdracht en mentoring van interne medewerkers; Bewaken van best practices op het gebied van security, performance en schaalbaarheid. Wij bieden jou Bij Brabant Water draait het om onze mensen. Daarom kun je bij ons rekenen op: Een uitdagende functie in een enthousiast team met volop ruimte voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling, eigen ideeën en initiatieven; Een maximumsalaris van € 7.507,- op basis van een 40-urige werkweek. Inclusief vakantiegeld en secundaire arbeidsvoorwaarden kan je salaris oplopen tot € 8.746,- bruto per maand; 31,5 verlofdagen per jaar; Een goede werk-privébalans door een thuiswerkregeling en de mogelijkheid om je eigen werk en werktijden flexibel in te richten; 100% OV-vergoeding of kilometervergoeding (€0,23) Een screening maakt onderdeel uit van de procedure. Jij brengt mee Een afgeronde opleiding op HBO of WO niveau; Minimaal 7 jaar ervaring als SAP specialist op asset management en/of supply chain processen, waarvan 2 jaar in een leidende of architectuur-rol. Ervaring met solution architecture binnen complexe enterprise omgevingen; Sterke communicatieve vaardigheden en stakeholdermanagement; Ervaring met Azure DevOps en Agile (scrum) werken is een pre; Certificeringen binnen SAP EAM en SAP SCM zijn een pre. Voor het succesvol uitvoeren van de functie van SAP Solution Architect is het volledig beheersen van de Nederlandse taal een absolute vereiste. Binnen Brabant Water is Nederlands de voertaal; zowel de samenwerking met collega’s als de communicatie met stakeholders, rapportages en documentatie vinden in het Nederlands plaats. Een uitstekende beheersing van de Nederlandse taal is daarom essentieel om analyses helder over te brengen en optimaal bij te dragen aan de organisatie. Heb je vragen? Neem contact op met Lonneke. Ze helpt je graag verder!
